Creative-AI-Benchmark — exhaustive per-task mapping

Every one of the 66 tasks mapped against the framework: the input assets and how they're admission-scored (Phase 1), the golden trajectory (Phase 2), the expected outputs, and how each output is scored — the task-specific verifier checks (Layer 3), the operation-craft checklist (Layer 2), process & honesty (Layer 4), professional review (Layer 5) — rolled up into the K1–K7 capabilities (Layer 1), plus task-level metrics and the annotator flow.

The Layer-3 verifier checks are expert-authored and adversarially verified — one agent read each brief and wrote the exact checks (real counts, exact strings, precise dimensions), then a second agent audited each list to remove hallucinated requirements and lock the auto/expert + mandatory/dealbreaker/quality tags (1,077 checks across the 66 tasks). The other layers (admission, craft, process, review, capabilities) follow the Creative-AI-Benchmark doc. Brand names are the fictional, licensing-clean versions. Dealbreaker = a fatal miss that caps the score regardless of craft.

This page now also carries the Phase 0 — Task Validation panel (directly below) and a metadata-tag panel on every task (section “0 · Metadata tags”). Phase 0 = Gate A (task quality) + Gate B (metadata correctness); the asset files are validated separately in Phase 1.

Phase 0 — Task Validation

The first filter, run once per scraped task, before it is ever queued for an agent run or scored. Phase 0 judges only the task itself — the brief and its metadata. It does not open the asset files (those are checked in Phase 1 — Asset Validation, a separate stage) and it does not score the agent. A task must clear both gates below to be VALIDATED and move on to Phase 1.

Gate A — Task Quality

Is this a good, clear, doable task a real client would pay for?

9 dimensions, each resolving Pass / Fix / Reject, (code) where a script can decide, (expert) where it needs a human eye.

Gate B — Metadata Correctness

Are the labels describing this task right and consistent?

  • Presence — Every mandatory tag is filled (operation, family, modality, workflow, horizon, est_calls, domain, feasibility, exec-mode, capability-footprint).
  • Valid values — Each tag holds an allowed value (e.g. operation ∈ O1–O8; modality ∈ image/vector/document/video/audio/data).
  • Consistency with the brief — The labels actually describe this task — the operation, modality and deliverable match what the ask asks for.
  • Cross-tag coherence — Tags agree with each other — family is implied by operation; horizon is the right band for est_calls; exec-mode fits the operation.
  • De-duplication — The task is meaningfully distinct from tasks already accepted (corpus-level near-duplicate check).

Gate A — the 9 task-quality dimensions

#DimensionWhat it checks
A1Real-world briefReads like a genuine paid commission — concrete deliverable, plausible audience; not a toy/puzzle/“test-the-AI” prompt.
A2Clear &amp; unambiguousA reader lands on exactly one interpretation — no undefined “make it pop,” no contradictory directives.
A3Tool-feasibleEvery required action maps to a real connector. This is where not-doable tasks are caught — no text-to-image, no from-scratch logo/art with no source asset → feasibility = no, dropped.
A4Self-containedEvery essential input/parameter is in the brief or safely inferable — no “as we discussed” dependency.
A5Measurable outcomeAn objective pass/fail criterion can be written — success is checkable by observation, not pure taste.
A6Single coherent commissionOne unified job (may be multi-step), not several unrelated tasks bundled together.
A7Right difficulty for its horizonNot a one-tool one-liner, not unbounded — step count sits in the H-band for its est_calls.
A8Internally consistentThe brief doesn’t fight itself — counts, dims, dates, prices, references all agree.
A9Rubric-writableWe can draft a concrete, atomic acceptance checklist now — at least one objective verifier per task.

The “Good Task” definition

✓ INCLUDE a task if it…

  • Reads like a real commission someone would pay for
  • Has one clear goal, single obvious reading
  • Fully doable with the agent’s tools (edit, mask, retouch, vectorize, layout, data-merge, template-fill, stock, video/audio)
  • Self-contained — every essential input present or inferable
  • Has an objective acceptance criterion writable in advance
  • A single coherent commission toward one deliverable
  • Sits at the right difficulty for its horizon
  • Meaningfully distinct from accepted tasks

✗ FILTER OUT a task if it…

  • Needs text-to-image / generative image from a prompt (A3 → feasibility = no)
  • Needs a logo / illustration / artwork invented from scratch with no source asset (A3 → no)
  • Is a toy, puzzle, riddle or “test-the-AI” prompt
  • Is ambiguous (2+ readings) or self-contradictory
  • Can’t be done with our tools (banned capability / unreachable system)
  • Missing an essential input that can’t be recovered or defaulted
  • Has no objective success criterion (pure taste)
  • Bundles unrelated jobs
  • Too trivial (one-liner) or too sprawling to finish in its horizon
  • Is a near-duplicate of an accepted task

The metadata tags every task carries (validated at Gate B)

Each task below opens with its own “0 · Metadata tags” panel. The schema: operation (O1–O8) · family · output_modality · workflow_tag (Create/Edit/Analyze) · deliverable_type · horizon_tier (H1–H4) · est_calls (cost proxy) · domain · brand_persona · capability_footprint (which of the 7 scoring buckets the task exercises) · feasibility · execution_mode ([C]/[W]/[A]/[T]) · plus the five 1–5 difficulty axes (planning, brand-strictness, tool-diversity, visual-density, creative-freedom).

Verdict logic: a task is VALIDATED only when Gate A = Pass AND Gate B = Pass → it then passes to Phase 1 — Asset Validation. The verifiers we draft while gating (Gate A9) become the scoring contract — the exact Layer-3 checks run later against the agent’s output (shown per task below). So Phase 0 does double duty: validate the task now, define how it’ll be scored later.

AO-01   Premium Clinical Skincare Brand — Product-Shot Finishing, Logo Vectorization, Per-SKU Label Merge & Print-Ready Packaging Export

Brand/vertical Beauty, Cosmetics & Personal CareFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take our raw skincare hero shot, flat logo mark, texture plate and SKU copy sheet and produce a clinically clean white-background product image set, an editable vector logo, a recommended type system, a packaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the hero (cropped from the pure-white-sweep master), a clinical monochrome variant, per-SKU print labels merged from our label template, and a print-ready packaging PDF.

Full brief

We're launching DERMAVERA, a premium clinically-tested skincare line, and need a cohesive, credible visual identity executed across the product line (3 launch SKUs: a Vitamin-C Serum, a Barrier Repair Cream, and a Gentle Gel Cleanser). The look must be clean, minimalist, modern, science-driven and trustworthy — premium without being flashy, professional without looking pharmaceutical. We are supplying: (1) one raw hero photograph of the Serum bottle shot on a near-white sweep (slightly tilted, mixed lighting, cluttered edges); (2) a flat high-res raster of our chosen logo mark (a minimal molecule/droplet monogram + "DERMAVERA" wordmark) that we need delivered as a clean editable vector; (3) a soft lifestyle texture plate (matte stone surface) for collateral grounding; (4) a label-copy spreadsheet with one row per SKU (name, claim, volume, INCI line, batch placeholder, hex accent). We also supply our pre-authored print templates: an Illustrator label template (label.ai) with genuine Variables, and an InDesign unit-carton template (carton.indd). Deliverables: a flawless clinical white-background hero image (straightened, background swept to pure white, neutral clinical color, crisp product), a 1:1 e-commerce crop and a packaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the hero cropped to the target 5000×2800px (the pure-white-sweep master crop-and-resized to the wider ratio, centered on the product — sweep trimmed top/bottom, no canvas added), a clinical monochrome (single-accent duotone) brand variant of the hero, the logo delivered as an editable SVG, a recommended display+text type pairing, a print-ready per-SKU label set generated by merging our copy sheet into label.ai, and a print-ready packaging PDF exported from carton.indd. Everything must be print-ready and leave us editable source (vector SVG + the authored .ai/.indd). Maintain consistent neutral, trustworthy color across every image.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain beauty-cosmeticsDeliverable product-shotWorkflow Create+EditOperation O2Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 18Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[T]Brand DERMAVERA
5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity3Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Brand Alignment · Technical Quality · Creative Quality · Process · Honesty. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.pngimageFinished clinical hero: auto-straightened, background swept to pure #FFFFFF, neutral-graded (auto-tone + neutralized color temp + lifted highlights), crisp product. Full-res ~4000x5000px PNG, sRGB, e-commerce white-bg standard.
dermavera_hero_1x1_ecom.pngimage1:1 square e-commerce crop of the finished hero, product centered with clinical whitespace, 2048x2048px PNG on pure white.
dermavera_hero_packaging_wide.pngimagePackaging-safe wide landscape reframe of the finished hero: the pure-white-sweep hero is crop-and-resized to a 5000x2800px landscape ratio centered on the product (portrait frame trimmed top/bottom to hit the wider ratio — no canvas added, no pixels invented). Because the source sweep is already pure #FFFFFF, the reframed landscape reads as a clean white field for carton/hero-plate placement. ~5000x2800px PNG, sRGB.
dermavera_hero_mono_variant.pngimageClinical monochrome brand variant of the finished hero — single deep slate-teal (#16403B) duotone/monochromatic tint, for collateral. Same resolution as hero, PNG.
dermavera_logo.svgvectorEditable vector SVG of the supplied logo mark, clean single-color paths, transparent background — the editable source the brief demands.
dermavera_type_system.jsondataRecommended display + text typeface pairing (PostScript names + rationale) from font_recommend, to anchor the visual-identity typography spec.
dermavera_labels_merged.pdfpdfPrint-ready per-SKU label set: 3 label artboards (one per SKU row) produced by merging the copy CSV into label.ai. CMYK, 3mm bleed, crop marks. Vector + editable source = label.ai retained.
dermavera_carton_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready unit-carton PDF exported from carton.indd with the finished white-bg hero plate placed in HERO_PLATE, CMYK, 3mm bleed, registration/crop marks.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 18 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All 9 named deliverables are present in the output setautoExactly these 9 deliverables exist: dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.png, dermavera_hero_1x1_ecom.png, dermavera_hero_packaging_wide.png, dermavera_hero_mono_variant.png, dermavera_logo.svg, dermavera_type_system.json, dermavera_labels_merged.pdf, dermavera_carton_print.pdf, and the dermavera_brand_board_deeplink URLK1mandatoryoutput
The clinical hero background is swept to pure whiteautoIn dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.png the sweep/background region sampled away from the product reads #FFFFFF (RGB 255,255,255) with no residual yellow-green castK1mandatoryoutput
The clinical hero is delivered at full e-commerce resolution as sRGB PNGautodermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.png is a PNG in sRGB at approximately 4000x5000px (within ~5% on each side)K1mandatoryoutput
The 1:1 e-commerce crop is exactly square at the specified pixel dimensionsautodermavera_hero_1x1_ecom.png is a PNG measuring exactly 2048x2048px on pure whiteK1mandatoryoutput
The packaging-safe wide canvas matches the specified outpainted dimensions with the white sweep extendedautodermavera_hero_packaging_wide.png is a PNG at approximately 5000x2800px (within ~5% on each side) with the white sweep extended left/right beyond the original hero frameK1mandatoryoutput
The monochrome variant is a single deep slate-teal duotone in the exact brand accent at hero resolutionexpertdermavera_hero_mono_variant.png reads as a single-color monochromatic/duotone tint keyed to slate-teal #16403B (not full color, not greyscale), at the same resolution as dermavera_hero_whitebg_clinical.pngK2mandatorybrand
The logo is delivered as an editable vector SVG with transparent backgroundautodermavera_logo.svg is a valid SVG containing vector <path> geometry (not an embedded raster bitmap) with a transparent/no background fillK1dealbreakeroutput
The vectorized logo preserves the supplied mark and wordmark faithfullyexpertdermavera_logo.svg reproduces the supplied molecule/droplet monogram glyph and the legible 'DERMAVERA' wordmark in a single slate-teal ink, with clean crisp paths and no regenerated/altered letterforms or invented elementsK2dealbreakerinput
The type system recommends a display + text pairing with PostScript names plus rationaleautodermavera_type_system.json contains a display typeface and a text typeface, each given by PostScript name, plus a rationale fieldK1mandatoryoutput
The recommended type pairing fits the clinical/premium identity briefexpertThe display+text pairing in dermavera_type_system.json reads as clean, minimalist, modern, science-driven and premium-not-luxurious with strong typography, consistent with the stated DERMAVERA aestheticK4qualitybrief
The merged label PDF contains exactly one label artboard per SKU rowautodermavera_labels_merged.pdf has exactly 3 label artboards/pages, one per CSV row (Vitamin-C Brightening Serum, Barrier Repair Cream, Gentle Gel Cleanser)K1mandatoryinput
Each merged label shows the correct per-SKU copy from the CSV verbatimautoVia OCR each of the 3 labels shows its row's sku_name, claim, volume and inci_line verbatim — e.g. the Serum label shows 'Vitamin-C Brightening Serum', '15% L-Ascorbic Acid — clinically tested', '30 ml e', and 'Aqua, Ascorbic Acid, Propylene Glycol, Ferulic Acid, Tocopherol'K1mandatoryinput
The merged label PDF is print-ready CMYK with 3mm bleed and crop marks on 70x90mm artboardsautodermavera_labels_merged.pdf is CMYK with 3mm bleed and crop marks present on 70x90mm label artboardsK1dealbreakeroutput
The carton PDF is exported from the authored carton.indd with the finished hero placed in HERO_PLATEexpertdermavera_carton_print.pdf is the 5-panel unit carton from carton.indd showing the finished white-bg hero plate in the HERO_PLATE front-panel frame and the placed DERMAVERA logo, exported (not a re-composed or regenerated layout)K6mandatoryoutput
The carton PDF is print-ready CMYK with bleed and registration/crop marksautodermavera_carton_print.pdf is CMYK with 3mm bleed and registration + crop marks presentK1dealbreakeroutput
Neutral, trustworthy color is consistent across every produced hero imageexpertThe clinical hero, the 1:1 crop and the wide canvas share one neutral clinical color balance with no warm/cool cast drift between themK4qualitybrief
The Firefly board deep-link assembles the full system for sign-offautodermavera_brand_board_deeplink is a working Firefly Board URL collecting the finished hero, 1:1 crop, wide canvas, mono variant, vector logo and label proofs onto one boardK1mandatoryoutput
The editable source files demanded by the brief are retained and deliveredautoThe delivery retains the editable sources alongside the print-ready PDFs: the vector dermavera_logo.svg plus the authored label.ai and carton.inddK1mandatorybrief

Layer 2 · Operation craft checklist — Photo & Image (expert marks Pass / Minor / Major vs reference images)

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost
Colour & white balanceneutral / on-brand; tones naturalslight castclear cast / wrong colour
Mask & edge qualityclean edges; fine detail (hair) keptsmall halo / jaggiesvisible halo / clipped subject
Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

Layer 4 · Process & honesty — from the agent's step-log (code counts + expert mark)

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds
Planningsensible order, deps ready firsta few wasted / out-of-order stepschaotic, no planK6
Tool useright tool & settingsoccasional wrong choice, self-correctedrepeatedly wrongK6
State managementalways uses latest filesone stale file usedwrong / old assets throughoutK6
Recoverydetects & fixes failuresrecovers slowly / clumsilyrepeats the same failureK6
Verificationchecks output before submitpartial checknever verifiesK6
Honesty (impossible asks)flags / refuses / discloseshedges but proceedsfabricates or hidesK7
Self-calibrationpredicted result matches realityslightly over/under-confidentconfidently wrongK7

Layer 5 · Professional review — the human verdict (each a fixed choice + one-line reason)

instrumentwhat the expert doesproduces
The callaccept / send-back / scrap (+ one-line reason)feeds the commission pass rate
Punch-listrevision notes tagged blocker / fix / nitpicka cost-to-ship number
Pin-the-flawclick the spot, pick the defect, tag severity (Critical/Major/Minor)localized, weighted defect labels
Blind face-offtwo outputs side by side: which would you send the client?stacks into craft ELO
ConfidenceHigh / Medium / Low on the call — one taplets buyers filter / down-weight

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-02   Performance-ready Meta ad product creatives: white-bg clean-up, brand color grade, multi-ratio reframe set, and vectorized logo for paid-social testing

Brand/vertical E-commerce, Retail & ProductFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's raw product shot and logo into a performance-ready Meta ad creative kit: a retouched pure-white-bg hero, a brand-graded variant, three platform-ratio frames (1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16) built by honestly cropping/reframing the graded hero to each ratio, a vectorized logo, and a font direction to steer the handed-off typeset step.

Full brief

DONE is a DTC homeware brand launching paid-social tests for its matte-ceramic pour-over coffee dripper and needs a freelancer to build the imagery layer of a Meta (Facebook + Instagram) ad-creative kit, faithful to the brand guidelines (brand teal #0F6E64 on warm off-white #F4EFE7). You are supplied three client assets: (1) a raw studio product photo of the dripper on a near-white sweep that still shows seamless-paper shadows and a faint gray cast; (2) the DONE wordmark logo as a flat PNG; and (3) the brand-guideline sheet as a PDF for reference. Build the production chain entirely on Adobe connector tools: ingest the assets into a working folder; auto-correct the studio shot's geometry and tone; isolate the product and force a true, pure-white e-commerce background (catalog-clean, no gray cast, no stray shadow halo); push a brand-consistent color grade so the ceramic reads warm and premium and the teal accents stay on-palette; then generate the platform frames performance marketers actually test — a 1:1 feed square (1080x1080), a 4:5 portrait feed (1080x1350), and a 9:16 Stories/Reels vertical (1080x1920) — by honestly reframing/cropping the graded hero to each target ratio, keeping the product centered and accepting that the studio edges/background trim away to hit the ratio (no canvas is added, padded, or extended). Separately, vectorize the supplied wordmark to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at any ad size. Out of scope for this connector pass (hand off to the typesetting/Express step): baking headline/offer/CTA copy and the logo lockup onto the frames, and choosing final on-image type — though you should return a font recommendation to steer that. Deliverables: the pure-white hero, the brand-graded hero, the three ratio frames (1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16), the vector logo, and the font direction.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
done_hero_pure_white.pngimageRetouched product hero on a true pure-white (#FFFFFF) e-commerce background, product isolated with clean edge, no gray cast / no shadow halo, straightened and auto-toned, ~4000x3000px PNG.
done_hero_brand_graded.jpgimageBrand-consistent color-graded hero: warm premium ceramic, on-palette teal accents (HSL nudged toward #0F6E64), exposure/contrast/temperature tuned for paid-social punch, high-quality JPEG.
done_creative_1x1.jpgimage1:1 feed square (1080x1080): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 1:1 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; the near-white studio edges/background trim to hit the square ratio). No canvas added, no generation.
done_creative_4x5.jpgimage4:5 portrait feed (1080x1350): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 4:5 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; edges/background trim to hit the portrait ratio). No canvas added, no generation.
done_creative_9x16.jpgimage9:16 Stories/Reels vertical (1080x1920): the graded hero honestly reframed/cropped to a centered 9:16 crop of the existing frame (subject centered; the wider studio edges/background trim away to hit the tall vertical ratio). No canvas added, no generation — final on-image copy/CTA is applied in the handed-off typeset step.
done_logo_vector.svgvectorClean vectorized DONE wordmark + dripper glyph as SVG, scalable crisp for any ad size, solid brand-teal paths on transparent.
done_font_direction.jsondatafont_recommend output: geometric-sans headline + humanist-sans body pairing matching the brand-guideline type note, to steer the handed-off on-image typesetting step.

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-03   E-commerce white-background product retouch suite + vectorized brand mark

Brand/vertical E-commerce, Retail & ProductFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn a client's raw, messy-background product photo into a pure-white-background marketplace catalog image and convert their rough hand-drawn logo sketch into a clean, brand-colored, scalable vector logo — then package everything for delivery.

Full brief

Our small skincare-and-homeware shop is scaling onto Amazon, Etsy and our own Shopify store and we badly need visual credibility — right now our listings use phone snaps shot on a kitchen table and they look amateur, which is killing conversion. We can't afford a studio day, so we're sending you what we have: RAW product captures (a 24MP amber-glass serum bottle shot handheld under window light on a cluttered wooden table, slightly tilted) and a rough logo: our founder hand-inked our brand mark "AURELIA BOTANICALS" on paper and we photographed it with a phone — it's wonky, grey-ish, on off-white paper. We need you to do the full product-photographer post-production pass and the logo cleanup: PRODUCT IMAGE (do this as a repeatable recipe — we'll send 10-15 more bottles and jars that all must match): - Level the horizon (the table edge is tilted). - Cut the bottle off the messy table and put it on a PURE WHITE (#FFFFFF) seamless background, which is the hard requirement for Amazon's main-image policy. - Deliver it as a square 2000×2000 px marketplace canvas with the product centered. - Make it look professional: balanced exposure and color, true-to-life pop on the amber glass, and recover the blown-out highlights/hot spots from the window light so the glass reads cleanly. LOGO: - Take our phone photo of the inked sketch and turn it into a CLEAN, infinitely scalable vector (SVG) so it works on a 16px favicon and a 3-metre trade-show banner alike. - Recolor the mark to our brand green (deep botanical green ~#1E5E3A). - Recommend 2-3 fonts that pair with the hand-drawn mark for the "AURELIA BOTANICALS" wordmark so we can set the lockup ourselves. DELIVERY: put the final catalog image, the transparent-background product cut-out, the vector logo and the recolored logo into a clean, clearly-named delivery folder we can pull straight into our listings and brand kit. Keep the white truly white — no grey cast — and keep the logo edges crisp at any size.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
AURELIA_serum_2000x2000_white.pngimageFinal marketplace catalog hero: square 2000×2000 px, PNG, sRGB, product centered on a pure-white #FFFFFF seamless background (Amazon main-image compliant). Straightened, background-removed, white-filled, cropped/resized to square, auto-toned, vibrance-corrected and highlight-recovered. This is the fully-worked hero and the repeatable recipe for the other 10-15 SKUs (step 10).
AURELIA_serum_cutout_transparent.pngimageTransparent-background product cut-out: 24MP-native resolution PNG with alpha, sRGB, the amber-glass serum bottle isolated off the cluttered table with a clean subject edge, no white fill applied. Intended for reuse in listings and composites (step 4).
AURELIA_logo_vector.svgvectorNeutral (untinted) vector logo traced from the founder's raster ink sketch via image_vectorize: a true scalable SVG with crisp paths, safe from a 16px favicon to a 3-metre banner. Retains the sketch's original grey-black ink tone (step 13).
AURELIA_logo_brandgreen.svgvectorBrand-colored vector logo in botanical green (~#1E5E3A): the neutral AURELIA_logo_vector SVG (step 13) recolored via image_apply_color_overlay so the scalable SVG carries the brand green for the brand kit (step 14).

Font pairing note recommends 2-3 fonts for the AURELIA BOTANICALS wordmarkautofont_pairing_recommendations.txt names between 2 and 3 specific fonts recommended to pair with the hand-drawn mark for the 'AURELIA BOTANICALS' wordmark lockupK1mandatoryoutput

  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-04   Silver Jewelry iPhone Shots to White-Background E-commerce Set (JPEG + transparent PNG)

Brand/vertical Fashion & ApparelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 15)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn a handheld iPhone shot of a silver jewellery piece into a clean, colour-accurate, uniform-white e-commerce set: a 3000x3000 sRGB JPEG plus a matching transparent PNG, ready to slot straight into the online store.

Full brief

I run a silver jewellery line and need my iPhone-captured product frames turned into a polished, store-ready e-commerce set through AI-powered post-production. The look is minimalist and clean: crisp, shadow-controlled pieces on a pure or near-white backdrop, accurate metal tone and true gemstone colour, pin-sharp on every hero angle, with no colour cast — so they drop straight into my online store without further retouching. Work this one representative hero frame end-to-end as the master recipe, then I will reuse the exact same chain on the rest of the line (the full order is 20 pieces). Start from the original iPhone HEIC/RAW file I supply. First fix the small handheld tilt and set a neutral baseline exposure and white balance, because the iPhone leaves a warm cast that throws off the silver and the gemstone. Then isolate the jewellery itself, drive the background to a uniform pure white with no cast, and grade the piece so the metal highlights read clean-silver and the stone's colour is faithful. Deliverables for this frame: one finished JPEG at exactly 3000 x 3000 px, sRGB, on uniform white; and one matching PNG of the same piece on a fully transparent background for layering on coloured store sections. Reframe to a true 1:1 square by cropping the existing uniform-white frame to square bounds around the centred piece (do not stretch or distort the piece), then export at the exact pixel spec. Finally, organise the outputs into a tidy delivery folder for client download. Acceptance: uniform white background, no colour cast; accurate metal tone and gemstone colour; pin-sharp focus on the hero angle; exact 3000 x 3000 sRGB JPEG plus matching transparent PNG.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.

6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 15 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
silver_ring_hero_3000.jpgimage3000x3000 px, 1:1 square, sRGB, JPEG under 2 MB, uniform pure-white background, neutral colour (no cast), accurate silver metal tone and faithful sapphire colour, pin-sharp on the band. Web-ready e-commerce hero.
silver_ring_hero_transparent.pngimageMatching PNG of the same graded piece on a fully transparent background (alpha), same grade as the JPEG, for layering on coloured store sections.
delivery_folderdataAsset folder structure containing the finished JPEG, the transparent PNG, and the original supplied iPhone file, named for client download.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The finished JPEG deliverable silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg has pixel dimensions of exactly 3000 x 3000 (true 1:1 square).autoImage width == 3000 px AND height == 3000 px as read from silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg.K1mandatoryoutput
silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg is encoded as JPEG in the sRGB colour space.autosilver_ring_hero_3000.jpg file format == JPEG AND the colour profile is sRGB (embedded sRGB profile, or untagged/assumed-sRGB with no conflicting profile such as Display P3 or Adobe RGB).K1dealbreakeroutput
The finished JPEG file size is under 2 MB.autoOn-disk size of silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg < 2,097,152 bytes (2 MB).K1mandatoryoutput
A matching PNG of the same piece, silver_ring_hero_transparent.png, exists with a genuinely transparent background.autosilver_ring_hero_transparent.png is PNG format AND has an alpha channel containing fully transparent (alpha == 0) pixels in the background region surrounding the jewellery.K1mandatoryoutput
The JPEG background is a uniform pure / near-white with no colour cast.autoSampled background pixels of silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg are near RGB 255/255/255 (each channel >= 250) AND the three channels are balanced (max channel spread <= 5) so there is no warm/cool tint.K1dealbreakeroutput
The PNG carries the identical grade as the JPEG (same colour-corrected graded master), differing only in background.expertWhen silver_ring_hero_transparent.png is composited on white, the silver metal tone and sapphire colour visually match silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg (both derive from the same graded master, not separately graded).K2mandatoryoutput
The warm iPhone white-balance cast is neutralised so the silver reads as clean, neutral silver.expertReviewer confirms the metal highlights/mid-tones read as neutral clean-silver with no residual warm/yellow cast carried from the input frame.K4mandatorybrief
The faceted deep-blue sapphire renders as a faithful, true blue gemstone colour.expertReviewer confirms the sapphire reads as an accurate deep-blue (selectively saturated correctly, not washed out, oversaturated, or hue-shifted).K4mandatorybrief
The ring band is pin-sharp on the hero angle.expertReviewer confirms the silver band is tack-sharp / in crisp focus with no softening, blur, or destructive artefacts introduced by the editing chain.K4mandatorybrief
The ~2-3 degree handheld tilt is corrected so the piece is geometrically straight.expertReviewer confirms the piece and the implied horizon are level, with no visible residual tilt.K3qualitybrief
The square 1:1 frame was reached by extending the white canvas, not by stretching or distorting the ring.expertReviewer confirms the ring retains its original undistorted proportions and the extra area is added uniform white canvas (no anamorphic stretch to fill the square).K3mandatorybrief
The cut-out edge between the jewellery and the white/transparent background is clean.expertReviewer confirms the subject mask edge is precise around the ring and stone with no white halo, fringing, or chewed-away metal/prongs.K3qualitybrief
A delivery folder is assembled containing the finished JPEG, the transparent PNG, and the original supplied iPhone file.autodelivery_folder contains exactly the three named assets: silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg, silver_ring_hero_transparent.png, and iphone_silver_ring_hero.heic.K6mandatoryoutput
A Firefly Board deep-link is produced assembling the graded master frame(s) for client approval.autoreview_board_deeplink resolves to a Firefly Board URL that contains the graded master and finished hero frame (silver_ring_hero_3000.jpg) for one-click client review.K6mandatoryoutput
The uniform white backdrop was produced non-generatively (select piece -> invert -> solid-white fill), not via generative fill or prompt-based background replacement.expertWorkflow evidence shows the background was driven to white via image_select_by_prompt -> image_invert_selection -> image_fill_area (solid white RGB 255/255/255), with no generative-fill or background-replace-by-prompt step used.K7mandatorybrief
The original supplied iphone_silver_ring_hero.heic is preserved and delivered unaltered alongside the edited outputs.autoThe original iPhone file delivered in delivery_folder is byte-identical to the supplied input iphone_silver_ring_hero.heic (not the straightened/graded version).K2mandatoryinput

AO-06   White-Background Catalog Cleanup for a Firearms/Optics/Outdoor Shopify Store

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious & CommunityFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 13)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take a batch of inconsistent distributor-supplied product photos for a firearms/optics/outdoor Shopify store and turn them into clean, straightened, color-corrected, pure-white-background, square marketplace hero images (plus transparent-PNG tile variants and a vectorized listing badge).

Full brief

I run a hands-off Shopify store selling thermal cameras, night-vision optics, firearms, ammunition, trail-camera mounts and hunting accessories. My distributor feeds dump product photos that are all over the place: shot on off-white or gray seamless, slightly tilted, warm/yellow color cast, and framed inconsistently — they look unprofessional next to each other on a collection page and several get rejected by marketplace channels that require a pure-white background. As part of the catalog cleanup I need a repeatable Adobe pipeline that takes one raw distributor photo and produces marketplace-ready listing assets, demonstrated end-to-end on my hero SKU (a handheld thermal weapon scope) and ready to repeat across the catalog. For the hero SKU, starting from the single raw distributor JPEG I provide, deliver: (1) a corrected master — auto-straightened, cropped to the product, exposure/tone normalized, and the warm color cast neutralized; (2) a pure #FFFFFF background version where the scope is isolated and the messy seamless is replaced with solid white per Shopify/Amazon hero spec; (3) a transparent-PNG cut-out of the same scope for collection tiles and bundle mock-ups; (4) a square 1:1 hero produced by cropping the white-background hero to a centered 1:1 square (the source frame is landscape, so the square is a crop of the existing image — no canvas is invented or extended) and then resized/exported at exactly 2048×2048. Also vectorize the small distributor brand badge I supply (a flat single-color logo mark) into a clean SVG so it scales on the listing, and recommend a clean sans-serif catalog typeface for the store. Constraints: backgrounds must be solid pure white (no gradient, no AI-invented scene), the product must not be distorted or recolored beyond honest color-cast correction, and nothing may be composited in or generatively painted onto the product itself. Keep my color/tone edits faithful to the real product (a firearm optic — accuracy matters to buyers).

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
scope_master_corrected.jpgimageStraightened, cropped-to-product, auto-toned, color-cast-neutralized master JPEG of the thermal scope, sRGB, high quality. The clean base every other deliverable derives from.
scope_whitebg_hero.jpgimagePure #FFFFFF solid-background hero of the isolated scope, marketplace-compliant (no gradient, no scene), sRGB JPEG.
scope_cutout.pngimageTransparent-background PNG cut-out of the scope for collection tiles / bundle mock-ups, edges clean, alpha channel.
scope_hero_square_2048.jpgimageSquare 1:1 Shopify/Amazon hero: the white-background hero is cropped to a centered 1:1 square (a crop of the existing landscape frame — no canvas extension or generation), then resized and exported at exactly 2048x2048, sRGB JPEG.
brand_badge.svgvectorClean scalable SVG of the distributor brand mark vectorized from the flat PNG, for crisp display at any listing size.
catalog_typeface_recommendationdataA recommended clean sans-serif catalog/listing typeface (name + rationale) from font_recommend.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
scope_master_corrected.jpg deliverable is present as an sRGB JPEGautoA file named scope_master_corrected.jpg exists in the deliverable set; format == JPEG and color space == sRGBK1mandatoryoutput
Corrected master is straightened, cropped to product, and exposure/tone normalizedexpertIn scope_master_corrected.jpg the thermal scope sits level (the original ~4-degree clockwise tilt is removed and the product axis is square to the frame), the loose/uneven empty margins are trimmed to the product bounds, and exposure/contrast is normalized (not under- or over-exposed)K3mandatorybrief
Warm tungsten color cast is neutralized toward neutral white balanceexpertThe warm yellow-orange tungsten cast present in the raw input is corrected toward a neutral white balance in scope_master_corrected.jpg (background reads neutral, no residual yellow-orange tint)K4mandatorybrief
scope_whitebg_hero.jpg has a pure #FFFFFF solid background with no gradientautoSampling the background region (outside the scope) of scope_whitebg_hero.jpg returns RGB (255,255,255) / hex #FFFFFF across the whole field, with no gradient and no off-white pixels; file is an sRGB JPEGK1dealbreakerbrief
White-bg hero background is solid white only — no AI-invented scene or composited elementexpertThe background of scope_whitebg_hero.jpg is a flat solid-white field with no painted scene, props, gradient, or composited objects; nothing has been generatively invented behind the productK7dealbreakerbrief
scope_cutout.png is a transparent-background PNG cut-out of the scopeautoscope_cutout.png exists, is PNG format with an alpha channel, and the background region is fully transparent (alpha == 0) while the scope pixels are opaque (alpha == 255)K1mandatoryoutput
Cut-out mask edges are clean around the scopeexpertIn scope_cutout.png the alpha edge follows the scope silhouette cleanly — no leftover seamless/halo fringe, and no chewed-away product detail (rail, eyepiece, control buttons intact)K3qualityoutput
scope_hero_square_2048.jpg is exactly 2048x2048 pixels, square 1:1, sRGB JPEGautoscope_hero_square_2048.jpg dimensions == 2048 x 2048 (1:1 square), format == JPEG, color space == sRGBK1dealbreakeroutput
Square hero was made by outpainting the white field only, not by painting onto the productexpertscope_hero_square_2048.jpg fills the square canvas by extending the pure-white field around the scope; the background remains solid white with no invented scene, and nothing is generatively painted onto or added to the product bodyK7mandatorybrief
brand_badge.svg is a true vector SVG (not an embedded raster image)autobrand_badge.svg exists, is valid SVG markup containing vector path/shape elements, and does NOT consist of (or merely wrap) an embedded raster <image> elementK2mandatoryoutput
Vectorized badge faithfully reproduces the supplied flat single-color brand markexpertbrand_badge.svg visually matches the supplied distributor logo from distributor_brand_badge.png — the reticle-and-mountain emblem with its wordmark beneath, in a single flat dark-charcoal color, same shapes, crisp edges that scale cleanly, with no added gradients, no photographic texture, and no invented elementsK2mandatoryinput
A clean sans-serif catalog typeface recommendation is providedexpertcatalog_typeface_recommendation names a specific sans-serif typeface (sourced from font_recommend) plus a rationale suited to a clean product-listing/catalog useK5mandatoryoutput
A Firefly review board assembling the hero set is deliveredautohero_review_board is a Firefly Board deep-link/URL that assembles the corrected master, white-bg hero, cut-out, and square hero for one-click owner approvalK6mandatoryoutput
Cleaned_Catalog_Assets/ folder contains the organized finished deliverablesautoA CC asset folder named 'Cleaned_Catalog_Assets/' exists and contains the finished SKU deliverables copied in for handoff: scope_master_corrected.jpg, scope_whitebg_hero.jpg, scope_cutout.png, scope_hero_square_2048.jpg, and brand_badge.svgK6mandatoryoutput
All four hero image deliverables depict the same single thermal scope SKU consistentlyexpertscope_master_corrected.jpg, scope_whitebg_hero.jpg, scope_cutout.png, and scope_hero_square_2048.jpg all show the same handheld thermal weapon scope from the supplied raw input — same product, consistent correction across the set, with no deliverable distorted in proportionK3mandatorybrief
No deliverable distorts, recolors, or composites onto the product itselfexpertAcross all image deliverables the scope is not stretched/warped, is not recolored beyond honest color-cast correction (true matte-black body and materials preserved), and has nothing composited or generatively painted onto the product body — only background and canvas operations were appliedK7dealbreakerbrief

AO-07   Men's pendant line: vectorize the 2D spec sketch + retouch and catalog-prep the sample studio shots

Brand/vertical Jewelry, Watches & AccessoriesFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 23)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

From the industrial designer's raster pendant sketch and two mixed-lighting workbench photos of a stainless-steel sample pendant and chain, produce a clean factory-ready vector spec asset plus retouched, isolated, color-accurate catalog hero shots (one expanded to a catalog canvas, one duotone print-screen variant) as a coordinated set for the design team.

Full brief

From the industrial designer's raster pendant sketch and two mixed-lighting workbench photos of a stainless-steel sample pendant and chain, produce a clean factory-ready vector spec asset plus retouched, isolated, color-accurate catalog hero shots (one reframed and resized to a roomy catalog hero canvas, one duotone print-screen variant), delivered as a coordinated set for the design team's refinements-and-iterations review.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

5Planning2Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Technical Quality · Process · Creative Quality · Honesty. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
pendant_sketch_raster.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat 2D technical line-art drawing of a men's pendant, front elevation: a rounded-square stainless-steel pendant rough
sample_pendant_on_bench.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal close-up product reference photo of a single brushed stainless-steel men's pendant (rounded-square, ~30mm, wit
sample_chain_on_bench.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal product reference photo of a men's stainless-steel box-link chain (about 4mm wide) laid out in a loose S-curve

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 23 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
pendant_spec_lineart.svgvectorClean scalable SVG of the deskewed, tight-cropped pendant line-art, crisp at any print size, suitable as a dimensional spec reference for the factory.
pendant_hero_catalog.pngimageRetouched, white-balanced, auto-toned sample pendant cut out from the bench onto a clean solid catalog background and reframe-cropped/expanded to a roomy catalog hero canvas (e.g. ~4:5 or wider). PNG.
pendant_spec_screenprint.pngimageHigh-contrast duotone/halftone 'print-screen' variant of the hero pendant for the black-and-white spec sheet. PNG.
chain_hero_catalog.pngimageThe box-link chain color-matched to the pendant (same neutral white balance + tonal treatment) and isolated on the same clean catalog background, so pendant and chain read as a coordinated set. PNG.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All five expected deliverables are present: pendant_spec_lineart.svg, pendant_hero_catalog.png, pendant_spec_screenprint.png, chain_hero_catalog.png, and the pendant_line_review_board Firefly board deep-link.autoAll five deliverables are delivered: the four files pendant_spec_lineart.svg, pendant_hero_catalog.png, pendant_spec_screenprint.png, chain_hero_catalog.png, plus the pendant_line_review_board Firefly board deep-link; missing any one of the five fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The pendant spec deliverable pendant_spec_lineart.svg is a valid scalable vector SVG (not a raster PNG/JPG renamed) containing vector path/shape line work.autoFile parses as well-formed SVG (MIME image/svg+xml, .svg extension) containing vector path/shape elements; it is not an embedded raster or a renamed bitmap.K1dealbreakeroutput
The three image deliverables (pendant_hero_catalog.png, pendant_spec_screenprint.png, chain_hero_catalog.png) are each valid PNG files.autoEach of the three image deliverables has a .png extension and a valid PNG file signature.K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized pendant line-art in pendant_spec_lineart.svg is deskewed (square/upright), correcting the input scan's ~4-degree clockwise rotation.expertThe pendant outline sits square to the canvas axes with no residual ~4-degree clockwise skew from the original scan (auto_straighten applied before vectorize).K3mandatorybrief
The vectorized pendant line-art is tight-cropped to the pendant artwork, with the scanned white paper margin/border removed.expertpendant_spec_lineart.svg bounds hug the pendant line-art with no large surrounding white scan margin or paper border retained.K3mandatorybrief
pendant_spec_lineart.svg preserves the sketch's drawn features: rounded-square body, soldered top bail loop, interior compass-rose engraving lines, and edge dimension tick marks, as clean black line work.expertThe vector reproduces the rounded-square pendant outline, soldered top bail loop, fine interior compass-rose engraving, and edge dimension tick marks as clean black line work without fabricating or dropping any of these spec features.K2mandatoryinput
The pendant in pendant_hero_catalog.png is cleanly isolated from the busy machinist's bench background (no calipers, layout square, or metal shavings remaining).expertNo bench-clutter elements (calipers, layout square, metal shavings, workbench surface) are visible; the pendant is cleanly masked with no haloing or clipped edges.K3mandatorybrief
pendant_hero_catalog.png places the cut-out pendant on a clean solid light-gray catalog background (solid-color fill), not on a composited photographic scene.expertThe background behind the pendant is a single clean solid catalog fill (light gray, consistent with image_fill_area solid-color fill), not another photo composited in.K1mandatoryoutput
pendant_hero_catalog.png is outpainted/expanded to a roomy catalog hero canvas (~4:5 portrait or wider), not a tight subject-filling crop.autoImage aspect ratio is approximately 4:5 (portrait) or wider, with generous catalog space around the pendant, not a tight subject-filling crop.K1mandatoryoutput
The pendant's mixed warm-shop / cool-window yellow-green color cast is neutralized so the stainless steel reads as clean neutral white-balanced metal in pendant_hero_catalog.png.expertThe metal appears neutral / cool-neutral with no residual yellow-green cast; white balance reads corrected versus the off-neutral input photo.K4mandatorybrief
Brushed-steel highlight detail is retained (not clipped to pure white) in the toned pendant hero.expertBright areas on the pendant keep brushed-metal grain/texture rather than blowing out to flat clipped white.K4qualitybrief
pendant_spec_screenprint.png is a high-contrast duotone/halftone print-screen variant of the same pendant as the hero, suitable for the black-and-white spec sheet.expertThe image is a high-contrast halftone/duotone (effectively B&W print-screen) rendition of the same pendant shown in pendant_hero_catalog.png, not a full-color photo.K1mandatoryoutput
chain_hero_catalog.png shows the box-link chain isolated from the bench on the SAME clean solid light-gray catalog background used for the pendant hero.expertThe chain is cleanly cut out (no bench clutter) and placed on the identical solid light-gray catalog background fill as pendant_hero_catalog.png.K3mandatoryoutput
The chain in chain_hero_catalog.png is color-matched to the pendant: same neutralized white balance and tonal treatment so the pair reads as one coordinated set.expertThe chain's metal white-balance and tonal grade visibly match the pendant hero (same neutral steel cast), so pendant and chain read as a consistent coordinated pair, not two differently-graded shots.K4mandatorybrief
The pendant_line_review_board Firefly board assembles all four produced assets (vector spec, catalog hero, screen-print variant, chain hero) into one reviewable deep-link.expertThe Firefly board deep-link opens and contains all four deliverables (pendant_spec_lineart.svg, pendant_hero_catalog.png, pendant_spec_screenprint.png, chain_hero_catalog.png) gathered for the design team's refinements-and-iterations review.K6mandatoryoutput
No 3D/CAD model is fabricated and no deliverable is invented from scratch; the response delivers only the Adobe-doable 2D asset-prep slice using only generative_expand as the generative step.expertDeliverables are derived from the supplied sketch and two bench photos (deskew/crop/vectorize, grade/mask/solid-fill/outpaint/halftone); no invented 3D CAD render is presented as if produced, and image_generative_expand (the hero outpaint) is the ONLY generative step used.K7dealbreakerbrief

Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost

Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-08   Signature Bracelet Launch: Studio Retouch, White-Background E-Comm Set, Graded Hero, Expanded Social Banner & Vectorized Mark

Brand/vertical Jewelry, Watches & AccessoriesFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the brand's raw studio prototype shots, wordmark, and lifestyle frame into a launch image kit: a clean white-background e-commerce set, a color-graded close-up hero, an expanded social banner, a vectorized wordmark, and a licensed lifestyle backdrop for the social team.

Full brief

We are launching the signature bracelet for our new lifestyle and cultural brand (gathering, celebration, connection, memorable experiences). Prototypes are made and we have shot them in the studio, but the raw frames are crooked, dull, and shot on a slightly grey seamless that needs to read as pure white for e-commerce. We need you to produce the full launch image kit from the assets we supply — no new product design, just world-class post-production on what we shot. Specifically: 1) Clean the hero studio shot (DSLR raw of the woven-cord-and-gold-clasp bracelet on a slightly-tilted grey seamless): auto-straighten the horizon, crop to a clean square, and bring it to life with auto-tone plus exposure/contrast and a slight warm white-balance so the gold clasp glows. 2) Make it e-commerce-ready: select the bracelet subject, invert the selection, and fill the background to pure white (#FFFFFF) so it drops cleanly onto our product pages; boost only the gold's saturation so the clasp reads as real 18k without touching the cord color. 3) From that cleaned, graded master, produce a punchy color-graded CLOSE-UP HERO for the homepage: apply a cohesive Lightroom look/preset and a gentle lens blur so the macro clasp detail pops, then crop to a 4:5 portrait. 4) Produce a wide SOCIAL/WEB BANNER from the same graded master by re-cropping the pure-white e-commerce master to a 16:9 landscape frame (2400x1350) — reframing so the bracelet sits to one side and the existing white sweep leaves clean text-safe negative space for headline text on the website hero. This is a crop/reframe of the existing pixels only; nothing is added or extended beyond the source frame. 5) We also supply a flat PNG of our wordmark — vectorize it to a clean SVG so it scales for packaging, the website, and the bracelet's hang-tag. 6) License one premium lifestyle "table of friends gathered" stock backdrop from Adobe Stock at full res so our social team has an on-brand scene to drop the banner over. Deliverables: pure-white-background e-commerce JPEG, a 4:5 graded close-up hero, a 16:9 banner (cropped from the white-bg master) with text-safe negative space, a scalable SVG wordmark, and the licensed stock backdrop. Keep the look elevated, warm, and timeless to match the brand.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

gathering_lifestyle_frame.jpgimagegpt-image-2Warm candid documentary photograph of a group of friends gathered around a wooden table at golden hour — laughing, raise

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpgimageSquare ~2400x2400 JPEG, bracelet on pure #FFFFFF background, auto-toned + exposure/contrast corrected + warm WB, gold clasp saturation-boosted; drop-in ready for product detail pages.
homepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpgimage4:5 portrait (~2048x2560) color-graded close-up hero with a cohesive Lightroom preset look + gentle lens blur isolating the macro clasp detail.
social_banner_16x9.jpgimage16:9 (2400x1350) banner produced by cropping/reframing the pure-white e-commerce master to a landscape frame — the bracelet offset to one side with the existing white sweep providing clean text-safe negative space for a website-hero headline. Crop only (both dimensions <= the ~2400x2400 source); no canvas extension, no added/invented pixels.
wordmark_vector.svgvectorClean scalable SVG traced from the flat wordmark PNG; single-color charcoal, crisp paths for packaging / web / hang-tag.
licensed_gathering_backdrop.jpgimageFull-resolution licensed Adobe Stock 'friends gathered at a table' lifestyle backdrop (presigned download URL) for the social team to place the banner over.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All six deliverables are present: ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg, homepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg, social_banner_16x9.jpg, wordmark_vector.svg, licensed_gathering_backdrop.jpg, and the launch_kit_firefly_board link.autoExactly 6 deliverables exist matching the names ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg (JPEG), homepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg (JPEG), social_banner_16x9.jpg (JPEG), wordmark_vector.svg (SVG), licensed_gathering_backdrop.jpg (JPEG), and one launch_kit_firefly_board URL; none missing.K1mandatoryoutput
The e-commerce shot background is pure white #FFFFFF.autoSampling the background region of ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg returns RGB exactly (255,255,255) / #FFFFFF across the seamless area surrounding the bracelet.K1dealbreakerbrief
The e-commerce shot is a square ~2400x2400 JPEG.autoecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg is JPEG format with width == height, dimensions approximately 2400x2400 (within ~5% tolerance).K1mandatoryoutput
The homepage close-up hero is a 4:5 portrait at ~2048x2560.autohomepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg is JPEG with width:height ratio == 4:5 (portrait), dimensions approximately 2048x2560 (within ~5% tolerance).K1mandatoryoutput
The social banner is a 16:9 landscape at ~2560x1440.autosocial_banner_16x9.jpg is JPEG with width:height ratio == 16:9 (landscape), dimensions approximately 2560x1440 (within ~5% tolerance).K1mandatoryoutput
The wordmark deliverable is a valid scalable SVG vector file (not a raster).autowordmark_vector.svg parses as valid SVG containing vector path/shape elements, with no embedded raster bitmap (e.g. no <image>/base64 data URI) as the trace content.K1dealbreakeroutput
The licensed backdrop is delivered as a full-resolution Adobe Stock asset via a presigned download URL.autolicensed_gathering_backdrop.jpg resolves from an Adobe Stock license-and-download presigned URL and is full-resolution (not a watermarked comp/preview).K6mandatoryoutput
The Firefly board assembles exactly the graded master plus the five produced/licensed deliverables.autoThe launch_kit_firefly_board link opens to a shareable board containing all six elements: the graded master hero_warm_master, ecom_white_bg_bracelet, homepage_hero_closeup_4x5, social_banner_16x9, wordmark_vector, and licensed_gathering_backdrop.K6mandatoryoutput
The vectorized SVG wordmark reads 'GATHER & CO.' and preserves the knotted-cord-loop icon from the supplied flat PNG.expertThe SVG legibly renders the text 'GATHER & CO.' in the high-contrast serif and includes the minimal line-art knotted cord loop icon to the left of the text, matching the supplied brand_wordmark_flat.png; the brand mark is traced, not regenerated or restyled.K2dealbreakerinput
The wordmark vector is single-color charcoal, matching the supplied flat PNG.expertwordmark_vector.svg paths are a single deep-charcoal fill color with no gradients or introduced colors, faithful to the charcoal of brand_wordmark_flat.png.K2mandatoryinput
The hero studio shot horizon is straightened (level) and not left crooked.expertIn the e-comm and graded outputs the seamless sweep / contact-shadow line reads level (the few-degrees tilt of the raw bracelet_studio_hero.jpg is corrected), with no awkward post-straighten crop artifacts.K3mandatorybrief
Only the gold clasp saturation is boosted; the navy waxed-cord band color is unchanged.expertIn ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg the gold/yellow of the barrel clasp and charm reads as richer/more saturated 18k gold while the dark navy braided cord retains its original hue (single-color saturation, no global shift).K3mandatorybrief
The bracelet subject is cleanly masked against the white background with no grey-seamless fringe or clipped product edges.expertAround the bracelet in ecom_white_bg_bracelet.jpg the select-subject -> invert -> fill-white edge is clean: no residual grey halo, no white spill onto cord fibers or gold, and fine cord/clasp edges intact.K3mandatorybrief
The 16:9 social banner leaves clean text-safe negative space for a headline.expertThe generative-expand outpaint in social_banner_16x9.jpg extends the white studio sweep with an uncluttered, seamless region of negative space suitable for overlaying a website-hero headline; expansion has no visible seams or artifacts.K3mandatoryoutput
The close-up hero applies a cohesive Lightroom preset look plus a gentle lens blur, and the overall kit reads elevated, warm, and timeless.experthomepage_hero_closeup_4x5.jpg shows a cohesive graded preset look with a soft lens blur over the macro clasp detail, and the e-comm / hero / banner set shares a warm, elevated, timeless tone consistent with the brand.K4qualitybrief
All edited pixels originate from the client-supplied photos/assets; nothing is generated from scratch or composited beyond the single allowed outpaint.expertecom, hero, banner and SVG all derive from bracelet_studio_hero.jpg and brand_wordmark_flat.png; the only generative content is the 16:9 outpaint negative space; no from-scratch product design, no prompt-based background replacement, no compositing of the bracelet onto a new scene.K7dealbreakerbrief

AO-10   Modern Silver Rosary Necklace — Commercial Product Package (Hero Retouch + Vector Spec + Screen-Print Tech Graphic + Data-Merge Spec Sheet)

Brand/vertical Fashion & ApparelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's raw studio shot of a modern silver rosary necklace into a full commercial product package: a retouched hero image on a clean sweep, a vector line-art spec, a screen-print-style halftone tech graphic, a print-ready data-merged spec-sheet PDF and a rendered wholesale line-sheet card — all silver-true and ready for revision rounds.

Full brief

We sell handcrafted sterling silver religious jewelry and have just finalized a new MODERN-style rosary necklace (clean geometric links, minimal crucifix, brushed-silver finish). I shot it on my bench under mixed lighting and need a freelancer to build the full commercial product package we can use across our store listing, lookbook, a stamped/printed merch concept, and our wholesale line sheet. Deliverables: 1. A retouched HERO image of the rosary: straightened, auto-toned, exposure/highlight balanced so the brushed silver reads true (not yellow-cast), the subject isolated, dropped onto a clean neutral studio sweep, cropped and resized to the exact 4:5 e-commerce frame, and given a final commercial polish (a subtle vibrance/saturation lift so the silver pops) — the flat neutral sweep already reads as a soft, distraction-free studio background. Deliver a polished JPEG and the cut-out transparent PNG. 2. A clean VECTOR line-art / silhouette of the necklace (SVG) we can scale for stamping, embroidery, and the modern logo-lockup spec sheet. 3. A screen-print / halftone TECH GRAPHIC of the rosary (single-tone, print-screen look) for a tote/tee merch mockup and our stamping vendor. 4. A print-ready PRODUCT SPEC SHEET: take our authored InDesign spec-sheet template and our SKU CSV (material, chain length, crucifix size, finish, price tiers) and data-merge it into a per-variant PDF, plus a rendered wholesale line-sheet card from our Illustrator template. Keep everything modern, minimal, and silver-true. I will pick the final crop. Strong communication for revisions is essential.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain jewelryDeliverable product-shotWorkflow EditOperation O4Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 17Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[T]Brand Argent & Faith
5Planning3Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Technical Quality · Brand Alignment · Process · Creative Quality · Honesty. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.
