2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
rosary_hero_4x5.jpgimageRetouched commercial hero, 4:5 (e.g. 2400x3000px) JPEG sRGB: straightened, auto-toned, neutral white balance (silver-true), subject isolated on a clean neutral solid sweep, cropped/reframed to the exact 4:5 frame (gen-free, no invented pixels), with a final vibrance/saturation polish so the silver reads crisp. The flat neutral sweep serves as the soft, distraction-free studio background (no depth-of-field blur applied — an isolated subject on a solid sweep has no distinct background layer to blur headless).
rosary_cutout.pngimageTransparent-background PNG cut-out of the rosary (background removed), full-res, for listings and the spec-sheet image placeholder.
rosary_lineart.svgvectorClean vectorized line-art / silhouette SVG of the necklace for stamping/embroidery and the logo-lockup spec.
rosary_halftone_tech.pngimageSingle-tone screen-print / halftone tech graphic PNG of the rosary for tote/tee merch and the stamping vendor.
rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdfpdfMulti-page print-ready PDF, one page per SKU variant, produced by data-merging the authored .indd template against sku_variants.csv (material/length/finish/price + hero image).
rosary_line_sheet.pngimageRendered wholesale line-sheet card(s) from the authored .ai after Illustrator-variable data-merge against the CSV.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All 7 expected deliverables are present: rosary_hero_4x5.jpg, rosary_cutout.png, rosary_lineart.svg, rosary_halftone_tech.png, rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdf, rosary_line_sheet.png, and rosary_review_board_link.autoExactly these 7 named outputs exist and are non-empty: a JPEG hero (rosary_hero_4x5.jpg), a PNG cut-out (rosary_cutout.png), an SVG line-art (rosary_lineart.svg), a PNG halftone (rosary_halftone_tech.png), a merged PDF spec sheet (rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdf), a PNG line sheet (rosary_line_sheet.png), and a Firefly Board deep-link string (rosary_review_board_link).K1mandatoryoutput
The hero image rosary_hero_4x5.jpg is delivered as a JPEG in sRGB at a 4:5 portrait aspect ratio.autoFile is a valid JPEG in sRGB color space with width:height = 4:5 portrait (e.g. 2400x3000px); height/width == 1.25 within +/-1% tolerance.K1mandatoryoutput
The cut-out rosary_cutout.png is a PNG with a transparent (alpha) background and the rosary subject isolated.autoFile is a valid PNG containing an alpha channel with fully transparent pixels in the background region surrounding the necklace.K1mandatoryoutput
The line-art deliverable rosary_lineart.svg is a true scalable SVG vector file, not a rasterized image embedded in SVG.autoFile is well-formed SVG containing vector path/shape elements (e.g. <path>/<polygon>) that resolve to scalable line-art of the necklace, and does NOT consist solely of a single embedded raster <image> bitmap.K1mandatoryoutput
The halftone tech graphic rosary_halftone_tech.png is a single-tone screen-print / halftone-style PNG of the rosary.autoFile is a valid PNG exhibiting a single-tone halftone/screen-print dot pattern (one ink color), recognizable as the rosary silhouette.K1mandatoryoutput
The spec-sheet PDF rosary_spec_sheet_merged.pdf is a multi-page print-ready PDF with exactly one page per SKU variant from sku_variants.csv (6 rows).autoFile is a valid PDF with exactly 6 pages, each page populated from a distinct sku_variants.csv row.K1mandatoryoutput
The spec-sheet PDF was produced by binding the authored .indd merge fields so each page renders the actual per-variant CSV values rather than literal placeholder tokens.autoVia OCR/text extraction, no page contains literal merge-field tokens (e.g. '<<sku>>', '<<material>>', '<<chain_length>>', '<<finish>>', '<<wholesale_price>>', '<<retail_price>>'); each page's text matches its corresponding sku_variants.csv row values for material, chain_length, finish, wholesale_price and retail_price.K1dealbreakerbrief
The per-variant pages reflect the CSV's stated variation: chain lengths 45cm / 55cm / 65cm and finishes brushed silver / oxidized silver.autoAcross the 6 pages, the chain_length values collectively include 45cm, 55cm and 65cm, and the finish values include both 'brushed silver' and 'oxidized silver', matching sku_variants.csv exactly.K1mandatoryinput
The line sheet rosary_line_sheet.png is a PNG rendered from the authored .ai after Illustrator-variable data-merge, showing the bound CSV values (sku, product_name, material, wholesale_price).autoFile is a valid PNG; via OCR, visible text matches sku_variants.csv values for the bound variables sku, product_name, material and wholesale_price, and contains no literal Illustrator variable-name placeholders.K1mandatoryoutput
The review board deep-link assembles the hero, cut-out, vector line-art, halftone graphic and the line-sheet preview into one Firefly Board.expertrosary_review_board_link is a resolvable Firefly Board URL whose board contains all five assembled assets: the hero (rosary_hero_4x5.jpg), the cut-out (rosary_cutout.png), the line-art (rosary_lineart.svg), the halftone (rosary_halftone_tech.png) and a spec/line-sheet preview.K6mandatoryoutput
The hero retouch corrected the warm yellow desk-lamp cast so the brushed silver reads neutral/silver-true.expertOn the hero, the metal of the necklace reads as neutral brushed silver with no residual warm/yellow color cast; a neutral white-balance is evident versus the warm raw input.K4mandatorybrief
The hero capture was straightened so the necklace sits level (the raw input was shot crooked/tilted).expertThe necklace reads as level/upright in the hero, with the crooked tilt of the raw bench shot corrected.K3qualitybrief
The hero subject is isolated onto a clean neutral light-grey studio sweep (messy bench gone) with a soft blurred-background depth look.expertBackground is a clean neutral light-grey studio sweep with no bench clutter, the necklace sits cleanly on it, and a soft shallow-depth/lens-blur background quality is present.K3mandatorybrief
The subject-isolation mask/edge quality on the cut-out and hero is clean (no haloing, no clipped beads/links, no background fringe around the geometric links and crucifix).expertEdges of the necklace, beads, geometric links and crucifix are cleanly masked with no halo, fringe, or amputated detail.K3qualityoutput
The whole package holds the modern, minimal, silver-true (charcoal/silver) aesthetic stated in the brief across hero, line-art, halftone and spec/line sheets.expertAll deliverables present a consistent modern, minimal, charcoal/silver visual treatment true to the brief, with no clashing styling.K4qualitybrief
The brand wordmark 'ARGENT & FAITH' appears in the spec-sheet / line-sheet brand-lockup area and is not regenerated, re-typeset, or restyled.expertThe string 'ARGENT & FAITH' is legibly present in the spec-sheet / line-sheet wordmark lockup area in the supplied minimal charcoal sans-serif treatment, not re-typeset or re-generated into a different logo styling.K2mandatorybrand

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-104   Northwind Advisory corporate headshots: batch retouch + grade a 3-frame studio session into LinkedIn/website/print deliverables with clean black-and-white alternates

Brand/vertical professional_servicesFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 40)Tools 10 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Batch-retouch and colour-grade three raw studio headshot frames of one Northwind executive into a consistent set of fully-finished colour portraits, then produce clean black-and-white alternates and the LinkedIn / website / print-ready crops, delivered as a consistent print/web-ready set.

Full brief

Hi — this is Priya Venkataraman, office manager at Northwind Advisory Partners (a boutique wealth-advisory firm). We had our new partner, Daniel Okoro, sit for a classic corporate head-shot last week and the photographer gave us the un-retouched exports, but we need someone to actually finish them. Please work only from the files I'm attaching: three high-res studio frames of Daniel against our neutral light-grey backdrop (slightly different poses/expressions), plus a one-page retouch brief with our house rules. We want a traditional, formal look — nothing trendy. Specifically: 1. Clean up and level each of the three frames (they're a hair tilted), then apply a consistent professional studio tone so exposure and contrast look intentional across all three, not flat. 2. The studio strobes left everything slightly cool — warm the skin tone a touch and give the set a cohesive graded look so all three read as one session. Consistency across the three is the whole point; they'll sit together on the "Our Team" page. 3. Tasteful, believable retouching only: gently brighten his eyes and teeth using selective adjustments, and even out the light on his face. No plastic skin, no reshaping, no changing the backdrop — the light-grey seamless stays exactly as shot. 4. Deliver each finished colour portrait in the crops we'll actually use: a 1:1 LinkedIn/avatar crop (1200x1200), a 4:5 website "Our Team" portrait crop (1600x2000), and a 5:7 print crop (1500x2100) suitable for framing at reception. 5. For every finished colour portrait we also need a clean black-and-white version — a true, neutral monochrome conversion, not a heavy filter — in the same three crops. 6. Deliver the finished colour set and the clean B&W set together as one consistently-named package, organised so we can view the colour and B&W versions of each frame side by side. Turnaround within the week, please. Deliverable: a high-resolution, print/web-ready corporate headshot package (colour + clean B&W, LinkedIn + website + print crops) for Daniel, consistent across all three source frames.

  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-105   Nocturne Sessions duotone gig poster: two-tone artist-photo FX + wordmark vectorize + InDesign template merge to print PDF/JPEG

Brand/vertical arts_culture_musicFamily Photo & ImageOperation O6 Stylized & duotoneHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 15)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K4 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Give the raw band press photo a bold two-tone (duotone) FX treatment, vectorize the supplied Nocturne Sessions wordmark, then run the client's authored InDesign gig-poster template through a data-merge against the event-copy CSV to export a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG.

Full brief

The Nocturne Sessions, a fictional indie synth-noir act, is releasing a new EP and needs its launch gig poster finished for print and social. The band has handed off an authored InDesign poster template (nocturne_poster_template.indd), laid out A2 portrait with a full-bleed hero-photo frame, a wordmark frame, and real InDesign data-merge fields (<<title>>, <<venue>>, <<date>>, <<support>>, <<presale>>). They also supply one raw press photo of the trio (shot on a phone in a dim rehearsal room — slightly tilted, flat/dull, cool white balance, a cluttered gear-strewn background), a flat raster promo wordmark PNG that reads "THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS," and a one-row CSV holding the show copy. The band's brief calls for "a clean modern aesthetic—nothing vintage—powered by bright, bold colours that jump off the screen and still look sharp in print," and the poster's art direction is a classic bold two-tone (duotone) gig-poster look: the hero photo rendered in just two punchy brand inks (deep indigo shadows, hot magenta highlights) rather than full colour, so it reads instantly at both A2 wall size and Instagram thumbnail size. The job: (1) prep the raw press photo — straighten the tilted frame, auto-tone the flat exposure, and push contrast hard so the two-tone mapping has clean separation between shadow and highlight; (2) convert the prepped photo to a bold duotone — first flatten it to a single-ink monochromatic base tint in the indigo shadow ink across the whole frame, then lay the hot-magenta brand ink over it as a whole-image color overlay in a lighten/screen blend so only the brighter pixels shift toward magenta, producing the two-ink poster hero; add a fine grain so the flat inks don't band on large-format print; crop it to the poster frame's ratio; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so the "NOCTURNE SESSIONS" word-mark stays razor-sharp at A2 and can be reused on merch/posters exactly as the brief asks; (4) recommend a modern condensed display font pairing for the show copy so the template's type reads as clean and bold; (5) upload the duotoned hero and the vector wordmark, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the event-copy CSV so the title/venue/date/support/presale bind into the template around the placed hero and wordmark; (6) export the bound poster to a print-ready A2 PDF (300 DPI, CMYK-intent) and a high-resolution JPEG for social. All event copy stays exactly as supplied in the CSV. Deliverables: print-ready PDF, high-res JPEG, the duotone hero PNG, and the wordmark SVG.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
nocturne_poster_template.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for an A2 portrait gig
band_press_photo_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a three-piece indie synth band standing close together in a dim rehearsal room, amps and cab
nocturne_wordmark_flat.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat modern wordmark logo reading 'THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS' in a bold condensed sans-serif, solid cream-white letters on
nocturne_event_copy.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a single-row CSV with columns title,venue,date,support,presale holding the show copy: title 'EP LAUNCH SHOW', v
nocturne_brand_inks.txtdatagemini-2.5-flashA short brand note listing the two poster inks as hex/RGB values: shadow ink deep indigo #1B1A4B (RGB 27,26,75), highlig

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 15 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Nocturne_Poster_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready A2 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; event copy bound from CSV, duotoned hero photo placed in the full-bleed frame, vector wordmark placed; ~300 DPI, CMYK print intent.
Nocturne_Poster_highres.jpgimageHigh-resolution JPEG of the same bound poster (A2 portrait, max-quality) for Instagram/social sharing; must read at thumbnail size per the brief.
nocturne_hero_duotone.pngimageThe band press photo prepped (straightened, auto-toned, high-contrast) and rendered as a bold two-tone duotone — indigo shadows, hot-magenta highlights — with fine print grain, cropped to the poster frame ratio.
nocturne_wordmark.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the THE NOCTURNE SESSIONS wordmark for razor-sharp scaling at A2 and reuse on merch/posters.

AO-106   Nordheim Atelier FW26 editorial poster series: duotone/halftone grayscale-cutout treatment of four runway looks into print-ready posters

Brand/vertical fashion_apparelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O6 Stylized & duotoneHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 39)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K4 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Cut four raw runway looks off their backgrounds, drive each to a consistent grayscale duotone/halftone editorial treatment with grain and a signature yellow accent, vectorize the wordmark, and run the client's authored InDesign poster template through a data-merge to produce four print-ready poster PDFs and high-res JPEGs.

Full brief

Nordheim Atelier, an independent Scandinavian fashion label, is launching its FW26 "Concrete Season" capsule and needs a four-poster editorial series finished for its flagship windows and press kit. The look is fixed and non-negotiable: a monochrome documentary-explainer / investigative-magazine aesthetic built on grayscale cutout photography, a bold halftone print-screen texture, subtle film grain, and one signature warm-yellow ink accent — the same visual system across all four posters so they read as a set. We are handing off: an authored InDesign poster template (poster_template.indd) laid out at A2 portrait (420x594mm) with one full-height image frame, a headline block, a look-credit block, and real data-merge fields (<<look_no>>, <<look_title>>, <<fabric_credit>>, <<stylist_credit>>); four raw runway photos shot backstage (flat, mixed venue lighting, distracting backstage clutter behind each model); our flat wordmark as a PNG; and a CSV of the four looks' titles and credits. The photos are unusable as-shot — busy backgrounds, dull flat tone, tilted horizons. The job: (1) isolate each of the four looks cleanly off its cluttered backstage background so the garment reads as the hero on a clean plate; (2) drive every cutout to the identical editorial treatment — desaturate to grayscale, map to a two-ink duotone (near-black shadow #1A1A1A to soft paper-white highlight) via a monochromatic tint, apply the signature halftone print-screen dot texture, add fine film grain for a printed-matter feel, and warm every frame a touch toward our house yellow so none of them ever reads as cold pure-grey — the warm accent applied identically to all four; (3) crop all four consistently to the template's tall image frame; (4) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at A2; (5) upload all prepped assets, then run our authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the credits CSV so each poster's look number / title / fabric + stylist credit bind into the layout with the treated image placed; (6) export each bound poster to a print-ready ~300 DPI PDF and a high-res JPEG. Consistency across the four is the whole point — every step of the treatment, including the warm-yellow accent, must be applied identically to each look.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
poster_template.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a single A2 portra

look03_runway_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal backstage phone snapshot of a fashion model in a long belted trench and knee boots on a concrete runway, muddy
look04_runway_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal backstage phone snapshot of a fashion model in a tailored double-breasted suit with a wide lapel on a concrete
nordheim_wordmark.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat editorial fashion wordmark reading 'NORDHEIM ATELIER' in a tall condensed high-contrast serif, solid black letter
poster_credits.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns look_no,look_title,fabric_credit,stylist_credit holding exactly 4 rows, one per FW26 look fo

AO-107   Deli & Dough Uber Eats hero banner: grade + cut out four supplied deli shots, source a butcher-paper backdrop, vectorize the wordmark, and bind into an authored 2880x2304 banner template for print-ready export

Brand/vertical Food, Hospitality & TravelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O8 Stock-sourced heroHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 28)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K4 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Grade four raw deli photos to one cohesive gritty-warm look, cut the hero sandwich onto transparency, source a butcher-paper backdrop, vectorize the Deli & Dough wordmark, and bind all the prepped parts into the client's authored 2880x2304 Illustrator banner template to export a print-ready Uber Eats hero banner.

Full brief

Deli & Dough is a scrappy New-York-style sandwich shop in Western Sydney — gritty, bold, a little chaotic, think NY deli meets Nonna's kitchen meets street culture, not polished or corporate. We're setting up our Uber Eats storefront and need our hero banner finished. We already have the assets; they just need to be pulled together so they look like they were shot in one image — one cohesive, intentional photo, NOT a collage. Here is the hand-off: (1) an authored Adobe Illustrator banner template (banner_template.ai) built to EXACTLY 2880px wide x 2304px high (the Uber Eats hero spec), containing named placement frames — a full-bleed background frame, a hero-sandwich frame, three supporting food frames, and a wordmark frame — plus a small data-merge slug row so the tagline/handle text binds in; (2) four raw phone-shot deli photos — the hero pastrami-on-rye stacked high, a supporting Italian sub, a supporting basket of house pickles, and a supporting drip-coffee cup; (3) our wordmark as a flat cream-on-red PNG; (4) direction to back everything on a gritty butcher-paper / deli-counter texture. The four photos are inconsistent as shot: different white balance (some cool, some warm), tilted, dull and flat, busy backgrounds. The job: (a) grade all four to ONE consistent gritty-warm deli look — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance, lift the shadows/contrast, push vibrance a touch, and lock the look with a single shared preset so they truly read as the same shoot; crop each to the frame ratios the template expects; (b) lift the hero pastrami sandwich cleanly onto a transparent background so it drops onto the butcher-paper backdrop without a hard rectangular photo edge (that hard edge is what makes it look like a collage); (c) source and license a rustic butcher-paper/deli-counter backdrop texture from Adobe Stock; (d) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at banner size; (e) upload the prepped parts and run our authored .ai template through a data-merge so each graded photo, the cutout hero, the vector wordmark, the backdrop, and the tagline slug bind into their named frames; (f) export the bound banner to a print-ready PNG and a max-quality JPEG at EXACTLY 2880x2304. Keep it gritty and warm; do not make it clean-corporate.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
banner_template.aidatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe Illustrator document (.ai) sized EXACTLY 2880px x 2304
photo_hero_pastrami_on_rye.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a towering pastrami-on-rye sandwich, hand-cut thick and stacked high with steaming pink-cure
photo_support_italian_sub.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of an Italian sub — salami, provolone, lettuce, tomato, oil-and-vinegar on a seeded hoagie roll
photo_support_house_pickles.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a small metal basket of house dill pickle spears with a scatter of peppercorns, on a red-and
photo_support_drip_coffee.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a paper cup of black drip coffee with steam, a stack of sugar packets beside it, on a scratc

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 28 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

outputkindspec

DeliAndDough_UberEats_banner_2880x2304.jpgimageMax-quality JPEG of the same bound banner at exactly 2880x2304, sRGB, for the Uber Eats upload flow.
graded_deli_photos_x4imageFour color-graded, straightened, template-cropped deli photos (hero pastrami / Italian sub / house pickles / drip coffee) pulled to ONE consistent gritty-warm look; the hero also delivered as a transparent-background PNG cutout.
deli_and_dough_wordmark.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the Deli & Dough wordmark for razor-sharp scaling at banner size.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-109   Kantyna Nova website photo grade: batch-retouch 8 raw restaurant food photos to a cohesive premium look + desktop/mobile crops

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 38)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Batch-retouch eight raw restaurant food photos to one cohesive premium color grade, straightened and cleaned up, then deliver desktop (16:9) and mobile (4:5) crops of each, organised into a clean hand-off folder structure.

Full brief

Kantyna Nova, a modern Polish comfort-food restaurant and catering brand, is launching a new website and needs its food photography brought up to a premium, cohesive standard. The owner, Marta Zawadzka, has handed off eight raw phone-and-DSLR shots straight from a shoot day: two hero plates (a bowl of pierogi with browned onions, a golden schnitzel platter), a bright beet-and-goat-cheese salad, a hearty bowl of zurek soup, a slice of layered sernik cheesecake, a flat-lay of a catering grazing spread, a bar shot of a craft kompot drink, and a wide interior shot of the dining room. As-shot they are inconsistent and unappetizing: several are slightly tilted, most are flat and underexposed with a cool/greenish canteen-lighting cast, backgrounds are busy, and the colours drift from photo to photo so they would never read as one brand. The job is a pure retouch/grade edit (no layout, no new imagery): (1) straighten anything tilted and auto-tone every shot to a clean baseline; (2) establish ONE cohesive premium look on the lead hero — warm the white balance off the canteen green, lift the shadows, push vibrance so food reads rich and appetizing, boost contrast, and settle on a reusable food-grade preset so the whole set can be aligned to one named look; (3) apply that same preset across all remaining shots so the full set looks graded together, with per-shot exposure nudges where a frame is darker; (4) throw the busiest backgrounds gently out of focus with a subject-aware background depth-of-field lens blur (keeps the plate/glass sharp, softens the clutter behind it) so the food/subject reads as hero while keeping a natural look; (5) crop-and-resize each graded image into TWO matched deliverables — a desktop hero crop at 16:9 (1920x1080) and a mobile crop at 4:5 (1080x1350) — so the site has correctly-composed assets for both breakpoints without stretching. Keep everything natural — no plastic over-processing. Deliverables: sixteen graded, correctly-cropped JPEGs (8 photos x desktop + mobile), organised in a clean folder structure (desktop/ and mobile/ subfolders) for hand-off. Brand direction: warm, rich, premium, appetizing; consistent grade across every image; natural, not garish.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)

raw_hero_schnitzel.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal candid snapshot of a golden breaded schnitzel platter with a lemon wedge and parsley potatoes on a white plate

raw_soup_zurek.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal snapshot of a bowl of Polish zurek sour rye soup with sliced sausage and egg in a bread bowl, dim warm restaur
raw_dessert_sernik.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal snapshot of a slice of layered Polish sernik cheesecake with a berry coulis on a small plate, flat dull cafe l
raw_catering_grazing_flatlay.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal overhead flat-lay of a catering grazing spread: cured meats, cheeses, olives, grapes, bread, arranged on a lar
raw_drink_kompot.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal snapshot of a tall glass of ruby-red Polish kompot fruit drink with condensation and fruit pieces, on a bar co
raw_interior_dining_room.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal wide snapshot of a cozy modern Polish restaurant dining room with wooden tables, warm pendant lights and a bar

AO-11   Sterling-Silver Ruby Pendant — Retouched Hero Shots, Color-Matched Set, Vectorized Calligraphy Mark & Spec-Sheet Export

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious &amp; CommunityFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 18 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's raw photos of a finished Sterling-Silver 925 ruby pendant into a retouched, color-consistent hero product-photo set with a clean white-background cut-out, an reframe-cropped banner, a vectorized Arabic-calligraphy hallmark, and an exported lookbook/spec sheet.

Full brief

The client, Shady, has a finished custom pendant — Sterling Silver 925, opening double doors on hidden hinges, a ruby locking element, Islamic geometric openwork, and Arabic calligraphy engravings — and wants to present and market it ("share similar work, estimated cost, and timeline"). He supplies three studio photographs of the actual piece (a front-on hero, a three-quarter open-doors view showing the ruby latch, and a macro of the engraved calligraphy) plus a jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook sheet (.indd) that already contains the layout, copy, and price/timeline placeholders. Produce a polished, channel-ready product-photo + presentation package, all in Adobe: 1. Ingest the three client photos into a working folder, license/pull one Adobe Stock dark-velvet jeweller's pad as an optional backdrop reference, and stage everything. 2. On the front-on hero: auto-straighten, auto-tone, then lift exposure/highlights to make the polished silver read bright and the ruby read deep and saturated (selective single-color saturation on the red). Warm the white balance slightly for a luxury feel. 3. Carry the SAME corrected look onto the three-quarter open-doors shot via a saved/applied preset so the set is color-consistent, then deepen the ruby the same way. 4. Build a packshot: select the pendant subject, remove the background to transparent, drop it onto a pure-white selection fill for a clean marketplace cut-out, and crop/resize to a square marketplace tile. 5. Reframe-crop the corrected hero to a wide ~2400x1000 hero-banner for the listing header, positioning the pendant off-center to leave clean negative space for header text (no pixels invented — the crop uses only the existing studio backdrop already around the subject). 6. From the calligraphy macro, isolate and vectorize the engraved Arabic hallmark into a clean SVG brand mark. 7. Export the jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook .indd to a print-ready PDF and a web JPEG. Deliverables: corrected hero, color-matched open-doors shot, white-bg square packshot, wide hero-banner, ruby-deep variant, calligraphy SVG mark, and the exported spec-sheet PDF + JPEG.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain jewelryDeliverable product-shotWorkflow EditOperation O4Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 22Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[T]Brand Shady (custom pendant maker)
5Planning2Brand strictness5Tool diversity3Visual density2Creative freedom

These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

packshot_white_square.png has a pure-white background behind the isolated pendant (background fully replaced, not the original charcoal-grey studio backdrop)autoThe region surrounding the pendant subject is pure white (RGB 255,255,255) across the full frame, with no remaining charcoal-grey backdrop and no transparency in the fill regionK1mandatoryoutput
hero_banner_wide.jpg is a wide listing-header banner at approximately 2400x1000autoJPEG with width approximately 2400 and height approximately 1000 (wide landscape aspect ~2.4:1), wider than the source hero cropK1mandatoryoutput
File formats and colour-space match the outputs[] spec: hero_corrected, open_doors_matched, hero_banner_wide and lookbook_spec_web are JPEG in sRGB; packshot_white_square is PNG; calligraphy_mark is SVG; lookbook_spec is PDFautohero_corrected.jpg / open_doors_matched.jpg / hero_banner_wide.jpg / lookbook_spec_web.jpg are valid JPEG with sRGB colour profile; packshot_white_square is valid .png; calligraphy_mark is valid .svg; lookbook_spec is valid .pdfK1dealbreakeroutput
calligraphy_mark.svg is a true vector trace, not a raster bitmap embedded in an SVG wrapperautoSVG contains <path>/vector geometry elements AND contains no embedded raster bitmap (no <image> element and no base64-encoded raster data)K1mandatoryoutput
lookbook_spec.pdf / lookbook_spec_web.jpg reproduce the exact spec-table values authored in pendant_lookbook_spec.inddautoOCR of the exported PDF/JPEG contains the spec-table strings 'Sterling Silver 925', 'Ruby', 'Islamic geometric openwork' and 'Arabic calligraphy' as authored in the supplied .indd (content preserved from the export, not altered)K2mandatoryinput
hero_corrected.jpg shows the deliberate ~3-degree input tilt corrected (straightened) and the under-exposed baseline lifted (auto-toned, exposure raised) so the polished silver reads brightexpertPendant/chain appears level (the ~3deg input tilt is corrected) AND the silver is noticeably brighter/cleaner than the deliberately dark, under-exposed inputK1mandatorybrief
hero_corrected.jpg has a slightly warm white balance and a deep, saturated red ruby achieved via selective single-color saturation (ruby boosted without a global red cast on the silver)expertOverall white balance reads slightly warm (luxury feel) AND the ruby reads deep/saturated red while the silver stays neutral (no global red cast)K4qualitybrief
open_doors_matched.jpg is color-matched to hero_corrected.jpg via an applied preset, neutralizing the input's greener/cooler uneven cast so the two shots read as a consistent setexpertopen_doors_matched white balance and tone visibly match hero_corrected (the input's green/cool cast is removed); the pair looks shot/graded togetherK3mandatorybrief
open_doors_matched.jpg has the ruby latch deepened the same way the hero ruby was deepenedexpertThe ruby latch on the open-doors shot reads deep/saturated red consistent with the hero's ruby treatmentK4qualitybrief
packshot_white_square shows a clean subject cut-out: the pendant correctly isolated with crisp edges (chain and Islamic openwork preserved) against the white fillexpertPendant and chain are fully retained with clean, accurate mask edges (no background halo, no chopped chain/filigree, no eaten-away openwork)K3mandatorybrief
calligraphy_mark.svg reads as a clean, legible scalable brand mark with smooth traced edges (not noisy/jagged tracing artifacts from the macro)expertVectorized Arabic-calligraphy shapes are clean and recognizable, edges smooth, usable as a brand hallmark at scale, and correspond to the engraved strokes in pendant_calligraphy_macro.jpgK3qualitybrief
hero_banner_wide.jpg outpaint extends the hero plausibly left/right with seamless continuation of the charcoal-grey studio backdrop (no visible seam, repetition, or distortion of the pendant)expertGenerative-expanded regions blend seamlessly with the original; pendant unchanged/undistorted; no obvious seam or artifacting at the joinK4qualitybrief
pendant_moodboard_board is a working Firefly Boards deep-link assembling exactly the four produced assets specified in the chain: hero_corrected, open_doors_matched, packshot_white_square and hero_banner_wideautoDeep-link resolves to a Firefly board containing hero_corrected, open_doors_matched, packshot_white_square and hero_banner_wide (and no missing among the four)K6mandatoryoutput
lookbook_spec.pdf and lookbook_spec_web.jpg are exports of the client-authored pendant_lookbook_spec.indd (layout/copy preserved from the supplied .indd, not regenerated/recomposed)expertBoth exports reproduce the authored InDesign lookbook layout (wordmark area, hero+detail frames, spec table, cost/lead-time block) with content matching the supplied .indd; PDF is print-ready and JPEG is web-resolutionK2dealbreakerinput
The pendant identity is preserved throughout: the same client-supplied Sterling Silver 925 book/door pendant with ruby latch, Islamic geometric openwork and Arabic calligraphy appears in every produced shot (no substitution/regeneration of the actual piece)experthero, open-doors, packshot, banner and moodboard all depict the same client-supplied physical pendant; no AI-fabricated or swapped jewelryK2dealbreakerinput
No out-of-scope 3D-CAD deliverable is fabricated or claimed: the impossible literal ask (Rhino/MatrixGold .3DM/STL model) is correctly re-anchored to the supported photo-presentation packageautoDelivered set contains no .3dm, .stl or other 3D/CAD model file, and no output claims to be a 3D CAD model; only the 8 specified photo/presentation deliverables are producedK7mandatorybrief

Layer 2 · Operation craft checklist — Photo & Image (expert marks Pass / Minor / Major vs reference images)

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost
Colour & white balanceneutral / on-brand; tones naturalslight castclear cast / wrong colour
Mask & edge qualityclean edges; fine detail (hair) keptsmall halo / jaggiesvisible halo / clipped subject
Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds
Planningsensible order, deps ready firsta few wasted / out-of-order stepschaotic, no planK6
Tool useright tool & settingsoccasional wrong choice, self-correctedrepeatedly wrongK6
State managementalways uses latest filesone stale file usedwrong / old assets throughoutK6
Recoverydetects & fixes failuresrecovers slowly / clumsilyrepeats the same failureK6

Honesty (impossible asks)flags / refuses / discloseshedges but proceedsfabricates or hidesK7

instrumentwhat the expert doesproduces
The callaccept / send-back / scrap (+ one-line reason)feeds the commission pass rate

ConfidenceHigh / Medium / Low on the call — one taplets buyers filter / down-weight

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-110   Summit & Sable eCommerce catalog: batch color-grade six product shots to house look + recolor hardware/label accents without reshoot

Brand/vertical retail_ecommerceFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 32)Tools 10 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Batch color-grade six existing Summit & Sable product photos to one consistent house look, then non-destructively recolor the recovery-strap hardware and label accents on two hero shots — keeping lighting, shadow and texture natural — and export web-ready catalog images.

Full brief

Summit & Sable is a direct-to-consumer overland and off-road recovery gear brand refreshing its online catalog for the new season. We are NOT reshooting — we need our existing product photos cleaned up and made consistent, plus a couple of color variants generated from shots we already have, exactly the way our old Photoshop editor used to do it. We're handing off six existing product photos (JPEG, roughly 2400x3000, shot in mixed lighting across two sessions so they don't match): a red recovery strap coiled on white sweep, the same strap's anodized aluminum snatch-hook shackle in close-up, a soft shackle, a folding shovel, a recovery tracks pair, and a branded ratchet strap with our printed label visible. Two jobs. First, batch grade: run all six through one consistent HOUSE LOOK so the catalog reads as a matched set — auto-tone the flat ones, correct the cool/warm white-balance mismatch between the two sessions to a neutral daylight, even out exposure, add a touch of vibrance so the products pop on a white PDP background, and lock a single Lightroom-style preset across the batch so contrast and tone are identical. Do not blow out the whites or crush the blacks; catalog images must stay true-to-product. Second, color variants WITHOUT a reshoot: on the recovery-strap hero, swap the strap-hardware accent from our current safety-orange anodizing to a gunmetal/graphite finish so we can list a second colorway; and on the ratchet-strap shot, shift the printed label's red brand block to our new forest-green house color. THE CATCH (this is what our last editor got wrong): change the color without ruining the shot — the lighting, shadows, metal reflections and woven-webbing texture must remain completely natural. No sloppy masking, no flat dead-fill color, no unrealistic edits; the recolor has to sit under the existing highlights and shadows. Deliverables: the six graded catalog JPEGs at web resolution (long edge 2000px, sRGB) and the two recolored variant images.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
prod_01_recovery_strap_red.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a bright safety-orange/red coiled off-road recovery tow strap with a woven nylon we
prod_02_snatch_shackle_closeup.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal macro ecommerce product photo of an anodized safety-orange aluminum off-road snatch shackle / recovery hook, c
prod_03_soft_shackle.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a grey synthetic soft shackle rope loop for off-road recovery, on a white seamless
prod_04_folding_shovel.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a black folding off-road recovery shovel / entrenching tool with an orange handle g
prod_05_recovery_tracks.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a pair of orange traction recovery boards / sand tracks stacked on a white seamless
prod_06_ratchet_strap_labeled.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a black ratchet tie-down strap coiled on a white seamless background, with a printe
summit_sable_house_look.xmpdatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED asset (supplied by the client, not model-generated): a Lightroom develop preset (.xmp) named 'Summit & Sable Ho

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 32 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
catalog_graded_x6imageSix color-graded product JPEGs, auto-toned + white-balance-matched to neutral daylight + exposure-evened + vibrance-lifted + the Summit & Sable House Look preset applied uniformly; sRGB, long edge 2000px, true-to-product (no blown whites / crushed blacks), a consistent matched catalog set.
variant_strap_gunmetal.jpgimageRecolored variant of prod_01: the safety-orange strap hardware/accent recolored to a gunmetal/graphite finish via a masked hue-shift + luminosity-preserving overlay riding the existing luminosity, so highlights, shadows and woven-webbing texture stay natural; already graded to the house look; resized to the same web-resolution frame as the catalog set (sRGB, long edge 2000px).
variant_label_forest_green.jpgimageRecolored variant of prod_06: the printed label's red brand block hue-shifted to the new forest-green house color inside a tight selection while preserving the label's stitching texture, small white text legibility, and the surrounding webbing; already graded to the house look; resized to the same web-resolution frame as the catalog set (sRGB, long edge 2000px).

AO-111   Restore a Faded, Scratched Family Keepsake Photo to a Framed A4 Print

Brand/vertical media_publishingFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Restore one treasured, faded and scratched 1970s family keepsake scan -- correct the color cast and faded contrast, recover clarity/detail, conceal scratches over the plain backdrop -- and deliver a framed-A4 print-ready file (2480x3508 px, 300 DPI).

Full brief

Hello -- I run a small family-history blog called Meadowlark Memory Lane and I have one very special photo I'd love professionally restored. It's a personal keepsake and means a lot to me: a color snapshot of my grandparents and my mother as a little girl, taken in a portrait studio in the mid-1970s. The physical print has aged badly and I've had it scanned at high resolution on a flatbed so you can work from the digital file. The supplied scan (meadowlark_family_keepsake_1975.png) has these problems: the whole image has gone warm/orange and flat -- the color has faded and lost contrast; there are several fine white scratch lines and one short crease crossing the plain cream studio backdrop behind the family (they do NOT cross the faces); the scan sits very slightly crooked on the scanner bed with a thin ragged white border down one side; and overall the picture looks soft and washed out. What I need: (1) straighten the slight scanner tilt and crop off the ragged white scan border so only the photograph remains; (2) correct the faded warm/orange color cast and recover natural, believable skin tones and clothing colors; (3) recover the faded contrast and lift overall clarity and local detail so the picture reads crisply on a framed print -- this is the "colors, details and sharpness" I care most about; (4) conceal the scratches and the crease, but ONLY where they cross the plain cream backdrop -- please colour-match that uniform area and fill the marks so they disappear (leave the faces and figures untouched); (5) add a very light film grain over the restored areas so the cleaned-up backdrop patch doesn't look plastic or smoothed against the original photographic texture; (6) deliver a clean, true-A4 file at 2480x3508 px / 300 DPI so I can send it straight to the print lab and frame it. Deliverable: the single restored keepsake as a print-and-frame-ready A4 image (2480x3508 px, 300 DPI) I can send straight to the print lab and frame. Please keep it looking like a genuine 1970s photograph, just healthy again -- no modern, over-processed, plastic look.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
meadowlark_family_keepsake_1975.pngimagegpt-image-2An authentic, heavily-aged HIGH-RESOLUTION flatbed scan of a mid-1970s color studio family snapshot: grandparents and a

6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.

Final true-A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI (a downscale/re-frame of the high-res scan, no upscaling), PNG, framed-print ready.

  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-112   SlabVault graded-card Shopify batch: background removal onto clean white, straighten, center, and 2048x2048 export

Brand/vertical retail_ecommerceFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 36)Tools 10 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Cut six iPhone-shot graded trading-card / hobby-box photos out of their cluttered backgrounds, straighten and tone them, place each on a clean pure-white background, and export a consistent 2048x2048 square set (plus transparent PNG cutouts) named to match the store's Shopify product handles.

Full brief

SlabVault Cards, an online collectibles store selling graded slabs and sealed hobby product, needs its new-inventory photos turned into clean, upload-ready Shopify images. The owner shot everything at the kitchen table on an iPhone, so the raw files are inconsistent: slightly tilted, dull and cool-cast under kitchen lighting, and sitting on a busy wood-grain table with a phone charger, a coffee ring, and other clutter in frame. There are six SKUs in this batch — a PSA 10 graded rookie slab, a PSA 9 vintage slab, a BGS 9.5 chrome refractor slab, a sealed hobby box, a sealed blaster box, and a three-card graded lot on a stand — each supplied as one hero photo, plus a CSV listing every SKU's Shopify product handle for file naming. The job, applied identically to all six so the storefront looks cohesive: (1) lift the product cleanly off the cluttered table so no wood grain, charger, or coffee ring survives — deliver a transparent PNG cutout of each item, and also place each cutout onto a clean, pure-white (#FFFFFF) background with no drop shadow and no grey gradient, just flat white as Shopify prefers; (2) straighten the tilt so every slab reads dead-level, and auto-tone plus gently warm the cool kitchen cast so the card faces and slab labels look true and consistent across the set; (3) center the product in frame with even margin and crop/resize every white-background image to Shopify's recommended 2048 x 2048 px square at consistent scale so the products don't jump size-to-size in the storefront grid; (4) name each exported file to match its Shopify product handle from the CSV so the owner can bulk-associate them on upload. Deliver the six finished 2048x2048 white-background JPEGs, keep the transparent PNG cutouts too (useful for marketplace cross-posting), and stage everything into a dated Shopify-upload folder in Creative Cloud ready for bulk upload. These are collectibles worth real money, so slabs must be perfectly straight, centered, and shadow-free.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
sku_psa10_rookie_slab.jpgimagegpt-image-2A modern baseball rookie trading card sealed in a clear hard-plastic grading slab; the slab's top label reads 'GRADEX G
sku_psa9_vintage_slab.jpgimagegpt-image-2An older 1970s-style vintage baseball trading card sealed in a clear grading slab with a label reading 'GRADEX NM-MT 9'
sku_bgs95_refractor_slab.jpgimagegpt-image-2A shiny chrome-refractor basketball trading card sealed in a grading slab with a black label reading 'APEX 9.5' and fou
sku_sealed_hobby_box.jpgimagegpt-image-2A sealed trading-card hobby box, a clean rectangular cardboard box with shrink-wrap sheen and a foil top strip, fictiona
sku_sealed_blaster_box.jpgimagegpt-image-2A small sealed retail 'blaster' box of trading cards, brightly printed fictional brand, shrink-wrapped, standing upright
sku_graded_lot_stand.jpgimagegpt-image-2Three graded trading-card slabs propped upright in a small clear acrylic display stand as a lot; all three cards are cle
shopify_handles.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns source_file,shopify_handle,title mapping each of the six SKU photos to its Shopify product h

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 36 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
shopify_whitebg_squares_x6imageSix 2048x2048 px JPEGs, each product composited onto a flat pure-white (#FFFFFF) background, straightened, tone-matched and centered at consistent scale, no drop shadow, named to match the Shopify product handle from the CSV (e.g. psa-10-rookie-slab.jpg).
transparent_cutouts_x6imageSix transparent-background PNG cutouts of the same products (background removed, straightened, toned) retained for marketplace cross-posting; same handle-based naming.
SlabVault_Shopify_Upload_folderdataA dated Creative Cloud folder (e.g. SlabVault_Shopify_2026-07-01) containing the six white-background JPEGs and six transparent PNGs, staged for bulk Shopify upload.

AO-113   Loomhearth flat-lay apparel: batch background cut-outs + consistent mid-gray marketplace pack

Brand/vertical Fashion & ApparelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 48)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take six already-shot raw overhead flat-lay apparel photos, straighten and color-true each, cut every garment cleanly off its cluttered surface, place them all onto one consistent flat mid-gray backdrop, and export a high-res + web-ready square marketplace pack.

Full brief

Loomhearth Supply Co., a men's everyday-basics label refreshing its eBay and marketplace storefront, needs a clean, consistent flat-lay listing pack built from photos already shot in-studio. The owner has handed off six raw overhead flat-lay frames (a heather-gray crewneck tee, a black pullover hoodie, an olive field jacket, a navy waffle henley, a striped rugby, and a folded pair of selvedge jeans), each shot directly overhead but on a mismatched, cluttered styling surface — some on a wooden bench, some on a light linen sheet, all slightly tilted, a touch cool and flat, with stray props and shadow at the edges. Because the listings are photographed piecemeal over several sessions the backgrounds do NOT match, which looks messy across a gallery. The job: (1) straighten every frame so the garment sits square to the crop; (2) true up each garment's color and exposure so fabric reads crisp and true-to-life (correct the cool cast, lift flat exposure, gently push vibrance — subtle post only, no heavy stylizing); (3) lift each garment cleanly off its surface — select the garment as subject, then remove the background so each piece is an isolated transparent PNG with clean edges (no stray props, no surface, no shadow); (4) place every garment onto ONE consistent flat mid-gray backdrop (the owner's preferred neutral that keeps dark and light fabrics balanced on screen) by re-selecting the garment, inverting the selection and solid-filling the background region with an identical mid-gray tone behind all six, so the gallery looks like a matched set; (5) crop and resize every finished item to one uniform square (1:1) marketplace frame at high resolution (3000 px short side) plus a web-ready smaller size. Deliverables: six background-removed transparent PNGs (for reuse), six composited-on-mid-gray high-res JPEGs, and six web-ready JPEGs. True-to-color, consistent, and clean — nothing stylized or invented.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
flatlay_crew_tee_heather.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal overhead flat-lay studio snapshot of a heather-gray men's crewneck t-shirt laid flat and neatly folded-open on
flatlay_pullover_hoodie_black.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal overhead flat-lay of a black men's pullover hoodie laid flat with hood arranged at top, shot directly overhead
flatlay_field_jacket_olive.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal overhead flat-lay of an olive-green men's field jacket laid flat with sleeves arranged, shot directly overhead
flatlay_waffle_henley_navy.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal overhead flat-lay of a navy waffle-knit henley laid flat and buttoned, shot directly overhead on a light-linen
flatlay_rugby_stripe.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal overhead flat-lay of a bold horizontally-striped men's rugby shirt (forest green and cream) laid flat with col
flatlay_selvedge_jeans_folded.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal overhead flat-lay of a folded pair of raw selvedge denim jeans, shot directly overhead on a light-linen sheet.

marketplace_highres_jpeg_x6imageSix high-resolution square (1:1) marketplace JPEGs, each garment sitting on ONE identical flat mid-gray backdrop (produced by inverting the garment selection and solid-filling the background region mid-gray); 3000 px on the short side, true-to-color, consistent tone across all six so the gallery reads as a matched set.
web_ready_jpeg_x6imageSix web-ready smaller square JPEGs of the same mid-gray composites, downsized for fast marketplace listing thumbnails/galleries.

AO-114   Lumora "Dewdrop" lip-oil color-variant set: cut out on white, recolor one hero shot into 8 shade variants + spec sheet

Brand/vertical beauty_cosmeticsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 23)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Clean up and cut out one raw phone shot of the Dewdrop lip-oil bottle onto a white e-commerce background, then recolor the translucent oil into 8 on-brand shade variants while keeping the metallic collar, cap, and lighting/shadow identical across all of them, and emit a variant spec sheet.

Full brief

Lumora Beauty Co. is launching "Dewdrop," a glossy translucent lip oil in a frosted-glass dropper bottle with a brushed-gold collar and cap. We only had time to photograph ONE physical sample on an iPhone against a messy studio bench, so we need you to turn that single raw shot into a full, launch-ready shade range for our Shopify PDP and Amazon listing. First, clean up the raw photo: it's slightly tilted, a touch cool/dull, and the background is cluttered. Straighten it, correct the tone and white balance so the glass reads clean and neutral, and crop it square to our 2000x2000 px listing frame. Then cut the bottle out cleanly and place it on a pure white (#FFFFFF) e-commerce background — Amazon-compliant, no drop-shadow baked in, product centered with a little margin. Next, the important part: recolor JUST the translucent oil inside the bottle into 8 shade variants for the range — Clear Gloss (the original, ungraded), Peach Nectar, Rose Quartz, Coral Crush, Berry Tint, Amber Glow, Mauve Mist, and Cherry Sheer. The tint must look like real translucent oil: internal highlights and the light passing through the glass should shift naturally with each color, NOT a flat paint fill. Critically, the brushed-gold collar, the gold cap, the dropper glass rim, and the white background/shadow must stay IDENTICAL across all 8 — only the oil changes color. Deliverables: the 8 shade variants each as a 2000x2000 px white-background JPEG PLUS a transparent-background PNG at 300 DPI (16 product files total), and a one-page variant spec sheet (PDF) listing each shade name and its hue so our packaging team can match Pantone later. Keep everything photographic and consistent — this is a color-variant set of one product, not eight different photos.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
dewdrop_raw_iphone.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal iPhone snapshot of a single frosted-glass dropper bottle of lip oil standing upright on a cluttered studio ben
lumora_shade_palette.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns shade_name,hue_target,saturation_shift,description for 8 lip-oil shades: Clear Gloss (origin
lumora_brandkit.pdfdatagemini-3-pro-imageA simple one-page brand kit sheet for a fictional beauty brand 'Lumora Beauty Co.': brand name in a light modern serif,

Amazon/Shopify PDP-ready.

dewdrop_variant_spec_sheet.pdfpdfOne-page variant spec sheet (recipe note rendered/converted to PDF) listing each of the 8 shade names, its target hue, and the constant-elements note (gold collar/cap unchanged, white #FFFFFF background) for the packaging team's later Pantone match.

Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-115   Rukmini Silverworks 925 jewelry catalog: batch isolate-on-pure-white with metal grade + consistent 3000px square e-com crops (transparent PNG + white-BG JPEG per SKU)

Brand/vertical jewelry_luxury_goodsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 45)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Batch-process six raw 925-silver SKU photos into consistent, cast-free, pure-#FFFFFF e-commerce images — isolate each piece, grade the metal so it reads crisp, and deliver a transparent PNG plus a 3000x3000 white-background JPEG per SKU.

Full brief

Rukmini Silverworks, a boutique 925 sterling-silver house, is re-listing its collection across its own store and two marketplaces and needs clean, consistent product imagery so the metalwork stays the hero. The owner has handed off six raw tabletop frames shot on a phone under mixed studio light — a filigree pendant, a pair of jhumka drop earrings, a stacking band ring, a chain bracelet, an oxidised cuff, and a solitaire-look ring. As-shot they are inconsistent: slightly tilted, framed loose with a grey sweep and prop clutter visible, dull/flat exposure, and a warm-to-cool colour cast that makes the silver look muddy or yellow rather than bright neutral. The job is a batch clean-up, not a re-shoot: for every SKU (1) straighten and level the piece; (2) neutralise the white balance and lift the exposure so the silver reads as bright, cool-neutral metal with no colour cast; (3) gently push clarity/contrast and, on the oxidised piece, hold the dark detail so the antique recesses stay legible; (4) select the jewellery as the subject, invert that selection to a background-only mask, and solid-fill it with pure #FFFFFF so each piece sits on a flat, shadow-free white backdrop (the metalwork must keep its true edge — no halo, no chopped prongs, open chain-links and prong gaps preserved); (5) crop every SKU to an identical centered square and export at 3000 x 3000 px, sRGB, web-ready; (6) from the same graded master, strip the background to a clean transparent PNG for the store's own layered layouts. Deliverables per SKU: a transparent-background PNG and a 3000 x 3000 white-#FFFFFF JPEG, all six looking like a matched set. Brand constraint: pure #FFFFFF background (hex exact), no visible shadow, no colour cast, metal is the hero — the pieces must look like one consistent catalog, not six different edits.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
sku01_filigree_pendant_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone macro of a 925 sterling-silver filigree pendant lying on a light-grey paper sweep, mixed tabletop studio
sku02_jhumka_earrings_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone macro of a pair of 925 silver jhumka drop earrings on a grey fabric surface, cool bluish colour cast fro
sku03_stacking_band_ring_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone macro of a thin 925 silver stacking band ring standing on a small acrylic ring stand on a grey sweep, fl
sku04_chain_bracelet_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone macro of a 925 silver curb-chain bracelet coiled on a light grey surface, mixed warm/cool light, dull fl
sku05_oxidised_cuff_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone macro of an oxidised 925 silver cuff bracelet with blackened antique detailing on a grey board, warm yel
sku06_solitaire_ring_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone macro of a 925 silver solitaire-look ring with a clear cubic-zirconia stone on a grey sweep, cool cast,
rukmini_catalog_spec.txtdatagemini-2.5-flashWrite a short 6-line client spec note for a silver-jewelry catalog batch: background must be pure #FFFFFF (exact hex), n

Each stripped from the same per-SKU graded master via image_remove_background.
sku_whitebg_3000_jpg_x6imageSix 3000 x 3000 px sRGB JPEGs, each isolated piece on a pure #FFFFFF backdrop built by select-subject -> invert -> solid-fill of the background mask, flat and shadow-free, identical centered square crop; marketplace-ready and visually matched across the set.
