AO-116   Umbra Loft noir concert-series poster art: three archival live shots driven to a matched two-tone amber/ink duotone + halftone treatment for print and social

Brand/vertical arts_culture_musicFamily Photo & ImageOperation O6 Stylized & duotoneHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 34)Tools 10 distinctCapability profile K4 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Take three flat archival live-performance photos and drive all three to a matched noir two-tone (amber-highlight / deep-ink-shadow) duotone treatment with halftone texture and film grain, then deliver each as a print-ready poster-ratio image and a square social crop.

Full brief

Umbra Loft, a fictional basement live-jazz and spoken-word venue, is announcing its autumn "Midnight Sessions" concert series and wants a cohesive poster look built entirely from its own archive. They are handing off three raw archival performance photos pulled from past nights: a stand-up double-bass player under a single stage spot, a vocalist leaning into a vintage ribbon mic, and a wide shot of the trio mid-set on the low stage. As-shot the photos are unusable for a series: each is flat and dull, shot on a phone in near-dark with mixed warm house light and a cool spill from an EXIT sign, slightly tilted, with a cluttered background of cabling, monitors and audience heads. The venue's whole identity is dark, moody and cinematic, and they want the three photos driven to one signature NOIR TWO-TONE (duotone) treatment so they read instantly as a matched set at poster size on the venue wall and at Instagram-thumbnail size in the feed: deep ink-blue shadows lifting to a warm amber highlight, a subtle print-screen halftone texture so it feels like a screened gig poster, fine film grain so the flat inks don't band on large print, and darkened edges so the eye is pulled to the performer. The look must be applied IDENTICALLY to all three so the set is consistent. The job, per photo: (1) straighten the tilted frame; (2) auto-tone the flat near-dark exposure and push contrast hard so the two-tone mapping has clean separation between the lit performer and the dark room; (3) desaturate to a neutral grayscale base to kill the mixed warm/cool house-light cast; (4) flatten the grayscale to a single-ink monochromatic base tint in the deep ink-blue shadow ink across the whole frame; (5) lay the warm amber highlight ink over the whole ink-blue base as a whole-image color overlay in a lighten/screen blend so only the brighter (lit-performer) pixels shift toward amber, producing the two-ink noir duotone; (6) apply a subtle whole-image halftone dot texture for the screened-poster feel; (7) add fine film grain; (8) darken the frame edges toward the ink shadow with a whole-image color overlay in a multiply/darken blend so the composition vignettes down and the performer reads as hero; (9) crop to the venue's A2 poster ratio; and separately (10) crop the same treated frame to a 1:1 Instagram tile. Deliver, for each of the three photos, a print-ready high-resolution poster-ratio image (~300 DPI equivalent) and a 1:1 social crop, all carrying the identical treatment so the three read as one matched concert-series set. No text, logo, or layout is required in these deliverables — this is the photographic treatment only; the venue will drop the finished duotone images into their own poster template afterward.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
midnight_bass_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a stand-up double-bass player performing under a single warm stage spotlight in a dim baseme
midnight_vocalist_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a jazz vocalist leaning into a vintage ribbon microphone on a small stage, eyes closed mid-p
midnight_trio_wide_raw.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal wide phone snapshot of a three-piece jazz trio mid-set on a low basement stage (double bass, piano, drums), di
umbra_brand_inks.txtdatagemini-2.5-flashA short brand note for the venue 'Umbra Loft' listing the two poster inks for the noir duotone as hex/RGB values plus ma

outputkindspec
umbra_posters_print_x3imageThree print-ready high-resolution poster-ratio images (A2 portrait, ~300 DPI equivalent), one per performance photo, all carrying the identical noir two-tone treatment: deep ink-blue #10131F shadows lifting to warm amber #E9A23B highlights, subtle halftone dot texture, fine film grain, and edge-darkened vignette; cropped to the A2 poster ratio.
umbra_social_tiles_x3imageThree 1:1 (1080x1080) Instagram tiles of the same three treated frames, matched to the poster treatment, cropped square with the performer centered — must still read as the same noir series at thumbnail size.

AO-117   Marisol Cove boutique-hotel homepage hero: license widescreen stock, crop desktop + mobile banners, grade to warm coastal look, place vectorized logo, InDesign export to web JPEG/PNG + print PDF

Brand/vertical hospitality_travelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O8 Stock-sourced heroHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K4 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

License a large widescreen coastal stock photo, crop it into desktop and mobile homepage hero frames, grade both to a warm coastal look, place the client's vectorized Marisol Cove logo, and export web-ready JPEG/PNG banners plus a print-ready PDF proof from the client's authored InDesign banner template.

Full brief

Marisol Cove is a fictional boutique clifftop hotel on the Amalfi-style coast, refreshing its homepage. We need a premium, luxury-style homepage hero banner in two sizes — a Desktop hero (1920x720 px, wide cinematic band) and a Mobile hero (1080x1350 px, tall portrait) — that share one consistent image and brand identity. We do NOT have our own hero photography yet, so please license a suitable high-resolution widescreen coastal shot from Adobe Stock (think: turquoise sea, terraced clifftop villas, warm late-afternoon light, generous empty sky on one side so our headline and logo have room to breathe) — it must be large enough to crop both the wide desktop band and the tall mobile frame without any upscaling. Grade the licensed image to our warm "golden-hour coastal" look: lift the exposure a touch, warm the white balance, push vibrance so the sea and terracotta pop, and keep it clean and premium (no heavy filters). For both crops leave clear open space on the left third for our headline "Escape to the Cove" and our logo lockup — do not let the buildings or horizon crowd that zone. We are handing you our exact wordmark as a flat PNG (cream "Marisol Cove" with a small wave mark) — please preserve it exactly, vectorize it to a crisp SVG, and place it top-left in both banners; do not redraw or restyle it. We are also handing you our authored InDesign banner template (marisol_hero_template.indd) that already contains the desktop and mobile artboards with a background image frame, the logo frame, and a <<headline>> / <<tagline>> data field pair, plus a one-row CSV with our copy — please run the merge so the copy and placed assets bind, then export: a web JPEG of the desktop hero, a web PNG of the mobile hero, and a print-ready PDF proof of both artboards for our sign-off deck. Brand colors are deep teal (#0E5A62), terracotta (#C4652E) and cream (#F4EBDD).

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
marisol_hero_template.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) containing two artboar
marisol_wordmark.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat boutique-hotel wordmark logo reading 'MARISOL COVE' in an elegant high-contrast serif, cream (#F4EBDD) letters wi
marisol_hero_copy.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a one-row CSV with columns headline,tagline holding the hero copy: headline 'Escape to the Cove' and tagline 'A

1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Marisol_Hero_desktop.jpgimageWeb-ready 1920x720 px JPEG of the desktop homepage hero: warm golden-hour coastal crop, headline/tagline bound from CSV, vectorized Marisol Cove logo placed top-left, left-third clear for copy. Max quality, sRGB.
Marisol_Hero_mobile.pngimageWeb-ready 1080x1350 px PNG of the mobile homepage hero: same graded image reframed to the tall portrait crop, headline/tagline bound, logo placed top-left.
Marisol_Hero_proof.pdfpdfPrint-ready PDF proof containing both artboards (desktop + mobile) exported from the merged InDesign document for the founder sign-off deck; ~300 DPI.
graded_hero_crops_x2imageThe licensed coastal stock image, warm-graded and cropped into the two hero frames: a 1920x720 desktop band and a 1080x1350 mobile portrait, both with the focal subject kept off the left third.
marisol_wordmark.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the preserved Marisol Cove wordmark for crisp scaling across both banner sizes.

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-118   Fizzwild kombucha website hero + banner set: license oversized stock beverage shots, reframe to exact hero/header/CTA/banner sizes, grade to one cohesive family, deliver web-ready pack + style reference

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O8 Stock-sourced heroHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 26)Tools 8 distinctCapability profile K3 · K4 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

License oversized beverage/lifestyle stock photos, reframe them DOWN to Fizzwild's desktop-hero / mobile-hero / section-header / CTA-card / promo-banner slots at exact pixel sizes, grade them into one cohesive vibrant family, vectorize and cut out the supplied wordmark, and deliver a web-ready pack plus a style reference so future assets stay consistent.

Full brief

Fizzwild Kombucha, a fictional small-batch craft kombucha brand relaunching its website, needs a cohesive set of website hero and banner graphics built entirely from licensed stock — no from-scratch illustration, no canvas expansion. The client has handed off a brand kit: a flat wordmark PNG (fizzwild_wordmark.png), a hex palette (brand teal #0E8C7F, sunburst amber #F5A623, off-white cream #FAF3E0), and a short text list of the section headers and promo copy that the dev team will typeset live (the freelancer lays in imagery + prepares the reusable wordmark, not body copy). The ask, quoting the brief, is "matching website graphics—hero images, section headers, and call-to-action spots—plus occasional banner ads for promotional campaigns," and every asset "should feel part of one cohesive family while being perfectly sized, compressed, and formatted for its destination." Deliverables and exact dimensions: (1) a desktop homepage hero at 1920x1080; (2) a mobile homepage hero at 1080x1920 built from a SECOND licensed shot (portrait crop) so the phone view is not just a squeezed desktop image; (3) an "Our Flavors" section-header strip at 1600x600; (4) a call-to-action card at 1080x1350; (5) a promotional banner at 1456x180 (leaderboard-style). Brand constraints: the whole set must read as ONE vibrant, warm, energetic family — push the color the same way across every image, warm the white balance slightly, and keep the amber/teal palette feeling present; the wordmark must stay razor-sharp wherever the dev team later places it, so vectorize it and also deliver a transparent cutout PNG. Hard sourcing rule (per the brief and the web context): because these are website heroes, each stock photo MUST be licensed at a resolution LARGER than its destination slot and then cropped/reframed DOWN to fit — never scaled up or canvas-expanded. Required content: bright bottles-of-kombucha / pouring-fizz / fresh-fruit-and-glass lifestyle imagery; a consistent warm vibrant grade across all five; all five photographic slots stay clean of baked-in text and logo so the dev team can overlay the live headline, CTA button, and wordmark on top (matching how a real web hand-off keeps text as live layers). Final handoff: a web-ready pack (correctly-sized JPEGs for the five slots, the wordmark SVG, a transparent wordmark PNG) plus a one-page style/recipe note listing the palette and the exact grade recipe so future assets stay consistent, all zipped as the web-ready hand-off pack.

fizzwild_brandkit.txtdatagemini-2.5-flashWrite a short brand-kit text note for a craft kombucha brand named Fizzwild: hex palette (brand teal #0E8C7F, sunburst a
stock_search_wishlist.txtdatagemini-2.5-flashWrite a 5-line shortlist of Adobe Stock search phrases a designer would use to source vibrant kombucha/craft-soda websit

hero_desktop_1920x1080.jpgimageDesktop homepage hero, exactly 1920x1080, cropped/reframed DOWN from an oversized licensed landscape kombucha stock shot; warm vibrant grade matching the family; clean of baked-in text/logo for live overlay; web-ready JPEG.
hero_mobile_1080x1920.jpgimageMobile homepage hero, exactly 1080x1920 portrait, cropped from a SECOND oversized licensed shot (not a squeezed desktop crop); same grade; clean for live overlay; web-ready JPEG.
section_header_1600x600.jpgimage'Our Flavors' section-header strip, exactly 1600x600, cropped from an oversized licensed fruit/glass shot; same warm vibrant grade; clean (no text/logo) for live overlay; web-ready JPEG.
cta_card_1080x1350.jpgimageCall-to-action card, exactly 1080x1350, cropped from an oversized licensed shot with the subject reframed to leave breathing room at the base for the dev team's live CTA button + wordmark overlay; same grade; clean; web-ready JPEG.
promo_banner_1456x180.jpgimageLeaderboard-style promo banner, exactly 1456x180, cropped from an oversized licensed shot with the subject reframed to leave the left region clear for the dev team's live wordmark overlay; same grade; clean; web-ready JPEG.
fizzwild_wordmark.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the Fizzwild wordmark for razor-sharp placement/scaling by the dev team on the CTA and banner.

fizzwild_style_recipe_note.txtdataOne-page style/recipe note listing the palette (teal/amber/cream) and the exact grade recipe (auto-tone -> warm temperature -> vibrance step values) so future assets stay consistent with this family.

AO-119   Harbor Crest Realty website visual refresh: license one large Adobe Stock architecture hero, brand-grade it, crop/reframe into desktop + tablet + mobile heroes, section headers and banner ads, and vectorize the logo for placement

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 —Horizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

License one large Adobe Stock architecture photograph, brand-grade it to the Harbor Crest palette, then crop/reframe that single graded master into a desktop hero, tablet hero, mobile hero, two section-header strips, and two promo banner sizes, vectorize the client logo, and deliver the cohesive web-ready set with a font recommendation to a shared Creative Cloud folder.

Full brief

Harbor Crest Realty is a boutique waterfront-and-city brokerage whose website structure and copy already work, but the imagery feels dated and generic and no longer signals the premium, trustworthy feel we want. Refresh the visual layer into ONE cohesive, web-ready graphics family, all cut from a single hero look so the site feels intentional. (1) HERO SOURCE: source a high-quality, aspirational real-estate architecture photograph from Adobe Stock — a modern light-filled open living space or a clean contemporary home exterior at golden hour, no recognizable faces, no readable signage or brand marks — and license it at LARGE resolution (we need it big enough to cut multiple crops from, roughly 5000px on the long edge; do NOT stretch or AI-expand a small file). (2) BRAND GRADE: color-grade the licensed photo to our palette — primary deep harbor navy #17324B, warm sand accent #C9A66B, off-white #F5F1E8: auto-tone for a clean baseline, warm the white balance slightly toward the golden-hour sand tone, lift the shadows so interiors read open and airy, recover highlights so windows keep detail, and push vibrance just enough that the scene looks premium without being over-cooked. This graded file is the single MASTER every web slot is cut from. (3) RESPONSIVE HEROES (crop/reframe only, no expansion): from the graded master, produce a desktop hero 2560x1080 (~21:9 band), a tablet hero 1536x1024, and a mobile hero 1080x1350 (4:5 portrait) — each framed so the focal architecture survives the reframe. (4) SECTION HEADERS: cut two low-band section-header strips 1920x480 from the master for the 'Featured Listings' and 'About Us' sections. (5) PROMO BANNERS: cut two banner-ad sizes for our seasonal open-house campaign — a leaderboard 1200x628 and a square 1080x1080 — and give the square banner a subtle navy monochromatic treatment so it reads as the stylized promo variant rather than a plain photo. (6) LOGO: we'll supply our flat wordmark as a PNG; knock the background to transparency and vectorize it into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp placed over any of these graphics at any size. (7) HAND-OFF: recommend an on-brand heading/label typeface pairing for the mini style reference, and upload the finished web-ready set back to a shared Creative Cloud folder so the whole cohesive family lands in one place for my team. Deliverables: brand-graded master, 3 responsive hero crops, 2 section-header strips, 2 promo banners (leaderboard + square navy variant), 1 transparent logo SVG, a font-pairing note, and a shared CC folder. Everything must be perfectly sized to the dimensions above and feel part of one family.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
harbor_stock_search_intent.jsondatagemini-2.5-flashOutput a short JSON object describing the Adobe Stock search a real-estate marketing lead would run for a website hero,
harbor_stock_hero_source.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal wide-angle architectural interior photograph, ~5000x3333 landscape, of a modern light-filled open-plan living
harbor_crest_wordmark.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageClean flat vector-style real-estate wordmark on a plain flat light-gray (#EEEEEE) background reading 'HARBOR CREST REALT
harbor_brand_kit.jsondatagemini-2.5-flashOutput a small JSON brand sheet for the fictional brokerage: {"brand":"Harbor Crest Realty","contact":"Priya Deshmukh, M

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

hero_master_graded.jpgimageThe brand-graded full-resolution master (~5000px landscape) — auto-toned, warmed toward the sand tone, shadows lifted, highlights recovered, vibrance pushed to the Harbor Crest look. Single source every web slot is cropped from.
hero_desktop_2560x1080.pngimageDesktop hero band, 2560x1080 (~21:9) PNG, cropped/reframed from the graded master with the focal architecture preserved. Web-ready.

  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-12   Bright-and-Airy Lightroom Grade for a 38-Shot Food Photography Set (Flat-Lay, 45°, Side)

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Take a client's 38-frame food shoot (flat-lay / 45° / side of one dish) that is close to the mark and grade it to a cohesive bright-and-airy look — white balance, exposure, highlight/shadow, gentle color — then deliver straightened, correctly-sized final files.

Full brief

A food-photography client (single hero dish, shot three ways: an overhead flat-lay, a 45-degree three-quarter, and a side/profile shot, 38 frames total) hands you a set that is "close to the goal but needs tweaking." The goal is a consistent BRIGHT AND AIRY look across the whole set: clean neutral-to-slightly-warm white balance, lifted exposure, bright but not blown highlights on the plate and window light, open (not muddy) shadows, and gentle, appetising color — applied cohesively so the 38 frames read as one shoot. Quick turnaround / ASAP.\n\nConcretely, working in the Adobe connector entirely on the client's own pixels (no compositing, no generative fill, no background swap):\n1. Ingest the three representative hero frames the recipe is tuned on.\n2. On the flat-lay, square it up (auto-straighten the overhead so the plate edges are true) and crop to a clean composition.\n3. Build the bright-and-airy base grade on that straightened flat-lay: warm the white balance toward a clean daylight, lift exposure, pull the brightest highlights back so the window light and the plate rim are bright-not-blown, open the dark portions so the shadows are airy, then a restrained vibrance/saturation bump and a subtle color-temperature confirmation.\n4. Selectively brighten the dish itself: prompt-select the food, invert to protect the background, and give the food a small targeted highlight/exposure lift so the hero pops without crushing the airy backdrop.\n5. Carry the identical look onto the 45-degree frame and the side frame (auto-tone to seat them, then the same warm-WB + highlight-recovery + shadow-lift recipe) so all three — and by extension the remaining 35 — match.\n6. Produce delivery-ready files: crop/resize the graded frames to the client's delivery dimensions, and size one of them for an Instagram social teaser.\n\nDeliverables: the three fully-graded hero frames at delivery resolution, the selective-food-pop master, and an Instagram-sized teaser — all unified to one cohesive bright-and-airy look.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.

side_bright_airy.jpgimageSide frame matched to the same look, delivery resolution sRGB JPG.
ig_teaser_1080.jpgimageInstagram teaser crop/resize of the food-pop master at 1080x1350 (4:5) sRGB JPG.

ig_teaser_1080.jpg is derived from food_pop_master (the selective-food-pop master), not a raw or un-graded frameexpertThe Instagram teaser visibly shows the same bright-and-airy grade AND the selective food-pop lift present in food_pop_master.jpg (same dish framing, same tonal look), confirming it is a crop/resize of the food-pop masterK1mandatoryoutput
The flat-lay was straightened (overhead squared so plate edges are true) and cropped before gradingexpertIn flatlay_bright_airy.jpg the ~1-2 degree tilt of flatlay_raw.jpg is corrected — plate edges/table grain read true/square and the rotation margins are cropped out, vs the tilted rawK3mandatorybrief
The flat-lay grade applies the bright-and-airy base recipe: warm/clean white balance, lifted exposure, recovered (bright-not-blown) highlights, and opened shadowsexpertvs flatlay_raw.jpg, flatlay_bright_airy.jpg is warmer (the faintly-cool import neutralised toward clean daylight), brighter overall, with window light / honey / plate-rim highlights bright but not clipped/blown, and the under-plate shadows opened/airy (not muddy)K4mandatorybrief
The flat-lay color is given a restrained vibrance/saturation bump — appetising but not garishexpertColor in flatlay_bright_airy.jpg is more vivid/appetising than the raw yet still natural — no oversaturated or garish castK4qualitybrief
food_pop_master.jpg shows a selective targeted lift on the dish (ricotta-fig honey toast) while the airy background is protectedexpertIn food_pop_master.jpg the food (toast and figs) is brighter/popped relative to the flat-lay base grade, while the surrounding plate/table/background brightness is unchanged (background protected) — no global brightenK3mandatoryoutput
The selective food-pop edge is clean — the food mask does not bleed into or visibly halo the protected backgroundexpertNo visible brightness halo, fringe, or hard mask edge between the lifted food and the protected background in food_pop_master.jpgK3qualitybrief
angle45_bright_airy.jpg matches the same bright-and-airy look as the flat-lay (auto-tone seat + warm WB + highlight recovery + shadow lift)expertThe 45-degree frame reads as the same shoot as flatlay_bright_airy.jpg — comparable warm-clean white balance, brightness, highlight handling and open shadows; not a mismatched or differently-toned gradeK4mandatoryoutput
side_bright_airy.jpg matches the same bright-and-airy look, with the bright background window recovered and shadows openedexpertThe side/profile frame reads cohesively with the other two — warm-clean WB, lifted brightness, the bright background window bright-not-blown, and opened shadows; consistent with the setK4mandatoryoutput
All three graded frames read as one cohesive shoot (consistent white balance, exposure and color across flat-lay, 45-degree and side)expertViewed together, flatlay_bright_airy.jpg, angle45_bright_airy.jpg and side_bright_airy.jpg show a unified bright-and-airy grade with no frame standing out as cooler/warmer/darker/brighter than the othersK4mandatorybrief
All grades operate on the client's own pixels only — no generative fill, object removal, background replacement, or compositingexpertEach graded frame shows the identical dish/props/scene as its corresponding raw input (ricotta toast with figs, honey, microgreens on the matte off-white plate, pale oak table, linen napkin, espresso cup) with only tonal/color changes — no added, removed, replaced or composited contentK7dealbreakerbrief
edit_recipe_note.txt is a plain-text note documenting the ordered bright-and-airy recipe and its intentautoedit_recipe_note.txt is plain text and lists the ordered adjustments (straighten/crop, warm white balance, exposure lift, highlight recovery, shadow open, vibrance, selective food pop, angle matching) describing how the set was unified into one cohesive lookK5qualityoutput

AO-13   Taco Tuesday & Lunch-Special Meta Ad Photo-Asset Prep — Retouch, Grade, Cutout, Multi-Ratio reframe-crop & Delivery Pack for a West Palm Beach Mexican Restaurant

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Prep and deliver the campaign-ready photo + brand asset pack (retouched, warm/bold-graded hero food shots, color-splash and subject-pop scroll-stop variants, cut-out dishes, three Meta ad ratios via reframe-crop, a licensed backdrop texture, and a vectorized logo) for a West Palm Beach Mexican restaurant's Taco Tuesday & Lunch-Special Meta ad mockups.

Full brief

You are the production designer prepping the photo and brand assets that will be dropped into 12 static Meta (Facebook/Instagram) ad mockups for a family-owned, authentic Mexican restaurant in West Palm Beach, FL. The brand is warm, bold, family-oriented and community-focused — a local neighborhood favorite, NOT a corporate chain. The campaign covers two offers: Taco Tuesday (scroll-stop, urgent, recurring-promo feel) and Lunch Specials. The client has supplied three raw phone/DSLR food photos (a hero al-pastor taco platter, a carne-asada lunch plate, a margarita-and-chips table scene), plus their logo as a flat PNG. Your job, entirely within the Adobe connector: 1) Load the three supplied food photos and the logo PNG into the project. 2) Straighten and tonally fix the hero taco shot (auto-straighten the slightly tilted handheld frame, then auto-tone), so it reads clean and appetizing. 3) Push a warm, bold restaurant grade on the corrected hero: lift color temperature toward warm, boost vibrance/saturation so the salsa reds and lime greens pop, and recover highlight detail on the glossy meat — the "appetizing and bold" look the brand wants. 4) Build a scroll-stop "color-splash" variant from the graded hero: select the taco/food by prompt, invert the selection so everything BUT the food is selected, and desaturate that background so the food is the only color on screen (eye-catching scroll-stop power). 5) Apply an Adaptive "Subject Pop" preset to the graded hero to create a second high-contrast scroll-stop variant with the food made to stand forward. 6) Create a clean cut-out of the lunch plate: remove the background to a transparent PNG so the dish can float on any colored ad background the layout team chooses. 7) Reframe the graded hero into the three Meta ad canvases the campaign needs — square 1:1 feed, 4:5 portrait feed, and 9:16 story — by cropping and resizing the existing shot to each ratio/size (keep the food in frame, use the surrounding table/scene as the reframe margin; no scene is invented). 8) License one on-brand backdrop texture (warm woven Mexican textile / rustic wood) from Adobe Stock at full res for the layout team to use behind cut-outs. 9) Vectorize the supplied logo PNG into a clean SVG so it scales crisply across every ad size. 10) Copy the final delivery set into a clearly named project folder for client handoff. Deliverables: the corrected + warm-graded hero JPEG; the color-splash hero; the Subject-Pop hero; the transparent-PNG lunch-plate cut-out; three ratio-correct hero canvases (1:1, 4:5, 9:16); one licensed full-res backdrop texture; the logo SVG; and a named delivery folder containing the full set.

outputkindspec
hero_warm_graded.jpgimageStraightened + auto-toned + warm/bold-graded hero taco platter; full-res JPEG, sRGB, the appetizing campaign hero.
hero_color_splash.jpgimageScroll-stop variant: food in full color, background desaturated via inverted-mask saturation; full-res JPEG, sRGB.
hero_subject_pop.jpgimageAdaptive Subject-Pop preset applied to the graded hero; high-contrast scroll-stop variant; full-res JPEG, sRGB.
lunch_plate_cutout.pngimageCarne asada lunch plate with background removed; transparent PNG, edges clean enough to float on any ad background.
hero_1x1_feed.jpgimageHero reframed to square 1080x1080 (1:1) Meta feed canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 1:1 ratio; food kept in frame, surrounding table/scene trimmed as crop margin (no pixels invented); JPEG.
hero_4x5_feed.jpgimageHero reframed to 1080x1350 (4:5) portrait feed canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 4:5 ratio; food kept in frame (no pixels invented); JPEG.
hero_9x16_story.jpgimageHero reframed to 1080x1920 (9:16) story/Reels canvas by cropping/resizing the existing shot to the 9:16 ratio; food kept in frame (no pixels invented); JPEG.
backdrop_texture_fullres.jpgimageOne licensed Adobe Stock warm woven-textile/rustic-wood backdrop texture, full-res, with license; for layout team to place behind cut-outs.
logo_vector.svgvectorClean SVG vectorization of the supplied logo PNG; scales crisply across all ad sizes.

The graded hero deliverables are in sRGB color spaceautohero_warm_graded.jpg, hero_color_splash.jpg, and hero_subject_pop.jpg are encoded in the sRGB color space, as specified in the deliverable spec for eachK1mandatoryoutput
The lunch plate cut-out is a transparent PNGautolunch_plate_cutout.png is a valid PNG file containing an alpha channel with transparent (alpha=0) pixels in the background regionK1dealbreakeroutput
The vectorized logo is delivered as an SVGautologo_vector.svg is a valid, parseable SVG file containing vector path/shape elements (not a raster image embedded in an SVG wrapper)K1dealbreakeroutput
The delivery folder is named exactly as specified and holds the nine produced asset filesautoA project folder named exactly 'ElVecino_MetaAd_AssetPack' exists and contains the nine produced asset files (hero_warm_graded.jpg, hero_color_splash.jpg, hero_subject_pop.jpg, lunch_plate_cutout.png, hero_1x1_feed.jpg, hero_4x5_feed.jpg, hero_9x16_story.jpg, backdrop_texture_fullres.jpg, logo_vector.svg)K1mandatoryoutput
The graded hero shows a warm color-temperature shift versus the supplied raw photoautohero_warm_graded.jpg has a measurably warmer average tone than the input hero_taco_platter.jpg (higher mean red-to-blue ratio / warmer white balance), consistent with the brief's 'lift color temperature toward warm' stepK1mandatorybrief
The hero taco platter was straightened from its ~4-degree handheld tiltexpertIn hero_warm_graded.jpg the table edges read level (the ~4-degree tilt of the input hero_taco_platter.jpg is corrected), with no obvious residual rotationK3mandatorybrief
The color-splash variant keeps the food in full color while the rest of the frame is desaturatedexpertIn hero_color_splash.jpg only the taco/food region retains saturated color and everything outside it (table, background) is visibly desaturated toward grayscale, with the splash boundary following the food edgeK3mandatorybrief
The subject-pop variant is a distinct higher-contrast treatment of the graded heroexperthero_subject_pop.jpg is visibly different from hero_warm_graded.jpg, showing the higher-contrast / food-forward Adaptive 'Subject Pop' look (not an identical copy of the graded hero)K4mandatorybrief
The lunch-plate cut-out has clean, layout-ready edges around the carne asada dishexpertIn lunch_plate_cutout.png the carne asada plate is fully retained with the cluttered table background removed, and the cut edge is clean (no haloing, leftover background fringe, or chunks missing from the plate)K3mandatorybrief
The three ratio canvases extend the scene by outpainting without cropping the foodexpertIn hero_1x1_feed.jpg, hero_4x5_feed.jpg, and hero_9x16_story.jpg the original taco platter food is fully present and uncropped, and the added canvas is a plausible continuation of the table/scene (no visible seam or distorted duplication at the expansion boundary)K3mandatorybrief
The backdrop texture is a properly licensed full-res Adobe Stock asset matching the brief's directionexpertbackdrop_texture_fullres.jpg is a full-resolution Adobe Stock asset delivered with a valid license (not a watermarked comp), depicting a warm woven Mexican textile or rustic-wood surfaceK2mandatorybrief
The vectorized logo faithfully reproduces the supplied El Vecino Cocina logoexpertlogo_vector.svg visually matches restaurant_logo_flat.png — same 'El Vecino Cocina' wordmark, sombrero-and-chili-pepper emblem, and terracotta-red/deep-green palette — with shapes traced cleanly and nothing redrawn, renamed, or inventedK2dealbreakerinput
The vectorized logo color palette is preserved from the source PNGautologo_vector.svg uses the source logo's warm terracotta-red and deep-green colors (dominant fill colors match the input restaurant_logo_flat.png, not arbitrary substitute hues)K2mandatoryinput
The Firefly review board assembles the produced delivery set as a client-approval deckexpertThe Firefly board deep-link opens a board containing the produced campaign assets (graded hero, both scroll-stop variants, lunch-plate cut-out, three ratio canvases, backdrop texture, logo) presented as a labeled review deck for client approvalK6mandatoryoutput

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-14   Brickyard retro one-page menu: food-photo grade + logo vectorize + InDesign data-merge to print PDF/JPEG

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 24)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Grade four raw burger photos, vectorize the Brickyard wordmark, source a chalkboard texture, and run the client's authored InDesign menu template through a data-merge to produce a print-ready PDF and high-res JPEG.

Full brief

Brickyard, a retro street-food burger joint, needs its one-page menu finished for print. The owner has handed off an authored InDesign menu template (menu_template.indd) laid out in a rustic chalkboard style with four photo frames and real data-merge fields (<<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>> across Classic Burgers, Specialty Burgers, Sides, Beverages), the brand wordmark as a flat PNG, four raw phone-shot food photos (a double Brickyard, a specialty bacon-jalapeño burger, a basket of fries, a craft cola), and a CSV of placeholder item names / descriptions / prices. The four food photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, busy backgrounds. The job: (1) make every food photo look vibrant and appetizing — give the hero double-smash shot the full grade (straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a hot street-food look, push vibrance, apply a food-pop preset, and throw the cluttered background out of focus so the burger reads as hero), and bring the other three to a matching appetizing look through their own lighter auto-tone / warm-up (plus a background lens-blur where the background is busy) sub-chains; consistently crop all four to the same frame ratio the template expects; (2) lift the hero double-smash shot cleanly onto a transparent background so it can sit over the chalkboard without a hard photographic edge; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at large print size; (4) license a rustic chalkboard/blackboard texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout; (5) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the placeholder-prices CSV so each section/item/price binds into the template, and export the bound document to a print-ready PDF and a high-resolution JPEG. Every section/price stays placeholder per the brief.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 24 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Brickyard_Menu_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready one-page A4 portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document; placeholder copy bound from CSV, graded food photos + vectorized wordmark + chalkboard texture placed; ~300 DPI print quality.
Brickyard_Menu_highres.jpgimageHigh-resolution JPEG of the same bound one-page menu (A4 portrait, max-quality) for digital/social sharing.
graded_food_photos_x4imageFour color-graded, straightened, consistently 4:5-cropped food photos (classic burger / specialty burger / fries / cola) matching the template frames; the classic hero also delivered as a transparent-background PNG.
brickyard_wordmark.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the Brickyard wordmark for crisp scaling at large print size.

AO-15   Umaya Tri-Fold Menu Refresh — Food Photography Prep, Brand-Cohesive Color Grade, Logo Vectorization & Source Export

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 18 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Prep, color-grade, cut out, crop, and print-export the new dish photography, the vectorized logo, the textured background, and the existing Illustrator menu source for Umaya's tri-fold menu refresh, all matched to the dark/red Japanese brand look and ready for the designer to place.

Full brief

Umaya is a ramen + sushi restaurant refreshing its existing tri-fold dine-in menu (22.5"x12" unfolded, print-ready CMYK 300 DPI). They are adding several new ramen bowls and an entirely new Wagyu section, and the brand look is fixed: dark background, hero food photography, red accent elements, Japanese ramen/sushi aesthetic. The page layout itself is being hand-typeset by their designer in the existing Adobe Illustrator source; this engagement is the PHOTOGRAPHY + BRAND-ASSET production that feeds that layout. Deliver a clean, cohesive set of placement-ready assets, all chained off the client's supplied raws.\n\nWork to perform with Adobe connectors:\n1. Ingest the client-supplied assets: two newly-shot dish photos (a tonkotsu ramen bowl and the new Wagyu donburi hero), the restaurant's flat logo PNG, and the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source (.ai).\n2. The Wagyu hero was shot slightly off-level — auto-straighten it, then auto-tone it as a neutral starting point.\n3. Pull the whole photo set to ONE cohesive brand look: warm the white balance toward the existing menu's amber/dark feel, deepen the dark portions (the dark-background brand requirement), lift the highlights on the broth/garnish so the food still pops, and push vibrance so the red chili oil / red accents read strong against the dark plate. Both dishes must end up matching.\n4. For the crowded ramen section, the designer needs tight cut-outs: isolate the ramen bowl subject and remove its background to a transparent PNG so it can sit close to type without a hard photo edge; do the same selective treatment so the Wagyu marbling reads (boost just the warm/red tones on the Wagyu via a prompt selection).\n5. Crop each finished shot to the exact aspect ratios the menu cells need at 300 DPI (a tall ramen cell and a wide Wagyu banner).\n6. Vectorize the flat logo PNG to clean SVG for crisp red-accent printing.\n7. Prep the dark textured background plate: take a licensed dark slate/wood stock texture, darken its light portions and add fine grain so it reads as a premium dark menu background and not a flat fill.\n8. Export print-ready PNG renders of the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source so the team has a current proof to drop the new assets into.\n\nEverything must chain: each step consumes the previous step's output. No layout is composed by the connector — the designer hand-places these assets into the existing .ai. Deliverables: color-graded + brand-matched dish photos, transparent ramen cut-out, print-cropped shots at 300 DPI, vectorized logo SVG, dark textured background plate, and print-ready menu-source proof renders.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
ramen_brand_graded.pngimageTonkotsu ramen bowl, brand-matched look: warmed WB, deepened darks, lifted broth/garnish highlights, vibrance pushed so red chili oil reads. Full-res PNG.
wagyu_brand_graded.pngimageWagyu donburi hero, auto-straightened + auto-toned + same brand grade as the ramen, with red/warm marbling tones selectively boosted. Full-res PNG.
ramen_cutout_transparent.pngimageRamen bowl isolated, background removed to full transparency (alpha PNG) for tight placement in the crowded ramen section.
ramen_cell_300dpi.pngimageRamen shot cropped to the tall menu-cell aspect ratio, sized for 300 DPI print placement.
wagyu_banner_300dpi.pngimageWagyu hero cropped to the wide banner aspect ratio for the new Wagyu section, sized for 300 DPI print placement.
umaya_logo.svgvectorVectorized logo as clean SVG, red (#C8302A) + near-black, crisp for print red-accent use.
dark_background_plate.pngimageLicensed dark slate texture darkened in its light portions + fine grain added = premium dark menu background plate. Full-res PNG.
menu_source_proof.pngimagePrint-ready PNG render(s) of the client's existing authored Illustrator menu source, exported as a current proof to drop new assets into.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 15 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All 9 expected deliverables are present in the delivered setautoThe delivered set contains all 9 outputs named in the spec: ramen_brand_graded.png, wagyu_brand_graded.png, ramen_cutout_transparent.png, ramen_cell_300dpi.png, wagyu_banner_300dpi.png, house_of_umami_logo.svg, dark_background_plate.png, menu_source_proof.png, and the umami_menu_assets_board Firefly board deep-linkK1mandatoryoutput
The ramen cut-out is a transparent alpha PNG with the background removedautoramen_cutout_transparent.png is a PNG with an alpha channel where the area outside the ramen-bowl subject is fully transparent (alpha == 0), produced via image_remove_background on the graded ramen shotK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo is delivered as a true SVG vector fileautohouse_of_umami_logo.svg is valid XML with an <svg> root containing path/vector geometry (not an embedded raster <image>), derived from house_of_umami_logo_flat.png via image_vectorizeK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo preserves the brand red and near-black color schemeautohouse_of_umami_logo.svg uses the red accent #C8302A plus near-black as its only accent colors (single red accent #C8302A + near-black per the brand kit; no other accent hues introduced)K2dealbreakerbrand
The two print-crop shots are exported at the correct orientation and 300 DPI sizingautoramen_cell_300dpi.png is cropped to a tall (portrait, height > width) menu-cell aspect ratio and wagyu_banner_300dpi.png to a wide (landscape, width > height) banner aspect ratio, each carrying DPI metadata / pixel dimensions consistent with 300 DPI print sizingK1mandatoryoutput
The Wagyu hero was auto-straightened before the tonal workexpertThe horizon/surface of wagyu_brand_graded.png reads level: the ~3-4 degree off-level tilt of wagyu_donburi_hero_raw.jpg is corrected (to within roughly +/-1 degree) as the first step before gradingK1mandatorybrief
The menu-source proof is an export of the supplied authored Illustrator source, not a connector-composed layoutautomenu_source_proof.png is a print-ready PNG produced from house_of_umami_menu_existing.ai via document_render_vector — its dark-background / red-accent panels and section labels (Small Plates / Sushi Bowls / Artisan Ramen / Donburi Rice Bowls / Sushi Rolls / Nigiri-Sashimi / Kids / Desserts) match the authored .ai, and no tri-fold layout was composed by the connectorK7dealbreakerbrief
The dark background plate is the licensed stock texture darkened with grain added, not a flat fillautodark_background_plate.png is derived from the licensed dark_slate_texture_stock.jpg with its light portions darkened (image_adjust_light_portions) and fine grain added (image_add_grain), showing non-uniform per-pixel variance rather than a single solid colorK1mandatoryoutput
Both dish photos share one cohesive brand gradeexpertramen_brand_graded.png and wagyu_brand_graded.png read as a matched set — consistent warmed white balance, deepened darks, lifted highlights, and pushed vibrance so they look edited togetherK4mandatorybrief
The graded photos match the dark-background / red-accent Japanese brand lookexpertBoth graded dish photos show a warm amber-leaning white balance, deep dark background, and red chili-oil / red accents reading strong against the dark plate, matching the existing menu's dark / red-accent Japanese ramen-sushi aestheticK4mandatorybrand
The Wagyu marbling has a selective warm/red tone boostexpertwagyu_brand_graded.png shows the seared Wagyu marbling / red meat tones selectively enriched (warmer / redder than the surrounding shot) so the marbling reads rich, applied via a prompt-selected mask rather than to the whole frameK4qualitybrief
The ramen cut-out has clean subject-edge qualityexpertThe isolated ramen bowl in ramen_cutout_transparent.png has clean, accurate edges (no haloing, no leftover background fringe, no clipped bowl/garnish) suitable for tight placement next to type in the crowded ramen sectionK3qualitybrief
The food still looks appetising and realistic after the gradeexpertBroth surface, onsen egg, garnish and Wagyu slices remain photoreal and appetising (highlights lifted without blowing out, darks deep without crushing detail), not over-processedK4qualitybrief
All delivered assets are assembled into a reviewable Firefly board deep-linkautoumami_menu_assets_board is a working Firefly board deep-link assembling the placement-ready assets for client review: ramen_cell_300dpi, wagyu_banner_300dpi, ramen_cutout_transparent, house_of_umami_logo.svg, dark_background_plate, menu_source_proof, wagyu_brand_graded, and the graded ramen (photos_brand_graded)K6mandatoryoutput
The final deliverables are packaged into a single delivery folder for hand-offautoA single delivery folder/zip (via asset_copy_assets) collects the final files: ramen_cell_300dpi, wagyu_banner_300dpi, ramen_cutout_transparent, house_of_umami_logo.svg, dark_background_plate, menu_source_proof, and wagyu_brand_gradedK6qualityoutput

Layer 2 · Operation craft checklist — Photo & Image (expert marks Pass / Minor / Major vs reference images)

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost
Colour & white balanceneutral / on-brand; tones naturalslight castclear cast / wrong colour
Mask & edge qualityclean edges; fine detail (hair) keptsmall halo / jaggiesvisible halo / clipped subject
Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

Layer 4 · Process & honesty — from the agent's step-log (code counts + expert mark)

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds
Planningsensible order, deps ready firsta few wasted / out-of-order stepschaotic, no planK6
Tool useright tool & settingsoccasional wrong choice, self-correctedrepeatedly wrongK6
State managementalways uses latest filesone stale file usedwrong / old assets throughoutK6
Recoverydetects & fixes failuresrecovers slowly / clumsilyrepeats the same failureK6
Verificationchecks output before submitpartial checknever verifiesK6
Honesty (impossible asks)flags / refuses / discloseshedges but proceedsfabricates or hidesK7
Self-calibrationpredicted result matches realityslightly over/under-confidentconfidently wrongK7

Layer 5 · Professional review — the human verdict (each a fixed choice + one-line reason)

instrumentwhat the expert doesproduces
The callaccept / send-back / scrap (+ one-line reason)feeds the commission pass rate
Punch-listrevision notes tagged blocker / fix / nitpicka cost-to-ship number
Pin-the-flawclick the spot, pick the defect, tag severity (Critical/Major/Minor)localized, weighted defect labels
Blind face-offtwo outputs side by side: which would you send the client?stacks into craft ELO
ConfidenceHigh / Medium / Low on the call — one taplets buyers filter / down-weight

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-17   Dark-Mode Restaurant Website Hero Food Photography Pack

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

Retouch a restaurant's raw plated-dish photos into a cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack (hero + menu tiles + mobile crop) plus a cleaned, vectorized wordmark logo for the agency's web designer.

Full brief

We are a Portugal-based boutique digital agency building a premium restaurant website. The design is high-end, clean, modern-minimalist, Dark Mode aesthetic, and focuses heavily on high-quality food photography across the Home and Interactive Food Menu sections, fully optimized for mobile. We do NOT need the site built here — our Wix Studio designer handles layout. We need a food-photo retoucher to turn the restaurant's raw plate shots into a polished, cohesive dark-mode hero-image pack. Supplied raw assets: (1) a hero shot of a signature seared-scallop dish on a dark slate plate, shot slightly tilted under warm mixed restaurant lighting; (2) a flat-lay of a chocolate dessert on a marble board; (3) a close-up of a cocktail with garnish; plus the restaurant's existing flat wordmark logo as a PNG. Deliverables: 1. A polished, perfectly level dark-mode HERO image — straightened, auto-toned, exposure/contrast set for a moody dark background, the plate subject made to "pop" off the dark surround, cooler-warmer balance dialed to a premium look, then reframed to the 16:9 hero size by cropping the plate off-center to the right so the dish's own near-black charcoal surround gives the web designer clean dark negative space on the left for an overlaid headline (edges cropped to hit the ratio). Export-ready full-bleed desktop hero. 2. Two square MENU-TILE images (dessert + cocktail) graded to match the hero's dark, moody, consistent look so the Interactive Food Menu feels cohesive. 3. One full-bleed 9:16 MOBILE crop of the hero, subject-aware, for the mobile Home section. 4. The flat wordmark logo cleaned to a transparent PNG and vectorized to crisp SVG for retina/responsive use on the dark site. The whole set must read as one premium, dark, cohesive system. Match grade across all food images. Keep skin/food tones appetizing — dark mode, not muddy.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain food-bevDeliverable hero-image-packWorkflow EditOperation O1Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 22Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand generic
4Planning3Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density3Creative freedom

These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

restaurant_wordmark_logo.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA minimalist flat restaurant wordmark logo, the words rendered in an elegant thin serif typeface, solid near-black charc

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_dark_desktop.pngimageFull-bleed desktop website hero, ~2880x1620 (16:9), straightened, dark-mode graded, subject popped off a moody dark surround, reframed by cropping the native 3:2 to 16:9 with the plate offset right so the existing dark surround forms clean headline negative space on the left (edges cropped to hit the ratio), sRGB PNG.
menu_tile_dessert.pngimageSquare 1:1 menu-tile image (~1200x1200), graded to MATCH the hero's dark moody look, sRGB PNG.
menu_tile_cocktail.pngimageSquare 1:1 menu-tile image (~1200x1200), graded to MATCH the hero's dark moody look, sRGB PNG.
hero_mobile_9x16.pngimageFull-bleed mobile Home crop, 1080x1920 (9:16), subject-aware crop of the graded hero, sRGB PNG.
logo_wordmark.svgvectorTransparent, cleanly vectorized wordmark logo as crisp SVG for retina/responsive use on the dark site (plus the transparent-PNG intermediate).

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 15 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All six required deliverables are present: hero_dark_desktop.png, menu_tile_dessert.png, menu_tile_cocktail.png, hero_mobile_9x16.png, logo_wordmark.svg, and a moodboard_deeplink URL.autoExactly these 6 named outputs exist (4 PNG images, 1 SVG vector, 1 Firefly Boards deep-link URL); any missing item fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The desktop hero (hero_dark_desktop.png) is a 16:9 full-bleed image at approximately 2880x1620.autoWidth:height ratio == 16:9 and dimensions are ~2880x1620 (within rounding); file is PNG.K1mandatoryoutput
Both menu tiles (menu_tile_dessert.png, menu_tile_cocktail.png) are square 1:1 images at approximately 1200x1200.autoEach tile has width == height (1:1) at ~1200x1200 px; both are PNG.K1mandatoryoutput
The mobile crop (hero_mobile_9x16.png) is a full-bleed 9:16 image at 1080x1920.autoDimensions == 1080x1920 (9:16 portrait); file is PNG.K1mandatoryoutput
All four exported raster deliverables are sRGB PNG files.autohero_dark_desktop.png, menu_tile_dessert.png, menu_tile_cocktail.png, and hero_mobile_9x16.png are all PNG format in sRGB color space.K1dealbreakeroutput
The logo deliverable is a valid vector SVG with a transparent background, accompanied by a transparent-PNG intermediate.autologo_wordmark.svg parses as SVG containing vector path data (not an embedded raster), has no opaque background fill, and a transparent-PNG intermediate of the wordmark also exists.K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo preserves the original wordmark's letterforms, the thin serif typeface, the inter-word leaf glyph, and the single near-black charcoal ink color without regenerating, restyling, or recoloring the mark.expertlogo_wordmark.svg visually matches restaurant_wordmark_logo.png: same two words/letterforms, elegant thin serif, small leaf glyph between the two words, solid near-black charcoal color; no redrawn, restyled, or recolored logo.K2dealbreakerinput
The logo background was removed by dropping the flat white field to transparency (not by masking or erasing the wordmark itself), with no halo or fringe.expertThe transparent-PNG intermediate shows the charcoal wordmark and leaf glyph fully intact on full transparency, with the white background cleanly removed and no white halo, fringe, or matte ring around the letters or glyph.K2qualitybrief
The desktop hero horizon is level — the ~4-degree raw tilt has been corrected.expertThe plate/table horizon in hero_dark_desktop.png reads level with no residual tilt; the original ~4deg slant is removed.K3mandatorybrief
The desktop hero canvas is extended on the LEFT with clean dark negative space for an overlaid headline.expertThe left side of hero_dark_desktop.png shows added, clean, dark empty space (outpainted), leaving room for a headline; the scallop plate subject sits to the right of that negative space.K3mandatorybrief
The hero is rendered in a moody dark-mode look with the scallop plate subject popped off a dark surround.expertBackground/surround in hero_dark_desktop.png is dark and moody; the scallop plate subject is visibly brighter/more saturated and stands out from the dark background (selective subject pop), not muddy or flat.K4mandatorybrief
The mobile 9:16 crop keeps the scallop plate subject framed (subject-aware) rather than clipping it, and is taken from the graded hero.expertThe 1080x1920 hero_mobile_9x16.png is derived from the graded desktop hero with the scallop plate subject substantially in-frame and not awkwardly clipped.K3mandatorybrief
Both menu tiles are grade-matched to the hero's dark, moody look so the food set reads as one cohesive system.expertmenu_tile_dessert.png and menu_tile_cocktail.png share the hero's dark moody base, contrast, and warm-cool color balance; the originally cooler/brighter dessert and neutral-cool cocktail no longer look mismatched against the hero.K4mandatorybrief
Across all three food/drink images, the food tones remain appetizing and are not crushed into muddy darkness.expertScallops, chocolate dessert, and cocktail retain natural, appetizing color and visible detail in the shadows; the dark-mode grade has not flattened them into muddy/blocked tones.K4qualitybrief
The Firefly Boards moodboard deep-link assembles the final hero, both menu tiles, and the logo as the dark-mode visual-direction reference.expertThe moodboard_deeplink opens a Firefly board containing the finished hero_dark_desktop, menu_tile_dessert, menu_tile_cocktail, and the logo (4 final assets) presented together as one cohesive dark-mode reference.K6mandatoryoutput

AO-18   Premium Dark-Mode Restaurant Food-Photography Asset Suite (Hero, Menu Tiles, Logo Vector)

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 25)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K5 · K6 · K7

From the client's raw food photos and logo, produce a dark-mode-ready food-photography asset suite — a color-graded wide hero banner, clean-backdrop menu tiles, a mobile crop, and a vector logo — for a premium restaurant website.

Full brief

We are a boutique digital agency building a premium, dark-mode restaurant website (Home, Interactive Food Menu, Contact/Booking) that focuses heavily on high-quality food photography and must be perfectly optimized for mobile. We are NOT asking you to build the site here — we have a Framer developer for that. What we need from YOU is the polished food-photography asset suite the design hangs on, all prepared from our raw shoot so it drops straight into the dark aesthetic. Deliverables, all derived from the assets we supply: 1) HERO BANNER — take our raw hero shot (a signature octopus dish, slightly crooked off the handheld shoot) and turn it into a wide, cinematic, dark-mode hero: straighten it, apply a cohesive warm-but-moody grade so it reads premium on a near-black background, then crop it to a full ultra-wide 21:9 banner ratio (a cinematic letterbox reframe of the existing wide shot) so our developer has clean headline space. Mobile-optimized vertical crop of the same hero, too. 2) MENU TILES — for two menu items (the octopus and a steak plate), isolate the plated dish onto a clean, uniform near-black backdrop (so all menu tiles match), color-match them to the hero grade, and deliver square tiles. 3) LOGO MARK — our logo is supplied as a flat raster; we need a crisp vector version (SVG) for retina/responsive use across the dark site. House look: warm highlights, deep controlled shadows, rich but not cartoonish saturation, consistent across every tile so the menu grid feels like one shoot. Keep everything cohesive — the whole point of the dark-mode design is that the photography carries it.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 25 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_banner_ultrawide.pngimage21:9 ultra-wide dark-mode hero banner, straightened + warm-moody graded, then cinematic letterbox reframe cropped from the existing wide 3:2 source (no invented/extended pixels), ~3440x1440 PNG, ready for headline overlay by the dev
hero_mobile_vertical.jpgimageMobile hero crop of the same graded hero, 4:5 portrait ~1080x1350 JPEG, subject-safe framing
menu_tile_octopus.jpgimageSquare menu tile, plated octopus isolated on uniform near-black (#0B0B0D) backdrop, grade-matched, ~1200x1200 JPEG
menu_tile_steak.jpgimageSquare menu tile, plated steak isolated on the SAME near-black (#0B0B0D) backdrop, color-matched to hero grade, ~1200x1200 JPEG
logo_mark.svgvectorClean 2-color vector logo mark (monoline octopus + wordmark), scalable SVG for retina/responsive use

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 17 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
hero_banner_ultrawide.png is delivered as a valid PNG fileautoA file named hero_banner_ultrawide.png exists and has a valid PNG file signature (89 50 4E 47)K1dealbreakeroutput

menu_tile_octopus.jpg is delivered as a square JPEG at the specified sizeautoA file named menu_tile_octopus.jpg exists, is a valid JPEG, and is ~1200x1200 (1:1 square, within +/-2% tolerance on each dimension)K1mandatoryoutput
menu_tile_steak.jpg is delivered as a square JPEG at the specified sizeautoA file named menu_tile_steak.jpg exists, is a valid JPEG, and is ~1200x1200 (1:1 square, within +/-2% tolerance on each dimension)K1mandatoryoutput
Both menu tiles place the dish on the SAME uniform near-black backdrop color #0B0B0DautoThe background region of both menu_tile_octopus.jpg and menu_tile_steak.jpg samples to a uniform solid near-black of #0B0B0D (within small JPEG tolerance) and the two backdrop colors are identical between the tilesK1mandatoryoutput
logo_mark.svg is delivered as a true scalable vector SVG, not a raster embedded in an SVG wrapperautoA file named logo_mark.svg exists, parses as valid SVG, and contains vector path/shape elements (path/polygon/circle/etc.) rather than a single embedded base64 raster <image>K1dealbreakeroutput
The full deliverable set is complete with all five named assets presentautoAll five named outputs are present: hero_banner_ultrawide.png, hero_mobile_vertical.jpg, menu_tile_octopus.jpg, menu_tile_steak.jpg, logo_mark.svgK1mandatoryoutput
restaurant_photo_suite.zip bundles all five deliverables in a /hero, /menu, /logo folder structureautoA zip named restaurant_photo_suite.zip exists containing exactly the five deliverables organized as /hero (hero_banner_ultrawide.png + hero_mobile_vertical.jpg), /menu (menu_tile_octopus.jpg + menu_tile_steak.jpg), and /logo (logo_mark.svg)K1mandatoryoutput
The hero banner canvas was extended/outpainted beyond the original 3:2 frame to reach the 21:9 ultra-wide ratioexpertThe 21:9 banner shows believable generative-expand bleed room on the sides — the octopus subject is framed within the original content and the extended area is seamless and plausible, not stretched, mirrored, or letterboxedK3mandatorybrief
The hero horizon was straightened from the ~4-degree handheld tilt in hero_octopus_raw.jpgexpertThe plate/table horizon in the hero banner reads level, with no residual ~4-degree tilt from the raw handheld shootK3mandatorybrief
The dish in each menu tile is cleanly isolated from its original backgroundexpertThe plated octopus and steak are masked with clean edges against the #0B0B0D fill, with no original wood/marble/slate background remnants and no ragged or haloed edgesK3mandatorybrief
The mobile hero crop carries the same grade as the ultra-wide banner and uses subject-safe framingexperthero_mobile_vertical.jpg shows the same warm-moody grade as hero_banner_ultrawide.png and the octopus subject is fully framed (not cropped through the dish)K3mandatoryoutput
The hero carries the warm-but-moody dark-mode house grade (warm highlights, deep controlled shadows, rich-but-not-cartoonish saturation)expertHero shows warm highlights, deep controlled shadows, and rich-but-not-cartoonish saturation, reading premium on a near-black backgroundK4mandatorybrief
The two menu tiles and the hero are color-matched so the menu grid reads as one cohesive shootexpertOctopus and steak tiles share the same warm house grade as the hero (the steak's original tungsten cast corrected), with consistent warmth, shadow depth, and saturation across both tiles and the heroK4mandatorybrief
logo_mark.svg reproduces the supplied 2-color monoline CASA POLVO logo faithfully (octopus-in-ring mark + serif wordmark)expertThe vector logo preserves the monoline octopus-in-circular-ring mark and the 'CASA POLVO' serif wordmark from restaurant_logo_flat.png without redrawing, altering, or omitting elementsK2dealbreakerinput
logo_mark.svg preserves the supplied warm-gold brand color #C9A24BautoThe fill/stroke color of the vector logo elements samples to warm-gold #C9A24B (within small tolerance), matching the supplied restaurant_logo_flat.pngK2mandatoryinput

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
