7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-20   Premium corporate-website photo retouch: grade, declutter, and export a consistent responsive image set

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take two client-shot raw corporate images (a plant-floor engineer-by-equipment hero and an executive portrait), professionally retouch and color-grade them to one consistent premium look, declutter the busy backgrounds the allowed way, then export a web-optimized responsive set (desktop/tablet/mobile).

Full brief

We are launching a new corporate website for our industrial / CNC-machining business and need a Photoshop expert to professionally retouch a set of images so they look premium, modern, and consistent. Attached are two client-shot RAW-ish JPEGs straight off the camera: (1) HERO — a process engineer standing beside a CNC machining centre on the plant floor, shot under mixed fluorescent + window light so it is slightly underexposed, cool/green-cast, and the background is cluttered with parts bins, cables and a fire extinguisher; (2) a paired executive half-body portrait shot in the same facility, warmer but flat and low-contrast.\n\nWe need you to: correct the lighting imbalance and exposure on the hero; pull the white balance neutral and add real contrast, color and sharpness so it reads premium; clean up the distracting background clutter behind the engineer (we don't want a fake studio look — keep it authentic, just calm it down) by selecting the subject, inverting to a background-only region and laying a neutral fill over the clutter, then give the whole frame a gentle lens-blur soft-focus so it feels like a shallow-depth premium shot (the subject stays clean because the clutter around it was filled, not erased with AI); then carry the EXACT same finished look onto the executive portrait so the two images are visually consistent as a set. Once the look is locked, prepare web-optimized, responsive exports of the hero at desktop (1920px wide), tablet (1024px) and mobile (640px) sizes, all centred on the subject and fast-loading. Deliver the graded hero, the matching portrait and the three responsive crops so our marketing lead can approve the style before we roll it across the rest of the site library. Keep everything natural and realistic — this is a real factory, not a render.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain automotiveDeliverable responsive-photo-setWorkflow EditOperation O4Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 20Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand generic
4Planning2Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density3Creative freedom

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_graded_master.pngimageFull-resolution retouched, color-graded and preset-locked hero master (this is the hero_look_locked URN that fans out to the responsive crops): exposure/WB corrected, contrast+color+sharpness enhanced, cluttered backdrop region calmed with a solid neutral fill (subject preserved by the inverted subject mask), a whole-image lens-blur soft-focus finishing pass, then the shared 'corporate premium' preset applied. ~6000x4000px PNG, the locked premium look.
portrait_graded_master.pngimageExecutive portrait carrying the IDENTICAL finished look (same preset) as the hero so the pair reads as one consistent set. ~6000x4000px PNG.
hero_desktop_1920.pngimageWeb-optimized desktop hero crop centred on the subject, 1920px wide (16:9), from the graded master.
hero_tablet_1024.pngimageResponsive tablet crop, 1024px wide, subject-centred, from the graded master.
hero_mobile_640.pngimageResponsive mobile crop, 640px wide, subject-centred, fast-loading, from the graded master.

Hero shows enhanced contrast, color and sharpness reading as a premium lookexperthero_graded_master has visibly added contrast, richer color and improved sharpness versus the flat raw input, reading premiumK4mandatorybrief
The cluttered hero background (parts bins, cables, fire extinguisher) is calmed via a solid neutral fill on the inverted background mask, NOT AI object removal, with the subject untouchedexpertDistracting background clutter behind the engineer is suppressed by a neutral solid fill over the background region while the engineer remains intact and unaltered; no generative/AI object-removal artifacts presentK3mandatorybrief
The hero background is thrown out of focus (lens blur) so the subject popsexperthero_graded_master shows the calmed background softened/out of focus relative to the subjectK3qualitybrief
The executive portrait carries the identical finished look (same shared preset) as the hero so the pair reads as one consistent setexpertportrait_graded_master and hero_graded_master share the same tone/color/contrast grade so the originally warm-flat portrait and cool-underexposed hero now read as one visually consistent setK4mandatorybrief
The retouch stays natural and realistic — authentic factory subject, no fake studio/CGI lookexpertBoth graded images retain authentic skin texture, natural hands and a believable real-factory environment with no glossy/CGI/over-retouched appearanceK4mandatorybrief

AO-21   Creative Business Headshot — Studio Retouch, Color Grade & Brand-Profile Asset Pipeline

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take the client's raw studio headshot frame and brand monogram and produce a retouched, color-graded, creatively recomposed professional-profile headshot set (color + monochrome + 16:9 dynamic + LinkedIn 1:1 + banner crops) plus a clean vector logomark.

Full brief

I'm a founder who needs a creative, dynamic headshot for my professional business profile (LinkedIn + website "About"). My photographer shot me in-studio against a seamless mid-gray backdrop but only gave me the flat, un-retouched RAW-export JPEG plus my simple wordmark monogram as a PNG — I need the actual finished, polished delivery set. Please, working only from the files I provide: 1. Straighten and clean up the frame, then apply a professional studio tone so the exposure and contrast look intentional, not flat. 2. Warm the skin tone slightly (studio strobes left it a touch cool) and give the whole image a cohesive graded look — I want it to read "premium business portrait," not phone snapshot. 3. Do tasteful, believable retouching: brighten my eyes and teeth a little using selective adjustments (no plastic skin, no face reshaping). 4. Make it feel "dynamic": separate me from the background with a soft blur behind the subject, and reframe the shot into a wider 16:9 environmental crop (using the empty backdrop room on one side) so it works as a hero image with breathing room for text. 5. Give me a tasteful monochrome alternate of the final color portrait for places that want B&W. 6. Produce the profile crops I'll actually upload: a 1:1 LinkedIn/avatar crop and a 1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop, both off the finished color portrait. 7. Vectorize my flat monogram PNG into a clean SVG logomark so it stays crisp on print and large web. Deliverable: a print/web-ready professional headshot package for my business profile — color hero, mono alt, 1:1 avatar, banner, and vector logo.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain professional-servicesDeliverable headshotWorkflow EditOperation O4Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 20Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand generic
5Planning2Brand strictness5Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpgimageFinal retouched + color-graded creative headshot, cropped/reframed (no pixels invented) to a 16:9 environmental hero (e.g. 2560x1440) by trimming the tall studio frame down to landscape while preserving the subject and headroom, warm studio skin tone, brightened eyes/teeth, soft background separation blur. JPEG, sRGB.
headshot_hero_mono.jpgimageMonochrome (monochromatic-tint) alternate of the final color hero, same 16:9 framing. JPEG, sRGB.
headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpgimage1:1 avatar/profile crop (e.g. 1000x1000) derived from the finished color portrait, subject-centered. JPEG, sRGB.
headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpgimage1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop derived from the finished color portrait/hero, subject offset with text room. JPEG, sRGB.
monogram_logomark.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the 'AV' ring monogram, single-color paths, scalable for print + large web.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The color hero deliverable headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg is present and exactly 16:9 aspect ratioautoA file named headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg exists and its pixel width:height ratio equals 16:9 (1.7778 within rounding, e.g. 2560x1440)K1mandatoryoutput
The monochrome alternate headshot_hero_mono.jpg is present at the same 16:9 framing as the color heroautoA file named headshot_hero_mono.jpg exists and is 16:9 aspect ratio (1.7778 within rounding) matching the color hero dimensionsK1mandatoryoutput
The LinkedIn avatar crop headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg is present at exactly 1:1 aspect ratioautoA file named headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg exists with equal pixel width and height (1:1, e.g. 1000x1000)K1mandatoryoutput
The LinkedIn banner crop headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg is present at exactly 1584x396 pixelsautoA file named headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg exists with pixel dimensions exactly 1584x396 (the verbatim LinkedIn banner size in the brief)K1dealbreakeroutput
All four raster image deliverables are JPEG format in the sRGB color spaceautoheadshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg, headshot_hero_mono.jpg, headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg, and headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg are each encoded as JPEG and in the sRGB color space (no CMYK or wide-gamut/non-sRGB ICC profile)K1dealbreakeroutput
The vector logomark monogram_logomark.svg is present and is a valid scalable SVG containing vector paths (not an embedded raster)autoA file named monogram_logomark.svg exists, parses as valid SVG, and contains <path> vector geometry with no embedded bitmap (no <image> element or base64 raster data)K1dealbreakeroutput
The vectorized logomark renders the 'AV' two-letter monogram inside a thin circular ring as a single ink colorexpertRendered SVG visibly shows the serif letters 'AV' within a thin circular ring, traced as clean single-color paths faithful to the supplied monogram_flat.png (solid charcoal #2B2B2B, no gradients/texture)K2dealbreakerinput
All five deliverables are placed inside a single CC asset folder named Headshot_Delivery/autoA folder named Headshot_Delivery/ exists and contains exactly the five deliverables: headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg, headshot_hero_mono.jpg, headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg, headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg, and monogram_logomark.svgK1mandatoryoutput
An inline preview of the populated delivery set is produced for client reviewautoAn inline preview of the populated Headshot_Delivery/ folder is rendered and returned to the userK6mandatoryoutput
The hero frame has been straightened from the raw ~1.5-degree tilt and shows a level horizon/verticalsexpertThe subject and seamless backdrop in the color hero appear level (no residual ~1.5-degree tilt) compared to the supplied tilted studio_headshot_raw.jpgK3qualitybrief
The color hero is tonally graded with intentional exposure/contrast and warmer skin tone than the flat, cool raw inputexpertCompared to studio_headshot_raw.jpg, the color hero shows added contrast/exposure grade and a warmer (less cool/blue) skin tone, reading as a premium business portrait rather than a flat phone snapshotK4mandatorybrief
Eyes and teeth are selectively brightened while skin texture is preserved and the face is not reshapedexpertEyes and teeth read brighter than the raw input, with natural skin pores/texture retained (no plastic-smooth skin) and no face/feature reshapingK7dealbreakerbrief
The background is softly blurred to separate the subject while the subject stays sharpexpertIn the color hero the backdrop shows a soft separation blur with clean subject edges while the subject (face/shoulders) remains in focusK3mandatorybrief
The 16:9 hero was extended wider via outpaint, adding seamless backdrop room for textexpertThe color hero shows a widened canvas with extended seamless backdrop (consistent outpainted continuation of the mid-gray studio backdrop) giving negative space for text, beyond the original portrait framingK3mandatorybrief
The monochrome alternate is a true B&W/monochromatic-tint version of the same finished color heroexpertheadshot_hero_mono.jpg shows the identical composition to the color hero rendered in monochrome/monochromatic tint with no residual color castK4mandatoryoutput
The 1:1 avatar and 1584x396 banner crops are derived from the finished color portrait, with the subject centered in the 1:1 and offset for text room in the bannerexpertheadshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg shows the subject centered and headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg shows the subject offset leaving clear text space, both clearly cropped from the same finished color heroK3qualityoutput

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-23   Teaser, Thumbnail and End-Card Content Package for a Luxury Manhattan Real-Estate YouTube Channel

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Produce the full per-video content package for one luxury Manhattan real-estate YouTube video — a retouched high-contrast thumbnail base plate, a cinematic cold-open teaser cut (YouTube + LinkedIn sizes), and a branded end card — all from the broker's supplied portrait, penthouse still, raw walkthrough footage, end-card backplate, and wordmark logo.

Full brief

A top-producing Manhattan luxury real-estate broker runs a YouTube channel (market insights, property walkthroughs, deal-level expertise) at 3-4 videos/month and needs the full content package built around each video, with luxury-brand restraint and a 48-72h turnaround. For THIS video — a 12-minute Tribeca penthouse walkthrough that opens in the great room — deliver three things. THUMBNAIL: a high-contrast, click-worthy, on-brand thumbnail base plate at 1280x720, NOT a generic headshot with a text box. Start from the broker's supplied studio portrait and the penthouse hero still. Straighten and auto-tone the portrait, isolate the broker from the background so the figure reads cleanly, and grade the penthouse hero into a moody high-contrast luxury look (deepened shadows, controlled highlights, warm prestige temperature, punchy local contrast). Crop/resize the hero to the 16:9 thumbnail frame and, since the wide-angle source still has room to spare, crop the graded hero to the exact 1280x720 thumbnail bounds so it fills the full frame without stretching. Also vectorize the broker's wordmark logo to a clean SVG for crisp placement at any size. The on-thumbnail HEADLINE TEXT is the single step the image connector cannot do (it cannot set type), so it is finished in one local packaging step over the delivered base plate. TEASER: from the raw 1080p walkthrough footage, produce an AI highlight cut that finds the single most compelling 60-90 seconds for a cinematic cold-open — a strong visual hook in the first 5 seconds that hard-cuts before the reveal; then reframe that cut to a 1080x1080 square for LinkedIn cross-posting (same length, no trim). Deliver the YouTube-native teaser and the LinkedIn square, with prestige tone and no cheap transitions. END CARD: from the client's pre-shot branded end-card backplate footage (10-15s), reframe it to the exact 1920x1080 YouTube delivery dimensions so the subscribe prompt, suggested-video slot and CTA sit correctly and stay consistent across videos. All image work must be done on the connector (straighten, tone, mask, grade, crop, vectorize); all video work via the async media tools (quick-cut, resize). Only the final headline typesetting on the thumbnail base plate is local.

See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

penthouse_hero.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal interior real-estate hero shot of a Tribeca penthouse great room at blue hour: floor-to-ceiling windows with M

endcard_backplate_1080p.mp4videoveo-3.1-generate-preview12-second branded outro backplate for a luxury real-estate channel: slow drifting aerial of the Manhattan skyline at dus

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
thumbnail_base_1280x720.pngimage1280x720 (16:9) high-contrast luxury thumbnail BASE plate: straightened + auto-toned broker figure isolated from background, moody graded penthouse hero, cropped to the exact 1280x720 bounds from the wide-angle source (no pixels invented), no stretching. PNG, sRGB. Headline text added in the single local packaging step.
broker_portrait_isolated.pngimageTransparent-background PNG of the retouched broker (auto-straightened, auto-toned, subject-isolated) for flexible placement on the thumbnail and future videos.
logo_wordmark.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the 'STERLING ROW' wordmark, scalable for thumbnail and end-card placement.
teaser_youtube_1920x1080.mp4video30-90s cinematic cold-open teaser, strong visual hook in first 5s, hard-cut before resolution, 1920x1080 YouTube-native, prestige tone, no transitions.
teaser_linkedin_1080x1080.mp4videoSame teaser reframed to 1080x1080 square for LinkedIn cross-posting, same length (no trim).
endcard_1920x1080.mp4video10-15s branded end card reframed to exactly 1920x1080 with clear space for subscribe prompt, suggested-video card and CTA, consistent across videos.

AO-24   Open House Finance Flyer: property-photo retouch, brass/gold brand look, logo vectorize, and InDesign price-scenario data-merge for a Solano County mortgage lender

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 23)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Prep and grade the hero property photo and lender headshot into a black + brass/gold luxury look, vectorize the lender wordmark, then export the authored InDesign Open House Finance flyer (front/back) and data-merge the monthly-payment / down-payment / appreciation scenarios from a CSV into print-ready pages.

Full brief

I'm a mortgage lender working Solano County and the greater Bay Area, positioning myself as the local financing expert. I need the Adobe production pieces that go into my "Open House Finance Flyer" marketing package (front and back, print + digital). Style must read sophisticated, luxury-but-approachable, clean and modern, in black/white with brass/gold accents that appeal to Bay Area homebuyers. Assets I'm giving you: a hi-res exterior listing photo of the open-house property (shot slightly tilted, mixed afternoon light), my professional headshot, my brand wordmark as a flat PNG, my CLIENT-AUTHORED InDesign flyer template (front/back, with image frames + a tagged payment-scenario table carrying real <<merge fields>>), and a CSV of monthly-payment / down-payment / appreciation scenarios for several price points. What I need you to do: 1. Straighten and clean up the property exterior, fix the tilt and the mixed-light exposure, and crop it to the hero frame. 2. Give the property shot a consistent luxury grade — warm brass/gold-leaning, rich blacks — and make a banner-ratio version by cropping and resizing the graded hero to the wide hero-band ratio so it fills the wide hero band on the flyer without cropping the house. 3. Isolate the house from its background so I have a clean cut-out version for the digital/social variant. 4. Retouch and color-match my headshot to the same luxury look so it sits next to the property shot. 5. Vectorize my wordmark to a clean SVG so it stays crisp at any print size and on the QR-code/contact strip. 6. Export the finished front/back flyer from my authored InDesign template, and run the InDesign data-merge so each price scenario in my CSV produces its own print-ready page (purchase price, down-payment options, monthly payment, appreciation projection). Deliver the graded hero photo (print + banner), the cut-out, the matched headshot, the SVG wordmark, the exported flyer PDF, and the merged multi-page scenario PDF.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 23 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_property_print.jpgimageStraightened, exposure-corrected, luxury-graded (warm brass/gold cast, rich blacks) hero exterior at the flyer's print hero frame ratio, 300 DPI, sRGB JPEG
hero_property_banner.jpgimageWide banner-ratio version of the graded hero, produced by cropping and resizing (reframing) the graded print hero into the flyer's full-width hero-band ratio — a horizontal slice through house/sky/foreground so the house is not distorted or dropped; no invented pixels, crop-and-resize only (never larger than the source in the widened dimension)
property_cutout.pngimageHouse isolated from background, transparent PNG, for the digital/social flyer variant
headshot_matched.jpgimageRetouched lender headshot color-matched to the same warm luxury grade, sRGB JPEG, sized for the flyer headshot frame
wordmark.svgvectorClean vectorized single-color wordmark, scalable SVG for print + contact strip
open_house_finance_flyer.pdfpdfFront/back Open House Finance Flyer exported from the authored .indd, US-Letter, print-ready PDF
payment_scenarios_merged.pdfpdfMulti-page print-ready PDF from the InDesign data-merge — one page per CSV scenario row (purchase price, down-payment options, monthly payment, appreciation projection, address)

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 17 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
hero_property_print.jpg is delivered as an sRGB JPEG at 300 DPIautoA file named hero_property_print.jpg exists, is a valid JPEG with an sRGB color profile, and its DPI metadata reads 300K1mandatoryoutput
The hero property photo is straightened and tilt-corrected relative to the ~4-degree tilted raw inputexpertThe house/horizon in hero_property_print.jpg is visually level (no residual ~4-degree tilt versus property_exterior_raw.jpg) and the empty wedges left by straightening are cropped outK1mandatorybrief
The hero property photo carries the black + brass/gold luxury grade (warm cast, rich blacks) and the mixed-light exposure is correctedexperthero_property_print.jpg shows a warm brass/gold-leaning color grade with rich/deep blacks, blown sky highlights recovered and muddy shadows lifted, consistent with the brand's sophisticated/luxury black-white-and-brass/gold lookK4qualitybrief
hero_property_banner.jpg is delivered as a JPEG with a wider aspect ratio than hero_property_print.jpgautoA file named hero_property_banner.jpg exists, is a valid JPEG, and its width-to-height aspect ratio is wider than hero_property_print.jpg'sK1mandatoryoutput
The hero_property_banner is the graded hero with the canvas generatively extended (outpainted) and the house not croppedexperthero_property_banner.jpg is the same graded hero scene with the added sky/foreground generatively outpainted (not stretched, cloned, or tiled), and the full house is intact and uncroppedK1mandatoryoutput
property_cutout.png is the house isolated from its background as a transparent PNGautoA file named property_cutout.png exists, is a PNG with an alpha channel, and contains transparent (non-opaque) background pixelsK1mandatoryoutput
The property_cutout.png background isolation has clean edges around the houseexpertThe house is cleanly separated from its background in property_cutout.png with no background halo, fringing, or chunks of the house removedK3qualityoutput
headshot_matched.jpg is delivered as a valid sRGB JPEGautoA file named headshot_matched.jpg exists and is a valid JPEG with an sRGB color profileK1mandatoryoutput
The matched headshot's color/tone visually matches the hero property's warm luxury grade so they sit togetherexpertheadshot_matched.jpg shows the same warm brass/gold-leaning grade as hero_property_print.jpg (no residual cool/flat studio cast) while keeping natural skin texture (not airbrushed)K4qualitybrief
wordmark.svg is a clean vectorized single-color SVG of the lender wordmarkautoA file named wordmark.svg exists and is a valid SVG containing vector path geometry (not just an embedded raster image)K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized wordmark preserves the exact brand text 'HARLAN HOME LENDING' and the house-key monogram markexpertwordmark.svg renders the wordmark text 'HARLAN HOME LENDING' (correct spelling, high-contrast serif) with the small house-key monogram to the left, crisp at scale, traced from lender_wordmark.png rather than regenerated or alteredK2dealbreakerinput
open_house_finance_flyer.pdf is a front/back US-Letter print-ready PDF exported from the authored .indd templateautoA file named open_house_finance_flyer.pdf exists, is a valid PDF with exactly 2 pages (front/back), and each page is US-Letter sized (8.5 x 11 in)K1mandatoryoutput
payment_scenarios_merged.pdf is the InDesign data-merge output with one page per CSV scenario rowautoA file named payment_scenarios_merged.pdf exists, is a valid PDF, and contains exactly 6 pages (one per the 6 data rows in payment_scenarios.csv)K1mandatoryoutput
Each merged page binds the CSV row values into the merge fields with no unresolved placeholders remainingautoEvery page of payment_scenarios_merged.pdf shows resolved values for PurchasePrice, DownPaymentOptions, MonthlyPayment, AppreciationProjection and PropertyAddress, with no literal <<PurchasePrice>>/<<DownPaymentOptions>>/<<MonthlyPayment>>/<<AppreciationProjection>>/<<PropertyAddress>> placeholder text remaining on any pageK1mandatorybrief
The merged scenario values match the source payment_scenarios.csv exactlyautoFor each of the 6 pages, the PurchasePrice, DownPaymentOptions, MonthlyPayment, AppreciationProjection and PropertyAddress shown equal the corresponding row values in payment_scenarios.csvK1mandatoryinput
A Firefly approval board deep-link is delivered collecting the five final approved visual assetsautoAn approval_board Firefly board deep-link is returned and references all five final visual assets: the graded hero (hero_property_print), banner (hero_property_banner), cut-out (property_cutout), matched headshot (headshot_matched) and SVG wordmark (wordmark.svg)K6mandatoryoutput
The client's supplied assets (property photo, headshot, wordmark) are the same source assets used in the deliverables, not regenerated or substitutedexperthero_property_print.jpg depicts the same house as property_exterior_raw.jpg, headshot_matched.jpg is the same person as lender_headshot_raw.jpg, and wordmark.svg traces the supplied lender_wordmark.png — none are AI-regenerated or replaced with different assetsK7dealbreakerinput

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-25   Premium, consistent HDR grade for a Caribbean vacation-rental villa listing set (interior + pool + sea/sky), delivered as matched web-ready JPEGs plus a 16:9 listing hero

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 22)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take a property's three raw Nodalview-HDR exports (luxury interior, pool deck, sea/sky terrace) and finish them into a consistent, natural, premium listing set — level horizons, balance exposure/white-balance, gently enhance pool water, sea color, sky and vegetation, brighten interiors and balance the bright window view, then deliver matched 2048px web JPEGs plus a 16:9 listing hero.

Full brief

We manage ~100 luxury vacation rentals in Saint-Martin and need a consistent, premium visual identity across the whole portfolio. Photos come out of a Nodalview HDR workflow already roughly exposed; your job is to enhance and FINALIZE them while keeping a natural, realistic look — never a fake-HDR, over-saturated, or unrealistically-blue-pool result. For this batch, finish one representative villa's three hero frames: (1) a luxury living-room interior shot through to a bright sea-view window, (2) a pool deck with turquoise water, and (3) a wide terrace looking out to sea and sky. Required, in order: straighten any tilted horizon; apply a clean auto-tone baseline; do fine exposure and white-balance correction so the three frames read as one coherent set (no green/magenta casts, neutral whites indoors); lift interior shadows for brightness and clarity WITHOUT crushing the bright window view (balance the indoor/outdoor exposure); then do RESTRAINED, targeted enhancement of the exterior color story — pool water and sea color enhanced via selective hue/saturation of the cyans/blues only (not a global saturation pump), sky improved gently, vegetation greens enriched naturally. Keep overall vibrance gentle. Export all three at a matched 2048px long edge, sRGB JPEG q90, plus one 16:9 hero crop of the best exterior for the listing header. This representative set defines the look we will approve before rolling it across the portfolio. Out of connector scope and to be flagged for the human finisher: converging-vertical / perspective warp and any cable/reflection/stain cloning — the automated pass does everything else.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 22 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
interior_seaview_final.jpgimageFinished luxury interior, straightened + auto-tone + exposure/WB-corrected + shadow-lifted with the bright window view held, neutral whites, no green cast. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quality 90.
pool_deck_final.jpgimageFinished pool deck with naturally enhanced turquoise water (selective cyan/blue saturation, not global), enriched-but-natural vegetation greens, gently improved sky. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quality 90.
terrace_seaview_final.jpgimageFinished sea/sky terrace, leveled horizon, natural contrast, gently enhanced sea and sky color. sRGB JPEG, 2048px long edge, quality 90.
hero_16x9.jpgimage16:9 listing-header hero cropped from the finished terrace frame. sRGB JPEG, 1920x1080.

Vegetation greens are enriched naturally and the sky is gently improved in the pool frameexpertpool_deck_final.jpg shows richer-but-natural palm/foliage greens and a gently improved (de-hazed) skyK4qualitybrief

The three frames read as one coherent, consistent setexpertinterior_seaview_final.jpg, pool_deck_final.jpg and terrace_seaview_final.jpg share consistent exposure, white-balance and matched cyan/blue treatment so they read as one propertyK3mandatorybrief
The terrace frame has added natural contrast and gently enhanced sea/sky color consistent with the pool treatmentexpertterrace_seaview_final.jpg shows natural contrast (no longer flat) and the muted teal sea/sky is enhanced with the same restrained cyan/blue treatment as the pool frameK4qualitybrief
Out-of-scope items (converging-vertical/perspective warp and cable/reflection/stain cloning) are flagged for the human finisher rather than silently droppedexpertThe deliverable communication explicitly states that converging-vertical/perspective correction and cable/reflection/stain removal are out of automated connector scope and left for the human finisherK7mandatorybrief

AO-26   High-Volume Real Estate Listing Retouch — HDR Tone-Map, Window Pull, Lawn Boost & Consistent-Style Delivery

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Edit one real-estate listing's frames into MLS-ready, consistently styled images: tone-map the wide-DR exterior (window pull), enhance the sky, pop the lawn, straighten and crop, then lock that look as a preset and push it across the interior frames for a uniform property set.

Full brief

You're the agency's go-to editor. We shot a listing and are sending you the frames; you handle the editing and return MLS-ready images with one consistent style across the whole property. For this batch, work the three representative frames we've attached (exterior hero + two interiors) end-to-end, then hand back a uniform set. EXTERIOR HERO (wide dynamic range, blown-out windows and bright sky over a shaded facade and front lawn): 1. Auto-straighten the frame — the verticals are leaning and the horizon is off. 2. Crop to clean bounds after straightening to remove the skewed edges (this is our lens-distortion/alignment cleanup). 3. Recover the overall tone so the shaded facade reads properly — apply auto-tone as the base. 4. WINDOW PULL: select the bright windows and pull their highlights down so the glass shows interior/structure instead of blowing out; also tame the overall highlights. 5. SKY: select the sky and enhance it — recover the blown highlights and deepen the blue (we are NOT swapping the sky, just making the existing one read well and MLS-safe). 6. LAWN ENHANCEMENT: select the front lawn/grass and lift its green saturation and luminance so it looks healthy and inviting. 7. Warm the white balance very slightly and add a touch of vibrance so the whole exterior feels bright and welcoming. STYLE LOCK + CONSISTENCY: 8. Pull the current Lightroom preset library so we can pick the house style. 9. Apply our chosen consistent "bright & airy real-estate" preset to the finished exterior — this is the look every frame in the property must match. 10-11. Apply that SAME preset to interior frame 1 and interior frame 2, then bright-window pull / highlight-tame each interior so the windows aren't blown and the rooms feel open — giving us one uniform style across the property. DELIVERY: return each finished frame as a high-quality PNG, style-matched so the three read as one consistent property look, before you do the rest of the 20-40 on this listing. Turnaround 24-48h, bulk per-listing.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 20 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
exterior_hero_MLS.pngimageFinished exterior hero: straightened, cropped, auto-toned, window-pulled (windows + global highlights recovered), sky enhanced (masked highlight recovery + blue boost), lawn green lifted (masked), warmed WB + vibrance, then the locked bright-and-airy preset applied. High-quality PNG, MLS-ready.
interior_living_room_MLS.pngimageLiving-room interior with the SAME locked preset applied + window/highlight tame. High-quality PNG, style-matched to the exterior hero.
interior_kitchen_MLS.pngimageKitchen interior with the SAME locked preset applied + window/highlight tame. High-quality PNG, style-matched to the set.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Three finished image deliverables are returned, one per frameautoExactly three finished output image files exist, named/derived as exterior_hero_MLS.png, interior_living_room_MLS.png, and interior_kitchen_MLS.png (one per attached frame)K1mandatoryoutput
All three finished frames are delivered as PNG filesautoEach of the three deliverables (exterior_hero_MLS, interior_living_room_MLS, interior_kitchen_MLS) is a valid PNG (PNG file signature), not JPEG/WebP/other — the brief requires 'high-quality PNG'K1dealbreakeroutput
A consistency preview of all three finished frames is shown for sign-offautoAn asset_preview_file step (output named listing_consistency_previews) is produced that displays all three finished frames (exterior_hero_MLS, interior_living_room_MLS, interior_kitchen_MLS) together so the agency can confirm one consistent style before the rest of the 20-40K1mandatoryoutput
The exterior hero is auto-straightened then cropped to clean bounds before tonal editsautoThe exterior pipeline runs image_auto_straighten (step 2) then image_crop_to_bounds (step 3), in that order, before image_apply_auto_tone — and in exterior_hero_MLS.png the leaning verticals/tilted horizon of exterior_hero_wideDR.png are corrected and the skewed edges removedK6mandatorybrief
Base tonal recovery (auto-tone) is applied to the exterior so the shaded facade readsautoimage_apply_auto_tone (step 4) is applied to the cropped exterior frame as the tonal base before the masked window/sky/lawn editsK6mandatorybrief
Window pull: the bright front windows are masked and their highlights pulled downexpertIn exterior_hero_MLS.png the previously blown-out front windows show recovered detail/structure rather than pure white, produced via image_select_by_prompt (windows) -> image_adjust_highlights with that maskK3mandatorybrief
Sky is ENHANCED, not replacedexpertThe original sky from exterior_hero_wideDR.png is retained in exterior_hero_MLS.png with recovered highlights and a deeper blue (via image_select_by_prompt sky -> masked image_adjust_single_color_saturation); no new/foreign sky is composited or swapped inK7dealbreakerbrief
Lawn enhancement: the front lawn/grass is masked and its green liftedexpertIn exterior_hero_MLS.png the front lawn shows increased green saturation and luminance (healthier/greener) vs the source, applied within a lawn mask via image_select_by_prompt -> image_adjust_hsl, without recoloring non-lawn areasK3mandatorybrief
Exterior white balance is warmed slightly and a touch of vibrance addedexpertexterior_hero_MLS.png shows a slightly warmer white balance and modestly increased vibrance vs the auto-toned exterior (via image_adjust_color_temperature + image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation), reading bright and welcoming without over-saturationK4qualitybrief
The Lightroom preset library is pulled before any preset is appliedautoimage_list_presets (step 13) is called to retrieve the preset catalog prior to any image_apply_preset callK6mandatorybrief
The SAME locked bright-and-airy preset is applied to all three framesautoimage_apply_preset is invoked on the exterior hero (step 14), the living-room interior (step 16), and the kitchen interior (step 19) using one identical preset identifier selected from the catalog (same preset across all three apply calls)K1mandatorybrief
Both interior frames receive a window/highlight tame after the presetexpertIn interior_living_room_MLS.png and interior_kitchen_MLS.png the bright/near-blown windows are tamed (highlights recovered via image_adjust_highlights applied after the preset) so the windows are not blown and the rooms read openK3mandatorybrief
The three finished frames share one consistent bright-and-airy property styleexpertexterior_hero_MLS.png, interior_living_room_MLS.png and interior_kitchen_MLS.png look like one cohesive set (consistent tone, brightness and color cast from the shared locked preset) — a uniform bright-and-airy MLS-ready look across the propertyK4mandatorybrief
No object removal or other generative cleanup was performedexpertThe finished frames contain the same physical objects/scene as their sources; 'general cleanup' was delivered as tonal/color + straighten/crop only, with no AI object removal, no gen-fill, and no compositingK7dealbreakerbrief
Masked exterior edits stay within their target regionsexpertThe window, sky, and lawn adjustments on exterior_hero_MLS.png are confined to their respective masked regions with clean edges (no halo/spill of the highlight pull, blue boost, or green lift onto adjacent areas)K3qualitybrief
Honest capability substitutions are disclosed to the clientexpertThe delivery message flags that the sky was enhanced (not swapped/replaced), that the HDR look came from single-file tone-mapping (auto-tone + highlight/shadow recovery) rather than a true bracket MERGE, and that 'general cleanup' excluded object removal — matching the spec's stated MLS-safe substitutionsK7mandatorybrief

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)

K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-27   Streetline-style real-estate interior edit: straighten, HDR-look tone, white balance, window pull, outlet clean-up

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Photo & ImageOperation O4 Preset retouch & look-devHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Edit a Dutch real-estate interior to a bright, clean, realistic Streetline-style finish — straighten verticals, recover an HDR-look exposure, fix white balance, pull the blown-out window view back, neutralize one distracting wall outlet, and deliver a high-res JPG.

Full brief

Kimberley is launching a real-estate photography business in the Netherlands and needs a reliable editing partner for an initial batch of 25-30 listing photos shot for the Dutch/European market (Streetline-style expectations). Using ONE representative hero frame of a living room, run the standard per-image edit recipe end-to-end on the Adobe connector so it can be looped across the whole set with a consistent style: 1) Load the client's supplied hero frame into CC storage (the well-exposed hero interior). 2) Auto-straighten the hero to fix tilted verticals/horizontals (walls must read straight), then crop to clean bounds removing the rotation wedge. 3) Apply auto-tone, then recover exposure / lift the dark portions / tame the highlights to build the bright-but-realistic, HDR-style interior WITHOUT an oversaturated or unrealistic HDR look. 4) Neutralize the warm/cool color cast: correct color temperature for accurate white balance, then gently lift vibrance (no oversaturation) so colors stay natural. 5) Window pull: select the windows by prompt, pull their highlights and exposure down on that mask so the outdoor view (garden/street) reads through the glass instead of blowing out white. 6) Clean one distracting element where necessary: select the small white wall power outlet and solid-fill it to match the surrounding wall color (a legal solid-fill clean-up, not AI object removal). 7) Apply a final consistent, realistic look and retrieve the delivered high-resolution JPG master for client hand-off. Deliverable: one high-resolution, Streetline-ready JPG of the interior — straight verticals, neutral white balance, bright realistic interior, intact through-window exterior view, distractor cleaned — that defines the repeatable editing style for the remaining images in the batch.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
livingroom_streetline_final.jpgimageHigh-resolution JPG (3:2, full sensor resolution preserved, sRGB), Streetline-style finish: straightened verticals, cropped clean (no rotation wedge), bright-but-realistic interior with recovered highlights/shadows, neutral white balance, natural non-oversaturated color, windows pulled so the exterior garden view reads through the glass, single wall outlet neutralized to wall color, consistent realistic final look. Delivered as the client-approved master that sets the editing style for the remaining 24-29 images.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Final deliverable livingroom_streetline_final.jpg is a valid JPG fileautolivingroom_streetline_final.jpg is a valid JPEG (magic bytes FF D8 FF, extension .jpg, MIME image/jpeg)K1dealbreakeroutput
Final JPG is encoded in sRGB / RGB color space (not CMYK)autolivingroom_streetline_final.jpg channel mode is RGB and its embedded/assumed color profile is sRGB, not CMYK or another profile, per the output spec 'sRGB'K1dealbreakeroutput
Final JPG preserves a 3:2 aspect ratio at full hero resolution (no downscaling)autoWidth:height of livingroom_streetline_final.jpg equals 3:2 within rounding, and its pixel dimensions equal the supplied hero_livingroom_interior.jpg full resolution (allowing only the straighten/crop reduction), with no additional downscalingK1mandatoryoutput
An inline web preview of the finalized image is produced for client sign-offautolivingroom_streetline_final_preview exists as a bounded inline JPEG/PNG render derived from livingroom_streetline_final.jpg (asset_inline_preview output present)K1mandatoryoutput
Vertical wall lines in the final image read straight (tilt corrected)expertWall verticals appear plumb/upright in livingroom_streetline_final.jpg; the few-degree input tilt from hero_livingroom_interior.jpg is removed so walls read straightK3mandatorybrief
The image is cropped to clean rectangular bounds with no rotation wedgeexpertNo transparent/black triangular wedge or skewed corner from the straighten step is visible in livingroom_streetline_final.jpg; the frame is a clean rectangleK3mandatorybrief
Interior is bright but realistic with lifted shadows and tamed highlights, no unrealistic HDR lookexpertDark portions of the room are lifted and global highlights pulled back so the interior reads bright yet natural, with no HDR halo, crunch, or oversaturated HDR artifacts, per the brief 'bright but realistic interiors' and 'No oversaturated colors or unrealistic HDR effects'K4mandatorybrief
White balance is corrected so the warm/tungsten cast is neutralizedexpertThe slightly-warm input cast is neutralized; the white plaster walls read neutral white rather than warm/yellow in livingroom_streetline_final.jpg, per the brief 'Color correction and white balance adjustment' / 'Natural-looking colors'K4mandatorybrief
Colors are natural with only a gentle vibrance lift, not oversaturatedexpertColors read natural with only a gentle vibrance lift and no saturation push; no oversaturated greens/colors, consistent with the brief 'No oversaturated colors'K4mandatorybrief
The blown-out left garden windows are pulled so the exterior view reads through the glassexpertThe previously near-white left-hand windows now show the outdoor view (green Dutch garden, wooden fence, grey sky) through the glass, matching the exterior in window_bracket_reference.jpg, with highlights and exposure pulled down on the window regionK4mandatorybrief
The window pull is selective and does not darken the surrounding interiorexpertExposure/highlight reduction is confined to the window mask; the rest of the bright interior is unaffected, with no obvious masking halo at the window edgesK3qualitybrief
The small white wall power outlet is neutralized to the wall colorexpertThe distracting small white double outlet low on the right-hand wall is filled to match the surrounding wall color and no longer reads as a distractor in livingroom_streetline_final.jpgK3mandatorybrief
The outlet clean-up is a solid-color fill, not AI object removal or generative compositingexpertThe cleaned outlet area is a flat solid wall-matched fill (image_fill_area), with no synthesized texture, generative content, or composite artifactsK7dealbreakerbrief
Only connector-legal Adobe tools are used; no HDR-merge, generative-fill, AI object removal, compositing, upscaling, or Express/Canva tools appearautoThe executed tool set is a subset of the spec tools_used (asset_initialize_file_upload, asset_finalize_file_upload, image_auto_straighten, image_crop_to_bounds, image_apply_auto_tone, image_adjust_dark_portions, image_adjust_highlights, image_adjust_color_temperature, image_adjust_vibrance_and_saturation, image_select_by_prompt, image_adjust_exposure, image_fill_area, image_apply_preset, asset_inline_preview); no true multi-bracket HDR-merge, generative-fill, AI-removal, compositing, upscaling, or Express/Canva tool is presentK7dealbreakerbrief
A single consistent realistic interior preset is applied as a repeatable batch lookexpertA neutral bright-clean look (image_apply_preset) is applied that reads as a realistic Streetline-style finish suitable to loop across the remaining 24-29 images, not a heavy stylized filterK4qualitybrief
Overall finish matches the Streetline reference: bright, clean, realistic, professionalexpertFinal master reads as bright, clean, realistic, and professional per the brief's style bar 'similar to Streetline: bright, clean, realistic, and professional', with straightened verticals, neutral white balance, intact through-window exterior view, and cleaned outlet all cohering into one believable real-estate listing photoK4qualitybrief

AO-29   Continental 2026 Cup Stars Poster — Cinematic Grade, Flag-Colour Accents & Print/Web Master Delivery

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 22)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take the client's supplied composited Continental-stars hero plate (three fictional star strikers — Solano, Ferraz and Rocher — plus the trophy already arranged) plus a flag-pattern swatch, and finish it into a cinematic, high-contrast, signature-colour-accented A2 300-dpi print master plus social-media versions using only Adobe connector tools.

Full brief

I am commissioning the FINISHING and COLOUR-GRADE pass on a 2026 Continental Cup hero poster. My photo team has already arranged the licensed shots — three of our fictional Continental Cup star strikers, Diego Solano, Marco Ferraz and Théo Rocher, positioned dramatically around the Continental Cup trophy on a night-stadium background — into a single flattened HERO PLATE (5000×7000 px, slightly soft, flat ungraded log-style colour). I am also handing you a single seamless flag-pattern swatch (a tasteful woven blend of the three stars' signature team palettes — sky-blue/white, red/green, and blue/white/red) to use as a subtle graphic accent. All per-player grading is done directly on the hero plate — I do NOT need separate cut-out plates; you can isolate each player region with prompt-masking on the plate itself.\n\nYour job, using Adobe tools only, is:\n1. Straighten and auto-tone the hero plate, then build a punchy cinematic grade — lift the key/rim contrast so the figures carve out of the dark stadium, warm the floodlight highlights, cool the shadows for depth, and push overall high-contrast \"stadium floodlights at night\" mood.\n2. Weave each star's signature TEAM COLOURS in tastefully, not as emblems: use prompt-masking on the hero plate to isolate each player region and nudge the dominant accent colour (Solano → sky-blue energy, Ferraz → red/green, Rocher → blue/white/red) via selective saturation, HSL and a soft directional colour overlay so the palette reads as rim-light and energy streaks.\n3. Add believable night atmosphere — a gentle lens/atmospheric haze in the deep background only, plus fine film grain so it prints organically rather than digitally clean.\n4. Turn the supplied flag-pattern swatch into a clean reusable VECTOR accent (SVG) for the studio's wider kit.\n5. Crop the graded plate to the true A2 aspect ratio, resize to exact A2 at ≥300 dpi, and deliver: a flattened high-res print master (TIFF), a press-ready A2 PDF exported from our print shell, plus correctly-cropped 1:1 and 9:16 social versions — all carrying the same grade. (I understand the connector delivers a flattened working master rather than a layered PSD — the flattened TIFF/PDF is the working master I need.)\n\nAcceptance: (a) consistent high-contrast cinematic grade across the whole frame; (b) obvious-but-tasteful signature-colour integration per player, no literal flags; (c) A2 print-ready master at ≥300 dpi + web/social versions that match the grade; (d) a clean vector flag-accent asset.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 22 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
continental_poster_print_master_A2.pdfpdfA2 (420×594 mm) press-ready PDF at ≥300 dpi, exported from the authored .indd with the fully graded full-bleed poster placed; flattened, CMYK-intent print master.
continental_poster_print_master_A2.tiffimageFlattened high-resolution RGB TIFF of the finished graded A2 poster (≥4960×7016 px ≈ A2@300dpi), emitted directly by the step-18 crop_and_resize raster export at print intent — the deliverable flattened working master named in the brief (the connector delivers this flattened master, not a layered PSD).
continental_poster_social_1x1.jpgimage1080×1080 1:1 Instagram-feed crop of the graded poster, subject-aware crop keeping all three figures + trophy, same grade.
continental_poster_social_9x16.jpgimage1080×1920 9:16 story/reel crop of the graded poster, same cinematic grade.
flag_accent.svgvectorClean vectorised SVG of the flag-pattern swatch for reuse across the studio kit (scalable, no raster).

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Print master PDF is delivered at A2 page size (420×594 mm)autocontinental_poster_print_master_A2.pdf exists and its page/trim box measures A2 = 420×594 mm portrait within ±1 mmK1mandatoryoutput
Print master PDF resolution is at least 300 dpiautoThe placed full-bleed image in continental_poster_print_master_A2.pdf resolves to ≥300 dpi at A2 (effective raster ≥4960×7016 px)K1mandatoryoutput
Print master PDF is a flattened, CMYK-intent press-ready masterautocontinental_poster_print_master_A2.pdf is single-page, flattened, and carries CMYK output intent / colour space (not RGB-only)K1dealbreakeroutput
Flattened TIFF print master is delivered at A2 300-dpi pixel dimensionsautocontinental_poster_print_master_A2.tiff exists, is a flattened TIFF, and measures ≥4960×7016 px (≈A2 at 300 dpi)K1mandatoryoutput
1:1 Instagram-feed social crop is delivered at exact dimensionsautocontinental_poster_social_1x1.jpg exists and is exactly 1080×1080 px JPEGK1mandatoryoutput
9:16 story/reel social crop is delivered at exact dimensionsautocontinental_poster_social_9x16.jpg exists and is exactly 1080×1920 px JPEGK1mandatoryoutput
Flag-accent asset is delivered as a true scalable vector SVGautoflag_accent.svg exists, is a valid SVG containing vector <path>/shape geometry, and embeds no raster image dataK1mandatoryoutput
A Firefly review board deep-link is delivered assembling the graded master, the three per-player accent stills and the two social cropsautocontinental_review_board is a resolvable Firefly board deep-link that assembles the graded A2 master/hero, the three per-player accent stills (left/centre/right) and the two social crops (1:1 and 9:16) for client sign-offK6mandatoryoutput
The 1:1 social crop is subject-aware and keeps all three figures plus the trophyexpertIn continental_poster_social_1x1.jpg all three footballer figures and the Continental Cup trophy remain fully visible and uncropped at the edgesK3mandatoryoutput
A consistent high-contrast cinematic 'stadium floodlights at night' grade is applied and carried across every deliverableexpertThe graded master shows punchy high contrast with figures carving out of dark stadium shadows, warm floodlit highlights and cooled shadows, and the SAME grade is visibly carried into the TIFF, the PDF print master and both social crops (1:1 and 9:16)K4mandatorybrief
Per-player flag-colour accents are integrated with the correct palette for each figureexpertLeft figure reads sky-blue (Argentina-style) accent, centre figure reads red/green (Portugal-style) accent, right figure reads blue/white/red (France-style) accent, appearing as rim-light/energy streaks rather than flat fillsK4mandatorybrief
Flag colours are woven in tastefully with NO literal flags or national emblemsexpertNo recognisable national flag, crest or emblem appears anywhere in the poster deliverables or in flag_accent.svg; colour accents read only as lighting/energy/graphic textureK2dealbreakerbrief
Believable night atmosphere is added — background haze/floodlight bloom plus fine film grainexpertThe deep background shows gentle atmospheric haze / floodlight bloom and the frame carries fine organic film grain so it prints organically rather than digitally clean, ideally without softening the three figures and trophyK3qualitybrief
The vectorised flag accent faithfully reproduces the supplied flag-pattern swatch with clean pathsexpertflag_accent.svg cleanly reproduces the woven sky-blue/white, red/green, blue-white-red bands of flag_pattern_swatch.png with smooth vector paths and no tracing artefactsK3qualityinput
Player likenesses, scene composition and the trophy come only from the client-supplied plates, not regeneratedexpertThe figures, trophy and night-stadium arrangement in all deliverables match the supplied hero plate, with no faces, trophy, or scene content invented or altered by generative tools beyond the A2 bleed outpaintK7dealbreakerinput
Generative expand is used only to extend the canvas to A2 bleed, not to add subject contentexpertGeneratively expanded margins appear only as background/bleed extension around the original framing; no new figures, trophy or subject content are introduced inside or around the original plate areaK7qualitybrief

Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic

Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-30   Daily Promo Spot — Color-Graded Key Frames, Thumbnail Pack & Sizzle Reel

Brand/vertical Music, Film, Publishing & MediaFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Take today's exported promo key frames, apply one consistent lively color grade across the frames, build a YouTube thumbnail from the hero shot, generate a sizzle cut and a content summary, and drop the finished, organized package back in the daily folder — same day.

Full brief

Fresh promotional footage lands in the shared drive each afternoon and the finished, post-ready package must go back in the same folder within 24 hours. For today's spot the client exported five representative key frames from the raw clips (hero close-up, two mid-shots, an environment/wide, and a product/detail shot), all shot in mixed afternoon lighting so they look uneven and slightly cool. Deliver a same-day "promo finishing package": (1) all five key frames carrying ONE consistent, lively-but-tasteful grade — auto-tone baseline, warmer temperature, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, a touch more vibrance, plus the same Lightroom look-preset on every frame so the set feels edited together and "accessible, not corporate"; (2) a 1920x1080 YouTube/social thumbnail built from the graded hero frame (subject popped from the busy background via a blurred-background treatment, then cropped to 16:9 with the subject weighted left so a title can sit right); (3) a 15-second vertical-friendly sizzle cut from the source clips for the social teaser, plus a short auto-generated content summary to seed the post caption/description. Package the graded frames, thumbnail and sizzle into a dated daily-delivery folder in CC and confirm done. Keep the turnaround fast and the look consistent across every frame.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
graded_keyframes/ (5 files)image5x 1920x1080 JPEG/PNG, each carrying the identical grade chain (auto-tone -> warmer temperature -> lifted shadows -> recovered highlights -> +vibrance -> same Lightroom look-preset), applied identically to all five frames (hero + 4). Visually consistent 'edited-together' lively look.
youtube_thumbnail_1920x1080.pngimage1920x1080 (16:9) PNG thumbnail from the graded hero frame: subject popped via the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset (subject stays sharp, busy background blurred), then cropped with subject weighted left, title-safe negative space on the right.
sizzle_15s_teaser.mp4video~15s AI engagement-cut sizzle reel assembled from the source clips for the social teaser (async-widget result in product).
content_summary.txtdataShort auto-generated scene summary of the source montage to seed the post caption + description.
daily_delivery_YYYY-MM-DD/dataDated CC asset folder containing all of the above, organized for same-day handback.

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
