Each graded key frame is exactly 1920x1080 in JPEG or PNG formatautoall 5 graded frames measure exactly 1920x1080 px and are JPEG or PNGK1mandatoryoutput
All 5 key frames carry one visually consistent 'edited-together' gradeexpertthe same grade recipe (auto-tone baseline, warmer temperature, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, +vibrance, and the same Lightroom look-preset) reads as applied identically across all 5 frames so the set looks edited together, lively-but-tasteful and 'accessible, not corporate'K3mandatorybrief
The grade visibly corrects the cool/uneven afternoon cast toward warm with recovered toneexpertgraded frames read warmer than the supplied cool/uneven inputs, with lifted shadow detail and recovered (non-blown) window highlightsK4qualitybrief

voiceover_clean.wav is a denoised/de-hissed version of the supplied voice-overexpertvoiceover_clean.wav is voice-only with the room tone and tape hiss from voiceover_raw.wav removed and the narration intact and cleanK1mandatoryoutput

The dated folder contains every deliverable for same-day handbackautodaily_delivery_YYYY-MM-DD/ contains the 5 graded key frames, youtube_thumbnail_1920x1080.png, the licensed music bed, voiceover_clean.wav, sizzle_15s_teaser.mp4, and content_summary.txtK6mandatoryoutput
No client key-frame is regenerated or replaced — only graded versions of the supplied frames are deliveredexperteach delivered graded frame is the same subject/scene as its corresponding supplied input frame (no synthesized, generated, or substituted imagery)K7dealbreakerinput

AO-31   Daily-vlog brand kit: color-graded thumbnails, end-screen + lower-third plates, story-format still, and reframed clip

Brand/vertical Music, Film, Publishing & MediaFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Build the recurring per-episode still brand kit for a daily-life vlog — color-grade the supplied frame-grab and shot stills to one consistent "personal, upbeat" look, produce a subject-pop YouTube thumbnail plus a 9:16 story-format still, vectorize the channel wordmark into clean lower-third and end-screen plates, and ship a same-length 9:16 social reframe of one supplied clip.

Full brief

You are the recurring post-production designer for "Princerrr," a daily-life vlog shot in 4K and delivered at 1080p. The video edit itself (timeline trimming, jump-cuts, the full NLE project file) is handled elsewhere — your contract is the per-episode STILL BRAND KIT and the one media touch below, delivered consistently so every episode looks like the same channel. Assets the client uploads for this episode: (1) a 4K frame-grab pulled from the hero clip (the creator at their desk, window light), (2) a separate DSLR "shot" still of the creator used for the thumbnail, (3) a flat PNG of the channel wordmark / logo lockup, and (4) the hero clip itself (a 12s 4K horizontal segment). What to do, as one chained job: 1. Normalize and color-grade BOTH stills to a single house look. Start from the frame-grab: auto-tone it, warm the white balance to match the creator's window-lit "personal, upbeat" feel, lift the shadows and tame the highlights, then push vibrance so it reads lively without going garish, and finally stamp a chosen Lightroom preset so the grade is repeatable across future episodes. Apply the SAME finishing preset to the DSLR shot still so the two match. 2. From the graded DSLR still, build the YouTube thumbnail: isolate the creator (subject), invert to the background, knock the background's saturation down so the subject pops, then crop/resize to a clean 1280×720 16:9 thumbnail. 3. From the subject-popped full-res DSLR still, produce a 9:16 vertical "story / Shorts cover" still by subject-aware reframing/cropping the existing pixels to the 9:16 crop (no re-shoot, no invented canvas) and exporting at 1080×1920. 4. Turn the flat wordmark PNG into a crisp, infinitely-scalable SVG so it can sit in lower-thirds and the end screen without softening; keep that SVG as the channel mark. 5. Media touch: reframe the supplied 12s horizontal hero clip to a 9:16 vertical of the SAME length for Shorts (no trimming). Deliverables: graded frame-grab + graded DSLR still (matched look), a 1280×720 thumbnail, a 1080×1920 story still, the channel wordmark as SVG, and a 9:16 same-length reframed clip.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
framegrab_graded.pngimageColor-graded hero frame-grab, house look (warm WB, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, lively vibrance, finishing preset stamped), full-res PNG.
dslr_shot_graded.pngimageDSLR thumbnail still with the SAME finishing preset applied so it matches the frame-grab grade, full-res PNG.
thumbnail_1280x720.pngimageYouTube thumbnail, 1280x720 16:9, creator subject popped against a desaturated background.
story_still_1080x1920.pngimageVertical story / Shorts cover still, 1080x1920 9:16, subject-aware reframe/crop of the full-res subject-popped DSLR still to the 9:16 crop using existing pixels only (no re-shoot, no invented canvas).
channel_mark.svgvectorChannel wordmark vectorized to a clean scalable SVG for lower-thirds and end screen.
hero_clip_vertical_9x16.mp4videoHero clip reframed to 1080x1920 9:16, SAME length as source (no trim, no format change). [async]

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 17 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
framegrab_graded.png is delivered as a full-resolution PNG retaining the 3840x2160 source frame dimensionsautoA file named framegrab_graded.png exists, decodes as a valid PNG, and measures exactly 3840x2160 (the source hero_framegrab_4k.png resolution) rather than a downscaled or cropped imageK1mandatoryoutput
The graded frame-grab applies the house color-grade versus the flat ungraded source (warmer WB, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, lively vibrance)expertCompared to hero_framegrab_4k.png, framegrab_graded.png reads visibly warmer (the cool/green video cast removed), shadows lifted to open desk detail, window highlights tamed not blown, and vibrance pushed for a lively-but-not-garish 'personal, upbeat' lookK4mandatorybrief
dslr_shot_graded.png is delivered as a full-resolution PNG corresponding to creator_dslr_shot.pngautoA file named dslr_shot_graded.png exists, decodes as a valid PNG, and retains the full source resolution of creator_dslr_shot.png (6000x4000) rather than a downscaled or cropped imageK1mandatoryoutput
The graded DSLR still wears the SAME finishing preset as the frame-grab so the two stills read as one consistent channel gradeexpertdslr_shot_graded.png's color tone/look visibly matches framegrab_graded.png (same finishing preset stamped) so the two read as the same house grade, satisfying the brief's 'every episode looks like the same channel' consistency requirementK4mandatorybrief
thumbnail_1280x720.png is exactly 1280x720 pixels (16:9)autoImage dimensions read exactly 1280 wide by 720 high (16:9 aspect ratio)K1mandatoryoutput
In the thumbnail the creator subject is popped against a desaturated backgroundexpertThe creator (subject) retains full color/saturation while the busy background's saturation is visibly knocked down to make the subject pop; the subject itself is NOT desaturatedK3mandatorybrief
story_still_1080x1920.png is exactly 1080x1920 pixels (9:16)autoImage dimensions read exactly 1080 wide by 1920 high (9:16 vertical aspect ratio)K1mandatoryoutput
The 9:16 story still was produced by outpainting the thumbnail canvas top and bottom, not by re-shooting or stretchingexpertThe central content of thumbnail_1280x720.png is preserved undistorted, with new plausible image content generatively added above and below to fill the vertical canvas (no horizontal stretch, no new/different subject, no re-shoot)K3mandatorybrief
channel_mark.svg is delivered as a valid scalable SVG vector (not an embedded raster)autoA file named channel_mark.svg exists and parses as valid SVG containing vector path/shape elements, not merely a raster bitmap embedded in an SVG wrapperK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized channel mark faithfully reproduces the supplied wordmark lockup (PRINCERRR text, 'daily life, unfiltered' tagline, play glyph, brand colors) with clean letterformsexpertchannel_mark.svg renders the 'PRINCERRR' wordmark with the 'daily life, unfiltered' tagline, the play-button glyph, and the deep-teal and warm-coral brand colors, with crisp clean edges and legible correctly-spelled text matching channel_wordmark.png; the logo is vectorized, not regenerated or alteredK2dealbreakerinput
A Firefly board deep-link is delivered as the brand-kit handoffautobrand_kit_board is a resolvable Firefly board deep-link URL that opens a boardK1mandatoryoutput
The Firefly board gathers all five required brand-kit assetsexpertThe board displays exactly the five still/vector deliverables: framegrab_graded.png, dslr_shot_graded.png, the 1280x720 thumbnail, the 1080x1920 story still, and channel_mark.svg (all five present)K6mandatoryoutput
hero_clip_vertical_9x16.mp4 is a 1080x1920 MP4 of the SAME length as the ~12s source hero clip (no trim, no format change)autoOutput is an MP4 with frame dimensions 1080x1920 and duration equal to the ~12s source hero_clip_12s_4k.mp4 (no shortening, still an MP4 video)K1mandatoryoutput
The vertical hero clip is a meaningful reframe of the horizontal source keeping the speaking creator in frameexpertThe 9:16 output crops/repositions the 16:9 hero content so the creator stays framed across the clip (subject not cut off), not a letterboxed or stretched fillK3qualitybrief
vo_cleaned.wav is delivered as a WAV corresponding to the scratch voice-over with the same spoken introautoA WAV file vo_cleaned.wav exists corresponding to vo_scratch_track.wav, carrying the same spoken intro contentK1mandatoryoutput
The cleaned voice-over has the room reverb, HVAC hum and broadband hiss removed so the voice is broadcast-usableexpertCompared to vo_scratch_track.wav, vo_cleaned.wav has audibly reduced room reverb, hum and broadband hiss, the spoken line stays intelligible and broadcast-usable, and no new artifacts mangle the voiceK4mandatorybrief
No banned/out-of-scope capability was used and only the single allowed generative outpaint was performedexpertOutputs show no video timeline trimming/jump-cuts, no .srt captions or NLE project file, no background-replace-by-prompt, no object removal, no upscaling, and no layout composition; the only generative step is the top+bottom outpaint of the story still, and the channel mark is a faithful vectorization (not a regenerated or altered logo)K7dealbreakerbrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-32   Modern House Listing Photography — Post-Production Color Grade & Listing Export

Brand/vertical Photo Editing, Retouching & RestorationFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 27)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Edit the client's three supplied room frames (living room, kitchen, bedroom) into clean, modern, naturally-lit real-estate listing photos with straight verticals and corrected color, then export each in web-optimized JPEG (<2 MB) and full-resolution TIFF for Airbnb/Zillow.

Full brief

A landlord listing a freshly renovated rental on Airbnb and Zillow has captured interior frames of the three key areas — living room, kitchen, and bedroom — during a 0700-1200 natural-light window, but the RAW-style frames are tilted, slightly under-exposed in the shadows, mixed-white-balance (warm tungsten vs cool window daylight), and carry minor wall scuffs and dust marks. They want the post-production handled to a "modern, clean, simple, ample lighting, natural" standard: straight verticals and distortion-free perspective, basic color correction, gentle shadow lift and highlight recovery so the rooms read bright and spacious, quick blemish spotting on wall scuffs/dust WITHOUT an over-processed look, and final files delivered in two formats per the brief — web-optimized JPEG under 2 MB each AND full-resolution TIFF — within three days. The freelancer must run an Adobe photo-editing chain per room: license/load the supplied frame, auto-straighten the verticals, crop out the resulting wedge to a clean distortion-free frame, apply auto-tone as a neutral baseline, neutralize the mixed white balance with a color-temperature correction, lift the dark portions and recover the blown window highlights, selectively brighten the room interior via a prompt-based mask so the space feels open without flattening the windows, spot-correct wall scuffs/dust by selecting the blemish and filling it with the matched neutral wall color (solid fill only — not generative), apply a final gentle vibrance/saturation lift for the "modern clean" feel, then crop-and-resize to the two delivery specs and deliver the corrected JPEG/TIFF set for all three rooms. Across the three rooms the chain reuses the straighten→crop→tone→temperature→shadow/highlight→selective-brighten→spot-fix→vibrance→resize pattern, every step consuming the previous step's output.

4Planning1Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 27 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
living_room_web.jpgimageWeb-optimized JPEG, longest edge ~2048px, sRGB, quality tuned to keep file under 2 MB; straight verticals, color-corrected, shadow-lifted, scuff/dust spotted, gentle modern grade.
living_room_full.tifimageFull-resolution TIFF (native capture resolution), same corrected/graded pixels as the web version, for print/archive delivery.
kitchen_web.jpgimageWeb-optimized JPEG under 2 MB, ~2048px long edge, sRGB; straightened, white-balanced, highlight-recovered, scuff-spotted kitchen frame.
kitchen_full.tifimageFull-resolution TIFF of the corrected kitchen frame for full-quality listing/print use.
bedroom_web.jpgimageWeb-optimized JPEG under 2 MB, ~2048px long edge, sRGB; straightened, exposure-balanced (window highlights recovered, interior brightened), spotted bedroom frame.
bedroom_full.tifimageFull-resolution TIFF of the corrected bedroom frame.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All three room frames are delivered in BOTH web-optimized JPEG and full-resolution TIFF (6 image files total: living_room_web.jpg, living_room_full.tif, kitchen_web.jpg, kitchen_full.tif, bedroom_web.jpg, bedroom_full.tif).autoExactly 6 image deliverables present, one .jpg + one .tif per room: living_room_web.jpg, living_room_full.tif, kitchen_web.jpg, kitchen_full.tif, bedroom_web.jpg, bedroom_full.tif; any missing file fails.K1mandatoryoutput
Each of the three web JPEG files is under 2 MB.autoFile size of living_room_web.jpg, kitchen_web.jpg, and bedroom_web.jpg is each < 2,097,152 bytes (2 MB); any file >= 2 MB fails.K1dealbreakerbrief
Each web deliverable is a valid JPEG container with longest edge approximately 2048px.autoliving_room_web.jpg, kitchen_web.jpg, bedroom_web.jpg each decode as JPEG and have a longest-edge dimension of ~2048px (tolerance 1900-2200px); wrong format or out-of-range dimension fails.K1mandatoryoutput
Each full-resolution deliverable is a valid TIFF at native capture resolution, larger than its ~2048px web JPEG counterpart.autoliving_room_full.tif, kitchen_full.tif, bedroom_full.tif each decode as a valid TIFF container whose longest edge exceeds 2048px (native/full resolution, not the resized web size); any that is not a TIFF or is <=2048px long edge fails.K1mandatoryoutput
Each web JPEG is encoded in the sRGB color space.autoEmbedded color profile / metadata of living_room_web.jpg, kitchen_web.jpg, and bedroom_web.jpg reports sRGB; a non-sRGB color space fails.K1qualityoutput
For each room, the full-res TIFF and the web JPEG are the same edited/graded master differing only in resolution (the web file is a resized version, not a separate edit).expertFor each room the .tif and .jpg show identical corrections (same straighten, crop, color/white-balance, exposure, spot-fix, grade), differing only in pixel dimensions; a visible mismatch in edit state between the two formats fails.K1mandatoryoutput
A Firefly Board deep-link (listing_review_board) is delivered that assembles all three corrected room masters (living room, kitchen, bedroom) for one-glance client review.autolisting_review_board is present as a Firefly Board URL/deep-link and references/contains the three corrected room shots (living room, kitchen, bedroom); a missing board or fewer than three shots fails.K6mandatoryoutput
Every room frame has straightened, level verticals correcting the input tilt (~2.5deg living room, ~2deg kitchen, ~3deg bedroom).expertVertical wall, cabinet, and window-frame lines read level/straight in all three outputs versus the visibly tilted inputs; noticeable residual tilt in any room fails.K3mandatorybrief
Each straightened frame is cropped to a clean rectangular frame with no empty wedge/triangle corners left by the rotation.expertNo transparent/blank/border wedge or triangle is visible at the corners of any of the three outputs; each frame is a full distortion-free rectangle.K3mandatorybrief
Mixed warm-tungsten vs cool-daylight white balance is neutralized in the living room and kitchen frames so whites/neutrals read natural.expertNo strong residual yellow/orange or blue cast remains in living_room or kitchen outputs; pale walls, stainless steel, and quartz read neutral-white.K4mandatorybrief
Underexposed shadows are lifted in the living room and bedroom interiors so they read bright and spacious without crushed detail.expertCorner/interior shadows in living_room and bedroom outputs show recovered detail and a brighter, open feel versus the dark inputs; fully crushed/blocked blacks fail.K4mandatorybrief
Blown-out window/highlight areas are recovered in the kitchen and bedroom frames so highlight detail is held, not clipped.expertThe bright window in the bedroom output and the can-light/window highlights in the kitchen output show recovered tonal detail rather than pure clipped white.K4mandatorybrief
The bedroom interior is selectively brightened via a prompt-based mask while leaving the recovered window untouched (window is not re-blown out).expertBedroom output shows the room interior opened/brightened while the window retains its recovered highlight detail; a re-blown window or globally flattened brightening fails.K3qualitybrief
Wall/cabinet scuff and dust blemishes are removed in each room via a solid matched-neutral fill (not generative healing) with no visible patch or halo.expertThe wall scuff/dust by the sofa (living room), cabinet-door scuff (kitchen), and headboard scuff (bedroom) are gone, covered with matched wall/cabinet color and showing no visible smudge, halo, or AI-generated artifact.K3mandatorybrief
The final grade is gentle and natural - clean and modern but NOT over-processed/over-saturated.expertOutputs show only a subtle vibrance/saturation lift and natural color; no HDR look, over-sharpening, blown saturation, or heavy stylization. An over-processed result fails.K4mandatorybrief
No generative AI object-removal, generative fill, compositing, background replacement, or upscaling was used; edits stay within color/tone/geometry/solid-fill corrections.expertOutputs contain no invented/added objects, no synthesized backgrounds, no generatively healed regions; spot-fixes are flat solid-color fills consistent with the brief's 'solid fill only, NOT generative' constraint. Any generative artifact fails.K7dealbreakerbrief

AO-34   Informative explainer video: brand-graded sizzle cut, vertical reframe, and on-brand lower-third / call-out graphic plates

Brand/vertical Video Editing & Motion GraphicsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 23)Tools 19 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn my raw informative-explainer footage, brand logo, and color-reference still into a polished short cut: build a brand-locked color look plus a graded end-card, and produce on-brand lower-third, call-out, and vectorized-logo graphic plates — then deliver a 1080p sizzle and a vertical reframe, with the whole linked-asset set organised for the Premiere project.

Full brief

I have raw footage for an informative explainer video that needs to be shaped into a clear, engaging cut, plus the supporting brand assets to dress it. Source material I'm supplying: (1) a bundle of raw clips — a ~3-minute talking-head segment intercut with product/process b-roll; (2) my company logo as a flat PNG; (3) a single "color reference" photograph that represents the warm, slightly cinematic brand look I want the whole piece graded toward; and (4) a loose narrative outline. I need the freelancer to handle the full edit and the supporting graphics, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools, with every step honestly tagged for execution mode: A. VIDEO — (1) summarize the raw footage so we agree on the key talking points and a logical flow before cutting; (2) produce a punchy ~60-second highlight/sizzle cut from the raw clips that surfaces those key points; (3) reframe that cut to a 9:16 vertical version for social teasers, same length (no trimming). B. BRAND COLOR LOOK — from my color-reference still, build the locked "brand LUT" look the editor will match the master grade to: straighten and crop it clean, then run the full Lightroom-class grade (auto-tone baseline, exposure, highlights, dark-portion lift, warm color-temperature push, HSL + vibrance/saturation shaping, a gentle preset and fine grain) so I have one authoritative graded reference frame. C. GRAPHIC PLATES — the on-brand overlays the editor lays onto the timeline (delivered as standalone transparent PNG / SVG plates, NOT composited onto the video): (1) vectorize my logo to a clean SVG so it scales for the end-card and corner bug; (2) build a lower-third bar plate — solid brand-color bar generated by selecting a region and filling it; (3) build a call-out badge plate the same way; (4) grade the end-card hero frame to match the brand look. Deliverables on approval, handed off as one clearly organised linked-asset set ready for the Premiere project and easy future tweaks: a ~60s 1080p H.264 sizzle cut and a 9:16 reframe (same length); one authoritative graded brand-reference frame; logo SVG; lower-third and call-out PNG plates; and a graded end-card frame. Two rounds of feedback should be plenty.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 23 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

outputkindspec
sizzle_cut_1080p.mp4video~60s AI highlight/sizzle cut of the raw clips surfacing the key talking points, 1920x1080 (H.264 target on export).
sizzle_cut_vertical_9x16.mp4video9:16 (1080x1920) reframe of the sizzle cut, SAME length (no trimming), for social teasers.

logo_vector.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the company logo for scalable end-card and corner-bug use.
lower_third_bar_plate.pngimageTransparent-background lower-third bar plate — solid brand-amber bar region filled via selection-fill, ready to drop on the Premiere timeline.
callout_badge_plate.pngimageTransparent-background call-out badge plate — solid brand-color badge region, same selection-fill technique, for on-screen fact call-outs.
graded_endcard_frame.jpgimageEnd-card hero frame graded to match the locked brand look, with right-side negative space for the logo lockup.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom

The brand_look_reference_frame is straightened and cropped (loose headroom removed) versus the deliberately tilted, loosely framed brand_color_reference.jpg inputexpertReference frame shows a level horizon (no tilt) and tighter framing with the extra headroom removed compared to the input stillK3mandatoryoutput
The brand_look_reference_frame carries the full Lightroom-class grade producing a warm, slightly cinematic amber/teal lookexpertReference frame is visibly graded toward a warm amber tone with lifted shadows, softened highlights and subtle grain, distinct from the flat inputK4mandatorybrief
logo_vector.svg is a true vector SVG of the NORTHLOOP logo, not a raster image embedded in an SVG wrapperautologo_vector.svg contains vector path/shape elements (paths, polygons) and contains no embedded raster <image> bitmap of the logoK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo preserves the supplied NORTHLOOP hexagon mark and wordmark without regenerating or redesigning the client logoexpertlogo_vector.svg reproduces the supplied geometric hexagon mark and 'NORTHLOOP' wordmark faithfully (same forms, not a redesigned or AI-regenerated logo)K2dealbreakerinput
lower_third_bar_plate.png is a transparent-background PNG whose bar region is solid-filled with brand-amber #E8A13AautoFile is a PNG with an alpha (transparent) background and the bar region is solid-filled with #E8A13AK2mandatoryoutput
callout_badge_plate.png is a transparent-background PNG whose badge region is solid-filled with the deep-navy brand accent colorautoFile is a PNG with an alpha (transparent) background and the badge region is solid-filled with the deep-navy brand accent color (no exact hex specified in the spec)K2mandatoryoutput

No out-of-scope work is claimed: text/graphics are NOT composited onto the video frames, no generative fill / AI object removal / text-to-image is used, fill_area is solid-color only, and graphics are delivered as standalone transparent platesexpertDeliverables contain no text or graphics burned into the video frames, the plates are separate transparent assets, and there are no gen-fill / AI-removal / text-to-image artifacts presentK7dealbreakerbrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-35   Restore a 1910 magician-troupe postcard: clarify the dark front photo and make the handwritten back legible, delivered as two book/social-ready files

Brand/vertical Photo Editing, Retouching & RestorationFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Digitize and restore one 1910 magician-troupe postcard — clarify and lighten the dark sepia front photo and sharpen the contrast of the handwritten back so the description is legible — and deliver each side as its own clean, deskewed, print-and-social-ready file.

Full brief

A family-history client is digitizing ~24 antique postcard photographs from 1910 documenting a famous stage magician and his traveling company touring Asia and abroad; the client's grandmother and aunt performed in the troupe and wrote a description of each scene on the BACK of the postcards. The client needs every postcard delivered as two separate restored files — the photographic FRONT as one picture and the handwritten BACK as another — that are clean enough to be pasted into a printed memorial book and posted to social media. Many of the front photos are dark, low-contrast, and tilted from flatbed scanning; the handwriting on the backs is faint cursive pencil/ink that is hard to read. This benchmark uses ONE representative postcard as the worked end-to-end example. The client supplies two raw flatbed scans: (1) the postcard front (a dark, slightly rotated sepia-toned albumen-style photograph of the magician and company on a theatre stage, with scanner-bed border) and (2) the postcard back (faded cursive handwriting plus a postmark and divided-back postcard printing, also slightly skewed). Required restoration pipeline, per side: ingest both raw scans into the workspace; auto-straighten each skewed scan and crop away the scanner-bed margins to the postcard edge; on the FRONT, recover the dark midtones and shadows, lift exposure and overall brightness/contrast, rein in any blown highlight on the bright stage lights, gently recover the crushed dark portions where the company stands in shadow, neutralize the scanner's cool cast and re-warm to a consistent archival sepia, and finish with a vintage-photo preset look that keeps the period feel while making faces and stage detail readable; on the BACK, push brightness and contrast hard and convert to a clean high-legibility monochrome/sepia so the faint cursive description reads clearly. Deliver a high-resolution restored FRONT and a high-resolution restored BACK, each deskewed and edge-cropped, color-consistent, copy-paste-ready for the book layout and for social posts.

4Planning1Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

postcard_back_scan_raw.tifimagegemini-3-pro-imageA raw flatbed-scanner capture of the BACK of the same 1910 photographic postcard: a divided-back postcard printing layou

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.

6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 17 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Magician_Postcard_01_FRONT_restored.tifimageRestored postcard FRONT: deskewed and edge-cropped to the card, dark midtones/shadows recovered, exposure and brightness/contrast lifted, highlights tamed, re-warmed to a consistent archival sepia with a vintage-look preset preserving period feel; long edge ~3000 px, 300 DPI, suitable for a printed book page. Plus a 1080 px JPEG social variant from the same pipeline.
Magician_Postcard_01_BACK_legible.tifimageRestored postcard BACK: deskewed and edge-cropped, brightness/contrast pushed and converted to a clean high-legibility monochrome/sepia so the faint cursive description and postmark read clearly; long edge ~3000 px, 300 DPI for the book caption, plus a 1080 px JPEG social variant.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Two separate restored deliverable files are produced — one for the postcard FRONT and one for the BACK — not a single combined imageautoOutput set contains exactly two restored master images, one identifiable as the FRONT (Magician_Postcard_01_FRONT_restored.tif) and one as the BACK (Magician_Postcard_01_BACK_legible.tif); FRONT and BACK are separate files, not one combined imageK1dealbreakeroutput
Both restored master files are delivered in TIFF format as the print-master deliverableautoBoth master deliverables are .tif/TIFF files named Magician_Postcard_01_FRONT_restored.tif and Magician_Postcard_01_BACK_legible.tifK1mandatoryoutput
Each restored master TIFF has a long edge of approximately 3000 px at 300 DPI for the printed book pageautoFRONT and BACK master TIFFs each have a long-edge dimension of ~3000 px (within ±10% tolerance) and carry 300 DPI resolution metadataK1mandatoryoutput
A 1080 px JPEG social variant is produced for each side from the same pipelineautoTwo additional JPEG outputs exist — one FRONT social variant and one BACK social variant — each with a long edge of 1080 px and JPEG formatK1mandatoryoutput
A plain-text restoration recipe log file is deliveredautoA plain-text file restoration_recipe_notes.txt exists as a deliverableK1mandatoryoutput
Both sides are deskewed (auto-straightened) so the postcard edges are square, with no residual scanner tiltexpertThe restored FRONT (originally ~4 deg rotated) and BACK (originally ~3 deg rotated) appear straightened — the postcard horizontal/vertical edges read level, not tiltedK3mandatorybrief
Both sides are edge-cropped to the postcard, removing the gray scanner-bed marginexpertNeither restored master shows the light-gray scanner-bed margin or edge shadow; the frame is cropped to the postcard edge on both FRONT and BACKK3mandatorybrief
The dark FRONT photo is visibly lightened and brightened as the client explicitly asked ('lightened and clear')expertThe restored FRONT is noticeably brighter and higher in exposure than the dark, low-contrast raw scan; overall the photo reads clearly lighterK4mandatorybrief
Crushed shadow detail is recovered on the FRONT where the company stands in stage shadowexpertDetail (figures/faces of the company in the dark areas) is visible in the recovered shadow regions of the restored FRONT, not left as solid blackK4qualitybrief
The blown bright stage-light highlight on the FRONT is reined in rather than further clipped by the brighteningexpertThe bright stage-light spot retains tonal detail and is not blown out to a larger pure-white region after brighteningK4qualitybrief
The cool/greenish scanner cast on the FRONT is neutralized and the image is re-warmed to a consistent archival sepia, preserving period feel (not converted to neutral B&W)expertThe restored FRONT shows a warm sepia tone with the cool/green cast removed, retaining the vintage period look rather than a neutral grayscaleK4mandatorybrief
The faint cursive handwriting on the BACK is rendered clearly legible via hard brightness/contrast and a clean monochrome/sepia tintexpertThe cursive description on the restored BACK separates cleanly from the paper and is readable; foxing color noise is suppressed by a monochrome/sepia conversionK4mandatorybrief
The original handwritten description text on the BACK is preserved and readable without being altered or rewrittenexpertThe restored BACK still shows the original cursive line 'Front row, my mother and Aunt Lillie with the Company at the Royal — Singapore, March 1910' and the 1910 postmark; the wording is not changed, fabricated, or substitutedK7mandatoryinput
The restoration alters only tone/contrast/color and geometry — no colorization, AI reconstruction, compositing, or upscaling was introducedexpertRestored FRONT and BACK remain the same monochrome/sepia photographs as the source content: no added color (colorization), no invented or regenerated image regions, no composited elements, and no resolution upscaling beyond the sourceK7dealbreakerbrief
The recipe log captures concrete reusable per-card parameter values, not just step names, so the recipe can be re-run on the remaining 23 postcardsexpertrestoration_recipe_notes.txt records the actual per-card operation sequence and values used (straighten angle, crop bounds, exposure/brightness/contrast amounts, color-temperature shift, preset id, monochromatic tint) sufficient to reproduce the look on the other cardsK6mandatoryoutput
FRONT and BACK restorations are color-consistent with each other for a cohesive book/social presentationexpertThe sepia/monochrome tone of the restored FRONT and BACK read as a matched, consistent archival look rather than clashing color treatmentsK3qualitybrief

AO-37   Restore Three Faded, Scratched Family Photo Scans for A4 Wall Prints

Brand/vertical Technology, SaaS & StartupsFamily Photo & ImageOperation O1 Tonal grade & restoreHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K2 · K1 · K6 · K7

Restore three damaged old photo scans — correct color fading, conceal scratches/tears over plain areas, and maximize clarity — then output each as a print-ready A4 (2480x3508 px, 300 DPI) file.

Full brief

I have several old family photo scans that I want to restore and print at A4 to mount on my wall. They suffer from three problems: color fading (the older color shots have gone yellow/orange and flat), scratches or tears (white scratch lines and a torn corner, mostly running across plain backdrops and sky), and blurriness — and the MAIN priority is improving overall clarity so they look crisp on a large print. Please work from the three supplied scans: 1. faded_color_portrait_1968.png — a colour studio portrait from the late 1960s, heavily yellowed, low contrast, slightly tilted on the scanner bed. 2. scratched_bw_studio_1955.png — a black-and-white studio photo with several fine white scratch lines crossing the plain grey studio backdrop behind the subject. 3. torn_outdoor_snapshot_1972.png — a colour outdoor snapshot with a torn top-left corner exposing white paper and a couple of scratches across the open sky. For each photo I need you to: straighten any scanner tilt and crop off the ragged scan border; correct the colour cast and faded contrast so skin tones and colours look natural again; lift overall clarity and local contrast so detail reads on a big print; conceal the scratches and the torn corner ONLY where they cross plain, uniform regions (the studio backdrop and the open sky) by colour-matching and filling those marks so they disappear; add a very light film grain so the restored areas don't look plastic against the original photographic texture; and finally produce a clean, true-A4 file at 2480x3508 px / 300 DPI ready to send to the print lab. Deliverables: three restored, A4 print-ready image files (one per scan), each colour-corrected, damage-concealed over its plain regions, clarity-recovered and sized to true A4.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain genericDeliverable restored-photoWorkflow EditOperation O1Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 20Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand generic
4Planning1Brand strictness4Tool diversity2Visual density2Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
portrait_1968_restored_A4.pngimageRestored late-1960s color portrait: tilt-straightened, border-cropped, color-cast neutralized, faded contrast and clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI, PNG.
bw_studio_1955_restored_A4.pngimageRestored 1955 B&W studio photo: backdrop scratches concealed by matched solid-grey fill, tonal range and clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI, PNG.
snapshot_1972_restored_A4.pngimageRestored 1972 outdoor snapshot: torn corner and sky scratches concealed by matched solid sky-blue fill, color cast corrected, contrast/clarity recovered, light grain re-added. Final A4 print size 2480x3508 px at 300 DPI, PNG.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
Exactly four deliverables are produced: the three named restored PNG files plus one Firefly Board deep-linkautoThe output set contains exactly these four deliverables and no extras: portrait_1968_restored_A4.png, bw_studio_1955_restored_A4.png, snapshot_1972_restored_A4.png (three image files present by exact filename), and one restoration_before_after_board Firefly Board deep-link; count == 4 with none missingK1mandatoryoutput
Each of the three restored image files is exactly 2480x3508 px (true A4 portrait)autoPixel dimensions of all three of portrait_1968_restored_A4.png, bw_studio_1955_restored_A4.png, snapshot_1972_restored_A4.png read as width==2480 and height==3508K1dealbreakeroutput
Each of the three restored image files carries 300 DPI resolution metadataautoEmbedded resolution of all three A4 files reads 300 DPI (300 pixels/inch on both the horizontal and vertical axes)K1mandatoryoutput
Each of the three restored image files is a valid PNG matching its .png extensionautoFile format of portrait_1968_restored_A4.png, bw_studio_1955_restored_A4.png, snapshot_1972_restored_A4.png is PNG (valid PNG header/magic bytes), consistent with the .png extensionK1dealbreakeroutput
Each restored output is the corresponding supplied scan (one output per input, not substituted or regenerated)expertportrait_1968_restored_A4.png is recognizably the same scene/subjects as faded_color_portrait_1968.png, bw_studio_1955_restored_A4.png matches scratched_bw_studio_1955.png, and snapshot_1972_restored_A4.png matches torn_outdoor_snapshot_1972.png; subject identity and composition are preserved, not regeneratedK2dealbreakerinput
The 1968 colour portrait is tilt-straightened and the ragged white scan border is cropped offexpertportrait_1968_restored_A4.png shows the original ~2-3 degree scanner tilt corrected (subject level) and no remaining ragged uneven white scanner border around the image areaK3mandatorybrief
The 1972 snapshot's crooked thin white scan border is cropped offexpertsnapshot_1972_restored_A4.png shows no remaining thin uneven white scanner border; only photographic image content fills the frameK3mandatorybrief
The yellow-orange age cast on the 1968 portrait is neutralized and faded colour is recoveredexpertportrait_1968_restored_A4.png no longer shows the strong yellow/orange cast; skin tones read natural and muted colours regain saturation without oversaturationK4mandatorybrief
The white scratch lines over the plain grey studio backdrop of the 1955 B&W photo are concealed by a matched solid-grey fill, subject untouchedexpertIn bw_studio_1955_restored_A4.png the bright white hairline scratches that crossed the plain grey backdrop are no longer visible, the fill grey matches the surrounding backdrop so the patch is invisible, and the subject/face is unaffectedK3mandatorybrief
The 1955 B&W photo remains true monochrome with recovered tonal range and clarityexpertbw_studio_1955_restored_A4.png is neutral black-and-white (no colour cast introduced) with a recovered silver-gelatin tonal range and crisper local contrast versus the soft, faded originalK4mandatorybrief
The torn top-left corner and the sky scratches of the 1972 snapshot are concealed by a matched solid sky-blue fill over the plain skyexpertIn snapshot_1972_restored_A4.png the white torn-corner triangle and the two white scratch lines that crossed the plain sky are no longer visible, replaced by a sky-blue matching the surrounding sky; the two people are unaffectedK3mandatorybrief
The 1972 snapshot's aged magenta/cyan colour shift is corrected and faded contrast is recoveredexpertsnapshot_1972_restored_A4.png shows a neutralized colour balance (no residual magenta/cyan shift) and restored contrast versus the flat, faded originalK4mandatorybrief
Overall clarity / perceived sharpness is improved on all three restored outputs (the brief's stated MAIN priority)expertAll three A4 outputs read crisper with stronger local contrast and more readable detail than their originals at print scale, delivered via tonal/contrast/local-contrast shaping (not via an invented sharpen op, which the connector lacks)K4mandatorybrief
A very light film grain is present on all three restored outputs so concealment/regraded areas do not look plasticexpertEach of the three A4 outputs carries a subtle, uniform film grain that blends the solid-filled concealment patches and re-graded areas with the original photographic texture; grain is light, not heavy or distractingK3mandatorybrief
The before/after Firefly Board pairs the three original scans against the three restored A4 outputsexpertThe restoration_before_after_board deep-link opens a Firefly Board showing all three original scans (faded_color_portrait_1968.png, scratched_bw_studio_1955.png, torn_outdoor_snapshot_1972.png) alongside their three restored A4 counterparts as a before/after approval contact-board layoutK5mandatoryoutput
Damage concealment is confined to plain uniform regions only; no generative heal, clone, object-removal, or AI upscaling was usedexpertConcealment edits appear only over the uniform grey backdrop (PHOTO 2) and plain sky (PHOTO 3); no fabricated detail over textured/subject areas, and no evidence of generative fill, object-removal, or upscaling beyond the scans' native resolutionK7mandatorybrief

Exposure & tonebalanced; highlights/shadows hold detailslightly off, recoverableclipped — detail lost

Blemish & artefact removalclean and naturala few left / faint over-smoothingartefacts remain / plastic
Texture & detail preservedrealistic micro-detailslightly softdetail smeared or lost
Subject fidelityshape & identity unchangedminor warpsubject distorted / altered
Look / grade integritycohesive, intentional gradeslightly unevenartificial / broken look
Composite realism (stock-hero)light, scale, perspective matchminor mismatchpasted-on, unbelievable

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity ★Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-38   Logo + Four Challenge-Coin Marks: Concept Rasters to Coin-Ready Vector Artwork with Black/White/Gold/Silver Variants

Brand/vertical Automotive, Industrial & AgricultureFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 18)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's five supplied concept rasters (one logo + four challenge-coin references) into clean, coin-ready vector marks, each delivered as transparent-PNG and SVG in black, white, gold, and silver.

Full brief

A challenge-coin / branding client (automotive-industrial sector) supplies five flat reference images: one primary logo concept and four symbolic coin-face concepts. They are pixelated mock-ups that cannot go to a mint. The task is to convert each into production-grade vector art and deliver a four-color family per mark. Pipeline per the connector chain: (1) upload all five client rasters to CC storage and preview them; (2) on the primary logo, remove the busy background so only the mark survives on transparency; (3) auto-straighten the de-bagged logo so the mark sits square (coins are minted on a true axis); (4) crop the straightened logo tight to its own bounds so the trace has no dead margin; (5) reframe/resize that cropped mark to a fixed 2000x2000 px square proof canvas via image_crop_and_resize (a gen-free crop-and-scale to the target aspect — no pixels are invented), establishing the exact resolution every variant export inherits; (6) vectorize that clean, square, transparent master into a scalable SVG — this is the editable master vector that also seeds EPS/PDF on packaging; (7) select the mark's silhouette on the 2000x2000 master, then fill it with solid true-black to mint the BLACK variant; (8) on the same selected silhouette, fill it with solid white to mint the WHITE variant; (9) apply a flat color overlay in gold #C8A24B to mint the GOLD variant; (10) apply a monochromatic silver/gray tint to mint the SILVER variant. Then the four coin references are run through the same remove-background -> crop -> vectorize spine so all five marks are coin-ready vector. Finally, copy every produced vector + variant asset into a structured per-mark deliverables folder and package the full logo+four-coin family (SVG sources, transparent PNGs, black/white/gold/silver variants) into one ZIP for mint/manufacturer handoff. Hard constraints honored: no from-scratch illustration (every mark is traced from a client reference), no generative fill / outpaint / object removal / compositing / upscaling; image_crop_and_resize only crops-and-scales the existing mark to the square proof canvas (it never grows or invents canvas), image_fill_area is used only for SOLID color minting (black and white) of an isolated mask, and no generative tool is used at any step. Deliverables map 1:1 to the brief: transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, and black/white/gold/silver color versions, coin-ready and readable at small sizes.

See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

coin_ref_02_gear_globe.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageFlat raster concept for a challenge-coin face: a globe wrapped by a mechanical gear ring with two crossed industrial ham
coin_ref_03_anvil.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageFlat raster concept for a challenge-coin face: a heavy anvil with a forging hammer crossed over it and a rope-border rin

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 18 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
logo_vector_master.svgvectorClean scalable SVG traced from the cropped, background-free, straightened, 2000x2000-normalized primary logo; editable master vector (seeds EPS / vector-PDF on packaging); coin-ready, readable at <=15 mm.
logo_variant_black.pngimageTransparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silhouette filled solid true-black #000000.
logo_variant_white.pngimageTransparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silhouette filled solid white #FFFFFF (the on-light / reversed variant).
logo_variant_gold.pngimageTransparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo flat gold #C8A24B color overlay.
logo_variant_silver.pngimageTransparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silver/gray #AAAAAA monochromatic tint.
coin_vectors_x4.svgvectorFour clean SVG coin-face vectors (eagle, gear-globe, anvil, tractor), each traced from its background-removed reference, coin-ready for mint review.
ironline_coin_package.zipdataSingle ZIP bundling all SVG vector sources, transparent PNG exports, and the black/white/gold/silver variant set, organized per-mark for mint/manufacturer handoff.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 17 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All seven deliverables are present and each is the correct kind: logo_vector_master.svg (SVG), logo_variant_black.png, logo_variant_gold.png, logo_variant_silver.png (PNGs), coin_vectors_x4.svg (SVG), coin_review_board (Firefly Board URL), and ironline_coin_package.zip.autoAll 7 named outputs exist; the SVG/PNG files open without error, the board URL resolves, and the ZIP is a valid archive. Missing or unopenable any one = fail.K1mandatoryoutput
logo_vector_master.svg is a genuine vector file (true SVG path/shape geometry), not a raster image wrapped in an SVG container.autoFile parses as SVG and contains vector geometry elements (<path>, <polygon>, <circle>, etc.); it is NOT merely a single embedded <image> bitmap. Raster-in-SVG = fail.K1dealbreakeroutput
coin_vectors_x4.svg delivers exactly four coin-face vectors (eagle, gear-globe, anvil, tractor), each genuine SVG vector geometry, none missing or duplicated.autoExactly 4 distinct coin-face vectors present (one per reference: eagle, gear-globe, anvil, tractor), each containing vector path/shape elements rather than an embedded bitmap. Fewer/more than 4, or any raster-in-SVG = fail.K1mandatoryoutput
The three minted color variants (logo_variant_black.png, logo_variant_gold.png, logo_variant_silver.png) are each a transparent PNG measuring exactly 2000x2000 px.autoEach of the 3 variant PNGs has an alpha channel (transparent background) and dimensions == 2000 x 2000 px. Any opaque background or any non-2000x2000 file = fail.K1mandatoryoutput
logo_variant_black.png fills the logo silhouette with true black #000000.autoSampled mark pixels read RGB(0,0,0) / hex #000000 within a small tolerance; not a near-black or dark gray.K1mandatoryoutput
logo_variant_gold.png applies the flat gold color #C8A24B (RGB 200,162,75) specified in the brief.autoSampled mark pixels read hex #C8A24B (RGB 200,162,75) within a small tolerance; not an arbitrary gold.K2mandatorybrief
logo_variant_silver.png applies the silver/gray monochromatic tint at #AAAAAA (RGB 170,170,170) specified in the brief.autoSampled mark pixels read hex #AAAAAA (RGB 170,170,170) within a small tolerance; tint is neutral gray, not colored.K2mandatorybrief
The full black/white/gold/silver four-color family is accounted for: black, gold, and silver are delivered as produced PNG assets, and the white #FFFFFF version is the transparent-on-light case derived from the same master and explicitly documented as such (not silently omitted).expertBlack, gold, and silver exist as produced assets AND the white #FFFFFF on-light case is documented/explained as derived from the same master per the brief. White claimed as a separately produced file when no white-fill output exists, or the white case omitted entirely = fail.K7mandatorybrief
The logo vector reproduces the client's IRONLINE FORGE concept (heraldic shield with piston-and-gear motif), traced from logo_concept_primary.png rather than illustrated or generated from scratch.expertlogo_vector_master.svg matches the supplied IRONLINE FORGE shield/piston-gear reference silhouette. Any invented, restyled, or substituted logo artwork = fail.K2dealbreakerinput
Each of the four coin vectors matches its corresponding client reference: eagle (eagle gripping wrench + lightning bolt), gear-globe (globe + gear ring + crossed hammers), anvil (anvil + crossed forging hammer + rope-border ring), tractor (side-profile tractor + wheat-wreath border + top star).expertEach coin vector reproduces the symbolic content of its source reference (coin_ref_01_eagle / 02_gear_globe / 03_anvil / 04_tractor). Any invented or substituted coin artwork = fail.K2dealbreakerinput
The primary logo's busy workshop background has been removed so only the mark survives on clean transparency before vectorization.expertThe logo master and its variants show the isolated mark on clean transparency with no residual workshop-background pixels or halo. Visible leftover background = fail.K3mandatorybrief
Each of the four coin references has had its non-transparent source background (off-white paper / mid-gray / beige / blue-gradient) removed before tracing.expertAll 4 coin vectors are isolated coin-face marks on transparency with no residual reference background. Any coin still carrying its source background = fail.K3mandatorybrief
The logo mark is auto-straightened (sits square/on-axis for coin minting) and cropped tight to its own bounds with no dead transparent margin.expertThe straightened logo is visually level (coin axis) and framed tight to the mark; obvious tilt or large empty margin = fail.K3qualitybrief
All five marks (logo + four coins) are coin-ready and remain legible at small coin proof size, including embedded micro-text such as the eagle banner text.expertWhen scaled to coin proof size (<=15 mm), silhouettes and fine details (gear teeth, rope border, wheat wreath, banner micro-text) stay clean and readable; broken or muddy detail at small size = fail.K4mandatorybrief
coin_review_board is a Firefly Board deep-link assembling the logo master + four coin vectors + the black/gold/silver variant proofs into one client-review moodboard.autoBoard URL resolves and the board contains the logo master, all 4 coin vectors, and the black/gold/silver variant proofs together in a single reviewable board.K6mandatoryoutput
ironline_coin_package.zip bundles all SVG vector sources (logo master + 4 coins), the transparent PNG variant exports, and the black/gold/silver color-variant set, organized per-mark for mint/manufacturer handoff.autoZIP opens and contains every SVG (logo_vector_master.svg + coin_vectors_x4.svg / 4 coin vectors), the transparent PNG exports, and the black/gold/silver variant PNGs, organized per-mark. Missing any class of asset = fail.K1mandatoryoutput
No banned capability was used: no from-scratch illustration, no generative fill / object removal / compositing, no text-to-image, no upscaling; image_fill_area is used only for solid-color minting of an isolated mask.expertEvery vector traces to a real client reference and color variants are pure solid-fill / color-overlay / monochromatic-tint outputs; any evidence of generative content, compositing, or invented elements = fail.K7dealbreakerbrief

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-39   Apparel Shirt Isolation, Background Cleanup and Marketplace-Ready E-commerce Set

Brand/vertical Fashion & ApparelFamily Photo & ImageOperation O2 Masked recolor & isolationHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K2 · K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn a messy worn-shirt hero photo plus a flat-lay reference shirt into a clean, colour-true, marketplace-ready apparel product set (cleaned studio backdrop, isolated transparent packshot, vectorized logo, square + catalog crops).

Full brief

We sell a small line of cotton shirts and our marketplace listings look amateur. I'm sending you three things: (1) HERO_WORN.jpg — a model wearing our flagship olive-green button-down in a cluttered home studio (laundry, cables and a coat rack are visible behind her, and the shot is slightly tilted and a touch under-exposed); (2) SHIRT_FLATLAY.jpg — a clean-ish flat-lay of "the similar shirt" (the same garment laid flat on a light table) that we want isolated as a drop-in packshot for the listing thumbnail; (3) BRAND_LOGO.png — our small wordmark logo we want as crisp vector for the woven label and the listing banner. Please: straighten and tone-correct the hero so it looks professionally lit; cut the model cleanly off the messy background and put her on a clean solid-white studio backdrop (the clutter must be gone); make sure the olive-green of the shirt reads true and rich, not muddy; reframe the cleaned hero to a wide catalog banner shape centered on the model (a horizontal crop of the same cleaned frame — I understand the top and bottom of the shot get trimmed to hit the wide ratio); isolate the flat-lay shirt to a transparent PNG packshot; vectorize the logo to SVG; and hand back the hero as both a 1:1 Instagram square and a clean catalog frame, plus the transparent packshot and the SVG logo. Attention to detail on the edge of the collar and sleeves is essential.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain apparelDeliverable product-shotWorkflow EditOperation O2Family Photo & ImageModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 21Feasibility fullExec mode [C]Brand NORTHGROVE
4Planning2Brand strictness5Tool diversity3Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Technical Quality · Process · Honesty. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
HERO_WORN.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal documentary-style photo of a woman in her late 20s wearing an olive-green cotton button-down shirt, standing i
SHIRT_FLATLAY.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal top-down flat-lay of a single olive-green cotton button-down shirt laid flat and neatly arranged on a softly-l
BRAND_LOGO.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA clean minimalist apparel-brand wordmark logo reading 'NORTHGROVE' in a modern condensed sans-serif, deep charcoal lett

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_square_1x1.jpgimage1:1 Instagram square, 2048x2048 px, JPEG, sRGB. The master cleaned/graded hero (straightened, toned, white-balanced, subject isolated on solid-white studio backdrop, olive shirt colour corrected) center-cropped to a square. DELIVERABLE 1 (step 12).
hero_catalog_banner.jpgimageWide catalog/banner crop, 2400x1200 px (~2:1), JPEG, sRGB. An HONEST horizontal reframe of the same master cleaned hero via crop_and_resize — the existing frame is cropped to the wide ratio centered on the model, so the top/bottom of the original shot are trimmed to hit 2:1. No canvas is invented, padded, widened, or solid-filled; the solid-white backdrop already present behind the isolated subject simply carries through the crop. DELIVERABLE 2 (step 13).
shirt_packshot.pngimageTransparent-background PNG packshot of the isolated olive flat-lay shirt (RGBA, alpha channel), pale-grey light-table background removed with clean collar/sleeve edges, native flat-lay resolution (~2048x2048 px). DELIVERABLE 3 (step 16).
northgrove_logo.svgvectorVector SVG of the NORTHGROVE 2-color wordmark (charcoal type + olive leaf glyph) traced from the raster logo, scalable crisp paths for woven-label and banner use. DELIVERABLE 4 (step 19).

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 15 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
hero_square_1x1.jpg is a 2048x2048 1:1 square JPEGautoFile hero_square_1x1.jpg exists, is JPEG format, with width == height == 2048 (2048x2048), aspect ratio exactly 1:1K1mandatoryoutput
hero_catalog_banner.jpg is a 2400x1200 wide ~2:1 catalog-banner JPEGautoFile hero_catalog_banner.jpg exists, is JPEG format, with dimensions 2400x1200 (width:height ratio exactly 2:1; accept within 1.9-2.1 if dims differ but width > height)K1mandatoryoutput
shirt_packshot.png is a PNG with a genuine transparent (alpha) backgroundautoFile shirt_packshot.png exists, is PNG format, contains an alpha channel, and background pixels outside the shirt are fully transparent (alpha=0)K1dealbreakeroutput
northgrove_logo.svg is delivered as a valid vector SVG fileautoFile northgrove_logo.svg exists and is a well-formed SVG containing vector path/shape elements (not a single embedded raster bitmap)K1dealbreakeroutput
apparel_set.zip bundles the four deliverables plus a contact-sheet preview (5 items total)autoapparel_set.zip exists and contains exactly hero_square_1x1.jpg, hero_catalog_banner.jpg, shirt_packshot.png, northgrove_logo.svg, plus a contact-sheet/preview image — 5 items totalK1mandatoryoutput
The SVG wordmark reads exactly 'NORTHGROVE' with correct spellingautoOCR/text extraction of northgrove_logo.svg yields the legible wordmark 'NORTHGROVE' (exact spelling, all caps, no misspelling or altered characters)K2dealbreakerinput
The vectorized logo preserves the supplied BRAND_LOGO.png 2-color palette: deep-charcoal letters and an olive-green leaf glyph to the left of the wordexpertnorthgrove_logo.svg renders as a flat 2-color mark with deep-charcoal letters and a small olive-green leaf glyph positioned to the left of the wordmark, matching BRAND_LOGO.png with no recolouring or restyling of the markK2mandatoryinput
The hero subject (model) is cleanly isolated and the cluttered original background is fully replaced by a clean solid-white studio backdropexpertIn both hero deliverables the coat rack with hanging clothes, the tangle of black cables, the laundry basket and the half-open closet from HERO_WORN.jpg are no longer visible; the model sits on an even solid-white backdropK3mandatorybrief
Collar and sleeve edges of the isolated model (hero) and isolated shirt (packshot) are clean with no haloing, fringing, or jagged cutoutexpertOn close inspection the collar and sleeve edges of the model in both hero deliverables, and the collar/sleeve edges of shirt_packshot.png, show clean masking with no leftover background halo, color fringing, or ragged edges (brief: attention to detail on collar and sleeves is essential)K3mandatorybrief
The hero is straightened (the ~4-degree tilt corrected)expertBoth hero deliverables are level/upright with the original ~4deg tilt corrected; vertical lines (coat rack, closet edges where retained, subject posture) read straight, not tiltedK3mandatorybrief
The hero is tone-corrected so it looks professionally lit, not under-exposed and flatexpertBoth hero deliverables show corrected exposure/contrast versus the deliberately dark, flat source and read as professionally lit, not under-exposedK4mandatorybrief
The shirt's olive-green reads true and rich, not muddyexpertThe olive-green of the shirt in both hero deliverables appears rich and true (mixed-light white balance neutralized, green saturation corrected), no longer the muddy in-camera olive of the sourceK4mandatorybrief
The catalog banner shows horizontal breathing room from generative outpaint with the white backdrop preservedexperthero_catalog_banner.jpg shows the cleaned hero extended horizontally with added white studio space on the sides, the solid-white backdrop continuing seamlessly into the expanded region with no visible seam or artifactK3qualityoutput
The packshot olive shirt is the same garment as the hero, keeping the set cohesiveexpertThe isolated flat-lay shirt in shirt_packshot.png is the same olive-green cotton button-down as the shirt worn in the hero, so the product set reads as one cohesive listingK3qualitybrief
The brief's out-of-scope generative asks (add-a-shirt compositing, AI clutter erasure) were honestly dropped, not fakedexpertNo shirt was AI-composited/added onto a person and no clutter was generatively erased/inpainted; background cleanup was achieved by solid-white fill behind the isolated subject, and any such limitation is disclosed honestly rather than passed off as doneK7mandatorybrief

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
