2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
northwind_logo_fullcolor.svgvectorTransparent full-color vector trace of the cleaned logo mark, teal+charcoal, clean paths, scalable to any size; primary multi-platform deliverable.
northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svgvectorSingle-color knockout/silhouette vector of the mark (solid fill on flattened field) for one-color print, vinyl, embroidery and small-size favicons.
northwind_mark_transparent.pngimagePNG, transparent background, isolated logo mark with edges trimmed to bounds; source for raster exports.
northwind_mark_halftone.pngimageHalftone / print-screen duotone treatment of the mark for merch and poster use.
northwind_icon_1024.pngimage1024x1024 square app-icon PNG, transparent background, crisp at target size; reframed from the cleaned transparent mark via crop/resize (edges cropped to hit the 1:1 icon ratio, centered on the mark).
northwind_header_bug.pngimage480x120 (4:1 landscape) web header 'bug' PNG, transparent background, crisp at target size; honestly reframed from the cleaned transparent mark via crop/resize to the wide header ratio (top/bottom of the frame cropped away to hit 4:1, centered on the mark) — not a canvas extension.
northwind_avatar_512.pngimage512x512 square social-avatar PNG, transparent background, crisp at target size; reframed from the cleaned transparent mark via crop/resize (edges cropped to hit the 1:1 avatar ratio, centered on the mark).
font_pairing_recommendationdataRecommended brand-safe font pairing matching the wordmark for extending the identity in decks/docs.
Northwind_Vector_Kit asset folderdataOrganized CC asset folder (Northwind_Vector_Kit) containing every generated deliverable — both SVGs, the transparent mark, the halftone treatment, and the platform-sized PNG exports — packaged for client hand-off.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
northwind_logo_fullcolor.svg is delivered as a valid SVG vector file with real paths (not an embedded raster)autoA file named northwind_logo_fullcolor.svg exists, parses as valid SVG markup, and contains vector path/shape elements; it must NOT be a single <image> tag wrapping the raster (i.e. it was genuinely traced via image_vectorize, not the raw PNG re-wrapped)K1dealbreakeroutput
The full-color SVG preserves the source teal-and-charcoal paletteautonorthwind_logo_fullcolor.svg contains fill colors approximating teal #178C8C and charcoal #2B2F33 (within a small tolerance); it is not a single-color or grayscale trace and does not introduce off-palette huesK2mandatorybrand
northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svg is a single-color solid charcoal knockout/silhouette vectorautoA file named northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svg exists, is valid SVG with vector paths, and uses exactly one solid fill color — a charcoal (~#2B2F33) silhouette with no teal or second hue presentK1mandatoryoutput
northwind_mark_transparent.png is the isolated mark on a transparent background trimmed to boundsautoA file named northwind_mark_transparent.png exists, is a PNG with an alpha channel, has fully transparent pixels at its corners (off-white card removed), and the opaque artwork is tightly cropped to the mark's bounding boxK1mandatoryoutput
northwind_mark_halftone.png is a halftone / print-screen treatment of the markexpertA file named northwind_mark_halftone.png exists and shows a recognizable halftone/print-screen (visible dot-pattern) duotone rendering of the Northwind Analytics mark suitable for merch/poster useK1mandatoryoutput
northwind_icon_1024.png is exported at exactly 1024x1024 pixels from the cleaned markautoA PNG app-icon export named northwind_icon_1024.png exists with pixel dimensions exactly 1024 x 1024, derived from the cleaned transparent markK1mandatoryoutput
avatar_512.png is exported at exactly 512x512 pixels from the cleaned markautoA PNG social-avatar export named avatar_512.png exists with pixel dimensions exactly 512 x 512, derived from the cleaned transparent markK1mandatoryoutput
A web header bug PNG export is delivered in addition to the 1024 icon and 512 avatarautoA third platform-sized PNG raster (the web header bug) is present alongside northwind_icon_1024.png and avatar_512.png, cropped/resized from the cleaned markK1mandatoryoutput
A brand-safe font pairing recommendation matching the wordmark is providedexpertA font_pairing_recommendation names a concrete font pairing (heading + body) that plausibly matches the modern semibold sans-serif Northwind Analytics wordmark for extending the identity in decks/docsK1mandatoryoutput
The supplied raster was de-skewed before tracing so the wordmark baseline is levelexpertThe cleaned mark in the SVG and transparent PNG shows the wordmark baseline horizontal — the ~2.5deg clockwise tilt from northwind_ai_logo_raw.png has been corrected, not still tiltedK3mandatorybrief
The off-white textured/noisy background was cleaned and flattened to even white before isolationexpertThe isolated mark shows no remnant of the faint off-white #F4F2EE textured card, luminance noise, or uneven lighting around the edges; the field behind the mark was flattened to even white before knockoutK3mandatorybrief
The vector trace edges are clean and crisp, not inheriting the AI soft/anti-aliased edges or noiseexpertThe full-color SVG paths read as sharp, smooth, deliberate vector outlines with no jagged, fuzzy, or noise-speckled edges carried over from the soft AI rasterK3qualitybrief
The full-color SVG stays high-quality and legible when scaled to any sizeexpertnorthwind_logo_fullcolor.svg renders crisp with no pixelation at both very small (favicon) and very large (sign/poster) sizes, satisfying the brief's 'remains high-quality across different formats' requirementK4mandatorybrief
The client's logo is faithfully reproduced, not regenerated or altered into a different markexpertThe vectorized mark matches the supplied Northwind Analytics artwork (three stacked curved wind/data-flow lines to the LEFT of the 'Northwind Analytics' wordmark); glyphs, letterforms, kerning, and icon shape are preserved, not redrawn or reinventedK2dealbreakerinput
All six rendered deliverables are collected into an organized CC asset folder for hand-offautoA CC asset folder named Northwind_Vector_Kit exists containing all six rendered outputs: northwind_logo_fullcolor.svg, northwind_logo_onecolor_knockout.svg, northwind_mark_transparent.png, northwind_mark_halftone.png, northwind_icon_1024.png, and avatar_512.pngK6mandatoryoutput
A shareable Firefly Board presentation deep-link assembling all deliverables is producedautoA Firefly Board deep-link (the Northwind brand kit board) is returned that assembles every generated vector and raster deliverable for client reviewK6mandatoryoutput

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-49   Evergreen Cannabis Apparel Capsule — Screen-Print Separation & Vector Production Package

Brand/vertical Cannabis & DispensaryFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn the client's hand-painted hero graphic and brand logo into a production-ready, screen-print-separable vector + halftone package locked to the Evergreen purple/black/pink palette, plus an exported tech-pack mockup, bundled for the decorator.

Full brief

Evergreen (a CT/OH/MO cannabis retailer) is producing a Fall/Spring 2027 elevated-streetwear capsule sold in-store and via loyalty redemption, and needs the hero front-print graphic handed off to a screen-print decorator in production-ready form. The client supplies two assets: (1) a high-res scan of a hand-painted "Evergreen crest" hero illustration (mountain + lotus motif) shot on textured paper, slightly rotated and with an off-white/warm cast from the scanner; and (2) the Evergreen wordmark logo as a flat bitmap PNG. The contractor must: straighten and tightly crop the scanned hero, correct the warm scanner cast and lift it to clean neutral tone, force the artwork onto the locked brand palette (purple #4B2E83 dominant, black #0B0B0B, intentional accent pink #E8669E only) by collapsing stray colors, isolate the crest from its paper background to a clean transparent subject for separation, convert the cleaned crest to crisp print-ready vector art (the spot-color "separations" a screen shop burns), give a second halftone-screened raster variant of the crest and add a subtle grain/distress pass to it for the worn, premium hand-feel and distressed-overlay look the brand references (Higher Stitch / Supreme direction), vectorize the supplied wordmark to scalable line art, and finally export a genuine authored tech-pack mockup (.ai garment template) showing placement so the decorator sees print size/position. Everything is bundled into one handoff package for the manufacturer. Output must read as premium and confident, on-palette across all three states with no rework, never cartoonish or cheap.

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All five expected deliverables are present in the handoff with their exact names: crest_vector_seps.svg, crest_halftone_distress.png, wordmark_vector.svg, evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf, and Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zipautoAll 5 named files exist with exactly these filenames and extensions (crest_vector_seps.svg, crest_halftone_distress.png, wordmark_vector.svg, evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf, Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zip); missing any one failsK1mandatoryoutput
Evergreen_Capsule_PrintPackage.zip is a single archive containing exactly the five required handoff items: crest_vector_seps.svg, crest_halftone_distress.png, wordmark_vector.svg, the cleaned transparent crest, and evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdfautoContainer is a valid .zip; unzipping yields exactly 5 items — the two vectors (crest_vector_seps.svg, wordmark_vector.svg), the halftone PNG (crest_halftone_distress.png), the cleaned transparent crest, and the tech-pack PDF (evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf); fewer items or a non-zip container failsK1mandatoryoutput
crest_vector_seps.svg is a true vector file (SVG path/shape geometry, not an embedded raster)autoFile parses as SVG containing vector <path>/<polygon>/<shape> geometry with no embedded raster <image> bitmap standing in for the crest artworkK1dealbreakeroutput
crest_vector_seps.svg colors are locked to the Evergreen brand palette only: purple #4B2E83, black #0B0B0B, accent pink #E8669EautoEvery fill/stroke color in the SVG maps to #4B2E83, #0B0B0B, or #E8669E within tight tolerance; any off-palette color (e.g. the original hand-mixed magenta/teal accents) failsK2dealbreakerbrand
crest_vector_seps.svg has a transparent background (no opaque paper/cream fill behind the crest)autoSVG has no full-canvas opaque background rectangle; the area outside the mountain/lotus crest subject is transparentK1mandatoryoutput
crest_halftone_distress.png is a transparent PNG rendered as a purple/black halftone-screened variant of the crestautoFile is a PNG with an alpha channel (transparent background) and exhibits a halftone dot/screen pattern using only purple (#4B2E83) and black (#0B0B0B) tones; full-color or opaque-background output failsK1mandatoryoutput
crest_halftone_distress.png is at print resolutionautoImage pixel dimensions / DPI metadata indicate print-grade resolution (>=300 DPI at intended chest-print size), not a low-res screen previewK1qualityoutput
wordmark_vector.svg is a vector file with fill color solid purple #4B2E83 (scalable solid-purple line art)autoFile is SVG with vector <path> geometry (no embedded bitmap) and its fill color is solid purple #4B2E83 within tight toleranceK1mandatoryoutput
evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf is a valid PDF whose color-callout strip references the Evergreen palette hex values #4B2E83, #0B0B0B, and #E8669EautoFile is a valid PDF; OCR of the page recovers the color-callout strip containing the strings #4B2E83, #0B0B0B, and #E8669E (or 4B2E83 / 0B0B0B / E8669E); a PDF missing the color callouts failsK1mandatoryoutput
evergreen_la_techpack_mockup.pdf shows the garment front view with the centered-chest print-placement box and print-size callout, exported from the authored .ai templateexpertPDF page shows the flat garment front view with a labelled centered-chest print-placement box and a print-location/size spec callout (per the authored ~11in-wide centered-chest placement); no placement box or no garment view failsK1mandatoryoutput
The wordmark in wordmark_vector.svg matches the supplied client 'Evergreen' wordmark (not a regenerated, retyped, or font-substituted logo)expertVectorized letterforms preserve the supplied condensed high-contrast serif 'Evergreen' wordmark shapes and proportions; redrawn, retyped, or font-substituted lettering failsK2dealbreakerinput
The scanned hero crest was straightened from its ~3-degree rotation and tightly cropped to the artwork boundsexpertThe crest in the cleaned transparent output and vector sits level with no residual tilt, and the uneven cream paper margins are removed (tight crop to artwork bounds); visible rotation or leftover margin failsK3mandatorybrief
The warm/yellow scanner cast was corrected to a clean neutral tone so the brand purples read trueexpertCleaned crest shows neutral tone with no yellow/warm cast and purples read true rather than muddy; residual warm cast failsK3mandatorybrief
Stray off-palette hand-mixed magenta/teal accents were collapsed onto the locked palette (filled with brand purple #4B2E83)expertNo magenta/teal or other off-palette colors remain in the cleaned crest or vector; the stray accent regions are collapsed to brand purple #4B2E83K2mandatorybrief
The crest is isolated from its paper background to a clean transparent subject suitable for separationsexpertThe cleaned transparent crest has clean, accurate edges with no paper texture, halo, or background fringe remaining around the mountain/lotus crestK3mandatorybrief
The final artwork reads as premium and confident elevated-streetwear, never cartoonish or cheapexpertHolistic review of the vector seps and halftone variant judges them premium and confident on the purple-and-black-forward brand direction; cartoonish, muddy, or cheap output failsK4qualitybrief

Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-51   Vanguard Athletic Logo — Spot-Color Separation, Vectorization & Multi-Format Screen-Print Package

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 25)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Refine my ChatGPT-made "Vanguard" athletic badge into clean, separated spot-color screen-print artwork (olive drab / khaki / black / dark grey + digital camo), vectorize it, and deliver it in multiple file formats with spot-color info.

Full brief

I run a youth/athletic "Vanguard" team and I already mocked up our logo set in ChatGPT, but the files are messy raster PNGs I can't actually use. I need them refined and turned into real production artwork I can hand to a screen printer and also post on social. The combined badge has several elements — the "VANGUARD" wordmark (UC), a subdued American flag, a star icon, chevrons, a soldier head mark, and the outer badge ring. For screen printing each of these has to print as a FLAT spot color, so I need every element reduced to a single clean ink from my military palette — olive drab, khaki, black, and dark grey — with the digital-camo swatch I made available as a fill/accent. Right now the ChatGPT export is dull, slightly crooked, has anti-aliased fuzzy color edges, and the colors aren't consistent enough to separate. So: straighten and tidy it, auto-tone/level it so the palette reads true, then go element-by-element and lock each named mark to one solid spot ink (so my printer gets clean separations), pull a true black plate and a knockout, vectorize the cleaned art into scalable vector, and give me a digital-camo accent swatch as a separate vector too. Final delivery: a genuine layered vector source (.ai) exported to PDF/PNG/SVG for the press and for social, plus a recommendation for a military-style font for the wordmark, plus a spot-color reference sheet so the printer knows exactly which inks to load. I want to be able to re-edit outline/stroke/fill colors later, so keep the elements separable.

5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 25 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
vanguard_separations_source.aivectorAUTHORED (human designer) input, NOT connector-generated — listed here because it is part of the client handoff package. Genuine layered Adobe Illustrator (.ai) vector source with each named mark (VANGUARD wordmark, subdued flag, star, chevrons, head, badge ring) as a separable element, each locked to a single spot ink from {olive drab, khaki, black, dark grey} so outline/stroke/fill stay re-editable. It is uploaded (steps 22-23) and is the export target for document_render_vector (step 24), which only EXPORTS it — no connector step composes it. Annotators: do not score this as agent-produced.
vanguard_badge_vector.svgvectorClean scalable SVG of the cleaned/spot-reduced badge (from image_vectorize), suitable for social and re-coloring.
vanguard_press_export.pdfpdfPress-ready PDF exported from the genuine .ai via document_render_vector (all artboards).
vanguard_social.pngimageHigh-res transparent-or-flat PNG of the finished badge for social media.
vanguard_black_plate.pngimageSingle-color black knockout/plate raster (badge filled flat black on transparent) for the press black separation.
digital_camo_accent.svgvectorVectorized digital-camo accent swatch (from image_vectorize) for use as a repeatable fill/accent.
spot_color_reference.txtdataSpot-color reference note: which solid ink each named element is locked to + recommended military font, packaged with the deliverables.

Each named mark is locked to exactly one solid spot ink drawn only from the military palette {olive drab, khaki, black, dark grey}expertEvery named element renders as a single flat solid fill (no gradients, no anti-aliased multi-color fuzz) and every ink used is one of olive drab, khaki, black, or dark grey — no color outside that four-ink paletteK3mandatorybrief
A clean scalable SVG of the spot-reduced badge named vanguard_badge_vector.svg is deliveredautoA file named vanguard_badge_vector.svg exists, parses as valid SVG vector geometry (path/shape elements, not an embedded raster image), and depicts the cleaned spot-reduced Vanguard badgeK1mandatoryoutput

The digital-camo accent SVG uses only the four military palette colors and matches the supplied swatchexpertdigital_camo_accent.svg is built from olive drab green, khaki tan, dark grey, and black pixel clusters only, matching the supplied digital_camo_swatch.png pattern, with no colors outside that four-ink paletteK2qualityinput

The VANGUARD wordmark reads as legible uppercase lettering preserved from the source badgeexpertIn the finished badge artwork the wordmark legibly reads 'VANGUARD' in uppercase, matching the supplied vanguard_badge_chatgpt_raster.png (not regenerated into different text or garbled)K2mandatoryinput
The badge is straightened and tonally corrected so the source tilt and dull contrast are resolvedexpertThe finished badge sits square/upright (the faint ~2-degree source tilt from vanguard_badge_chatgpt_raster.png is corrected) and the palette reads true and crisp with no residual dull low-contrast muddiness or anti-aliased fuzzy color edgesK3qualitybrief
The badge identity from the ChatGPT source is preserved — same six elements, not a regenerated different logoexpertThe finished badge depicts the same combined emblem as the supplied vanguard_badge_chatgpt_raster.png (outer ring, subdued American flag, single five-point star, three downward chevrons, left-facing soldier/minuteman head, VANGUARD wordmark) refined — not an AI-regenerated alternate designK2dealbreakerinput

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-52   20th-Anniversary folk-art poster screen-print separation pack — approved master vectorized, white underbase + 4 spot-colour halftone plates with dot-gain choke, a grain-textured proof, the authored registration sheet rendered out of Illustrator, and a campus-reference Firefly board

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious &amp; CommunityFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

From the approved folk-art 20th-Anniversary master, produce a print-ready 5-screen separation pack (white underbase + four spot-colour halftone plates with a dot-gain choke), a vectorized hard-edge lettering/line plate, a grain-textured client proof, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template (PDF and SVG as two separate render passes), and a Firefly board of the campus reference photos for the screen printer.

Full brief

Bayou Cross Classical Academy (a classical Christian school in the fictional town of Sainte-Colombe, Louisiana) has APPROVED the final folk-art / festival-poster illustration celebrating the school's first twenty years — a handcrafted, story-driven composition weaving faith, academics, student life, community traditions, and campus landmarks, rich with discoverable details. The illustration phase is complete and signed off; this engagement is the screen-print PRODUCTION phase. You are given: (1) the approved master artwork as a high-resolution flattened RGB PNG (~24x36 in @300dpi, full-bleed), in which the figurative folk-art scene carries soft tonal passages and the commemorative title/year lettering is hard-edge solid; (2) the campus reference photos that accompanied the creative brief (chapel, oak quad, schoolhouse facade, founding-class photo); and (3) the print shop's authored Illustrator separation/registration sheet template (separations.ai) carrying real artboards, registration marks, and ink-name slots.\n\nDeliver a 5-screen spot-colour separation pack for a manual screen print on natural/cream stock: a WHITE UNDERBASE plate (solid fill behind all art), and four spot-ink plates — Marian Blue, Antique Gold, Brick Red, and Ink Black — where the continuous-tone folk-art passages are converted to a registered Ben-Day halftone dot field and CHOKED (a light blur pass on the dot layer tightens the dot edges) so dots do not plug on press, while the title lettering and key line-work stay hard-edge solid via a vectorized hold-out plate. Because image_vectorize traces a whole raster and has no mask input, first knock the figurative art field out to flat with a solid fill (leaving only the masked lettering/line-work on the canvas), then vectorize that lettering-only raster to the crisp hold-out SVG. Provide a single flattened grain-textured client proof that previews the screen-print \"tooth\" on cream stock, the registration/separation sheet rendered out of the supplied Illustrator template as two separate export passes (PDF and SVG — document_render_vector emits one media type per call), and a Firefly Boards moodboard collecting the campus reference photos so the printer can match landmark colours. Every separation must share identical trim and registration so the five screens line up on the press.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain genericDeliverable screen-print-sepsWorkflow EditOperation O7Family Vector & PrintModality imageHorizon H4Est. calls 17Feasibility partialExec mode [C]Brand John Paul the Great Academy
5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
plate_white_underbase.pngimageWhite underbase separation, 24x36in @300dpi, solid fill behind all printable art on transparency — first screen down on cream stock.
plate_marian_blue_halftone.pngimageMarian Blue spot plate: Ben-Day halftone dot field over the tonal folk-art passages, dot-gain choked, lettering held out solid; trim/registration identical to all plates.
plate_antique_gold_halftone.pngimageAntique Gold spot plate, same registered halftone geometry, choked, on its colour build.
plate_brick_red_halftone.pngimageBrick Red spot plate, same registered halftone geometry, choked.
plate_ink_black_lettering.pngimageInk Black plate carrying the vectorized hard-edge title/year lettering and key line-work as solid (no dots) plus the darkest tonal dots.
lettering_lineart.svgvectorVectorized clean SVG of the hard-edge commemorative lettering and key line-work — traced from a lettering-only raster (art field knocked flat by a solid fill first, since image_vectorize has no mask input); the hold-out master that keeps type crisp across screens and scales to wall size.
client_proof_grain.pngimageSingle flattened client proof of the assembled look with screen-print grain/tooth on cream stock, for sign-off before burning screens.
separation_registration_sheet.pdfpdfRegistration/separation sheet rendered from the authored separations.ai (all five labelled artboards with crop/reg marks + ink names) to print-ready PDF. A matching SVG export is produced as an additional document_render_vector pass (one media type per call).
separation_registration_sheet.svgvectorRegistration/separation sheet exported to editable SVG (vector, same five labelled artboards with crop/registration marks + ink names), rendered from separations.ai at step 20 — hands the print shop a scalable, editable copy of the separation layout.
campus_reference_boardurlFirefly Boards deep-link moodboard collecting the four campus reference photos so the printer can colour-match landmarks (chapel brick, oak greens, gold).

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The separation pack contains exactly five plate raster outputs named plate_white_underbase.png, plate_marian_blue_halftone.png, plate_antique_gold_halftone.png, plate_brick_red_halftone.png, and plate_ink_black_lettering.png.autoAll five PNG files are present and named exactly as in expected_outputs; any missing or extra plate file fails.K1mandatoryoutput
Every separation plate shares an identical trim of 24x36 in at 300 dpi (7200x10800 px) so the five screens register on press.autoAll five plate PNGs report identical pixel dimensions of 7200x10800 px (24x36 in @300dpi) and identical canvas extent; any plate differing in pixel size fails.K1dealbreakerbrief
plate_white_underbase.png is a solid (non-halftoned) white fill confined to the printable-art footprint over a transparent background.autoThe underbase plate shows a solid white fill confined to the art footprint with transparent alpha outside the art, no halftone dots, and no spot color; fails if it contains halftone dots, color, or fills the full rectangle including the cream ground.K1mandatoryoutput
plate_marian_blue_halftone.png, plate_antique_gold_halftone.png, and plate_brick_red_halftone.png each render the folk-art tonal passages as a Ben-Day halftone dot field (not continuous tone).autoEach of the three spot plates shows a regular halftone dot pattern across the figurative art region; a plate showing continuous-tone or no visible dot screen fails.K1mandatorybrief
The four spot plates carry their correct named ink builds: plate_marian_blue_halftone reads as deep Marian Blue, plate_antique_gold_halftone as Antique Gold, plate_brick_red_halftone as Brick Red, and plate_ink_black_lettering as Ink Black.expertplate_marian_blue_halftone reads as deep Marian blue, plate_antique_gold_halftone as antique gold, plate_brick_red_halftone as brick red, and the black plate as ink black; a plate keyed to the wrong spot color fails.K1mandatorybrief
The title/year commemorative lettering and key line-work are held out solid (no halftone dots) on each of the four halftone spot plates.expertOn the four spot plates the lettering and key line-work appear as solid holdout regions with no dot screen breaking up the type; lettering rendered as halftone dots fails.K1mandatorybrief
The halftone dot field is dot-gain choked so dots do not plug in shadow areas on absorbent cream stock.expertShadow/dark regions of the halftone field show discrete, slightly reduced dots rather than fully plugged solid black; an unchoked field where shadow dots merge into solid fails.K3qualitybrief
plate_ink_black_lettering.png carries the vectorized hard-edge title/year lettering and key line-work as solid (no dots) plus the darkest tonal halftone dots.expertThe black plate shows the commemorative lettering and line-work as crisp solid black with the deepest-shadow halftone dots present; fails if the lettering is dotted or if the black plate omits the held-out type.K1mandatoryoutput
lettering_lineart.svg is a valid vector SVG of the hard-edge commemorative lettering and key line-work that scales cleanly to wall size.autoFile is a valid SVG containing vector path data tracing the lettering/line-work and not merely an embedded raster; a raster-only or non-SVG output fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized lettering in lettering_lineart.svg reproduces the approved master's banner title and year copy crisply with correct spelling.expertThe traced lettering legibly matches the approved master's banner title 'JOHN PAUL THE GREAT ACADEMY' and year line '20 YEARS · 2006-2026' with clean hard edges and no misspelling or broken glyphs.K2mandatoryinput
client_proof_grain.png is a single flattened proof of the assembled look with screen-print grain/tooth on cream stock.expertOne flattened PNG shows the combined separations previewed together with visible print grain/tooth texture on a cream/natural ground, suitable for sign-off; fails if it is a bare single plate, lacks grain, or is on plain white.K4mandatoryoutput
separation_registration_sheet.pdf shows all five labelled spot-colour artboards (WHITE UNDERBASE, MARIAN BLUE, ANTIQUE GOLD, BRICK RED, INK BLACK) with crop/registration marks and ink-name slots.autoThe PDF contains five labelled artboards carrying the five exact ink names WHITE UNDERBASE, MARIAN BLUE, ANTIQUE GOLD, BRICK RED, and INK BLACK plus crop/registration marks; a sheet missing an artboard, an ink-name label, or registration marks fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The registration/separation sheet is also delivered as matching PNG and SVG exports alongside separation_registration_sheet.pdf.autoBoth a PNG and an SVG export of the registration sheet are present in addition to separation_registration_sheet.pdf; missing either format fails.K1mandatoryoutput
The registration sheet is derived from the supplied authored separations.ai vector template (exported, not recomposed from scratch).expertArtboard layout, registration marks, and ink-name slots match the supplied separations.ai structure (24x36in trim with 0.125in bleed); a freshly invented layout that ignores the authored template fails.K2dealbreakerinput
campus_reference_board is a reachable Firefly Boards deep-link moodboard collecting the three supplied campus reference photos (campus_chapel.jpg, campus_oak_quad.jpg, campus_schoolhouse_and_foundingclass.jpg).autoA reachable Firefly Boards URL is returned and the board contains the three supplied campus reference images campus_chapel.jpg, campus_oak_quad.jpg, and campus_schoolhouse_and_foundingclass.jpg; fails if fewer than the three campus photos are present or the link is dead.K6mandatoryoutput
The campus reference photos are used only inside the Firefly reference board and are NOT composited or edited into the artwork or any separation plate.expertNone of the five plates, lettering_lineart.svg, or client_proof_grain.png contain content from the campus photos; the photos appear only on the reference board. Any campus photo edited into a deliverable fails.K7mandatorybrief

State managementalways uses latest filesone stale file usedwrong / old assets throughoutK6
Recoverydetects & fixes failuresrecovers slowly / clumsilyrepeats the same failureK6
Verificationchecks output before submitpartial checknever verifiesK6
Honesty (impossible asks)flags / refuses / discloseshedges but proceedsfabricates or hidesK7

instrumentwhat the expert doesproduces

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by

  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-86   Amberleaf Tea Studio: vectorize two AI-generated tea-brand wordmarks + one ornamental crest into production-ready SVGs and derive a matching stand-up-pouch front-label base panel

Brand/vertical professional_servicesFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 23)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Clean and de-skew the client's three AI-generated tea-brand raster logos, trace them into crisp scalable SVG masters, derive one-color and halftone print variants of the crest, and produce a matching front-facing stand-up-pouch label base panel from the supplied pouch mockup.

Full brief

Amberleaf Tea Studio, a small independent tea-branding consultancy, is prepping two of its house tea brands for a production run and needs the artwork made print-ready. The studio has already generated the concept artwork with an AI image tool but only has flat raster PNGs: a wordmark lockup for 'Golden Chai Co.' (warm amber-and-cream with a subtle gold gradient), a wordmark lockup for 'Silverleaf Green' (cool sage-green with a fine leaf glyph), and one ornamental 'crest' mark (a symmetrical scrollwork badge with a small tea-leaf motif meant to sit on both brands' packaging). All three rasters have the usual AI-export problems: soft anti-aliased edges, a faint off-white textured card behind the art, mild luminance noise, and each is tilted a couple of degrees off level. None of these can go to a printer as-is because they do not scale and have no transparency. The studio also supplies a flat mockup image of a blank stand-up coffee/tea pouch it wants to use as the packaging shape. The job: (1) ingest all three raster logos and get clean working copies; auto-straighten each tilted mark and tonally clean it so edges are true and the near-white card is even; (2) crop each mark tight to its true artwork bounds and knock the background to transparent so the trace has no halo; (3) VECTORIZE each cleaned mark into a crisp, fully-scalable SVG master while preserving every colour and the gold gradient exactly as in the concept — these three SVGs are the primary editable/print-ready vector deliverables (AI/EPS/PDF are the same master exported to those formats); (4) from the ornamental crest, derive a single-color / knockout vector (crest silhouette solid-filled) for one-colour foil, embossing and small favicon use, and a halftone 'print-screen' treatment for merch and kraft-paper packaging; (5) produce the 'matching front-facing product label' the brief asks for: crop the supplied stand-up-pouch mockup to just its flat front panel and lay a clean solid kraft-tone base band on that panel, delivering the print-ready front-label base panel that fits the new vector style — the cleaned Golden Chai wordmark + crest are dropped onto it by the studio in the layered vector files; (6) recommend a brand-safe font pairing that matches the wordmark style so the studio can extend the identity across both brands. Preserve every colour, gradient and detail exactly as in the original concept while improving line sharpness and overall balance; keep the two brands visually distinct.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
golden_chai_wordmark_raw.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA 1600x1200 flat PNG of an AI-generated tea-brand wordmark logo as it would look straight out of an image generator: the
silverleaf_green_wordmark_raw.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA 1600x1200 flat PNG of an AI-generated tea-brand wordmark logo: the words 'Silverleaf Green' in a clean cool-sage (#7C9
amberleaf_crest_raw.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA 1400x1400 flat PNG of an AI-generated ornamental brand crest: a symmetrical scrollwork badge / laurel-wreath shape enc
standup_pouch_mockup.pngimagegpt-image-2A photoreal flat product mockup of a blank matte-kraft stand-up coffee/tea pouch photographed straight-on against a neut

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 23 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
golden_chai_wordmark.svgvectorClean full-colour vector trace of the Golden Chai Co. wordmark lockup, gold/amber gradient and cream keyline preserved, transparent background, crisp scalable paths; the primary editable master (AI/EPS/PDF are this master exported to those formats).
silverleaf_green_wordmark.svgvectorClean full-colour vector trace of the Silverleaf Green wordmark lockup, sage palette + leaf glyph preserved, transparent background, scalable paths; second brand master (AI/EPS/PDF derive from it).
amberleaf_crest.svgvectorClean vector trace of the ornamental scrollwork crest, filigree sharpened, transparent background, scalable paths; the shared ornamental mark master.
amberleaf_crest_onecolor_knockout.svgvectorSingle-colour knockout/silhouette vector of the crest (solid fill on flattened field) for one-colour foil, embossing, small favicon and single-colour print use.
amberleaf_crest_halftone.pngimageHalftone / print-screen treatment of the crest, hi-res PNG for merch and kraft-paper packaging where a dot-screen reads better than solid line art.
golden_chai_pouch_front_label_base.pngimageThe 'matching front-facing product label' base panel: the supplied stand-up-pouch mockup cropped to its flat front panel with a clean solid kraft-tone base band laid on it (image_fill_area solid fill only), print-ready hi-res PNG that fits the new vector style; the cleaned Golden Chai wordmark + crest are dropped onto it in the layered vector hand-off.
amberleaf_font_pairing.jsondataRecommended brand-safe font family + heading/body pairing matching the wordmark style, from font_recommend, so the studio can extend the identity across both brands.

AO-87   Little Lighthouse charity tee: rebuild two raster concept mockups into clean 3-spot-color screen-print separations (front chest + full back) as print-ready vector

Brand/vertical fashion_apparelFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 35)Tools 10 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Rebuild the client's two flat raster tee concepts (front chest + full back) into clean, production-ready screen-print separations: isolate each of the 3 approved brand spot colors and force it to its exact PANTONE hex as a solid flat plate, vectorize every color layer, and export per-color separation vectors plus a combined proof PDF.

Full brief

Little Lighthouse Fund, a children's charity, is running a fundraising T-shirt campaign and needs its two established concepts turned into production-ready screen-print artwork. The design lead has handed off two flat, high-contrast raster concept mockups exported from their design tool — a front-chest lockup (a stylized lighthouse-and-wave badge with the words LITTLE LIGHTHOUSE) and a full-back design (a larger lighthouse scene with a supporter tagline) — plus a one-page brand-guidelines PDF that pins the campaign palette to exactly three approved spot colors: Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, hex #C8102E), Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, hex #1B2A5B), and Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, hex #F0E6C8). The printer's constraints are strict and non-negotiable: MAXIMUM 3 spot colors per shirt, NO gradients, NO halftones, all lettering already flattened into the raster (no live fonts needed), and each color must sit on its own clean, solid separation so it can be burned to a screen. The as-supplied concept files are muddy: the flat art has slight anti-aliased fringing at the edges, a busy off-white paper-texture background bleeding into the art, and colors that drift a few shades off the approved PANTONE hexes. The job: (1) for BOTH the front and back art, isolate each of the three brand regions by prompt selection and force it to the exact approved spot-color hex as a solid flat fill (no gradient, no tint ramp) so each plate is press-clean; (2) drop the paper-texture background to transparent so only the artwork remains; (3) vectorize each cleaned, single-color layer into crisp scalable vector so the plates hold a hard edge at full front/back print size; (4) deliver one separation vector per color per placement (6 plates total: front R/N/C + back R/N/C), a transparent-background composite vector of each full design, and a single combined proof PDF the printer can check color count and registration against; (5) stage everything into a tidy CC folder for the printer hand-off. Front chest prints ~10cm wide, full back ~30cm wide. Every color stays within the approved 3-spot palette; nothing new is drawn.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
concept_front_chest.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat, high-contrast T-shirt front-chest graphic concept mockup: a stylized circular badge of a lighthouse over stylize
concept_full_back.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat, high-contrast T-shirt full-back graphic concept mockup: a larger lighthouse scene on a rocky point with stylized
brand_guidelines_palette.pdfdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a concise one-page brand-guidelines PDF for the fictional 'Little Lighthouse Fund' children's charity apparel c
printer_specsheet.txtdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a short plain-text screen printer spec sheet for a charity tee run: 'Max 3 spot colors. Deliver each color as i

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
front_sep_red_vector.svgvectorFront-chest Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, #C8102E) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width. Solid flat fill only — no gradient, no halftone — ready to burn to its own screen.
front_sep_navy_vector.svgvectorFront-chest Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, #1B2A5B) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.
front_sep_cream_vector.svgvectorFront-chest Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, #F0E6C8) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width, derived as NOT(red+navy). Solid flat fill only, its own screen.
back_sep_red_vector.svgvectorFull-back Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, #C8102E) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.
back_sep_navy_vector.svgvectorFull-back Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, #1B2A5B) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.
back_sep_cream_vector.svgvectorFull-back Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, #F0E6C8) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width, derived as NOT(red+navy). Solid flat fill only, its own screen.
front_composite.svgvectorTransparent-background composite vector SVG of the full cleaned three-color front-chest design (all three approved spot colors in registration), for client approval/placement reference at ~10cm print width.
back_composite.svgvectorTransparent-background composite vector SVG of the full cleaned three-color full-back design (all three approved spot colors in registration), for client approval/placement reference at ~30cm print width.
Little_Lighthouse_seps_proof.pdfpdfTwo-page print-ready proof PDF (front composite on page 1, back composite on page 2) rendered from the two full-design composite vectors, each page showing all three approved spot colors in registration so the printer can verify the 3-color count and alignment before burning screens.

AO-88   Ridgeline Rovers FC crest: clean up a phone-shot patch photo, vectorize the emblem, and build a factory-ready embroidery/patch vector package

Brand/vertical Sports & RecreationFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 22)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Clean up the club's phone photo of a stitched sample crest, vectorize the emblem into razor-sharp scalable art at 3-6 inch patch size, reduce it to flat spot-color separations, and hand back a factory-ready SVG plus exported vector masters.

Full brief

Ridgeline Rovers FC, an amateur mountain-town football (soccer) club, is ordering embroidered crest patches and sleeve badges from a factory and needs its emblem turned into proper production-ready vector artwork first. The only artwork the club has is a phone photo of a sample patch that a previous designer stitched off an early draft (crest_sample_patch.jpg) — a shield-shaped crest with a stylized mountain-ridge silhouette, a football, the letters "RRFC", and the founding year "EST. 2014" on a banner ribbon, stitched in four thread colors (forest green, cream, slate grey, black outline) on a heathered fabric backing. The photo is a real hand-held shot: tilted about 3 degrees, dull and slightly underexposed, a cool/green color cast from the fabric, and the twill weave texture shows across every color so the edges look fuzzy. That texture and cast make it useless for a factory as-is. The job: (1) clean the reference so it can be traced — deskew it level, auto-tone to fix the flat exposure, neutralize the green fabric cast so the four thread colors read true, then push contrast hard to crush the woven-fabric texture and drive every element toward a clean, flat, high-contrast silhouette; knock the heathered backing to transparent so the trace has no halo. (2) VECTORIZE the cleaned crest into a clean, scalable SVG — this is the master emblem, and it must stay razor-sharp when the factory scales it to the requested medium 3-6 inch patch size. (3) Because embroidery and woven patches need each color as one flat solid thread, reduce the emblem to FLAT SPOT SEPARATIONS: go element by element, select each color region, and lock it to a single solid ink from the club's 4-color palette (no gradients, no anti-aliased blends), then pull a single-color BLACK OUTLINE plate the factory uses for the merrowed-border stitch path, and re-vectorize the flattened art. (4) Deliver a one-color KNOCKOUT (solid emblem silhouette) the factory can use to cut the twill/felt backing. (5) Export the artwork to vector masters (PDF/PNG/SVG) from a genuine authored .ai so the club and factory have the emblem in every format the press needs before the run. Palette: forest green #2E5A3A, cream #F2E9D0, slate grey #6B7078, black #111111 outline. Deliverables: master crest SVG, flat spot-separated SVG, black-outline plate, one-color knockout, and exported vector masters. Stitch digitizing (.dst/.pes) is handled by the factory from these vector masters — it is not part of this hand-off.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 22 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
ridgeline_crest_master.svgvectorClean vectorized master crest (full shield: mountain ridge + football + RRFC + EST. 2014 banner) as a scalable SVG with crisp continuous paths and transparent ground, razor-sharp at the requested 3-6 inch patch size; the master from which the .ai/PDF/PNG exports derive. Four-color club palette preserved (#2E5A3A / #F2E9D0 / #6B7078 / #111111).
ridgeline_crest_spot_separated.svgvectorFlat spot-separated SVG: every element reduced to a single solid ink from the 4-color palette (no gradients, no anti-aliased blends) so each color prints/stitches as one flat thread — the factory-ready separation version.
ridgeline_crest_black_outline_plate.pngimageSingle-color black outline plate (solid black emblem linework on transparent) giving the factory the merrowed-border and detail-stitch path; hi-res PNG.
ridgeline_crest_knockout.pngimageOne-color solid knockout of the whole shield silhouette (flat fill on transparent) the factory uses to cut the twill/felt patch backing to shape.
ridgeline_crest_vector_mastersvectorExported vector masters (PDF + PNG + SVG) rendered from the genuine authored .ai built off the traced SVGs — for the factory press and for club use.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-89   LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE monochrome beauty brand mark: vectorize the supplied concept + build the full reversible variant system (master SVG, avatar, stacked lockup, one-color/mono-tint knockouts, print PNGs) with a font recommendation and palette recipe

Brand/vertical beauty_cosmeticsFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Clean and vectorize LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE's supplied flat monochrome logo concept into an editable SVG master, then assemble the full reversible variant system — black-on-white master, white-on-dark knockout, avatar/PFP crop, stacked lockup, one-color black print PNG, a mono-tint alt, a monochrome palette recipe, and a font recommendation — all scalable from avatar to signage.

Full brief

LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE is a fictional modern clean-beauty and skincare label preparing to launch across social, e-commerce, unit cartons, and large in-store signage. After an internal round we have approved ONE monochrome logo concept and now need it PRODUCTIONIZED into a fully editable, reversible brand-mark system — this is a finishing/assembly job, not a from-scratch design round. We supply a single flat, high-contrast raster export of the approved concept: a crisp geometric monogram ("LM" resolved into an abstract, symmetric negative-space shape) sitting above the wordmark "LUMIÈRE MÉNAGE" set in a clean modern condensed sans, rendered pure black on a near-white background, no gradients, no illustration, no flourishes. The concept scan is slightly skewed and the background is an off-white (#F5F5F3), not true white. Deliverables, all chained in the Adobe connector: 1) Ingest our supplied concept PNG and get a clean working copy — straighten the slight skew and auto-tone so the black is dense and the edges are true. 2) Knock the off-white background out to true transparency so the trace has no halo/fringe. 3) Vectorize the cleaned lockup into a clean, fully editable SVG — this is the MASTER vector (basis for the requested AI/SVG editable file + high-res PNG exports). 4) Produce the REVERSED knockout: a white-on-dark version of the mark that reads perfectly when reversed out on dark packaging and signage (the brief's key requirement). 5) Derive a tight AVATAR / social-PFP version from the monogram alone (square, centered) that stays recognizable at avatar size. 6) Build a STACKED lockup crop for narrow / vertical placements. 7) Produce a ONE-COLOR pure-black print PNG (for single-color foil/deboss and small-format print). 8) Produce a single MONOCHROMATIC brand-tint alt (one hue only — no multicolor, honoring "clean monochrome palette") for tonal applications. 9) Recommend a brand font family + heading/supporting pairing that matches the modern condensed wordmark tone. 10) Emit a MONOCHROME palette recipe (black / dark grey / off-white / paper hex callouts) as the color-palette deliverable. Hard constraints (honor the brief AND connector ground truth): stay strictly monochrome — no gradients, no multicolor, no vintage/distress treatment. Everything must reverse cleanly, scale from a social-media avatar up to large signage, and arrive as an editable vector plus high-res PNGs. No from-scratch logo drawing, no generative fill, no AI object removal, no compositing — the supplied concept is the single source of truth and every deliverable is derived from it by chaining. The requested AI/EPS export and the printed spec sheet are human/format-export finishing items layered on top of the connector-produced SVG + PNG masters.

Strictly monochrome black.
lumiere_menage_reversed_white_on_dark.pngimageReversed knockout: the mark converted to solid white (via monochromatic tint, high-lightness single hue) so it reads over dark packaging/signage, hi-res PNG — the brief's key 'must reverse out on dark backgrounds' requirement.

AO-90   Apexguard Auto Film: vectorize the AI-drafted master + two sub-brand marks into production SVGs and assemble a multi-size die-cut vinyl decal sheet on the supplied cut-line template

Brand/vertical automotive_transportFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 27)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Clean and de-skew Apexguard's three AI-drafted PPF/tint logo rasters, trace them into crisp production SVG masters, derive one-color knockouts for vinyl weeding, then assemble a multi-size die-cut vinyl decal/sticker sheet by binding the vector marks and the approved-sizes CSV into the client's authored cut-line template and exporting a press-ready PDF + SVG.

Full brief

Apexguard Auto Film, a fictional performance-automotive brand selling paint protection film (PPF) and window tint, is rebranding and needs its new identity turned into production-ready vector artwork and a print-ready vinyl decal sheet for its shop glass, product boxes, and installer-kit labels. The client has drafted the concepts with an AI image tool and only has flat raster PNGs: a master wordmark lockup for 'Apexguard' (a clean modern condensed sans in matte black with a single deep-blue accent chevron, premium/minimalist, no gradients), a sub-brand mark for 'Apexguard SHIELD' (the PPF line — the wordmark plus a simple hex-shield glyph), and a sub-brand mark for 'Apexguard TINT' (the window-tint line — the wordmark plus a tint-bar glyph rendered as flat bands). All three rasters carry the usual AI-export problems: soft anti-aliased edges, a faint off-white textured card behind the art, mild luminance noise, and each is tilted a couple of degrees off level. They cannot go to a vinyl cutter/printer as-is because they do not scale, have no clean cut path, and have no transparency. The client also supplies (a) an authored die-cut decal-sheet template (decal_sheet_template.ai) laid out on a single sheet with several registered sticker cells at different sizes — small installer-kit labels up to a large window/glass piece — each cell carrying a real placement/merge field plus a registered cut-line and bleed, and (b) a CSV of the approved sticker sizes and cell labels (cell_label, size_in, which_mark, mark) that matches the template's merge fields. The job: (1) ingest the three raster logos and get clean working copies; auto-straighten each tilted mark and tonally clean it so the black is true, the blue accent is crisp, and the near-white card is even; (2) crop each mark tight to its true artwork bounds and knock the background to transparent so the trace has no halo; (3) VECTORIZE each cleaned mark into a crisp, fully-scalable SVG master preserving the exact black + deep-blue palette — these three SVGs are the primary editable/print-ready vector deliverables (AI/EPS are the same master exported to those formats); (4) from the master wordmark, derive a single-color / knockout vector (solid-fill silhouette) for one-color window vinyl, etched glass and weeding-friendly cutter output, plus a white-on-dark knockout for dark boxes and tinted glass; (5) ASSEMBLE the multi-size vinyl decal/sticker sheet the briefs ask for: upload the cleaned vector marks, then run the client's authored decal_sheet_template.ai through a vector data-merge against the approved-sizes CSV so each registered cell is populated with the correct mark at the correct size behind its cut-line and bleed, and export the assembled sheet to a press-ready PDF and a production SVG; (6) recommend a brand-safe font pairing that matches the wordmark so the client can extend the identity across packaging and web. Keep it premium, minimalist and confident — no gradients on the master, no mascots, no vintage badges — and preserve the black + deep-blue palette exactly. Sizes and cell labels stay exactly as given in the CSV.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
apexguard_master_wordmark_raw.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA 1600x1000 flat PNG of an AI-generated automotive-brand master wordmark logo as it would look straight out of an image
apexguard_shield_submark_raw.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA 1500x1100 flat PNG of an AI-generated sub-brand mark: the wordmark 'APEXGUARD' with 'SHIELD' set smaller beneath it, n
apexguard_tint_submark_raw.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA 1500x1100 flat PNG of an AI-generated sub-brand mark: the wordmark 'APEXGUARD' with 'TINT' set smaller beneath it, nex
decal_sheet_template.aidatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe Illustrator document (.ai) for a single-sheet die-cut
decal_sizes.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns cell_label,size_in,which_mark,mark that assigns the approved sticker sizes to the template c

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 27 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
apexguard_master_wordmark.svgvectorClean full-color vector trace of the Apexguard master wordmark lockup, matte-black type + single deep-blue accent chevron preserved exactly, transparent background, crisp scalable paths; the primary editable master (AI/EPS are this master exported to those formats).
apexguard_shield_submark.svgvectorClean vector trace of the Apexguard SHIELD sub-brand mark (wordmark + flat hex-shield glyph), palette preserved, transparent background, scalable paths; PPF-line sub-brand master.
apexguard_tint_submark.svgvectorClean vector trace of the Apexguard TINT sub-brand mark (wordmark + flat-band tint-bar glyph), flat bands traced as discrete shapes, transparent background, scalable paths; tint-line sub-brand master.
apexguard_master_onecolor_knockout.svgvectorSingle-color knockout/silhouette vector of the master wordmark (solid fill on flattened field) for one-color window vinyl, etched glass, weeding-friendly cutter output and single-color print.
apexguard_master_white_on_dark.pngimageWhite-on-dark one-color knockout of the master wordmark, hi-res transparent PNG (white silhouette on transparency) for dark product boxes and tinted glass panels.
apexguard_decal_sheet_print.pdfpdfPress-ready single-sheet die-cut vinyl decal PDF assembled from the authored decal_sheet_template.ai via vector data-merge: each registered cell populated with the correct cleaned vector mark at the correct approved size, cut-lines on the CutContour spot layer, bleed and trim/registration marks intact, vector/~300 DPI print quality.
apexguard_decal_sheet_production.svgvectorProduction SVG export of the same assembled multi-size decal sheet (marks placed in their registered cells with cut-lines) for the vinyl cutter/plotter workflow.
apexguard_font_pairing.jsondataRecommended brand-safe font family + heading/body pairing matching the wordmark style, from font_recommend, so the client can extend the identity across packaging and web.

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-91   Maison Valcere jewelry house: vector & print-readiness audit of the supplied luxury wordmark pack against the brand guideline and press spec (marked-up findings report)

Brand/vertical jewelry_luxury_goodsFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 9 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Audit the supplied Maison Valcere luxury wordmark pack (master SVG + raster export) against the brand guideline and the press/foil spec, and return a marked-up, region-referenced vector & print-readiness findings report listing every hairline-stroke, line-weight, palette, kerning and non-print-ready-path defect and the exact rule it breaks.

Full brief

Maison Valcere is a fictional independent high-jewelry house (fine rings, pendants and bespoke pieces) that has just received its finished logo pack from an outside brand studio and, before it goes onto embossed presentation boxes, foil-stamped certificates, the e-commerce header and the video title cards, wants an independent set of eyes to confirm the mark is genuinely production-clean. The identity is meant to feel unmistakably premium — a deep-black canvas with crisp white typography, minimal ornamentation, purely typographic with no symbols or icons — and the house is strict that every asset stays consistent across packaging, print and screen. You are handed four things: (1) valcere_wordmark_master.svg, the "production" vector master the studio delivered; (2) valcere_wordmark_web.png, the flat raster export the house has been using online; (3) valcere_brand_guideline.pdf, the guideline stating the monochrome palette (Valcere Noir #0E0E10, Champagne #E8DCC2, Blanc #FAF8F3 — with the rule that the wordmark is "purely typographic, no symbols or icons, never recoloured outside the monochrome palette, minimum print width 22mm, minimum hairline stroke 0.35pt / 1.5px at favicon size"); and (4) valcere_press_spec.pdf, the printer/foil spec sheet (foil-stamp minimum stroke, CMYK/spot conversion requirement, "all strokes must be expanded to filled paths, no open contours, no RGB-only fills"). The job is an AUDIT, not a redraw — do NOT re-letter, re-trace, recolour or fix anything, and do NOT deliver a new logo. Go through the mark region by region and compare what the pack actually contains against the guideline and the press spec, then hand back a vector & print-readiness audit report that, for each issue, gives the mark region (which glyph / stroke / variant), a plain-language description of the defect, the specific guideline or press-spec clause it violates, the approved value vs. the observed value, and a severity (blocker / warning / nit). We expect you to catch things like: a hairline serif or connecting stroke thinner than the 0.35pt foil-stamp minimum that will drop out when stamped or shrunk to a favicon; a stray decorative anchor / tiny symbol that violates the "purely typography, no symbols" rule; the reverse (knockout) variant sitting on an off-palette near-black instead of Valcere Noir #0E0E10; kerning drift where the raster web export does not match the vector master's letter-spacing; and paths in the master SVG that are not print-ready (unexpanded strokes, open contours, RGB fills that were never converted for spot/foil). Deliver the report as a clean A4 portrait PDF plus the raw findings as a structured file, and include a one-page cover summary with the total count by severity and a go / no-go-for-press verdict. The report is for the house's creative director and the printer, not a redesign.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
valcere_wordmark_master.svgvectorn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine vector SVG 'production master' of a purely-typographic luxur
valcere_wordmark_web.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA 2400x900 flat PNG raster export of the same 'VALCERE' luxury jewelry wordmark as the studio delivered it for web: high
valcere_brand_guideline.pdfpdfn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: a genuine multi-page brand guideline PDF for the fictional high-jewelry house 'Maison Valcere'. Mu
valcere_press_spec.pdfpdfn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: a genuine one-to-two page printer/foil press specification sheet for the Maison Valcere logo pack.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Valcere_Vector_PrintReadiness_Audit.pdfpdfThe primary deliverable: a clean A4 portrait vector & print-readiness audit report PDF. Opens with a one-page cover summary (total findings by severity blocker / warning / nit and a go / no-go-for-press verdict), then one row per finding with the mark region (glyph / stroke / variant), a plain-language description of the defect, the exact guideline or press-spec clause it violates, the approved value vs. the observed value, and severity. This is a FINDINGS report, not a redraw of the logo.
valcere_audit_findings.jsondataThe raw structured findings emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {region, variant, defect, source_rule, approved_value, observed_value, severity} plus a summary block with counts by severity and the press verdict. Machine-readable companion to the PDF for the creative director's tracker and the printer.
