AO-92   Wandergrain Supply screen-print pre-flight: audit 4 supplied traveller tee vectors for separation-readiness, registration & spot-color count, emit a marked-up seps report + print-shop go/no-go note

Brand/vertical fashion_apparelFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 20)Tools 10 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K6 · K7

Pre-flight four supplied traveller-themed t-shirt vector graphics for screen-print separation readiness -- verify transparent backgrounds, count distinct spot colors per design, check for registration marks and thin-line survivability, vectorize/inspect to flag raster contamination -- and emit a marked-up per-design seps audit report with a go/no-go recommendation for the exposure room.

Full brief

Wandergrain Supply Co., a small-batch apparel screen-print shop, has just received four traveller-themed t-shirt graphics from a freelance designer for its new "Modern Wanderer" tee line (universal travel icons -- a passport-stamp badge, a latitude/longitude coordinate mark, a minimalist skyline, and a compass-rose crest). Before any of these hit the exposure room and burn screens, the shop needs an independent pre-flight / separation audit -- the designer swears the files are "pre-flighted for screen print," but the shop has been burned before by art that looked fine on screen and fell apart on the press. We are NOT redesigning anything and NOT producing final films; we need an honest QA report. For each of the four designs, please verify and document: (1) background integrity -- the art must sit on a genuinely transparent background with no stray off-white halo or leftover matte that would print as a phantom screen; (2) spot-color / ink count -- count the distinct flat spot colors in each design and flag any design that exceeds our 4-color manual-press limit, or that hides near-duplicate colors that should be merged to one screen; (3) registration & trap readiness -- check whether the art carries registration marks / crop guides and whether abutting colors are cleanly separable or will need a choke/trap note; (4) detail survivability -- flag any hairline strokes, tiny text, or fine detail below what a 156-mesh screen can hold, and confirm the "keep text editable" promise by checking whether type is still crisp vector or has been flattened to messy raster; (5) vector cleanliness -- confirm each design is truly vector (not a raster PNG masquerading as art) by test-vectorizing and comparing. Deliverables: a per-design marked-up preview of each graphic (so we can circle problems), plus a single written seps pre-flight report (a recipe/findings note) that lists, per design, the spot-color count, pass/fail on transparency, registration presence, minimum-detail concern, and a clear GO / FIX-FIRST / NO-GO verdict the exposure room can act on. Files supplied as an Illustrator-exported package: four graphics (three already flattened to PNG with transparency, one still a layered vector) plus the designer's own color-spec sheet. Keep everything spot-color / vector oriented -- no CMYK conversions, no re-coloring, no new artwork.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
tee_passport_stamp_badge.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat 2-color screen-print-style t-shirt graphic: a circular vintage passport-stamp badge reading 'MODERN WANDERER' aro
tee_coordinate_mark.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat minimalist screen-print t-shirt graphic: a latitude/longitude coordinate crosshair with the numbers '40.7128 N /
tee_city_skyline.pngimagegpt-image-2A flat screen-print-style t-shirt graphic of a generic minimalist city skyline silhouette at dusk, but rendered with a s
tee_compass_rose_crest.ai.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat 3-color screen-print t-shirt crest: a compass rose inside a shield banner reading 'WANDERGRAIN' with small regist
wandergrain_color_spec.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns design,claimed_ink_count,claimed_colors,has_registration,notes describing the designer's own

coordinate_mark_isolated_ink_masksimageSelection/preview masks isolating the near-duplicate dark tones in the coordinate-mark design, evidencing that its claimed '1 color' is actually 3 near-identical inks that must merge to one screen.
compass_crest_vector_trace.svgvectorA test-vectorization SVG of the compass-rose crest, used only as inspection evidence that the design is genuinely clean flat spot art with holdable edges (confirms the GO verdict); not a redesign or final film.

AO-93   Torqex Industrial nameplate logo pack: print-readiness preflight audit of the supplied vector artwork (open paths, RGB, un-outlined text, stray points) into a marked-up findings report

Brand/vertical manufacturing_industrialFamily Vector & PrintOperation O7 Vector & screen-printHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 9 distinctCapability profile K3 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Preflight-audit the supplied Torqex vector logo pack (.ai/.eps master, SVG, PDF, plus one-colour and reverse variants) against the manufacturer's nameplate print spec and return a marked-up, file-and-region-referenced findings report listing every open path, RGB colour, un-outlined text run, stray point, hairline stroke and background/bleed defect with the exact rule it breaks and a severity.

Full brief

Torqex Industrial is a fictional maker of hydraulic torque tools and machinery, and we are about to send our brand mark out to a nameplate vendor to be laser-etched onto anodized-aluminium equipment plates and screen-printed onto tool cases. A previous contractor delivered our 'Torqex' logo as a vector pack, but before it goes to the etching/print house we need a fresh set of eyes to confirm the artwork is genuinely production-ready — the last vendor rejected a batch because a logo file had live text and an RGB blue that shifted on the plate, and we cannot afford another rejected run. This is an AUDIT, not a redesign: do NOT redraw, restyle, re-colour or re-letter anything. You will receive two things. (1) torqex_logo_pack: the delivered artwork — torqex_master.ai (the editable vector master), torqex_master.eps and torqex_master.pdf (the same mark exported), torqex_logo.svg (the web export), plus torqex_onecolour_black.ai (single-colour knockout) and torqex_reverse_white.ai (reverse/knockout-on-dark variant). (2) torqex_nameplate_print_spec.pdf, the vendor's print-spec sheet stating the rules the pack must satisfy: all colours must be CMYK (or spot) — no RGB; the approved brand colour is Torqex Blue at C100 M72 K18 (do NOT ship RGB #1B49E5); all text must be converted to outlines (no live fonts); paths must be closed with no stray/open anchor points; the minimum printable stroke weight for laser etching is 0.25 pt (0.09 mm) — anything thinner drops out; the mark must sit on a transparent background with a 3 mm bleed on the placed variants; the one-colour and reverse variants must be a single flat colour with no gradients or tints. Go through every file, compare what is actually in the artwork against the spec, and hand back a print-readiness preflight report that, for each issue found, gives the file name, the region / element on the mark, a plain-language description of the defect, the specific spec clause it violates, the observed value vs the approved value, and a severity (blocker / warning / nit). We expect you to catch things like: the SVG and the .pdf export carrying RGB colour instead of CMYK; the tagline text in the master left as live (un-outlined) fonts; a couple of stray/duplicate anchor points and one un-closed path on the icon; a hairline register line under 0.25 pt that will drop out on the plate; the reverse variant accidentally carrying a faint gradient instead of a flat white; and one variant exported on a solid white background instead of transparent with no bleed. Some files are clean and compliant so the audit has to distinguish good from bad, not just flag everything. Deliver the report as a clean A4 PDF plus the raw findings as a structured file, with a one-page cover summary giving the total count by severity. The report is for our production manager and the nameplate vendor, not a design revision.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
torqex_master.aivectorn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe Illustrator vector file (.ai/.pdf-compatible) of a fic
torqex_master.epsvectorn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: an EPS export of the same TORQEX mark from the master .ai — carried forward with the SAME un-outli
torqex_master.pdfpdfn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: a print PDF export of the TORQEX mark. Planted defect: exported with an RGB colour profile so the
torqex_logo.svgvectorn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: the web/SVG export of the TORQEX mark. Planted defects: RGB colour (#1B49E5, inherent to SVG) whic
torqex_onecolour_black.aivectorn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: the single-colour KNOCKOUT variant of the TORQEX mark — the whole mark as one flat 100% black (K10
torqex_reverse_white.aivectorn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: the REVERSE variant of the TORQEX mark for dark plates — the mark in flat white on a transparent b
torqex_nameplate_print_spec.pdfpdfn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: the nameplate vendor's print-spec sheet PDF stating, in text the connector can read/summarize, the

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Torqex_Logo_PrintPreflight_Audit.pdfpdfThe primary deliverable: a clean A4 portrait print-readiness preflight report PDF. Opens with a one-page cover summary (total findings by severity: blocker / warning / nit, plus a per-file pass/fail matrix), then one row per finding with the file name, the region/element on the mark, a plain-language description of the defect, the exact print-spec clause it violates, the observed value vs the approved value, and severity. This is a FINDINGS report, not a redesign of the logo.
torqex_preflight_findings.jsondataThe raw structured findings emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {file, region, defect, spec_clause, approved_value, observed_value, severity} plus a per-file pass/fail matrix and a summary block with counts by severity. Machine-readable companion to the PDF for the production manager's tracker.
torqex_logo_proof_rastersimageThe per-file raster proofs produced by rendering each supplied vector/PDF file (the .ai/.eps/.pdf/.svg master + the two variants) to an image, used as the visual inspection surface for the audit and referenced by file name in the findings. Intermediate/working artifact retained alongside the report.

AO-62   Variable-Data Event Promo Catalog + Price-Tag Merge (Overset-Safe Auto-Fit Template)

Brand/vertical Party, Events & PromotionFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 15)Tools 14 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Run our variable-data merge into the auto-fit (shrink-to-fit) InDesign catalog template and the Illustrator price-tag template so long product names no longer overset, prepping the product hero art and licensed background first, and export print-ready PDFs.

Full brief

We print a per-event promotional product catalog and matching shelf price tags, and our records come from a spreadsheet (one row per SKU: product name, blurb, price, event, SKU). Our recurring headache is that when a product name or blurb is long, the merged text overflows the frame and gets clipped on export — InDesign won't shrink it to fit on its own. We have already rebuilt our InDesign catalog template (catalog_template.indd) and our Illustrator price-tag template (pricetag_template.ai) with auto-size / "shrink text to fit frame" enabled on every variable-data frame and real merge fields (<<ProductName>>, <<Blurb>>, <<Price>>, <<SKU>>, <<EventName>>) wired up, so the layout itself is now overset-safe. We need you to: (1) stand up an asset workspace; (2) take in our authored templates, our catalog data (catalog_data.csv, ~24 SKUs), our product hero photo (hero_product.jpg) and our logo (logo_master.png); (3) prep the art that flows into the merge — isolate the product from its messy shot, clean the tone, build a clean bleed-safe background by cropping a licensed stock texture to the A4-plus-bleed frame, and vectorize the logo so it stays crisp at any print size; (4) run the data merge into BOTH the catalog .indd (one catalog card per row) and the price-tag .ai (Illustrator Variables, one tag per row); (5) export both as print-ready PDFs (catalog at A4 with bleed, tags at tag size); and (6) hand back the finished print-ready files. The whole point: long records must render legibly via the template's auto-fit, never clipped.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain events-weddingsDeliverable catalogWorkflow Create+EditOperation O5Family Layout & DataModality documentHorizon H4Est. calls 18Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[T]+[L]Brand PartyWorks
5Planning3Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Technical Quality · Process · Honesty · Brand Alignment. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

product_art_prepped.pngimageBackground-removed, auto-toned, cropped-to-cell product hero PNG (transparent) used as the merge image for the hero SKU.

Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-63   Maple Cricket League — a streetwear cricket league: vectorize the logo system, build the sublimation/screen-print kit artwork, and grade the brand photography into a production-ready package

Brand/vertical Apparel, Fashion & SportsFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Turn Maple Cricket League's already-designed raster logo sketches and brand photos into a production-ready set of named files: clean vector marks, a halftone sublimation/screen-print kit treatment, cohesive streetwear color-grade, a cut-out product shot, and a wide banner reframed from the graded hero.

Full brief

Maple Cricket League (Backyard Cricket League Canada) is a streetwear-forward cricket league for the South Asian and Caribbean diaspora in the GTA, with an aesthetic in the lane of Rastah, OVO, Aimé Leon Dore, and IPL. The concept is fully developed; the client needs execution and production-ready, organized, named files. You are the creative director executing the Adobe production-art thread. The client supplies: (a) a flat raster scan/mockup of the full logo system art (crest + monogram + hat badge laid out together) on a plain background; (b) an authored Illustrator master file (.ai) of the same three marks set up on three artboards, ready to export; (c) a hero brand photo of a street cricket scene shot in mixed/overcast GTA light; (d) a studio-style photo of a folded home jersey on a plain seamless backdrop for the e-commerce/lookbook cut-out; (e) a flat photo of the away-kit chest crest graphic to be turned into a single-color screen-print/sublimation separation. Produce, by chaining Adobe connector tools: (1) a clean, scalable VECTOR of the logo system from the raster scan (raster→SVG), then an exported vector master from the supplied authored .ai for the manufacturer; (2) a one-color HALFTONE screen-print / sublimation treatment of the away-kit crest, straightened, cropped to the print bounds, and converted to a print-ready halftone separation; (3) a cohesive streetwear COLOR GRADE of the hero brand photo (auto-tone baseline → exposure/contrast → split-tone-style monochromatic warmth → grain) so the photography matches the OVO/ALD reference; (4) a transparent-background PRODUCT CUT-OUT of the folded jersey for the lookbook; (5) a wide BANNER crop of the graded hero for the site/social header, reframed and resized to the wide banner dimensions from the existing frame (cropped/resized to the target ratio, using the room already in the shot). Deliver every export as an organized, clearly named, production-ready file. Hard rules: stay production-faithful — no inventing new logo concepts, no from-scratch art, no text-to-image. The vector export must come from the genuine authored .ai the client supplies. Every step after each asset load must consume a prior step's output.

5Planning4Brand strictness4Tool diversity3Visual density2Creative freedom

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
MCL_logo_system_traced.svgimage/svg+xmlClean scalable vector of the full logo system (crest + monogram + hat badge) traced from logo_system_raster.png. Resolution-independent SVG with grouped/named paths, two flat fill colors (forest green + cream), no raster embeds; suitable for infinite scaling on apparel and print.
MCL_logo_master_export.pdfapplication/pdfVector master export of the client's authored 3-artboard logo_system_master.ai (crest, monogram, hat badge), all artboards exported together. Print-ready vector PDF (no rasterization), CMYK-intent for manufacturer handoff; accompanied by the co-exported PNG raster preview from the same render_vector call.
MCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.pngimage/PNGSingle-color (one-ink) halftone screen-print / sublimation separation of the away-kit chest crest, straightened and cropped to the print bounds. High-resolution 300 DPI at print size, grayscale/1-color halftone dot pattern on white, ready to hand to the screen-print / sublimation manufacturer as a single separation.
MCL_hero_graded.jpgimage/JPEGCohesive streetwear color-grade of the hero street-cricket photo (auto-tone baseline -> contrast -> warm monochromatic split-tone -> fine grain). Full-resolution graded master JPEG, sRGB, matching the OVO/ALD reference look; serves as the source for the wide banner reframe.
MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpgimage/JPEGWide site/social banner reframed from the graded hero by cropping and resizing the existing frame to the target wide banner ratio (e.g. ~1920x640, 3:1). Uses the room already in the documentary shot; content is reframed/cropped to hit the ratio (background and edges are cropped to fit), no pixels invented, canvas not extended. sRGB JPEG.
MCL_jersey_cutout.pngimage/PNGTransparent-background product cut-out of the folded home jersey for the lookbook / e-commerce, produced by removing the seamless backdrop. PNG with alpha channel, clean subject edge, subject preserved at source resolution, sRGB.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 16 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All seven required deliverables are present in the packageautoThe delivery contains exactly these seven named files: MCL_logo_system_traced.svg, MCL_logo_master_export.pdf, MCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png, MCL_hero_graded.jpg, MCL_jersey_cutout.png, MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg, and MCL_brand_delivery.zipK1mandatoryoutput
Each deliverable is in the file format/colour-mode the spec namesautoMCL_logo_system_traced.svg is a valid SVG vector file; MCL_logo_master_export.pdf is a valid PDF accompanied by a PNG proof; MCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png and MCL_jersey_cutout.png are valid PNG; MCL_hero_graded.jpg and MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg are valid JPEG; MCL_brand_delivery.zip is a valid ZIP archiveK1dealbreakeroutput
The traced logo SVG is genuine scalable vector geometry derived from the supplied raster, not an embedded rasterautoMCL_logo_system_traced.svg contains real <path> vector geometry and no embedded raster <image>/bitmap element (the file is a true raster->SVG trace of logo_system_raster.png, not a wrapped bitmap)K1mandatoryoutput
The traced SVG reproduces all three marks from the source logo sheetexpertMCL_logo_system_traced.svg reproduces all three marks present in logo_system_raster.png: the circular MCL crest, the interlocking MCL monogram, and the simplified hat badgeK2mandatoryinput
The traced SVG preserves the two ink colours of the source logoexpertThe vectorized trace renders in two flat ink colours matching the source (deep forest green and cream) with clean flat paths, and introduces no extra colours or gradientsK2qualitybrand
The logo master PDF is an export of the client's authored 3-artboard .ai, not a regenerated or newly composed logoexpertMCL_logo_master_export.pdf renders all three artboards (MCL circular crest, interlocking MCL monogram, simplified hat badge) from logo_system_master.ai together, with no new/invented logo concepts and no from-scratch redrawing of the marksK2dealbreakerinput
The away-crest halftone output is auto-straightened and cropped tight to the print boundsexpertMCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png shows the crest rotation-corrected (no longer ~3 degrees off straight, matching away_crest_graphic.jpg's intentional skew) and cropped to the crest graphic's bounds with the surrounding near-white jersey panel removedK1mandatoryoutput
The away-crest output is a single-colour halftone screen-print/sublimation separationexpertMCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png is one-colour with a visible halftone dot pattern, ready as a single-screen print/sublimation separation, with no full-colour tones, gradients, or multi-inkK3mandatoryoutput
The graded hero photo received the full streetwear grade chain in orderexpertMCL_hero_graded.jpg reflects, over the ungraded hero_street_cricket.jpg, an auto-tone baseline, an exposure/contrast punch, a warm monochromatic split-tone tint, and fine film grain — reading visibly warmer, more contrasted, and less flat than the cool/underexposed inputK4mandatorybrief
The graded hero matches the named OVO/Aimé Leon Dore/IPL reference lookexpertThe grade reads as cohesive warm streetwear photography consistent with the OVO/Aimé Leon Dore/IPL aesthetic reference, not a neutral/flat result or an over-stylized filterK4qualitybrief
The jersey cut-out has a fully transparent backgroundautoMCL_jersey_cutout.png has a transparent alpha channel where the light-grey seamless backdrop of home_jersey_flat.jpg was, retaining only the folded jersey subjectK3mandatoryoutput
The jersey cut-out edge quality is clean enough for the e-commerce/lookbook gridexpertThe cut-out edge around the folded jersey is clean with no leftover grey backdrop halo, no clipped fabric, and intact knit/fold detailK3qualityoutput
The wide banner is produced by outpainting the graded hero (canvas extended, original content not cropped)expertMCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg is wider than MCL_hero_graded.jpg with the original framing fully retained and new generated content extending the scene at the sides, not a crop or re-frame of the heroK3mandatoryoutput
The banner is derived from the already-graded hero, preserving the gradeexpertMCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg carries the same warm graded look as MCL_hero_graded.jpg (the outpaint extends the graded frame, not the raw input), with the extended side region tonally matching the originalK3qualityoutput
No from-scratch art or text-to-image generation was used for the logo deliverablesexpertThe logo deliverables originate solely from the raster trace (image_vectorize) and the authored .ai export (document_render_vector); there is no invented new logo concept, no text-to-image-generated mark, and no compositing/gen-fill beyond the single permitted generative outpaint (the banner)K7dealbreakerbrief
Final ZIP is organized into the named production-handoff folder structureautoMCL_brand_delivery.zip contains all six exports sorted into named production-handoff subfolders (e.g. logos/, kits/, photography/), not a flat unnamed dumpK6mandatorybrief

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)

  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-64   Premium skincare packaging: retouch hero shot, vectorize logo, render authored dieline + data-merge SKU labels, print-ready PDFs

Brand/vertical Beauty, Cosmetics & Personal CareFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Take Aurelia Botanics' supplied hero serum photo, logo, authored carton dieline (.ai) + label (.indd) and SKU data, and produce retouched/CMYK-graded imagery, a clean vector logo, data-merged print-ready label PDFs for all 3 SKUs, and high-res mockup assets.

Full brief

Aurelia Botanics is launching a premium 3-SKU facial-serum line (Rosehip Renewal, Niacinamide Clarity, Hyaluronic Hydra) and needs retail-ready packaging production. The client provides authored production files (they built the carton dieline in Illustrator and the label layout in InDesign with real merge fields) plus raw brand assets. Your job is the connector-doable production pipeline: 1) Upload all 7 supplied assets (hero product photo, logo, leaf-motif art, carton dieline .ai, label .indd, brand-guide PDF, SKU CSV) into a working folder. 2) Clean up the hero serum-bottle photograph: auto-straighten, auto-tone, then lift exposure and warm the color temperature so the amber glass reads premium and consistent under the studio lighting. 3) Apply a Lightroom "Subject Pop" style preset to make the bottle separate cleanly from the seamless backdrop. 4) Knock the retouched bottle out of its background to a transparent PNG so it can drop onto any mockup backdrop. 5) License and download a marble-surface stock backdrop, and crop/resize it to the exact mockup canvas. 6) Build the botanical side-panel texture: take the supplied leaf-motif art and convert it to a 2-color halftone/duotone in brand green so it prints clean as a spot-style panel. 7) Vectorize the supplied raster logo into a clean SVG for the carton. 8) Export the client's authored carton dieline (.ai) to a print PDF (this is the structural cutting layout — connector EXPORTS it, does not author it). 9) Data-merge the 3 SKU rows from the CSV onto the client's authored label template (.indd with <<product_name>>, <<key_actives>>, <<volume>>, <<batch>> fields) to produce one print-ready label per SKU. 10) Export the merged label document to a CMYK-ready, bleed-correct print PDF. 11) Convert the supplied brand-guide PDF into an editable .indd so the studio can keep the spec sheet in InDesign. Deliverables: retouched + background-removed hero PNG, marble mockup backdrop, brand-green duotone side panel, vector logo SVG, exported carton dieline print PDF, 3 data-merged label print PDFs, and an editable brand-guide .indd.

0 · Metadata tags Phase 0 · Gate B

Domain beauty-cosmeticsDeliverable packaging-print-prepWorkflow EditOperation O5Family Layout & DataModality documentHorizon H4Est. calls 17Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[T]Brand Aurelia Botanics
5Planning4Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 16 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

outputkindspec
hero_bottle_retouched_cutout.pngimageBackground-removed transparent PNG of the straightened, auto-toned, exposure-/temperature-corrected, Subject-Pop-graded amber serum bottle; full hero resolution (~4000x5000 region), ready to drop onto any mockup.
marble_mockup_backdrop.jpgimageLicensed marble-surface stock backdrop cropped/resized to the 3000x3000px mockup canvas.

labels_merged_print.pdfpdfOne print-ready, 3mm-bleed, CMYK label page per SKU (3 pages) produced by data-merging skus.csv onto the authored label .indd.

The hero cutout shows the full retouch chain was applied: straightened framing, lifted exposure, warmer color temperature, and a Subject-Pop separation on the amber serum bottleexpertBottle reads visibly straightened/level, brighter than a neutral baseline with a warm amber cast, and pops cleanly off the original seamless light-grey sweep, consistent with auto_straighten -> auto_tone -> exposure -> warmer temperature -> Subject Pop presetK3qualitybrief

The leaf side-panel deliverable leaf_duotone_panel.png is a 2-color halftone/duotone in brand forest-green, derived from the supplied leaf_motif.png artexpertPanel is a two-tone (forest-green + ground) halftone/duotone of the supplied eucalyptus/rosehip leaf motif with no full-color photographic content and no extra colorsK2mandatoryoutput
The logo deliverable aurelia_logo.svg is a valid vector SVG containing path/vector geometry, not an embedded raster bitmapautoaurelia_logo.svg parses as SVG and contains <path>/vector elements; it does not consist solely of an embedded <image>/base64 rasterK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo reproduces the AURELIA BOTANICS serif wordmark plus the leaf-sprig mark in a single solid spot color, faithful to the supplied aurelia_logo_raster.png and not redrawn or alteredexpertSVG renders the 'AURELIA BOTANICS' high-contrast serif wordmark and the leaf-sprig mark matching aurelia_logo_raster.png shape-for-shape in one solid color with clean closed paths; no new glyphs, ornaments, or color changes introducedK2dealbreakerinput
The carton dieline is delivered as a print PDF (carton_dieline_print.pdf) plus a preview PNG, exported from the client-authored aurelia_carton_dieline.ai and not authored from scratchautocarton_dieline_print.pdf exists alongside a preview PNG of the same dieline, and the PDF page corresponds to the supplied 40x40x120mm folding-carton dieline structureK1mandatoryoutput
The exported carton dieline PDF preserves the authored structural print elements: cut lines, fold/score lines, 3mm bleed and registration marks, in CMYKexpertDieline PDF shows distinct cut lines and fold/score lines, registration marks, and a 3mm bleed margin, and is CMYK, matching the authored aurelia_carton_dieline.ai it was exported fromK1mandatorybrief
The merged label deliverable labels_merged_print.pdf contains exactly 3 label pages, one per SKUautolabels_merged_print.pdf has exactly 3 pagesK1mandatoryoutput
Each of the 3 label pages carries the correct merged SKU data bound from skus.csv onto the product_name/key_actives/volume/batch fieldsautoThe 3 pages show, one per page: 'Rosehip Renewal Facial Serum' / 'Rosehip Oil + Vitamin C' / '30 ml' / 'AB-RR-0426'; 'Niacinamide Clarity Facial Serum' / '10% Niacinamide + Zinc' / '30 ml' / 'AB-NC-0426'; 'Hyaluronic Hydra Facial Serum' / '2% Hyaluronic Acid + B5' / '30 ml' / 'AB-HH-0426'K1dealbreakerinput
The label print PDF is a CMYK, 3mm-bleed print-ready file with no leftover literal <<field>> placeholdersexpertlabels_merged_print.pdf is CMYK with a 3mm bleed, and no unmerged '<<product_name>>'/'<<key_actives>>'/'<<volume>>'/'<<batch>>' placeholder text remains on any pageK1mandatoryoutput
The labels preserve the authored typographic hierarchy (product name large serif, actives medium, volume+batch small) and brand green/cream swatchesexpertOn each label the product name is the largest serif element, key_actives is medium, volume and batch are smallest, rendered with brand forest-green/cream swatches per the authored aurelia_label_template.inddK3qualitybrief
The brand-guide deliverable brand_guide_editable.indd is an editable InDesign document (delivered zipped) converted from the supplied 4-page brand_guide.pdfautoDeliverable is a ZIP containing an editable .indd (or .idml) document derived from the 4-page brand_guide.pdfK1mandatoryoutput
A Firefly concept board (aurelia_concept_board) deep-link is delivered assembling the six produced assets: hero cutout, marble backdrop, duotone panel, vector logo, dieline preview and label preview(s)autoA working Firefly Board link resolves to a board containing the 6 produced assets: hero_bottle_retouched_cutout, marble_mockup_backdrop, leaf_duotone_panel, aurelia_logo.svg, the carton dieline preview, and the label preview(s)K6mandatoryoutput

Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-67   Tamil Traditional Service-Promotion Large-Format Banner Set — Print-Prep & Source-File Production (6 sizes)

Brand/vertical General / Cross-Industry Branding & GraphicsFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 28)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Take my supplied Tamil banner master files plus hero photography and brand logo, and produce the complete print-ready production package — graded hero image with print bleed, a clean vector logo, a traditional halftone texture motif, and CMYK 150-dpi PDF/JPEG exports of all six large-format banners — with editable source files alongside.

Full brief

I run a traditional Tamil service business and I'm rolling out a promotion across six printed banners (design already laid out by my designer): 10x10 ft (3 pieces), 10x20 ft (2 pieces), 10x6 ft (1 piece). All copy is in Tamil and the look must stay clearly traditional — warm, festive, gold/maroon tones, eye-catching from a distance. I will supply you: (1) my authored Illustrator master files for the banners with all Tamil typography already set correctly in licensed Tamil fonts (do NOT re-typeset or translate — typography is locked), (2) one wide hero photograph of my premises/service scene that needs heavy correction and a warm traditional grade, (3) my brand logo as a flat raster file that I need cleaned up into crisp vector for large print, and (4) a low-res PDF proof of last year's banner that I want converted back into an editable layout source. What I need from you, all on the Adobe production side: - Fix and grade the hero photo: straighten it, auto-correct tone, warm it up, push the gold/maroon vibrance, pull back blown highlights, then isolate the main service element so its colour stays controlled. - The hero photo prints right to the edge, so I need 1 inch of real bleed around it for the largest 10x20 ft layout — since the source is an ultra-wide capture with room to spare, reframe and resize it to the bleed-inclusive target dimensions so the existing image content fills all the way into the 1-inch bleed margin (crop to fit, don't just pad). - Turn my flat logo into a clean scalable vector so it stays sharp at 10-foot height. - Build me a traditional half-tone / screen-print texture motif derived from the graded hero so it reads as a printed-poster aesthetic up close. - Export each of the six authored banner masters as a flattened high-res raster and as a print PDF — CMYK intent, 150 dpi minimum at full scale, 1 inch bleed all around. - Convert my old PDF proof into an editable InDesign source so I can reuse last year's layout. - Hand back a single packaged folder with every final and source file organised. Acceptance: traditional consistent look across all sizes, no damage to the locked Tamil typography, and print-ready files meeting CMYK / 150 dpi / 1-inch-bleed specs. One round of minor revisions expected after I review the first draft.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 28 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
hero_graded_bleed.pngimageCorrected + warm-traditional-graded hero photograph (straightened, auto-toned, highlights recovered, gold/maroon vibrance pushed, controlled subject color) with 1 inch of real reframe-cropped bleed on all sides for the 10x20 ft layout. High-res PNG, sRGB working copy for placement into the authored master.
brand_logo_vector.svgvectorClean scalable SVG vectorization of the flat brand logo, sharp at 10-foot print height, solid maroon/gold fills, Tamil emblem preserved.
halftone_texture_motif.pngimageTraditional screen-print / halftone texture motif derived from the graded hero, for the close-up printed-poster aesthetic. High-res PNG.
banner_10x20_print.pdf + .pngpdfPrint export of the authored 10x20 ft master: CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched. (CMYK intent applies to the PDF; the PNG is an RGB companion proof.)
banner_10x10_a_print.pdf + .pngpdfPrint export of the first authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_a): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
banner_10x10_b_print.pdf + .pngpdfPrint export of the second authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_b): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
banner_10x10_c_print.pdf + .pngpdfPrint export of the third authored 10x10 ft master (banner_10x10_c): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
banner_10x20_b_print.pdf + .pngpdfPrint export of the second authored 10x20 ft master (banner_10x20_b): CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
banner_10x6_print.pdf + .pngpdfPrint export of the authored 10x6 ft master: CMYK PDF plus flattened high-res sRGB PNG, full-scale 150 dpi, 1-inch bleed, traditional look intact, Tamil typography untouched.
banner_set_delivery_folderdataOrganised CC delivery folder (populated via connector-side asset copy, not a zip archive) containing the print PDF + high-res PNG exports for all six authored banner masters (3x 10x10, 2x 10x20, 1x 10x6), the editable .indd converted from last year's proof, the vector logo, the graded hero, and the halftone motif — every final and source file organised in one deliverable.

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
All six authored banner masters are exported as PDF + high-res PNG pairs matching the exact size breakdownautoThe deliverable contains a print PDF + high-res PNG for exactly 6 banners with the size breakdown 3x 10x10 ft, 2x 10x20 ft, 1x 10x6 ft (12 export files total, 2 per master)K1mandatoryoutput
The individually named 10x20 ft print export deliverable is present as PDF plus flattened high-res PNGautobanner_10x20_print.pdf and its companion flattened high-res PNG (banner_10x20_print.png) are both present in the packageK1mandatoryoutput
Each banner print PDF export carries CMYK colour intentautoEvery banner print PDF carries CMYK colour space / intent (the flattened PNG companions may be RGB/sRGB, since PNG cannot carry CMYK)K1dealbreakerbrief
Each banner export meets the resolution spec at full physical scaleautoEvery banner export is 150 dpi minimum at the full physical scale of its banner sizeK1mandatorybrief
Each banner PDF export carries 1-inch bleed on all four sidesautoEvery banner PDF export includes 1 inch of bleed on all four sidesK1mandatorybrief
The locked Tamil typography in the authored masters is left untouched (not re-typeset, translated, outlined, or misspelled)expertTamil headline/body text in all exports is identical to the supplied .ai masters — text frames preserved, no re-typesetting, no translation, no spelling alterations, no glyph corruptionK2dealbreakerbrief
The graded hero deliverable hero_graded_bleed.png is present as a high-res PNGautohero_graded_bleed.png is present in the package as a high-resolution PNG fileK1mandatoryoutput
The hero photo received the full ordered correction-and-grade pipelineexperthero_graded_bleed.png shows: straightened horizon (the ~3-degree tilt removed), auto-tone applied, blown highlights/sky recovered, warmed white balance toward gold, and pushed gold/maroon vibrance/saturationK4mandatorybrief
The focal service element's colour is selectively controlled via a prompt-built maskexpertThe main service element (e.g. brass lamp / seva counter) was isolated via a select_by_prompt mask and had single-colour saturation applied so its colour stays controlled, distinct from a global editK3qualitybrief
The hero bleed is real outpainted content, not paddingexperthero_graded_bleed.png has 1 inch of generatively expanded (outpainted) image content extending the scene on all four sides for the 10x20 ft layout — not solid/transparent padding or a stretched/mirrored borderK3mandatorybrief
The brand logo is delivered as a clean vector SVGautobrand_logo_vector.svg is present and is a true path-based vector SVG (not an embedded raster), scalable for 10-foot print heightK1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized logo preserves the original emblem in solid maroon/gold fillsexpertbrand_logo_vector.svg reproduces the supplied kuthuvilakku oil-lamp emblem, Tamil-script business name along the top arc, and lotus underline in solid maroon and gold fills with crisp edges — not a regenerated or altered logoK2dealbreakerinput
The halftone texture motif is delivered and derived from the graded heroexperthalftone_texture_motif.png is present as a high-res PNG showing a traditional screen-print / halftone dot texture visibly derived from the graded+bleed hero imageK4mandatoryoutput
The old PDF proof is converted into an editable InDesign sourceautolast_year_editable.indd is present as a genuine editable .indd file converted from last_year_proof.pdfK1mandatoryoutput
A single packaged delivery bundle contains every final and source file organised togetherautoThe banner_set_exports deliverable bundles, in one organised folder: the 6 banner PDF+PNG export pairs, brand_logo_vector.svg, hero_graded_bleed.png, halftone_texture_motif.png, and last_year_editable.inddK6mandatoryoutput
The traditional warm gold/maroon festive look is consistent across all six sizesexpertAll six banner exports and the graded hero read as a coherent traditional Tamil festive set — warm gold/maroon tones, ornamental treatment, eye-catching from a distance — with no inconsistent grading across sizesK4qualitybrief

AO-68   Roadside Coaching-Institute Banner: brand-graded hero, vector icon set, halftone geometry + print-ready CMYK PDF export

Brand/vertical Nonprofit, Religious & CommunityFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 16)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Prepare every visual asset for the DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES 10ft x 5ft roadside banner — brand-graded student hero, vector subject icons, brand-colour halftone geometry — and export the authored InDesign layout to a print-ready CMYK PDF for large-format flex printing.

Full brief

DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES needs a clean, premium, professional roadside banner (10 ft x 5 ft, 2:1) that stays readable from the road and presents the course/contact info without clutter. The brand palette is primary #046CB8 and secondary #02ADEF, headings/key numbers in bold Inter, with subject-hinting icons, a confident-but-minimal photo of students, and understated geometric elements. The freelancer has already authored the layered banner in InDesign with every line of verbatim copy (institute name, tagline \"Where Learning Meets Success\", the Pre-Foundation / 9th-10th / 11th-12th course list, \"ADMISSIONS OPEN\", the three phone numbers with 8103332901 most prominent, the \"Railway Station Road, Multai\" location, and the four feature bullets). That authored layout (.indd) plus a raw, slightly-tilted DSLR photo of students, a flat icon sheet, and the brand-color swatch file are the client inputs.\n\nYour Adobe production job, end to end:\n1. Stand up the project folders and load/preview the raw student photo and the icon sheet.\n2. Fix the photo geometry: auto-straighten the tilted horizon, then crop to the banner hero band aspect.\n3. Correct and grade it to the brand: auto-tone for a clean baseline, then warm/cool the white balance and push the dominant tones toward the #046CB8 / #02ADEF brand blues via a color overlay and a targeted single-colour saturation lift so the photo \"matches the specs\".\n4. Produce a clean placement plate: select the students as the subject and remove the background to a transparent cutout the banner can drop onto its geometric field.\n5. Reframe the graded photo to the full 2:1 banner hero ratio with a gen-free crop/resize (the 4:3 source is wider than tall enough for a 2:1 reframe, so the existing pixels are cropped/resized to the print-scale hero band — no invented pixels, no pixelation at full print size).\n6. Turn the flat icon sheet into crisp, infinitely-scalable print vectors (vectorize → SVG) so the subject icons stay sharp on a 10-foot banner.\n7. Build the \"understated geometric element\": take the brand-blue field and render a halftone/duotone texture in the brand colours for a modern, minimal accent.\n8. Export the client-authored InDesign banner layout to the final print-ready, high-resolution CMYK PDF for large-format flex printing.\n\nThe client signs off once the PDF opens cleanly, colours match #046CB8/#02ADEF in CMYK, and the text hierarchy is legible at full 10ft x 5ft size.

4Planning5Brand strictness5Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
students_hero_brandgraded_2x1.pngimageStraightened, cropped, auto-toned, white-balanced and brand-graded student hero photo pushed toward #046CB8/#02ADEF, reframed via a gen-free crop/resize to the full 2:1 (10ft x 5ft) banner hero band, full print resolution, no pixelation. The 4:3 source is wide enough to reframe to 2:1 by cropping height, so only existing pixels are used — no invented content.
students_cutout_transparent.pngimageBackground-removed transparent-PNG cutout of the student group for clean placement onto the banner's geometric field.
subject_icons.svgvectorBrand-blue subject icon set vectorized to clean scalable SVG paths that stay crisp at 10-foot print size.
brand_halftone_texture.pngimageModern, minimal halftone/duotone geometric texture in the #046CB8/#02ADEF brand colours for the understated background accent.
DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdfpdfPrint-ready, high-resolution CMYK PDF of the authored banner layout, 10ft x 5ft, exported for large-format flex printing — the primary client deliverable.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric expert-authored & adversarially verified · 17 checks

checkbyexact pass conditionfeedsweightfrom
The print-ready CMYK PDF deliverable DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf exists and is a valid PDFautoA file named DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf is delivered and opens cleanly as a valid PDF documentK1mandatoryoutput
The print PDF colour space is CMYKautoDYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf reports a CMYK (DeviceCMYK / ICC CMYK) colour space, not RGB, matching the brief's 'Print-ready PDF in CMYK' requirementK1dealbreakerbrief
The banner uses the 10ft x 5ft (2:1) aspect ratio at print scaleautoDYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_PRINT.pdf page geometry is 2:1 (width:height = 10ft:5ft), within a small toleranceK1mandatorybrief
The print PDF is high-resolution suitable for large-format flex printing with no pixelationexpertAt full 10ft x 5ft size the exported PDF shows no visible pixelation, blurring or upscaling artefacts in the hero photo, icons or texture, satisfying the brief's 'high resolution suitable for large-format flex printing (no pixelation)'K4mandatorybrief
The brand-graded student hero deliverable students_hero_brandgraded_2x1.png is delivered as a 2:1 PNGautostudents_hero_brandgraded_2x1.png is delivered as a PNG whose aspect ratio is 2:1 (the full 10ft x 5ft banner hero band produced by the outpaint/generative-expand step)K1mandatoryoutput
The student hero photo is graded toward the brand palette #046CB8 / #02ADEFexpertThe dominant tones of students_hero_brandgraded_2x1.png are visibly pushed toward the brand blues #046CB8 (primary) and #02ADEF (secondary) without unnatural skin tonesK2mandatorybrand
The student hero horizon is straightened and the crop is cleanexpertThe tilted horizon from the raw handheld DSLR photo is corrected to level and the hero is cropped to the banner hero band with no awkward cuts of the studentsK3qualitybrief
The student cutout deliverable students_cutout_transparent.png is a background-removed transparent PNGautostudents_cutout_transparent.png is delivered as a PNG with a real alpha channel and the original background fully removed to transparencyK1mandatoryoutput
The student cutout has clean mask edges with no background fringingexpertEdges of the student group in students_cutout_transparent.png (hair, shoulders, notebook edges) are cleanly masked with no halo, fringe or leftover backgroundK3qualityoutput
The subject icon set deliverable subject_icons.svg is true scalable vector outputautosubject_icons.svg is delivered as a valid SVG containing vector <path> geometry (not an embedded raster image)K1mandatoryoutput
The vectorized set contains the six subject icons, stays crisp at 10-foot print size, and is in brand blueexpertsubject_icons.svg contains the six subject icons (beaker, sigma/maths, globe, open book, DNA helix, balance scale) as clean closed brand-blue (#046CB8) line shapes that stay sharp when scaled to large-format print, with no raster blurK2qualityinput
The halftone texture deliverable brand_halftone_texture.png exists in the brand coloursautobrand_halftone_texture.png is delivered as a PNG rendering a halftone/duotone geometric texture using the #046CB8 / #02ADEF brand coloursK1mandatoryoutput
The halftone texture reads as an understated, modern, minimal accentexpertbrand_halftone_texture.png reads as a subtle understated geometric accent (not a loud or busy pattern), consistent with the brief's 'modern, minimalistic feel' and 'understated geometric elements'K4qualitybrief
The JPEG proof deliverable DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_proof.jpg is a flattened proof of the banner layoutautoDYNAMIC_SOLUTION_banner_proof.jpg is delivered as a flattened JPEG showing the full banner layout for on-screen reviewK1mandatoryoutput
All verbatim banner copy is present and legible in the exported layoutexpertThe exported PDF/proof shows, legibly, the institute name DYNAMIC SOLUTION CLASSES, tagline 'Where Learning Meets Success', the Pre-Foundation / 9th-10th / 11th-12th course list, 'ADMISSIONS OPEN', the three phones (8103332901, 8989871223, 9589110600), 'Railway Station Road, Multai', and the four feature bullets (Small Batch Size / Personal Attention / Regular Tests / Doubt Sessions)K1dealbreakerbrief
Text hierarchy is crystal clear at full size with 8103332901 most prominent and headings in bold InterexpertAt full 10ft x 5ft size the headings/key numbers read in bold Inter, the phone number 8103332901 is the most prominent of the three phones, and the overall hierarchy is legible from a distance (per the client sign-off bar 'text hierarchy is crystal clear at full size')K3mandatorybrief
The Firefly sign-off board deliverable DYNAMIC_SOLUTION_signoff_board assembles all review assetsautoDYNAMIC_SOLUTION_signoff_board is a Firefly board deep-link assembling the graded hero, cutout plate, vector icons, halftone texture and the JPEG proof for client sign-offK6mandatoryoutput

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-69   TitanSeal Sealants Launch — Logo-System Extension, Swappable SKU Label/Carton Data-Merge, and Print-Ready + Digital Asset Package

Brand/vertical Real Estate, Construction & PropertyFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 13 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Take the approved TitanSeal master logo plus the client's authored InDesign label .indd and Illustrator carton .ai mechanicals, derive the full logo-variant system, data-merge the three SKUs into swappable print-ready label/carton artwork, and output the print PDFs plus a correctly-sized digital-asset pack.

Full brief

TitanSeal is a Dubai-based B2B private-label sealants brand launching three SKUs in January 2027. The master logo is approved and supplied as a high-res transparent PNG; the client also supplies two desktop-authored print mechanicals carrying real merge fields/variables (an InDesign wrap label .indd and an Illustrator carton .ai) and a sku_variants.csv with the three SKUs (600ml MS Polymer grey, 600ml PU Construction grey, 300ml Neutral Silicone white). \n\nUsing ONLY the Adobe connector, the freelancer must: (1) upload the supplied logo and mechanicals into a project workspace; (2) build the LOGO-VARIANT SYSTEM from the one master logo — a clean vector SVG (for scalable lockups), a single-color BLACK mono version and a single-color WHITE reversed/knockout version (for the black-on-cardboard carton and dark backgrounds), and a brand-blue color-overlay version — without redrawing the mark; (3) build the wide HORIZONTAL LOCKUP working canvas by cropping/reframing the square logo composition to a 3:1 banner band (a gen-free crop of the source, no invented pixels); (4) run the swappable-SKU LABEL data-merge: the authored .indd + sku_variants.csv produce one print-ready label mechanical per SKU so SKU codes/volumes swap by editing the CSV, then export those merged labels to press-ready PDF; (5) run the CARTON data-merge from the authored .ai Variables + CSV for the 20-cartridge and 24-tube single-color cartons and export to PDF/SVG; (6) assemble the DIGITAL pack — license a construction/jobsite background from Adobe Stock, color-grade it on-brand, then crop-and-resize it to the exact platform specs (1080x1080 social tile, 1920x1080 web banner, 350x120 email signature, and a 1:1 WhatsApp catalog card). \n\nOUT OF SCOPE for the connector (flag as human/desktop finishing): authoring the 5–8pp brand-standards PDF body copy and typography pages, drawing any new layout from scratch, and the Amazon UAE listing page composition. The connector's job is the asset/variant system, the merged swappable SKU mechanicals, the export-ready print PDFs, and the correctly-sized digital tiles.

Domain ecommerce-retailDeliverable packaging-labelWorkflow Create+EditOperation O5Family Layout & DataModality documentHorizon H4Est. calls 22Feasibility partialExec mode [C]+[T]Brand TitanSeal
5Planning4Brand strictness4Tool diversity4Visual density2Creative freedom
Capability footprint exercised: Instruction Following · Asset Utilization · Brand Alignment · Technical Quality · Process · Honesty. These tags are validated in Phase 0 · Gate B (presence · valid values · consistency with the brief · cross-tag coherence · de-dup) before the task is admitted to Phase 1. See the Phase 0 panel at the top of the page.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
titanseal_logo.svgvectorClean scalable SVG vectorized from the master logo PNG; solid-fill paths, usable in print lockups at any size.
titanseal_logo_mono_black.pngimageSingle-color solid black version of the mark on transparent background, for the black-on-cardboard carton.
titanseal_logo_reversed_white.pngimageSingle-color solid white reversed/knockout version on transparent background, for dark/photo backgrounds.
titanseal_logo_brandblue.pngimageBrand-blue color-overlay version of the mark on transparent background.
titanseal_horizontal_lockup.pngimage3:1 horizontal-lockup band cropped/reframed from the square logo composition (a horizontal band of the source, no invented pixels, output no larger than the source), centered on the mark; the top/bottom of the square padding is cropped to hit the ratio. For web banner/email use.
titanseal_labels_merged.pdfpdfPress-ready multi-page PDF, one page per SKU, from the authored .indd + CSV data-merge; 3mm bleed, swappable SKU codes/volumes via CSV.
titanseal_cartons_merged.pdfpdfSingle-color black carton artwork PDF for 20-cartridge and 24-tube packs, from the authored .ai Variables + CSV; die-line preserved.
titanseal_social_tile_1080.pngimage1080x1080 on-brand graded social media tile (jobsite background graded + cropped).
titanseal_web_banner_1920x1080.pngimage1920x1080 web banner crop of the graded jobsite asset.
titanseal_email_signature_350x120.pngimage350x120 email-signature crop of the graded jobsite asset.
titanseal_whatsapp_card_1x1.pngimage1:1 WhatsApp catalog product card crop of the graded jobsite asset.

The merged label PDF pages carry the exact per-SKU data from sku_variants.csv (SKU_NAME, SKU_CODE, VOLUME, COLORWAY) bound to the .indd merge fieldsautoAcross the label PDF pages, OCR/text extraction finds 'MS Polymer Sealant'/OBC-MS600-GRY/600ml/Grey, 'PU Construction Sealant'/OBC-PU600-GRY/600ml/Grey, and 'Neutral Silicone Sealant'/OBC-NS300-WHT/300ml/White each on its own pageK1dealbreakerinput

AO-74   Crumb & Co. wholesale bakery catalog: prep 12 product photos + InDesign catalog data-merge to a print-ready B2B PDF and hi-res JPEG

Brand/vertical retail_ecommerceFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 45)Tools 11 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Grade and consistently crop twelve raw bakery product photos, vectorize the Crumb & Co. wordmark, upload the prepped assets, then run the client's authored InDesign catalog template through a data-merge against the wholesale roster CSV to produce a print-ready B2B PDF and a high-resolution JPEG.

Full brief

Crumb & Co., a small-batch wholesale bakery, needs its B2B distributor catalog finished for print and PDF hand-out. The owner has already authored an InDesign catalog template (crumbco_catalog_template.indd) — an 8.5x11 portrait, multi-page grid layout with repeating product cells, each cell holding an image frame plus real InDesign data-merge fields (<<product_name>>, <<sku>>, <<description>>, <<case_pack>>, <<wholesale_price>>) and section headers for Breads, Pastries, Cakes & Slices, and Cookies & Bars, with a masthead frame for the wordmark and a full-bleed cover panel. The owner has also handed off the wholesale product roster as a CSV (12 SKUs across the four sections, each with a keyword-rich product description, case-pack quantity, and B2B wholesale price) and twelve raw product photos shot quickly on the bakery counter — sourdough boules, seeded rye, croissants, cinnamon rolls, a layer cake, brownies, chocolate-chip cookies, and more. The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, flat and dull, cool fluorescent white balance, and cluttered counter backgrounds. The job: (1) make every product photo look like a "high-quality image" — straighten the tilted frames, auto-tone to fix the flat exposure, warm the cool white balance toward a golden bakery look, push vibrance so crust and glaze read appetizing, apply a consistent bakery-warm preset across all twelve so they match, and crop every photo to the identical square (1:1) ratio the template's cells expect; (2) vectorize the flat Crumb & Co. wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so the masthead stays razor-sharp at print size; (3) upload all the prepped assets (twelve graded 1:1 photos, the vector wordmark) to Creative Cloud storage; (4) run the client's authored .indd through an InDesign data-merge against the roster CSV so every product cell binds its name, SKU, description, case pack, and wholesale price, with each graded photo dropping into its matching cell; (5) export the merged catalog to a print-ready ~300 DPI PDF for the print run and a high-resolution JPEG of the cover/spread for emailing to distributors. Deliverable dimensions: US Letter 8.5x11 portrait, print-ready CMYK-quality PDF plus a max-quality JPEG. Brand constraints: keep the warm/golden bakery palette consistent across every photo, the wordmark must stay crisp at masthead scale, and all descriptions/prices bind verbatim from the supplied roster CSV — do not rewrite the copy.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
crumbco_catalog_template.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a multi-page US Le
crumbco_wholesale_roster.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns product_name,sku,description,case_pack,wholesale_price,image and a section column, holding 1

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 45 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
CrumbCo_Wholesale_Catalog_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready multi-page US Letter (8.5x11) portrait PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign catalog; every product cell bound from the roster CSV (name/SKU/description/case-pack/wholesale price) with its matching graded 1:1 photo placed, vector wordmark in the masthead; ~300 DPI print quality.
CrumbCo_Wholesale_Catalog_cover.jpgimageHigh-resolution JPEG of the bound catalog cover/first spread (US Letter portrait, max-quality) for emailing to B2B distributors.
graded_product_photos_x12imageTwelve color-graded, straightened, consistently 1:1-cropped bakery product photos matching the template cells, sharing a warm/golden bakery look.
crumbco_wordmark.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the Crumb & Co. wordmark for crisp scaling in the catalog masthead.

checkbypass conditionfeedsweight

Deliverable 'crumbco_wordmark.svg' deliveredautofile present, opens, format = SVG/vectorK1mandatory

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds

Layer 5 · Professional review — the human verdict (each a fixed choice + one-line reason)

instrumentwhat the expert doesproduces
The callaccept / send-back / scrap (+ one-line reason)feeds the commission pass rate
Punch-listrevision notes tagged blocker / fix / nitpicka cost-to-ship number
Pin-the-flawclick the spot, pick the defect, tag severity (Critical/Major/Minor)localized, weighted defect labels
Blind face-offtwo outputs side by side: which would you send the client?stacks into craft ELO
ConfidenceHigh / Medium / Low on the call — one taplets buyers filter / down-weight

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness ★A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-75   Northgate Dental Congress 2027: delegate name-badge data-merge from roster CSV into authored InDesign template, print-ready imposed PDF

Brand/vertical events_entertainmentFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 17)Tools 9 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Take our approved event wordmark, delegate roster CSV (~1,000 rows) and authored InDesign badge template, prep and vectorize the wordmark plus the four role QR marks, then run an InDesign data-merge to bind every delegate into a personalised name badge and export a print-ready imposed PDF for the lanyard printer.

Full brief

Northgate Dental Students' Congress 2027 is a two-day event bringing roughly 1,000 delegates from dental schools across the country together for speakers, workshops, a gala dinner and socials. Our brand identity is already signed off, and we now need the physical delegate name badges produced from our attendee list — one personalised badge per delegate. We have handed off everything the job needs: our approved event wordmark as a flat high-contrast PNG (northgate_wordmark.png); an authored InDesign badge template (badge_template.indd) laid out at 90x130mm portrait with 3mm bleed, containing real InDesign Data Merge fields (<<FullName>>, <<School>>, <<Role>>, <<QRPath>>), a placeholder frame for the vectorized wordmark, a full-width role colour-band frame, and a QR image frame; our delegate roster export (delegate_roster.csv) with one row per delegate holding full name, school, role (Delegate / Speaker / Committee / Press) and the matching role-QR filename; and four flat role-band QR marks as PNGs (one per role, each a solid-colour block with the check-in QR). The wordmark PNG is slightly oversized and off-centre for the badge frame and needs cleaning and vectorizing so it stays razor-sharp at print size; the four QR PNGs are inconsistent sizes and must be squared and cropped to the badge's QR frame. The job: (1) crop/clean the wordmark and square-crop the four role QRs to the exact frame ratios the template expects; (2) vectorize the flat wordmark into a clean SVG so it prints crisp on ~1,000 badges; (3) upload all prepped assets to our workspace; (4) run the InDesign data-merge so every roster row binds its name, school, role band and role QR into the authored template — one badge record per delegate; (5) export the merged document to a print-ready imposed PDF (90x130mm + bleed, ~300 DPI, crop marks) for the lanyard printer. Roles, names and schools all come from the roster as-is — nothing is invented; long names must bind into the template's auto-fit name frame without clipping.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)

delegate_roster.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a realistic ~1,000-row delegate roster CSV with header row exactly: FullName,School,Role,QRPath. Populate FullN
