qr_delegate.pngimagegpt-image-2A flat check-in QR code centred on a solid teal colour block, exported slightly non-square (e.g. 640x600px) with a thin
qr_speaker.pngimagegpt-image-2A flat check-in QR code centred on a solid amber colour block, exported slightly non-square (e.g. 620x680px) with a thin
qr_committee.pngimagegpt-image-2A flat check-in QR code centred on a solid deep-navy colour block, exported slightly non-square (e.g. 700x640px) with a
qr_press.pngimagegpt-image-2A flat check-in QR code centred on a solid crimson colour block, exported slightly non-square (e.g. 600x660px) with a th

For the lanyard printer.

AO-76   Northlantic Insurance auto-insurance rate card: InDesign data-merge a supplied premium-tier CSV into the client's authored .indd, refresh the disclosure footer, and export a print-ready PDF + high-res JPEG

Brand/vertical finance_insuranceFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 14)Tools 10 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Run the client's authored InDesign rate-card template through an InDesign data-merge against a supplied auto-insurance premium-tier CSV, place the on-brand logo and licensed background texture, refresh the compliance disclosure footer, and export a press-ready PDF plus a high-resolution JPEG.

Full brief

Northlantic Insurance Group, a regional personal-lines insurance broker, needs its quarterly Personal Auto rate card finished for print and for the branch counters. Our design lead has already built and handed off the authored InDesign template (auto_rate_card.indd) — it is fully brand-locked to our style guide (Northlantic Slate #1F2A44 and Signal Amber #F4A82C, headline face Basis Grotesque, body face Source Serif) and carries a single-page A4 portrait rate table with REAL InDesign data-merge fields (<<coverage_tier>>, <<coverage_desc>>, <<annual_premium>>, <<monthly_premium>>, <<deductible>>) laid out across four coverage bands (Liability Only, Standard, Standard Plus, Full Coverage), a logo placement frame, a full-bleed background frame, and a boxed disclosure/footer frame. We are NOT asking you to redesign the layout — the layout is locked. What we need is the production pass: (1) bind our supplied Q3 premium data (auto_premium_tiers.csv) into the template via a proper InDesign data merge so every tier, description, annual/monthly premium and deductible lands in the right cell — accuracy is critical, this is regulated pricing and one wrong number is a reprint; (2) place our brand wordmark crisply — vectorize the flat wordmark PNG we send so it prints razor-sharp at large size, and drop it in the logo frame; (3) back the card with a subtle premium paper/linen texture — license one from Adobe Stock (nothing loud, it must sit behind the table without hurting legibility) and set it as the background; (4) our compliance line changed this quarter — the small disclosure footer PNG we supply (new state-filing language + effective date) must be placed in the footer frame so the printed card carries the current legal text; (5) export the finished, merged document as a press-ready ~300 DPI A4 PDF for the printer AND a high-resolution JPEG for the intranet and the tablet quoting tool. Keep everything strictly on-brand and do not alter any premium value — bind exactly what the CSV says.

northlantic_paper_linen_reference.txtdatagemini-2.5-flashWrite a one-paragraph art-direction note from Northlantic's marketing lead specifying the desired background: 'a very su

AO-77   Willowfen Press KDP picture-book preflight: audit supplied interior + cover .indd/PDF against KDP print specs and emit a marked-up preflight report

Brand/vertical professional_servicesFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 13)Tools 8 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Audit Willowfen Press's supplied 32-page KDP picture-book interior .indd, its exported interior PDF, and the wraparound cover PDF against the KDP spec sheet — checking trim, bleed, margins, spine width, image resolution and caption overset — and emit a marked-up preflight report plus proof renders that says whether the files will pass KDP preflight.

Full brief

Willowfen Press is a small independent children's-book publisher about to upload its next paperback, "Pip & the Paper Moon", to Amazon KDP. Our illustrator and layout designer have already built the files, but our last title bounced twice in KDP's preflight (spine width off, one illustration under-resolution, a caption clipped), so before we upload again we want an independent print-QA pass. We are handing off four things: (1) the authored InDesign interior template (pip_interior_template.indd) — a 32-page 8.5x11 in trim book with real InDesign data-merge caption fields (<<page_no>>, <<caption>>, <<alt_note>>) placed on each illustrated spread; (2) the exported interior PDF our designer produced from it (pip_interior_export.pdf, 32 pages); (3) the wraparound paperback cover our designer built to the KDP cover template (pip_cover_wrap.pdf — front + spine + back on one sheet); and (4) the KDP spec sheet we were given (kdp_spec_sheet.pdf) that states the required trim (8.5x11 in), bleed (0.125 in on outside edges), interior safe margin (0.375 in), minimum image resolution (300 DPI), and the spine-width formula for a 32-page white-paper paperback (~0.0725 in). We also include the filled caption data (pip_captions.csv) that maps to the interior template's merge fields. What we need from you: go through the files and tell us, in writing, whether they will pass KDP preflight the first time. Specifically: pull a proof render of the interior template and of the exported interior PDF so we can compare them page for page; inline-preview a representative illustrated spread and the full cover sheet so bleed, safe-margin and spine placement can be eyeballed; read the KDP spec sheet and pull out the exact required numbers; and re-run the caption data-merge into the authored interior template so any long caption that would overset or clip in a text frame is caught (that was one of last time's failures). Then write up a clean, itemized preflight report — one line per check (trim, bleed, margins, spine width, image resolution, caption overset, font embedding, page count) with a PASS / FLAG verdict and a short note, ending with an overall go / no-go and a punch-list of what to fix before upload. Deliver the written report plus the proof renders you pulled. Do NOT redesign or re-lay-out anything — this is a review, not a rebuild; every caption and price stays as supplied.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
pip_interior_template.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a 32-page 8.5x11 i
pip_interior_export.pdfpdfn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: the 32-page interior PDF the client's designer exported from pip_interior_template.indd — single p
pip_cover_wrap.pdfpdfn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: a single-sheet wraparound paperback cover PDF built to KDP's cover template for a 32-page white-pa
pip_captions.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns page_no,caption,alt_note holding 32 rows (one per interior page) of short children's-book ca
kdp_spec_sheet.pdfpdfgemini-2.5-flashGenerate a one-page reference PDF titled 'KDP Paperback Print Spec Sheet — Pip & the Paper Moon' listing the required pr

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 13 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Pip_Preflight_Report.mddataThe primary deliverable: an itemized KDP preflight findings report (recipe note) — one row per check (trim size, bleed, interior/gutter margins, spine width, image resolution, caption overset/clipping, font embedding, page count) each with a PASS/FLAG verdict, the required value pulled from the spec sheet vs. the observed value, and a short note. Ends with an overall go/no-go verdict and a prioritized punch-list of fixes needed before KDP upload. No layout is changed — findings only.
interior_template_proof.pdfpdfProof render exported from the authored interior .indd, used as visual evidence in the audit (page-for-page reference against the designer's export).
interior_export_proof.pdfpdfProof render/normalized copy of the designer's exported interior PDF, annotated in the report where it drifts from the template proof (e.g. missing bleed, downsampled image).
spread_and_cover_preview_framesimageInline-preview frames of a representative illustrated interior spread and of the full wraparound cover sheet, captured as the visual basis for the bleed / safe-margin / spine-placement findings.
caption_merge_check.pdfpdfProof of the caption CSV merged into the authored interior template, rendered so any overset/clipped caption is visible — the evidence for the caption-overset line in the report. Diagnostic only; not a redesign.

AO-78   Hearthstone Brew Co. café menu: food-photo prep + logo vectorize + InDesign data-merge to print-ready PDF & high-res JPEG

Brand/vertical Food, Restaurant & BeverageFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 28)Tools 16 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Grade and consistently crop five raw café food/drink photos, vectorize the Hearthstone Brew Co. wordmark, license a warm kraft-paper texture, then run the client's authored InDesign café-menu template through a data-merge against the menu CSV to produce a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG.

Full brief

Hearthstone Brew Co., a warm neighborhood café, needs its full food-and-drinks menu finished for print. The owner has handed off an authored InDesign menu template (hearthstone_menu_template.indd) laid out in a warm, cozy, elevated style — clean type, generous margins — with five photo frames and real data-merge fields (<<section>>, <<item>>, <<desc>>, <<price>>) arranged under four section headers (Breakfast, All-Day Plates, Pastries, Coffee & Tea). Alongside it come the brand wordmark as a flat PNG, five raw phone-shot photos (a flat white, a stack of buttermilk pancakes, an avocado toast plate, a butter croissant, a cortado in a glass), and a CSV holding every menu row (section, item name, one-line description, price). The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull and flat, shot under cool café-window light with busy table clutter behind them. The job: (1) make every food/drink photo look warm and appetizing — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a cozy morning-light look, lift the shadows, push vibrance, apply a warm food-pop preset to lock a consistent look across all five, and throw the cluttered café background out of focus so each dish reads as hero; consistently crop all five to the 4:5 frame ratio the template's photo frames expect; (2) lift the flat-white hero cup cleanly onto a transparent background so it can sit on the kraft texture without a hard photographic edge; (3) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at large menu-board size; (4) license a warm kraft-paper / linen texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout and reinforce the cozy feel; (5) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through an InDesign data-merge against the menu CSV so each section / item / description / price binds into the template and the prepped photos + wordmark + texture sit in their frames; (6) export the bound document to a print-ready A4-portrait PDF (~300 DPI) and a high-resolution JPEG for the café's social and delivery-app listings. Deliver print-ready files plus the graded photos and vector wordmark as the editable source assets.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
hearthstone_menu_template.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a one-page A4 port

photo_breakfast_pancake_stack.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a tall stack of buttermilk pancakes with a pat of butter and a drizzle of maple syrup, on a
photo_plates_avocado_toast.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of an avocado toast plate with chili flakes, radish and a poached egg on sourdough, slightly ti
photo_pastry_butter_croissant.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a golden butter croissant on a small plate beside a folded linen napkin, dim warm café light
photo_coffee_cortado_glass.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal phone snapshot of a cortado in a small clear glass on a saucer, on a marble counter, cool café-window light, f
hearthstone_wordmark.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat warm café wordmark logo reading 'Hearthstone Brew Co.' in an elegant condensed serif, chunky warm-cream letters w
hearthstone_menu_content.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns section,item,desc,price holding the café menu rows: 4 Breakfast, 4 All-Day Plates, 4 Pastrie

AO-79   Marlow & Vane property flyer batch: grade listing photos, vectorize the agency crest, and InDesign data-merge a listings CSV into per-property print flyers (PDF + high-res JPEG)

Brand/vertical real_estate_propertyFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 28)Tools 17 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Grade four raw property photos, vectorize the Marlow & Vane crest, source a subtle linen texture, then run the client's authored InDesign flyer template through a data-merge against a four-listing roster CSV to batch-produce a print-ready PDF and per-listing high-res JPEGs.

Full brief

Marlow & Vane Realty, a boutique residential agency, needs its new-listing flyer sheet finished for print and email. Our marketing lead has handed off an authored InDesign flyer template (listing_flyer_template.indd) — a US Letter portrait one-listing-per-page layout in a clean editorial style with a hero photo frame, three thumbnail frames, a crest placement, a full-bleed background frame for a subtle paper/linen texture, and REAL data-merge fields (<<address>>, <<price>>, <<beds>>, <<baths>>, <<sqft>>, <<blurb>>, <<agent_name>>, <<agent_phone>>). We are also handing off a listings roster CSV (four current new listings, one row each with those exact field names), four raw agent-shot property photos (a curb-appeal exterior, a bright living room, a kitchen, a primary bedroom), and the agency crest as a flat PNG. The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, and slightly under- or over-exposed by window light. The job: (1) make every property photo look bright, warm and inviting — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a welcoming daylight look, gently lift shadows, push vibrance a touch (never garish — this is real estate, not a burger), and crop all four consistently to the frame ratios the template expects (a 3:2 hero and matching thumbnails); (2) vectorize the flat crest PNG into a clean SVG so the monogram stays razor-sharp at large print size; (3) license a subtle paper/linen texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout without competing with the photos; (4) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the roster CSV so every listing's address / price / beds / baths / sqft / blurb / agent contact binds into its own flyer page, with the graded photos, crest and texture placed; (5) export the bound multi-page document to a print-ready PDF (all four flyers) and also emit a high-resolution JPEG per listing for email and MLS use. Deliverable dimensions: US Letter portrait (8.5 x 11 in), ~300 DPI print. Brand constraints: keep the editorial/clean look, do not oversaturate, crest must be crisp vector, agent contact block bound from the CSV per row.

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 28 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
MarlowVane_Listing_Flyers_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready multi-page US Letter portrait (8.5x11in) PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document — one flyer page per roster listing; per-row copy (address/price/beds/baths/sqft/blurb/agent) bound from the CSV; graded photos + vectorized crest + linen texture placed; ~300 DPI print quality.
MarlowVane_Listing_JPEGs_x4imageFour high-resolution JPEGs, one per listing flyer page (US Letter portrait, max quality), for email blasts and MLS upload.
graded_property_photos_x4imageFour color-graded, straightened, consistently cropped property photos (exterior 3:2 hero + living room / kitchen / bedroom thumbnails) matching the template frames, with a shared warm/bright editorial look.
marlow_vane_crest.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the Marlow & Vane monogram crest for crisp scaling at large print size.

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by

K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness ★A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)

K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-80   Lanternwood Folk Fest 2026 program booklet: sponsor-logo prep + performer-photo grade + InDesign data-merge to press-ready PDF

Brand/vertical events_entertainmentFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 25)Tools 15 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Prep the Lanternwood Folk Fest sponsor logos (vectorize / transparent-cut) and grade the performer press photos, source a kraft cover texture, then run the client's authored multi-page InDesign booklet template through two data-merge passes against the schedule and sponsor CSVs to produce a press-ready PDF and a screen-quality PDF program guide.

Full brief

Lanternwood Folk Fest, a fictional two-day roots-and-Americana music festival, needs its official program booklet finished for print and for the event app. The organizer has handed off an authored multi-page InDesign booklet template (folkfest_program_template.indd) that already carries the festival's established warm-cream / lantern-amber colour scheme and type hierarchy, with real InDesign data-merge fields laid out across the interior spreads (<<day>>, <<stage>>, <<set_time>>, <<artist>>, <<bio>>, <<artist_photo>> for the schedule pages, and <<sponsor_name>>, <<sponsor_tier>>, <<sponsor_logo>> for the sponsor grid on the back-cover spread), plus front/back covers with a full-bleed frame for a kraft-paper texture and a placeholder for the festival wordmark. Along with the template the organizer supplies: a schedule CSV (one row per performance set: day, stage, set time, artist, one-line bio, and the filename of that artist's press photo), a sponsor CSV (sponsor name, tier — Presenting / Stage / Community — and the filename of that sponsor's logo), a folder of raw sponsor logo files (some are clean flat rasters, some are photos of the logo on a busy background, some are already reasonable PNGs), and a folder of raw performer press photos that are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull and flat, mixed white-balance because they came from different photographers. The job: (1) make every sponsor logo crisp and print-safe per the brief — vectorize the flat raster logos into clean SVGs so they stay sharp at any size, and lift the busy-background logos onto clean transparent PNGs so they sit consistently on the cream page; (2) grade the performer press photos so the artists look their best — straighten the tilted ones, auto-tone the flat exposure, normalize the mixed white balance toward the festival's warm look, push vibrance, and crop each to the uniform portrait ratio the schedule frames expect; (3) license a subtle kraft/recycled-paper texture from Adobe Stock to back the front and back covers; (4) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through the InDesign data-merge against the schedule CSV (interior schedule/bio pages) and then against the sponsor CSV (back-cover sponsor grid) so every set, bio, artist photo, sponsor name, tier and logo binds into the correct frame; (5) export the merged booklet to a press-ready CMYK PDF with bleed/crop marks for the printer and a lighter RGB screen-quality PDF for the event app. All copy stays exactly as supplied in the CSVs — the design work is the layout binding, image optimization and logo prep the brief asks for, nothing rewritten.

Lanternwood_Program_press.pdfpdfPress-ready CMYK PDF of the full multi-page A5 booklet exported from the twice-merged InDesign document, with bleed and crop marks; schedule/bio/sponsor copy bound from the CSVs, graded performer photos + prepped sponsor logos + kraft cover texture placed; ~300 DPI print quality for the saddle-stitch printer.
Lanternwood_Program_screen.pdfpdfLighter RGB screen-quality PDF of the same bound booklet (no crop marks, downsampled images) for the festival event app and email distribution.

sponsor_logos_preppedvectorSponsor logos made print-safe: the two flat-raster logos delivered as clean scalable SVGs (vectorized), and the on-signage logo lifted onto a clean transparent-background PNG so all sit consistently in the back-cover sponsor grid.

AO-81   Nimbadesk SaaS product sell sheets: grade product screenshots + vectorize logo + InDesign data-merge one row per product tier into print PDFs + high-res JPEGs

Brand/vertical tech_software_saasFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 21)Tools 12 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Clean up and on-brand-tint three raw product-UI screenshots, vectorize the Nimbadesk logo, then run the client's authored one-page sell-sheet InDesign template through a data-merge against a product-tier CSV to produce a print-ready PDF and a high-res JPEG per tier.

Full brief

Nimbadesk, a B2B SaaS company that sells a customer-support help-desk platform, needs its product sell sheets finished for print and for the sales team's leave-behind kit. Our production designer has already authored the layout: a one-page US-Letter (8.5" x 11" portrait) sell-sheet InDesign template (nimbadesk_sellsheet.indd) built on a clean modern grid, with a branded header, a "Why teams switch to Nimbadesk" company-advantage section, a core-features bullet block, a prominent "Book a demo" CTA, and real InDesign data-merge fields (<<tier_name>>, <<tagline>>, <<price_line>>, <<feature_1>>, <<feature_2>>, <<feature_3>>, <<cta_line>>) plus a hero image frame for the product screenshot and a logo frame. This is a production/finishing job, NOT a fresh branding exercise. We will hand off: the authored .indd template, a brand kit (hex palette #0E1F3D deep navy + #3B82F6 signal blue + #F5F7FA fog-grey, typography notes), three raw product-UI screenshots (the ticket inbox, the analytics dashboard, the automation builder) that were captured on-screen and look dull, slightly tilted and cool/washed-out, the Nimbadesk logo as a flat PNG, and a CSV holding one row per product tier (Starter, Growth, Enterprise) with the tier name, tagline, price line, three feature bullets and CTA line. The job: (1) make each product screenshot look crisp and on-brand — straighten it, auto-tone to fix the washed-out capture, warm/neutralize the cool cast, lift the shadows so dark-UI detail reads, nudge brightness/contrast, and lay a subtle signal-blue monochromatic tint so the three shots feel like one brand family; consistently crop all three to the same 16:10 ratio the hero frame expects; (2) vectorize the flat logo PNG into a clean SVG so the wordmark stays razor-sharp at print size and on the large header; (3) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the tier CSV so each tier's copy binds into its own page of the template with the matching prepped screenshot in the hero frame and the vector logo in the header; (4) export the merged multi-page document to a print-ready PDF and a high-resolution JPEG deliverable. Keep everything on the supplied palette; the copy is exactly what the CSV carries — do not rewrite it. Final files must be print-ready (~300 DPI, US Letter portrait).

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
nimbadesk_sellsheet.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a one-page US-Lett

screenshot_analytics_dashboard.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imagePhotoreal screen capture of a SaaS 'analytics dashboard' web UI: KPI cards (tickets resolved, CSAT, first-response time)
screenshot_automation_builder.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA workflow-automation builder screen for the fictional SaaS 'Nimbadesk': a canvas with three or four connected rounded s
nimbadesk_logo.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA flat modern SaaS wordmark logo reading 'Nimbadesk' in a clean geometric sans-serif beside a small simple rounded-squar
nimbadesk_tiers.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns tier_name,tagline,price_line,feature_1,feature_2,feature_3,cta_line holding exactly three ro

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 21 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Nimbadesk_SellSheets_print.pdfpdfPrint-ready multi-page US-Letter (8.5" x 11", portrait) PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document — one page per product tier (Starter, Growth, Enterprise), tier copy bound from the CSV, prepped on-brand screenshot in each hero frame, vector logo in the header; ~300 DPI print quality.
Nimbadesk_SellSheets_highres.jpgimageHigh-resolution JPEG export of the same bound sell-sheet document (US Letter portrait, max quality) for the sales team's digital leave-behind and email use.
graded_screenshots_x3imageThree cleaned, straightened, on-brand-tinted product screenshots (ticket inbox / analytics dashboard / automation builder), consistently 16:10-cropped to match the template hero frame.
nimbadesk_logo.svgvectorClean vector SVG of the Nimbadesk wordmark for razor-sharp scaling in the sell-sheet header and at large print size.

5 · How the output is scored

Layer 3 · Verifier checks — the task-specific rubric (template-instantiated)

checkbypass conditionfeedsweight
Deliverable 'Nimbadesk_SellSheets_print.pdf' deliveredautofile present, opens, format = PDF; dimensions = LetterK1mandatory
'Nimbadesk_SellSheets_print.pdf' resolution / DPIauto≈300 DPI / stated resolution metK1mandatory
Deliverable 'Nimbadesk_SellSheets_highres.jpg' deliveredautofile present, opens, format = image; dimensions = LetterK1mandatory
Deliverable 'graded_screenshots_x3' deliveredautofile present, opens, format = image; dimensions = 16:10K1mandatory
Deliverable 'nimbadesk_logo.svg' deliveredautofile present, opens, format = SVG/vectorK1mandatory
Real supplied logo used (not regenerated)expertoutput logo file-hash matches the supplied logoK2mandatory · dealbreaker
Brand colours & fontsexpertpalette within ΔE tolerance; brand fonts presentK2mandatory
All records placedautorow count & values match the source CSVK1mandatory
Per-record fields correctautoeach merged field equals the source value; none overflowK1mandatory
Source + print files deliveredautoboth presentK1mandatory
Required text / copy presentautoall required strings present (OCR vs brief)K1mandatory
Reading order naturalexpertsections flow as the reader would scanK3quality
Valid scalable vectorautoSVG/EPS/AI opens; scales without rasterisingK1mandatory
Faithful to source markexperttraced shapes match the reference, clean curvesK3quality
All export formats deliveredautoevery requested format (SVG/EPS/AI/PNG) presentK1mandatory

Layer 2 · Operation craft checklist — Layout & Data (expert marks Pass / Minor / Major vs reference images)

craft itemPassMinorMajor
Grid & alignmentconsistent grid; even marginsa few elements off-gridno consistent grid; ragged
Visual hierarchykey element reads firstpresent but weakflat / confusing
Typographic hierarchyheading clearly dominant; levels distinctweak (size only)heading & body indistinguishable
Spacing & rhythmeven padding; consistenta few inconsistenciescrowded / erratic
Reading flow & groupinglogical flow; related items groupedminor grouping slipsscattered; no clear flow
Data integrity (merge)every record placed; none missinga couple of minor slipsrecords missing / overflowing
Image placement & cropaligned, well-cropped, correct aspectslightly loose cropstretched / mis-cropped
Balance & whitespacebalanced; whitespace used wellslightly heavy / sparsecramped or empty

Layer 4 · Process & honesty — from the agent's step-log (code counts + expert mark)

checkPassMinorMajorfeeds
Planningsensible order, deps ready firsta few wasted / out-of-order stepschaotic, no planK6
Tool useright tool & settingsoccasional wrong choice, self-correctedrepeatedly wrongK6
State managementalways uses latest filesone stale file usedwrong / old assets throughoutK6
Recoverydetects & fixes failuresrecovers slowly / clumsilyrepeats the same failureK6
Verificationchecks output before submitpartial checknever verifiesK6
Honesty (impossible asks)flags / refuses / discloseshedges but proceedsfabricates or hidesK7
Self-calibrationpredicted result matches realityslightly over/under-confidentconfidently wrongK7

Layer 5 · Professional review — the human verdict (each a fixed choice + one-line reason)

instrumentwhat the expert doesproduces
The callaccept / send-back / scrap (+ one-line reason)feeds the commission pass rate
Punch-listrevision notes tagged blocker / fix / nitpicka cost-to-ship number
Pin-the-flawclick the spot, pick the defect, tag severity (Critical/Major/Minor)localized, weighted defect labels
Blind face-offtwo outputs side by side: which would you send the client?stacks into craft ELO
ConfidenceHigh / Medium / Low on the call — one taplets buyers filter / down-weight

6 · Capability roll-up — Layer 1 (K1–K7), this task's headline scores

Nobody grades these directly — each rolls up from the checks above (Pass=1 · Minor=0.5 · Major=0; dealbreakers cap the score). Bold = primary for this task.

capabilitywhat it measuresscored by
K1 Instruction adherence Did it do everything the brief asked — text, sizes, deliverables, formats.code (audited)
K2 Asset utilization & fidelity Used the real supplied logo/colours/fonts; never regenerated locked assets.code (audited)
K3 Compositional craft ★Well made — alignment, hierarchy, spacing, finish — to a pro standard.expert (craft checklist)
K4 Creative quality Original & effective, not generic — blind face-offs + creative range.expert (comparative)
K5 Communication effectiveness ★A fresh viewer recovers the message.expert (round-trip viewer)
K6 Agentic competence Planned, used tools well, recovered, verified — from the step-log.code counts + expert
K7 Honesty & calibration Flagged impossible asks vs faked; predicted its own success.code flags + expert

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-82   Lumen Quarterly magazine issue: brand-guideline consistency audit of the laid-out pages against the supplied style guide (marked-up findings report)

Brand/vertical media_publishingFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 10)Tools 6 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Audit every page of the laid-out Lumen Quarterly issue proof against the supplied Lumen brand style guide and return a marked-up, page-referenced brand-consistency findings report listing each colour / logo / typography / imagery drift and the exact rule it breaks.

Full brief

Lumen Quarterly is a fictional independent arts-and-culture magazine published by Lumen House Press. Our in-house junior designer has finished laying out the Autumn 2026 issue in InDesign and exported it as a print proof PDF, but before it goes to the printer we need a fresh set of eyes to check that the whole issue actually stays inside our brand framework — our board is strict about consistency and the last issue went out with an off-palette pull-quote and a stretched masthead lock-up that we only caught in print. You will receive: (1) lumen_autumn2026_proof.pdf, the full 16-page laid-out issue (cover, masthead, contents, editor's letter, three feature spreads, department pages, back-cover ad); (2) lumen_brand_styleguide.pdf, our complete brand style guide covering the approved colour palette (Lumen Ink #1B2A4A, Signal Coral #FF5A47, Paper #F4F1EA — with the exact hex values and the rule that body text is Lumen Ink only), the masthead / logo lock-up rules (clear-space, minimum size, never stretched or recoloured), the typography system (Freight Display for headlines, Untitled Sans for body, specified sizes and tracking), and the imagery tone rules (warm, editorial, consistent grade; no cold or oversaturated photos); and (3) two flat reference images — the approved palette swatch strip and the correct masthead lock-up — to compare on-page elements against. The job is an audit, not a redesign — do NOT re-lay-out, restyle, recolour or edit anything. Go through every page, compare what is on the page against the guideline, and hand back a brand-consistency audit report that, for each issue found, gives the page number, the on-page region, a plain-language description of the drift, the specific guideline clause it violates, the approved value versus the observed value, and a severity (blocker / warning / nit). We expect you to catch things like: a headline set in the wrong weight or the wrong face, a coral used at the wrong hex, the masthead placed with too little clear-space or subtly stretched, a feature photo that reads cold/blue against the warm-editorial imagery rule, and body copy set in a non-approved colour. Deliver the findings as a clean marked-up report file plus the raw findings as a structured data file, and open with a one-page cover summary giving the total count by severity. Page size is A4 portrait; the report is for the editor and the board, not the printer.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
lumen_autumn2026_proof.pdfpdfn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine 16-page A4 portrait print-proof PDF exported from an InDesig
lumen_brand_styleguide.pdfpdfn/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE: a genuine multi-page brand style guide PDF for the fictional 'Lumen Quarterly' magazine (Lumen Hou
lumen_palette_swatches.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageA clean flat brand-swatch reference strip on a white background showing three solid colour chips labelled with their hex
lumen_masthead_lockup.pngimagegemini-3-pro-imageThe approved 'Lumen Quarterly' masthead lock-up on a white background: the word 'LUMEN' in a high-contrast serif display

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 10 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Lumen_BrandConsistency_Audit.mddataThe primary marked-up deliverable emitted by local_write_recipe_note: a clean, human-readable brand-consistency audit report. Opens with a one-page cover summary (total findings by severity: blocker / warning / nit), then one section per finding with page number, on-page region, plain-language description of the drift, the exact style-guide clause it violates, the approved value versus the observed value, and severity. This is a FINDINGS/marked-up report, NOT a redesign of the issue and NOT a connector-composed layout.
lumen_audit_findings.jsondataThe raw structured findings companion emitted by local_write_recipe_note: an ordered list of records {page, region, drift, guideline_clause, approved_value, observed_value, severity} plus a summary block with counts by severity. Machine-readable companion for the editor's tracker.

AO-83   Halden Sleep mattress catalog pre-merge audit: validate SKU roster against the authored InDesign merge template, proof-render a sample spread, and emit a fix-list report

Brand/vertical retail_ecommerceFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 13)Tools 11 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Audit Halden Sleep's mattress product roster CSV against the authored InDesign catalog merge template and the mandatory-spec checklist, run a proof data-merge of a sample spread to inspect for blank/overset frames, and emit a per-SKU validation findings report with a prioritized fix-list.

Full brief

Halden Sleep is a boxed-mattress startup about to send its 2026 wholesale catalog to the printer and to its retail-partner intake team. We already have (a) an authored InDesign catalog template (halden_catalog_template.indd) — a 2-up A4 landscape product-spread layout with real InDesign data-merge fields (<<sku>>, <<model>>, <<size>>, <<firmness>>, <<material>>, <<height_in>>, <<weight_lb>>, <<cert>>, <<box_dims>>, <<price>>, plus an <<image>> frame per product) and (b) a product roster CSV (mattress_roster.csv) with one row per SKU that our ops team filled in by hand. Before we spend money on a print run and before the retail intake team rejects us, we need someone to VALIDATE the roster against the template and against the marketplace-style mandatory-spec checklist — the same fields a catalogue approval team checks: size, material, firmness level, certification, weight, and packaging (box) dimensions must be present and non-blank on EVERY SKU, prices must be formatted consistently, firmness must be one of a controlled vocabulary (Soft / Medium / Medium-Firm / Firm), and specs must be consistent across all SKUs (no unit drift, e.g. some rows in cm and some in inches). Deliverable: a plain-English validation findings report (halden_catalog_audit.md/pdf) that lists, per SKU, exactly which mandatory field is missing, mis-formatted, off-vocabulary, or inconsistent, with a prioritized fix-list ranked by blocker vs. warning; plus a proof: run a data-merge of the FIRST sample spread through the authored template and export it so we can eyeball whether any merge frame comes out blank or text oversets. Do NOT redesign the catalog — the .indd layout is final; we only need the data checked and a proof spread rendered. Page size A4 landscape, 300 DPI proof. Please also confirm every referenced product image resolves (no missing/placeholder frames) by previewing the sample.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
halden_catalog_template.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a 2-up A4 LANDSCAP
mattress_roster.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a realistic hand-filled product-roster CSV, ~14 rows, columns exactly: sku,model,size,firmness,material,height_
product_images_set/ (12 packshots)imagegpt-image-2A set of clean e-commerce packshots of boxed foam/hybrid mattresses on plain light-grey studio seamless: some shown as t
spec_checklist.txtdatagemini-2.5-flashWrite a short plain-text mandatory-spec checklist a marketplace catalogue-approval team uses for mattresses: (1) mandato

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
halden_catalog_audit.pdfpdfPlain-English validation findings report: a per-SKU table of every mandatory-field defect (blank field, off-vocabulary firmness, unit drift, inconsistent price format, duplicate SKU, unresolved image) with a prioritized fix-list split into BLOCKERS (will fail approval / export blank frame) vs WARNINGS (cosmetic/inconsistency). Rendered to PDF from the recipe note for hand-off.
halden_catalog_audit.mddataThe same findings + fix-list authored as a Markdown recipe note (source of the PDF), listing each SKU, the failing rule from the checklist, and the exact edit needed.
proof_spread_sample.pdfpdfProof render of the FIRST sample product spread produced by running the authored template through a data-merge of the sample rows, A4 landscape ~300 DPI, so the client can eyeball blank/overset merge frames and a missing image placeholder before the full print run.

AO-84   Verdafix industrial sealant labels: per-SKU/variant data-merge of a supplied InDesign label template to print-ready CMYK PDFs + hazard-panel photo prep

Brand/vertical manufacturing_industrialFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 19)Tools 11 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Prep the supplied Verdafix product photos and hazard/barcode graphics, then run the client's authored InDesign master label template through a data-merge against a per-SKU/variant CSV to produce one print-ready CMYK-with-bleed PDF per label variant.

Full brief

Verdafix Industrie GmbH, a European maker of construction sealants and adhesives, is converting its product-label artwork from legacy flat PDFs into a maintainable, print-ready system. The team has already rebuilt ONE master label into an authored InDesign template (verdafix_label_master.indd) - a 90 x 130 mm bottle/canister wrap laid out with 3 mm bleed, carrying real InDesign data-merge fields (<<product_name>>, <<variant>>, <<net_content>>, <<sku>>, <<batch_prefix>>, <<h_statements>>, <<p_statements>>, <<usage_de>>) plus image frames for the product shot, the GHS hazard pictogram, and the EAN barcode. What they need now is production, not design: bind the master to the full product range and output one press-ready file per SKU/variant. Supplied assets: a variants CSV (verdafix_label_variants.csv, one row per SKU/variant across the MS-Polymer, Hybrid and Acryl ranges in 290 ml and 600 ml sizes - 12 rows total) whose column headers exactly match the merge fields and which references each variant's product-photo and barcode filenames; a folder of raw product photos shot on the line (slightly tilted, dull, cool-cast studio table shots that need leveling, tone and a consistent crop to the template's photo frame); pre-rendered EAN-13 barcode PNGs and the GHS02/GHS05/GHS07 hazard-pictogram PNGs that must be vectorized so they stay crisp at press resolution. Brand + compliance constraints: labels are CMYK with 3 mm bleed at 300 DPI; German label copy and the H/P hazard statements come only from the CSV and must not be paraphrased; the hazard pictograms and barcodes must be sharp vectors, not soft raster; the recreated look must match the existing labels (no new design). Deliverable: the graded photo set, vectorized hazard/barcode graphics, and a set of print-ready CMYK PDFs - one per SKU/variant - merged from the master template, ready to hand to the label printer.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)
verdafix_label_master.indddatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a single 90 x 130
verdafix_label_variants.csvdatagemini-2.5-flashGenerate a CSV with columns product_name,variant,net_content,sku,batch_prefix,h_statements,p_statements,usage_de,photo_f
photo_ms_polymer_grau_600.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal studio product shot of a 600 ml grey construction-sealant cartridge/canister standing on a plain white line-si
photo_hybrid_weiss_290.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal studio product shot of a 290 ml white hybrid-sealant cartridge on a white bench, tilted a few degrees, underex
photo_acryl_grau_290.jpgimagegpt-image-2Photoreal studio product shot of a 290 ml grey acrylic-sealant cartridge standing on a workshop table, slightly off-leve
ean13_barcodes_batch.pngdatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): pre-rendered flat black-on-white EAN-13 barcode PNGs, one per SKU, gen
ghs_hazard_pictograms.pngdatan/a-authored-assetAUTHORED DELIVERABLE (standard regulated symbols): the official GHS02 (flame), GHS05 (corrosion) and GHS07 (exclamation)

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 19 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
Verdafix_labels_print_pdf_setpdfA set of print-ready CMYK PDFs with 3 mm bleed at ~300 DPI - one per SKU/variant row (12) - exported from the data-merged InDesign master; each carries the correct German copy, H/P statements, product photo, hazard pictogram and EAN barcode bound from its CSV row.
graded_product_photosimageThe supplied product photos, straightened, auto-toned, white-balance-corrected and consistently cropped/resized to the template's product-photo frame ratio so every label variant looks uniform.
verdafix_barcodes.svgvectorVectorized EAN-13 barcode graphics for crisp, scannable bars at any label size on press (no soft raster).
verdafix_ghs_pictograms.svgvectorVectorized GHS02/GHS05/GHS07 hazard pictograms that stay sharp at label print resolution.

7 · Task-level metrics & annotator flow

  • Commission pass rate — does this output pass all mandatory verifiers + admission, with no dealbreaker? (the headline "would a client accept it" number)
  • Craft ELO — from the blind face-offs (this output vs other runs / the human).
  • Human-parity — best-of-3 agent run vs the golden human output on "which would you send the client?" (win / tie / loss).
  • Per-capability profile — the K1–K7 vector above; variance is reported as a confidence interval across the 3 runs.
Annotator flow for this task: the reviewer opens the output beside the brief, the input assets, and the golden trajectory → runs the Layer-3 verifier checklist (auto results pre-filled, expert confirms the eye-checks) → marks the Layer-2 craft checklist Pass/Minor/Major against the reference images → marks Layer-4 process from the step-log → gives the Layer-5 call + punch-list + pin-the-flaw + confidence → a separate fresh "round-trip viewer" says what the design is selling (K5) → an art-director runs the blind face-off (craft ELO) and the best-of-3-vs-human pairing. Gold items + Krippendorff's α run in the background to measure agreement.

AO-85   Ironclad parts catalog pre-press audit: merge-proof render, spec-row extraction + CSV cross-check, overset/missing-field findings report

Brand/vertical manufacturing_industrialFamily Layout & DataOperation O5 Data-merge & layoutHorizon H3 composite (9–15 calls; this task 11)Tools 9 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K2 · K6 · K7

Render our authored InDesign parts-catalog template against the master parts CSV into a proof, then audit it page by page — read back every printed spec row, cross-check it against the source data, and hand back a findings report calling out overset/clipped part numbers, blank spec cells, and section-count mismatches before we send ~100 pages to print.

Full brief

Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply is a wholesale distributor of electrical and elevator replacement parts selling to service technicians, maintenance supervisors, and electrical contractors. Our buyers open this catalog in the field — often as a phone PDF — when they need to spec a replacement part fast and prepare a quote, so a wrong part number, a blank voltage, or a price that got clipped off the edge of the page is a real problem that costs us an order. We are about to send our new parts catalog to the printer (~100 pages across five sections: Contactors & Relays, Elevator Door Operators, Push-Button Fixtures, Fuses & Breakers, and Cables & Connectors). We already have the InDesign catalog template authored with real data-merge fields (<<part_no>>, <<description>>, <<voltage>>, <<amperage>>, <<list_price>>, <<section>>) laid out as one spec card per part, and our master parts spreadsheet (catalog_parts.csv, ~180 SKUs). Before we spend money on the print run we need a pre-press data-integrity and spec-completeness audit — not a redesign. Specifically: (1) run our authored template through the data merge against the CSV and export a proof PDF so we can see exactly what will print; (2) convert/inspect that proof page by page and read back what actually rendered on each card; (3) extract the printed spec rows and cross-check them against the source CSV to catch part numbers that ran overset (long codes clipped by the frame), any blank/empty voltage-amperage-price cells that would leave a technician guessing, and any section whose printed card count does not match the number of SKUs in the CSV for that section; and (4) deliver a written findings report — a table listing every flagged part number, its page, the field at fault, the CSV value vs. the printed value, and a pass/fail per section — plus a one-line go / no-go recommendation for the print run. Page size is US Letter portrait; keep our section order. Deliver the findings report table and the report summary.

catalog_audit_findings.csvdataFindings report table: one row per flagged item with columns part_no, page, section, field_at_fault, csv_value, printed_value, issue_type (overset_clip / blank_cell / count_mismatch), plus per-section pass/fail rows and a final go / no-go verdict line. The core analyze deliverable; its page column keys each finding to a page in the proof PDF.

AO-120   NOURA daily-greens DTC paid-social video ad package: UGC talking-head mashup cut with multi-hook variants, 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9 reframes, on-brand caption / callout graphic plates, and a graded scroll-stopper thumbnail

Brand/vertical health_wellnessFamily Motion & AudioOperation O3 Video & audioHorizon H4 long-horizon (16+ calls; this task 27)Tools 19 distinctCapability profile K3 · K5 · K1 · K6 · K7

Turn NOURA's batch of raw UGC creator talking-head clips plus product b-roll, a flat wordmark, a thumbnail hero still, a brand color reference, and a hooks/captions brief into a paid-social ad package: an AI mashup master cut plus two tighter hook-variant reels, each reframed to 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9 (same length), on-brand lower-third and callout graphic plates plus a vector logo, a fully graded scroll-stopper thumbnail, and a caption cue sheet — all copied into one handoff folder with a client share link.

Full brief

NOURA is a direct-to-consumer wellness brand — a daily greens + adaptogen powder that (in our words) 'actually tastes like something you'd drink on purpose.' We run paid social on TikTok, Instagram Reels, Meta feed and YouTube, and we're scaling our UGC ad program. For this assignment we're handing over a batch of raw creator footage and want it cut into a short-form ad package we can put straight into ad sets and A/B test. What we're sending: (a) three raw vertical UGC talking-head clips from three different creators — a kitchen 'morning routine' take, a bathroom-mirror 'get ready with me' take, and a post-workout porch take — all shot on phones, ungraded and flat; (b) one product b-roll clip — the powder being scooped into water, stirred, condensation on the glass, the tub and a single-serve sachet on a marble counter; (c) our wordmark logo as a flat PNG; (d) a photoreal hero still of a creator holding the tub, straight off the phone (flat, dull white balance), to become the ad thumbnail / cover; (e) a brand color-reference frame representing the bright, sunny, fresh-green look we grade toward; (f) a hooks CSV listing the different opening hooks / angles / CTAs we want to test; (g) a caption-cue JSON for the on-screen captions; and (h) a brand-kit note with our colors, fonts, tone, and a compliance line (structure/function language only — NO disease or medical claims on any asset). Deliverables we need, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools with every step honestly tagged for execution mode: A. VIDEO — (1) probe the raw clips so we know resolution / frame rate / duration before cutting; (2) summarize the footage + b-roll so we agree on the strongest visual moments and a logical flow, steered by our hooks CSV; (3) produce the primary deliverable — one polished ~35s UGC MASHUP master that weaves all three creators + b-roll around Hook A; (4) produce two tighter hook-variant reels as their own passes — an ~18s Hook B 'skeptic-turned-believer' cut and a ~15s Hook C 'results' cut (repurposed from the same footage, NOT exact-timestamp trims); (5) reframe: the master to 16:9 (YouTube / VSL) and 1:1 (feed), and each hook-variant reel to 9:16 (TikTok / Reels / Shorts) — same length, no trimming. B. THUMBNAIL / COVER — from the flat hero still, build a scroll-stopping graded 9:16 cover: auto-tone baseline, push the bright warm brand color temperature toward our reference, shape the fresh-green + warm-skin HSL, select and pop the creator/product subject, lift the highlights for the airy look, and crop clean to 1080x1920. C. ON-BRAND TEXT / GRAPHIC PLATES (standalone PNG / SVG references, NOT composited onto the video) — recommend an on-brand caption typeface; vectorize the wordmark to a clean SVG for the end-card and corner bug; build an opaque lower-third caption-bar plate and an opaque callout-badge plate by selecting a region on the graded canvas and solid-filling it with brand color (position/color references the editor rebuilds on the timeline); and a graded end-card the ads close on. Captions themselves ship as the supplied caption-cue sheet. D. PACKAGING — copy all the cuts + reframes, the thumbnail, the end-card, the logo SVG, the plates, and the caption sheet into one dated handoff folder and give us a share link. Heads-up we already understand: frame-accurate timeline cuts, animated motion graphics, burned-in captions/text, and hard transitions are a manual Premiere / After Effects finish and are OUT of scope for the automated connector pass. The automated pass delivers the AI mashup + hook variants, the reframes, the graded thumbnail and end-card, the vector logo, the caption/callout plates, and the caption sheet — the human editor does the final timeline polish, burns the captions/callouts using the plates, and locks the logo on top. Fast turnaround, lean budget — efficient workflow over flashy effects.

1 · Input assets — what the client hands over

assetkindgenerated bywhat it is (gen-prompt / requirement)

2 · How the input package is scored — Phase 1 admission gate (Pass / Fix / Reject)

Every dimension must be Pass before the task enters the benchmark. Items marked (code) are automatic; the expert does the rest. A task that can't reach Pass is Fixed (sent to the author) or Rejected.

dimensionwhat it certifies for THIS task
1 · Completenessevery asset the brief references is provided; each opens & is right format/res; brief states goal, deliverable, dims, brand, content.
2 · Feasibility integritydeliverable is producible with supplied assets + tools; fonts/colours available; no contradictory or accidentally-impossible requirement.
3 · Realism / difficultyinputs look like a real client handoff (slightly dull/tilted where intended, not pristine); difficulty matches the horizon tier.
4 · Brand coherencelogo, palette, fonts & imagery belong to one brand; no contradictory signals; brief's named colours/fonts match the kit.
5 · No-answer leakageno finished version / near-duplicate of the deliverable in the inputs; references guide style but don't hand over the solution. (code similarity)
6 · Provenance / licensingevery asset logged — generator+prompt or origin; SHA-256 recorded; flagged commercially-safe; third-party rights cleared. (code)
7 · Decision qualityinvented brand/persona details are sensible, documented, and don't contradict the brief/kit.
8 · Hallucination / contradictionbrief references only assets that exist; counts/dims/dates/prices internally consistent; no instruction contradicts another.

3 · Golden trajectory — Phase 2 (the human reference)

A creative expert completes this task on the same Adobe-connector tools: a step-log (each action from the function dropdown + a one-line reason) plus a silent screen recording. Expected ≈ 27 steps. This produces the golden output + golden trajectory the agent's 3 runs are measured against.

4 · Expected outputs — the deliverable(s)

outputkindspec
noura_master_16x9.mp4videoPrimary deliverable: polished ~35s UGC MASHUP master (Hook A), an AI Quick Cut weaving the three creator talking heads + product b-roll around the top hook, then reframed to 16:9 1920x1080 (H.264 target on export) for YouTube / VSL placements. Runtime is a best-effort target (the Quick Cut assembly + reframe are [A] async product actions whose exact final duration/spec are entitlement/widget-polled, not fully headless-retrievable). Frame-accurate cuts, burned-in captions, animated motion graphics, and hard transitions are the flagged manual Premiere finish and are NOT part of this automated deliverable.
noura_master_1x1.mp4video1:1 square (1080x1080) reframe of the Hook A mashup master, SAME length (no trimming), for Meta / Instagram feed placements. Produced by video_resize of the master cut. [A] async.
noura_reel_9x16_hookB.mp4video9:16 vertical reel (1080x1920) of the ~18s Hook B 'skeptic-turned-believer' variant — a separate tighter Quick Cut pass over the same footage (repurposed, NOT an exact-timestamp trim of the master), reframed to vertical, SAME length. For TikTok / Reels / Shorts. [A] async.
noura_reel_9x16_hookC.mp4video9:16 vertical reel (1080x1920) of the ~15s Hook C 'results / routine' variant — the tightest separate Quick Cut pass, reframed to vertical, SAME length. For TikTok / Reels / Shorts. [A] async.
noura_thumbnail_1080x1920.pngimageScroll-stopping graded 9:16 cover / thumbnail, PNG 1080x1920: auto-toned, warm/bright color-temperature-graded toward the brand reference, fresh-green + warm-skin HSL shaped, subject-popped (creator + product), highlight-lifted for the airy look, then subject-aware cropped/resized to exactly 1080x1920 from the hero still (a straight reframe of the existing frame — no canvas extension or padding). Built to carry the NOURA wordmark as a corner lockup; the actual logo placement/compositing is the flagged human-finish step (the connector chain produces this graded cover without the logo burned in).
noura_endcard_1080x1920.jpgimageOn-brand end-card the ads close on: the graded thumbnail cover with a subtle NOURA green/cream brand color overlay applied (image_apply_color_overlay), 1080x1920, leaving space for the logo lockup and CTA the editor adds during the manual finish.
noura_wordmark_logo.svgvectorClean vectorized SVG of the flat NOURA wordmark + leaf mark, for scalable end-card and corner-bug use. Produced by image_vectorize tracing the supplied flat PNG.
lower_third_caption_bar.pngimageOpaque lower-third caption-bar plate frame (1080x1920 PNG): a horizontal region of the graded end-card canvas solid-filled with brand green (#3FA45C) to give the editor an exact on-brand caption-bar swatch/position reference to drop on the timeline (solid-color fill only, no text composited; NOT a transparent cutout — headless selection-fill paints a region onto the existing opaque canvas). The on-brand caption typeface is delivered as a font recommendation, not burned into the plate.
callout_badge_plate.pngimageOpaque callout-badge plate frame (1080x1920 PNG): a badge-shaped region of the same end-card canvas solid-filled with warm cream (#F5EEDD) as an on-brand fact-callout swatch/position reference, same selection-fill technique (solid-color fill only; NOT a transparent cutout).
noura_caption_cues.jsondataOn-screen caption cue sheet: the supplied per-variant caption cues ({index, start_seconds, end_seconds, text}) finalized into the handoff folder as the on-screen text asset the editor burns onto the reels during the manual finish — the connector does not composite text onto video frames.
noura_ad_handoff_folderdataOne dated Creative Cloud handoff folder gathering all cuts + reframes (master 16:9, square 1:1, two 9:16 hook reels), the graded thumbnail, the end-card, the logo SVG, both plates, and the caption cue sheet, plus a client share link — the organized editable-asset set for the Premiere handoff.

5 · How the output is scored
